Genesis1:1 In the beginning
Genesis1:2 The earth was without shape and it was empty. Deep water covered the earth and everywhere was dark. The Spirit of God moved above the water.
Genesis1:3 God said
Genesis1:4 God saw that the light was good. He caused the light and the dark to be separate.
Genesis1:5 God called the light ‘day’. He called the dark ‘night’. Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the first day.
Genesis1:6 Then God said
Genesis1:7 So God made this wide space. He made the water under the space separate from the water that was above it. What God said happened.
Genesis1:8 God called the wide space ‘sky’. Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the second day.
Genesis1:9 God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry ground ‘land’. He called the water that had come together ‘sea’. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good.
Genesis1:11 Then God said
Genesis1:12 All kinds of plants and trees began to grow in the ground. The plants made seeds. The trees made fruits with seeds in them. Each plant made its own kind of seeds. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good.
Genesis1:13 Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the third day.
Genesis1:14 God said
Genesis1:15 The lights in the sky will give light to the earth.’ And what God said happened.
Genesis1:16 God made two great lights. The brighter light ruled over the day. The less bright light ruled over the night. God also made the stars.
Genesis1:17 God put all these lights in the sky to shine their light on the earth.
Genesis1:18 They were to rule over the day and night. They were to make the time of light separate from the time of dark. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good.
Genesis1:19 Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the fourth day.
Genesis1:20 God said
Genesis1:21 God made big animals to live in the sea. He made every different kind of living thing that filled the sea. He made every different kind of bird to fly in the sky. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good.
Genesis1:22 God blessed the animals and the birds. He said to them
Genesis1:23 Evening passed and then it was morning. That was the fifth day.
Genesis1:24 God said
Genesis1:25 God made the different kinds of wild animals and the different kinds of farm animals. He made all the different kinds of animals that move along the ground. God looked at what he had made. He saw that it was good.
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 God made humans. He made them to be like himself. He made them to be male and female.
Genesis1:28 God blessed them. He said to them
Genesis1:29 Then God said to them
Genesis1:30 I give to the animals green plants for their food. It will be food for all the animals on the earth
Genesis1:31 God looked at everything that he had made. He saw that it was very good. Evening passed. And then it was morning. That was the sixth day.
Genesis2:1 In that way God finished making the heavens and the earth
Genesis2:2 The seventh day came. Then God stopped the work that he had been doing. He had finished his work and he rested on the seventh day.
Genesis2:3 God blessed the seventh day. He made it a special day
Genesis2:4 This is what happened when God made the heavens and the earth. When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth
Genesis2:5 there were no plants or grasses growing on the earth. That was because the Lord God had not sent any rain on the earth. Also
Genesis2:6 But streams of water were coming up from the earth. The water made all the top of the ground wet.
Genesis2:7 Then the Lord God took some soil from the ground and he made a man. He breathed air into the nose of the man to give him life. So the man became alive.
Genesis2:8 The Lord God planted a garden. It was in the east
Genesis2:9 The Lord God made all kinds of trees grow from the ground there. The trees were beautiful to look at. The fruit from the trees was good for people to eat. In the middle of the garden were two special trees. One was the tree that gives life. The other was the tree that gives knowledge of what is good and what is evil.
Genesis2:10 A river began in Eden. It went through the garden and it gave water for the ground. After that
Genesis2:11 The name of the first river is the Pishon. It goes through the whole land of Havilah. There is gold in that land.
Genesis2:12 The gold there is very good. Bdellium and onyx stone are also in Havilah.
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is the Gihon. It goes through the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The third river is called the Tigris. It goes along the east side of Assyria. The fourth river is called the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 The Lord God took the man and he put him in the Garden of Eden. God wanted the man to work in the garden and to take care of it.
Genesis2:16 Then the Lord God told the man
Genesis2:17 But you must not eat any fruit from the tree that gives knowledge of good and evil. If you do eat fruit from that tree
Genesis2:18 Then the Lord God said
Genesis2:19 The Lord God took soil from the ground and he made all the animals and birds. He brought them to the man. God wanted to know what names the man would call them. Whatever the man called each living thing
Genesis2:20 So the man gave names to the farm animals
Genesis2:21 So the Lord God caused the man to sleep. While the man was sleeping
Genesis2:22 The Lord God used the man's rib to make a woman. He brought the woman to the man.
Genesis2:23 Then the man said
Genesis2:24 Because of this
Genesis2:25 The man and his wife were not wearing any clothes. But they did not feel ashamed.
Genesis3:1 The Lord God made many wild animals. But the snake was the most clever of them all. The snake asked the woman
Genesis3:2 The woman replied
Genesis3:3 But God said
Genesis3:4 Then the snake said to the woman
Genesis3:5 God knows that when you eat the fruit from this tree
Genesis3:6 The woman looked at the fruit on the tree. She saw that it would be good to eat
Genesis3:7 Then they understood what they had done. They realized that they were not wearing any clothes. So they took some leaves from fig trees. They tied them together to cover their bodies.
Genesis3:8 In the evening there was a nice cool wind. The Lord God walked in the garden. The man and the woman heard the sound of the Lord God. They hid themselves behind some trees
Genesis3:9 The Lord God called out to the man. He said to him
Genesis3:10 The man replied
Genesis3:11 The Lord God said
Genesis3:12 The man said
Genesis3:13 Then the Lord God said to the woman
Genesis3:14 The Lord God said to the snake
Genesis3:15 I will cause you and the woman to become enemies. Your descendants and her descendants will always be enemies. One of her descendants will attack your head. You will attack his heel.’
Genesis3:16 God said to the woman
Genesis3:17 Then God said to Adam
Genesis3:18 Thorn bushes and thistles will grow in the ground. But you will eat plants that grow in the fields.
Genesis3:19 You will have to work hard for a long time before you have any food to eat. You will do this for your whole life until you die. Then you will return into the ground. That is where you came from. I made you from the soil of the ground
Genesis3:20 Adam gave his wife a name. He called her Eve. This was because she would become the mother of all people.
Genesis3:21 The Lord God made clothes for Adam and Eve to wear. He used the skins from animals to make them.
Genesis3:22 The Lord God said
Genesis3:23 So the Lord God sent Adam out of the Garden of Eden. To get his food
Genesis3:24 Then God put cherubs to be guards for the garden. God put them on the east side of the garden. There was also a sword of fire that moved quickly from side to side. As a result
Genesis4:1 Adam had sex with his wife
Genesis4:2 Some time later
Genesis4:3 One day
Genesis4:4 But Abel brought to God some of the first lambs that his sheep had given birth to. He brought pieces of fat from the best of his lambs to offer them to God. Abel and his gift pleased the Lord.
Genesis4:5 But God was not pleased with Cain and his gift. So Cain became very angry. His face looked sad.
Genesis4:6 Then the Lord said to Cain
Genesis4:7 If you do what is right
Genesis4:8 One day Cain said to his brother Abel
Genesis4:9 Then the Lord said to Cain
Genesis4:10 Then the Lord God said
Genesis4:11 The ground received your brother's blood when you killed him. As a result
Genesis4:12 When you work hard to dig the ground
Genesis4:13 Cain said to the Lord
Genesis4:14 Now you are making me leave my home here on the land. I will be far away from where you are. I will be a traveller without a home. If someone sees me
Genesis4:15 But the Lord said to Cain
Genesis4:16 Cain went away from the Lord. He went to live in the land called Nod
Genesis4:17 Cain had sex with his wife. She became pregnant and she gave birth to Enoch. Cain started to build a city. He called the city Enoch because that was his son's name.
Genesis4:18 Enoch became the father of Irad. Irad became the father of Mehujael. Mehujael became the father of Methushael. Methushael became the father of Lamech.
Genesis4:19 Lamech married two women. One was called Adah. The other was called Zillah.
Genesis4:20 Adah gave birth to Jabal. Jabal became the father of people who live in tents and they take care of animals.
Genesis4:21 Jabal had a brother called Jubal. Jubal became the father of people who make music with harps and flutes.
Genesis4:22 Zillah gave birth to a son. His name was Tubal-Cain. He used bronze and iron to make tools. Tubal-Cain had a sister. Her name was Naamah.
Genesis4:23 Lamech said to his two wives
Genesis4:24 If someone kills Cain
Genesis4:25 Adam again had sex with his wife
Genesis4:26 Later Seth had a son and he called him Enosh. At that time
Genesis5:1 This is the report about Adam and his family. When God made men and women
Genesis5:2 God made male and female people and he called them ‘human’. He blessed them.
Genesis5:3 When Adam was 130 years old
Genesis5:4 After Seth was born
Genesis5:5 Adam was 930 years old when he died.
Genesis5:6 When Seth was 105 years old
Genesis5:7 After Enosh was born
Genesis5:8 Seth was 912 years old when he died.
Genesis5:9 When Enosh was 90 years old
Genesis5:10 After Kenan was born
Genesis5:11 Enosh was 905 years old when he died.
Genesis5:12 When Kenan was 70 years old
Genesis5:13 After Mahalalel was born
Genesis5:14 Kenan was 910 years old when he died.
Genesis5:15 When Mahalalel was 65 years old
Genesis5:16 After Jared was born
Genesis5:17 Mahalalel was 895 years old when he died.
Genesis5:18 When Jared was 162 years old
Genesis5:19 After Enoch was born
Genesis5:20 Jared was 962 years old when he died.
Genesis5:21 When Enoch was 65 years old
Genesis5:22 After Methuselah was born
Genesis5:23 Enoch lived on earth for 365 years.
Genesis5:24 Enoch lived to please God all this time
Genesis5:25 When Methuselah was 187 years old
Genesis5:26 After Lamech was born
Genesis5:27 Methuselah was 969 years old when he died.
Genesis5:28 When Lamech was 182 years old
Genesis5:29 He called his son Noah. Lamech said
Genesis5:30 After Noah was born
Genesis5:31 Lamech was 777 years old when he died.
Genesis5:32 After Noah was 500 years old
Genesis6:1 People began to grow in number all over the earth. They gave birth to daughters.
Genesis6:2 The sons of God saw that the daughters of men were beautiful. They chose for themselves any of them that they wanted to become their wives.
Genesis6:3 The Lord said
Genesis6:4 The Nephilites lived on the earth at that time
Genesis6:5 The Lord saw that men and women on the earth had become very bad. They thought only of evil things all the time.
Genesis6:6 The Lord was sorry that he had made humans and put them on the earth. He became very upset.
Genesis6:7 So the Lord said
Genesis6:8 But as for Noah
Genesis6:9 This is the report about Noah and his family. Noah was a good man. He was the only person at that time who lived in a completely right way. He stayed near to God in his life.
Genesis6:10 Noah became the father of Shem
Genesis6:11 God saw that everything on the earth had become spoiled. Everywhere
Genesis6:12 God saw how much the earth had become spoiled. Everyone was doing bad things.
Genesis6:13 God said to Noah
Genesis6:14 So make a ship for yourself. Use wood from cypress trees to make it. The ship must have rooms in it. Cover the inside and the outside of the ship with tar.
Genesis6:15 This is how you will build the ship: It must be 150 metres long
Genesis6:16 Make a roof for the ship. Leave a space 45 centimetres high all around under the roof. Put a door in the side of the ship. Inside the ship
Genesis6:17 I will cover the earth with deep water. I will destroy all living things
Genesis6:18 But I will make a covenant with you
Genesis6:19 You must also take two of every different kind of animal into the ship. One will be male and the other will be female. Then they will stay alive with you.
Genesis6:20 Bring two of every different kind of bird
Genesis6:21 You must also take every different kind of food. That will be food for you and food for the animals.’
Genesis6:22 Noah obeyed God. He did everything that God had told him to do.
Genesis7:1 Then the Lord said to Noah
Genesis7:2 Take seven pairs of every different kind of clean animal. Each pair will be a male and a female. Take two of every different kind of unclean animal. Take a male and a female together.
Genesis7:3 Take seven pairs of every kind of bird
Genesis7:4 In seven days
Genesis7:5 Noah did everything that God told him to do.
Genesis7:6 Noah was 600 years old when the deep water covered the earth.
Genesis7:7 Noah and his wife went into the ship. His sons and their wives also went in. They all went into the ship to be safe from the deep water.
Genesis7:8 Pairs of clean animals and unclean animals came to Noah in the ship. Also
Genesis7:9 Each pair was one male and one female. They all came to Noah in the ship. Everything happened as God had said to Noah.
Genesis7:10 Seven days later
Genesis7:11 When Noah was 600 years old
Genesis7:12 Rain fell on the earth for 40 days and 40 nights.
Genesis7:13 On the day that the rain began to fall
Genesis7:14 With them was every different kind of wild animal and every different kind of farm animal. They also had with them every kind of living thing that moves along the ground. Every different kind of bird and other flying things were there too.
Genesis7:15 Pairs of every kind of animal that lived came to Noah and went into the ship.
Genesis7:16 Each pair was one male and one female. This happened in the way that God had told Noah. When they were all in the ship
Genesis7:17 For 40 days
Genesis7:18 The water became even deeper and completely covered the earth. The ship went safely on top of the water.
Genesis7:19 More water came until it covered all the high mountains on the earth.
Genesis7:20 The water still became deeper so that it was more than 6 metres higher than the tops of the mountains.
Genesis7:21 As a result
Genesis7:22 Everything that lived on the dry land died. Nothing could breathe any more.
Genesis7:23 God destroyed everything that lived on the earth. He destroyed people
Genesis7:24 The deep water covered the earth for 150 days.
Genesis8:1 Then God thought of Noah and all the animals that were with him in the ship. He caused a wind to blow over the earth. As a result
Genesis8:2 Streams of water stopped coming up from below the earth. Rain stopped coming down from the sky.
Genesis8:3 The deep water continued to go down for 150 days.
Genesis8:4 On the 17th day of the seventh month
Genesis8:5 The water continued to go down until the tenth month. On the first day of the tenth month
Genesis8:6 After 40 more days
Genesis8:7 He sent a raven to fly away. The raven flew away from the ship and then it returned. It continued to do this until the earth was dry.
Genesis8:8 Then Noah sent out a dove. He wanted to see if the water had gone away from the top of the ground.
Genesis8:9 But the dove could not find anywhere to stand. Water still covered all the ground. So it returned to Noah in the ship. Noah put out his hand and took the dove back into the ship.
Genesis8:10 He waited for seven days. Then he sent the dove out of the ship again.
Genesis8:11 The dove returned to Noah in the evening. It carried a fresh leaf from an olive tree in its mouth. Then Noah knew that the water had gone down.
Genesis8:12 He waited for seven more days. Then he sent the dove out again. This time
Genesis8:13 Noah was now 601 years old. On the first day of the first month
Genesis8:14 By the 27th day of the second month
Genesis8:15 God said to Noah
Genesis8:16 ‘Come out of the ship. Bring your wife
Genesis8:17 Bring every different kind of living animal out of the ship. Bring out the birds
Genesis8:18 Noah went out of the ship
Genesis8:19 All the living animals also went out of the ship. The birds and the living things that move across the ground all went out. Every different kind of animal came out of the ship
Genesis8:20 Then Noah built an altar to make sacrifices to the Lord. He took one from each kind of clean animal and clean bird. He offered them to the Lord as sacrifices which he burned on the altar.
Genesis8:21 The Lord smelled the sacrifice and it pleased him. The Lord said to himself
Genesis8:22 As long as the earth continues to be here
Genesis9:1 God blessed Noah and his sons. He said to them
Genesis9:2 All the animals and all the birds will be afraid of you. You have authority over them. You also have authority over all the living things that move across the ground and all the fish in the sea.
Genesis9:3 You may now eat anything that lives and moves. Before
Genesis9:4 But you must not eat meat that still has the blood of life in it.
Genesis9:5 If any person or animal kills a human
Genesis9:6 God made people so that they were like himself. So if anyone kills a human
Genesis9:7 But as for you
Genesis9:8 God said to Noah and to his sons
Genesis9:9 ‘I promise you that I will obey the covenant that I am making with you. I promise this to you and to all your descendants.
Genesis9:10 I also promise this to all the animals and birds that came out of the ship with you. That is everything that lives on the earth.
Genesis9:11 This is the covenant that I promise to keep: I will never cause water to destroy all living things on the earth again. Deep water will never again come again to destroy the earth.’
Genesis9:12 Then God said
Genesis9:13 I have put my rainbow among the clouds in the sky. It will show you clearly that I have made this covenant with everything on the earth.
Genesis9:14 When I bring clouds over the earth
Genesis9:15 Then I will remember the covenant that I have made with you and with all the different kinds of living things. Never again will the water become so deep that it destroys everything that lives.
Genesis9:16 When I see the rainbow in the clouds
Genesis9:17 Then God said to Noah
Genesis9:18 The names of Noah's sons were Shem
Genesis9:19 All the people who live on the earth are descendants of Noah's three sons.
Genesis9:20 Noah was a farmer. He was the first person to plant vines.
Genesis9:21 He used the grapes to make wine. One day
Genesis9:22 Canaan's father
Genesis9:23 Then Shem and Japheth took Noah's coat and they put it between their shoulders. They walked into Noah's tent with their backs to Noah. In this way they covered their father with the coat. They looked away from their father so that they did not see him when he was not wearing any clothes.
Genesis9:24 Later
Genesis9:25 He said
Genesis9:26 Noah also said
Genesis9:27 I pray that God will make the land of Japheth and his descendants grow bigger. I pray that the descendants of Japheth will share good things with Shem. I pray that Canaan will become Japheth's slave.’
Genesis9:28 Noah lived for 350 years after God covered the earth with deep water.
Genesis9:29 In his whole life
Genesis10:1 This is the report about Noah's sons
Genesis10:2 These are the sons of Japheth: Gomer
Genesis10:3 These are the sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz
Genesis10:4 These are the sons of Javan: Elishah
Genesis10:5 These people lived by the sea. They lived in groups. Each group went to live in a different place. Each group spoke its own language.
Genesis10:6 These are the sons of Ham: Cush
Genesis10:7 These are the sons of Cush: Seba
Genesis10:8 Cush was also the father of Nimrod. Nimrod became the first brave soldier in the world.
Genesis10:9 The Lord helped Nimrod to be a great hunter. That is why people say
Genesis10:10 The first of Nimrod's lands were Babylon
Genesis10:11 From that land he went to Assyria. This is where he built Nineveh
Genesis10:12 He also built Resen. Resen is between Nineveh and Calah

Genesis10:14 Pathrusites

Genesis10:20 These are all descendants of Ham. They lived in groups in their own parts of the land. Each group spoke their own language.
Genesis10:21 Shem also had sons. Shem was Japheth's older brother. All the sons of Eber are descendants of Shem.
Genesis10:22 These are the sons of Shem: Elam
Genesis10:23 These are the sons of Aram: Uz

Genesis10:27 Hadoram
Genesis10:28 Obal
Genesis10:29 Ophir
Genesis10:30 The land where they lived went from Mesha to Sephar. This is in the land with hills in the east.
Genesis10:31 These are the descendants of Shem. They lived in groups in their own parts of the land. Each group spoke their own language.
Genesis10:32 All these people are descendants of Noah's sons. They went to live in different parts of the earth and their children became different nations. This happened after the time that deep water covered the earth.

Genesis11:2 People moved to the east. They reached a large flat piece of land called Shinar. They stayed there to live.
Genesis11:3 They said to each other
Genesis11:4 They said
Genesis11:5 But the Lord came down to see the city and the tower. He saw that the people had started to build them.
Genesis11:6 The Lord said
Genesis11:7 Now we must go down to them. We must cause their language to become confused. Then they will not be able to understand each other.’
Genesis11:8 So the Lord divided the people from one another. They went to live in different places all over the earth. They stopped building the city for themselves.
Genesis11:9 That is why people called the city Babel. It was because the Lord confused all the people of the world and he gave them different languages to speak. From that time

Genesis12:1 The Lord had said to Abram
Genesis12:2 I will cause your descendants to become a great nation. I will bless you. Everyone will know your name. You will bring my blessing to other people.
Genesis12:3 I will bless those people who bless you. But I will curse anyone who insults you. I will bless all the families of people on the earth because of you.’
Genesis12:4 Abram did what the Lord had told him. He left Haran. Abram was 75 years old when he left Haran. Lot went with him.
Genesis12:5 Abram took his wife Sarai and his nephew Lot. He took everything that belonged to them. He also took the people who worked for them in Haran. They all left to go to the land of Canaan. When they arrived in Canaan
Genesis12:6 Abram walked through the land. He went as far as Shechem
Genesis12:7 The Lord appeared to Abram there. He said
Genesis12:8 Then Abram left Shechem. He went to the hills that are on the east of Bethel. He put up his tent in that place. Bethel was towards the west
Genesis12:9 Then Abram took his tent and he left that place. He continued to travel towards the Negev.
Genesis12:10 There was a famine in the land of Canaan. So Abram went to live in Egypt for some time
Genesis12:11 When Abram came near to Egypt
Genesis12:12 When the Egyptians see you they will say
Genesis12:13 So tell them that you are my sister. Then they will do good things to me
Genesis12:14 When Abram arrived in Egypt
Genesis12:15 The king's officers saw her. They told Pharaoh that she was very beautiful. They took her to the king's palace.
Genesis12:16 Pharaoh did good things to help Abram because of Sarai. He gave Abram sheep
Genesis12:17 But the Lord made Pharaoh and the people in his palace very ill. The Lord did this because the king had taken Abram's wife Sarai.
Genesis12:18 So Pharaoh called Abram to come to him. Pharaoh said
Genesis12:19 You told me
Genesis12:20 Pharaoh told his officers what to do with Abram. They sent Abram away
Genesis13:1 Abram left Egypt and he travelled north to the Negev. He took his wife and everything that belonged to him. Lot also went with him.
Genesis13:2 Abram had many valuable things. He had many cows
Genesis13:3 Then Abram left the Negev and he went to different places. He arrived at Bethel. He returned to the place where he had put up his tent before. That place was between Bethel and Ai.
Genesis13:4 It was where Abram had first built an altar to worship the Lord.
Genesis13:5 Lot was travelling with Abram. Lot also had many cows
Genesis13:6 But the land could not grow enough food for all of them to eat. They could not all live together in the same place
Genesis13:7 Abram's shepherds and Lot's shepherds began to quarrel with each other. (At that time
Genesis13:8 Abram said to Lot
Genesis13:9 Look everywhere! There is enough land for all of us. We must go different ways. If you go to the left
Genesis13:10 Lot looked up and he saw the valley of the Jordan River
Genesis13:11 So Lot chose for himself the valley of the Jordan River. He travelled towards the east.
Genesis13:12 Abram stayed in the land of Canaan and he lived there. But Lot lived near the cities that were in the Jordan Valley. He put up his tents near the city of Sodom.
Genesis13:13 The people of Sodom were very wicked. They did not obey the Lord at all.
Genesis13:14 After Lot had gone away
Genesis13:15 I will give you all the land that you can see. I will give it to you and your descendants. It will belong to them for ever.
Genesis13:16 I will make your descendants become very many. Like the dust of the ground
Genesis13:17 Go and travel round all this land. Look at it
Genesis13:18 So Abram moved his tents. He went to live near the special oak trees of Mamre. That place is at Hebron. Abram built an altar to worship the Lord there.

Genesis14:9 They fought against Kedorlaomer king of Elam
Genesis14:10 Siddim Valley had many holes in the ground. The holes were filled with tar. The kings of Sodom and Gomorrah ran away. Some of their men fell into the deep tar. The other men ran away to the hills.

Genesis41:27 The seven thin cows that came out of the river mean seven years. And the seven groups of thin grains mean seven years. Both of these tell us about seven years of famine.
Genesis41:28 This is what your dreams mean. As I said
Genesis41:29 There will be plenty of food for seven years all over the land of Egypt.
Genesis41:30 But then seven years of famine will follow. People will forget about the seven years when they had plenty of food. The famine will destroy the land of Egypt.
Genesis41:31 No one will remember the seven good years
Genesis41:32 God gave you two dreams with the same meaning because these things will certainly happen. God will cause them to happen very soon.
Genesis41:33 Now Pharaoh should look for a clever man. He should look for a man who knows what is right and wrong. Tell that man to rule over the land of Egypt.
Genesis41:34 This is what you should do: Choose some officers. Give them authority to store food during the good years when there is plenty. They must take a fifth part of all the food that grows everywhere in Egypt. They must store this extra food during the good years that will come soon. Give these officers authority to store and to take care of the food in the cities.
Genesis41:35 
Genesis41:36 Then you will be ready for the seven years of famine that will happen everywhere in Egypt. The people in Egypt will have enough food

Genesis49:1 Then Jacob called for all his sons. He said
Genesis49:2 My sons
Genesis49:3 Jacob said
Genesis49:4 But you are as wild as the sea. You had sex on your father's bed with his slave wife. This bad thing brought me great shame. So you will not become great.
Genesis49:5 Simeon and Levi are brothers. They use their swords as weapons to destroy people.
Genesis49:6 I will not join them when they decide to do wicked things. I will not meet with them. They have killed people when they are angry. They have been cruel to oxen because it makes them happy.
Genesis49:7 Their anger is so strong that God will curse them. Their anger makes them do cruel things. So God will curse them. I will cause your descendants to divide from each other. They will live everywhere in the country of Israel.
Genesis49:8 Judah
Genesis49:9 Judah is like a young lion who has eaten good food. He has eaten what he killed and now he rests. Nobody would want to wake him up!
Genesis49:10 Judah's descendants will always rule as kings. They will continue to hold the stick and the sceptre that show the king's authority. They will rule until the man comes who truly has that authority. People from all nations will obey that king.
Genesis49:11 Judah will tie his young donkey to a vine. It will be a vine that has the best grapes. He will wash his clothes in the red wine that is made from those grapes.
Genesis49:12 His eyes will become red because he drinks so much wine. His teeth will become white because he drinks so much milk.
Genesis49:13 Zebulun will live by the shore of the sea. His town will be a safe place for ships to stay. His land will reach as far as Sidon.
Genesis49:14 Issachar is like a strong donkey that is lying down to rest between its two heavy bags.
Genesis49:15 He will see that he has a good place to live
Genesis49:16 Dan will be a ruler for his people. His descendants will be equal with the other tribes of Israel.
Genesis49:17 He will be small and dangerous
Genesis49:18 Lord
Genesis49:19 Robbers will attack Gad
Genesis49:20 Asher will have much good food. It will be good enough for kings to eat.
Genesis49:21 Naphtali is like a deer that runs freely and gives birth to beautiful babies.
Genesis49:22 Joseph is like a vine that has lots of fruit. It grows near a well and its branches reach over a wall.
Genesis49:23 His enemies will be angry with him. They will attack him with arrows.
Genesis49:24 But he will hold his own bow strongly. He will shoot his arrows well. The Mighty One of Jacob will give Joseph strength. God
Genesis49:25 The Almighty God
Genesis49:26 The blessings are great that I
Genesis49:27 Benjamin is like a hungry wolf. He kills an animal and he eats it in the morning. In the evening
Genesis49:28 All these are ancestors of the 12 tribes of Israel. This is what their father said to them when he blessed them. He spoke to each of his sons the words that were right for them and for their descendants.
Genesis49:29 Then Jacob said to his 12 sons
Genesis49:30 The cave is at Machpelah
Genesis49:31 That is where they buried Abraham and his wife Sarah. They also buried Isaac and his wife Rebekah there. I buried Leah there too.
Genesis49:32 Abraham had bought the cave and the field from the Hittites.’
Genesis49:33 Jacob finished telling his sons what they should do. Then he lay down on his bed again. He breathed for the last time and he died there.
Genesis50:1 Joseph bent down near his father's face. He wept over him and he kissed him.
Genesis50:2 Then Joseph told some of his servants to take Jacob's body. They were men who knew how to put oil on the body so that it would not spoil. Joseph's servants did what he told them to do.
Genesis50:3 The servants took care of the body for 40 days
Genesis50:4 The time for everyone to weep came to an end. Joseph said to Pharaoh's officers
Genesis50:5 My father made me promise him something. He said
Genesis50:6 Pharaoh said
Genesis50:7 So Joseph went to Canaan to bury his father. All Pharaoh's officers went with him. The important people who lived in Pharaoh's palace
Genesis50:8 Everyone in Joseph's family also went with him. His brothers and their families went
Genesis50:9 A very large group of soldiers went with them. Some of them rode in chariots and some rode on horses.
Genesis50:10 They came to Atad's threshing floor
Genesis50:11 The Canaanite people who lived near there saw how sad Joseph and his people were. So they gave Atad's threshing floor a different name. They called it ‘Abel Mizraim’. They said
Genesis50:12 So Jacob's sons did what he had told them to do.
Genesis50:13 They carried his body to Canaan. They buried him there in the cave
Genesis50:14 When Joseph had buried his father
Genesis50:15 Now Jacob was dead and Joseph's brothers became frightened. They said to each other
Genesis50:16 So they sent a message to Joseph. They said

Genesis50:18 His brothers came to him. They bent down low to the ground in front of Joseph. They said to him
Genesis50:19 But Joseph said to them
Genesis50:20 You decided to do a bad thing to me. But God caused a good thing to happen because of that. God brought me here to save the lives of many people. You can see now that it has really happened!
Genesis50:21 So do not be afraid. I will continue to take care of you. You and your children will receive what you need.’ In this way Joseph comforted them and he spoke kind words to them.
Genesis50:22 Joseph lived in Egypt
Genesis50:23 He was still alive to see Ephraim's children and grandchildren. He also saw Makir's children. Makir was Manasseh's son. Joseph accepted Makir's children as his own children.

Genesis50:25 He also said
Genesis50:26 So Joseph died when he was 110 years old. They put special oil on his body so that it would not spoil. Then they put his body in a box there in Egypt.

Exodus9:31 At that time

Exodus12:4 But the family may be too small to eat a whole lamb. Then they must share a lamb with another family that lives near. You must decide how much each person can eat and then count the number of people.
Exodus12:5 The lamb that you choose must have nothing wrong with it. It must be a male lamb that is one year old. It can be either a young sheep or a young goat.

Exodus20:6 But I will truly love all those who love me. They are the people who obey my laws. I will continue to love them and their families for ever.
Exodus20:7 You must not speak the name of the Lord your God in a wrong way. The Lord will punish anyone who speaks his name in a wrong way.

Exodus20:9 You have six days every week to do all your work.

Exodus20:14 You must not have sex with another person's husband or wife.

Exodus22:1 Perhaps someone may take another person's cow or sheep for himself. He may kill it or he may sell it. As punishment
Exodus22:2 Perhaps someone may catch a robber as he goes into another person's house. The owner of the house might knock down the robber so that he dies. If this happens at night
Exodus22:3 But if the owner of the house kills the robber after sunrise
Exodus22:4 You may find an animal that someone has taken for himself. If the animal is still alive
Exodus22:5 A man's animals might go into someone else's field or vineyard to eat what is growing there. Then the owner of those animals must pay back the value of what they have eaten. He must give his best grain and his best grapes to pay back the other man.
Exodus22:6 Someone might light a fire in his field to burn thorn bushes. But the fire might grow and it might burn the crops in another farmer's field. It may destroy the crops that are growing there or the grain that is ready for harvest. It might even destroy the whole field. Then the person who lit the fire must pay the farmer for the crops that the fire has destroyed.
Exodus22:7 Perhaps you may give some of your money or your valuable things to your neighbour. You may ask your neighbour to keep them safe for you. But a robber might take those things from your neighbour's house. If you catch the robber
Exodus22:8 But if you do not find the robber
Exodus22:9 Perhaps two people both say that something belongs to them. They may argue about a cow
Exodus22:10 Perhaps you may ask your neighbour to keep one of your animals safe for you. It may be a donkey
Exodus22:11 Your neighbour must make a serious promise to the Lord. He must promise that he did not take your animal for himself
Exodus22:12 But if a robber did take the animal from your neighbour
Exodus22:13 But perhaps a wild animal attacked it. Then your neighbour must show the pieces of your animal that are left. Then he will not have to pay you for the loss of your animal.
Exodus22:14 Your neighbour might lend one of his animals to you to do some work. Something bad might happen to the animal while its owner is not with it. It might die
Exodus22:15 But if the owner is with his animal when something bad happens to it
Exodus22:16 Perhaps a man may meet a young girl who has not had sex with any man before. He might deceive her to have sex with her. If she has not yet promised to marry another man
Exodus22:17 Her father might refuse to give her to the man to be his wife. But the man must still give the right gifts for a girl who is not married.
Exodus22:18 You must punish with death any woman who is a magician.
Exodus22:19 You must punish with death anyone who has sex with an animal.
Exodus22:20 You must completely destroy anyone who offers a sacrifice to a false god. You must only offer sacrifices to the Lord.
Exodus22:21 Never be cruel to a foreign person or give them trouble. Remember that you yourselves lived in Egypt as foreigners.
Exodus22:22 Never give trouble to any widow or to any children who have no family.
Exodus22:23 If you are cruel to them
Exodus22:24 I will be very angry with you. I will kill you in war. Your wives will become widows. Your children will no longer have any family.
Exodus22:25 You may lend money to any of my people who need help. But do not ask them to pay back more money than you have lent to them. That is what traders in money do.
Exodus22:26 You may take someone's coat as a guarantee that he will pay you back. But you must always return his coat to him before sunset.
Exodus22:27 He needs his coat to keep him warm at night. It is the only warm thing that he has to sleep in. If you keep his coat
Exodus22:28 Never insult God. Never curse anyone who is a leader of your people.
Exodus22:29 Do not refuse to offer your grain
Exodus22:30 Do the same thing with your cows and your sheep. Let them stay with their mothers for seven days. But on the eighth day
Exodus22:31 You must live as my special people. So do not eat any meat from your animals that wild animals have killed. Instead
Exodus23:1 Do not make false reports. Do not tell lies in court to help wicked people.
Exodus23:2 Do not join a group of bad people to do evil things. When you speak in court
Exodus23:3 Do not speak on behalf of a poor man only because you like him.
Exodus23:4 Perhaps you may find your enemy's cow or his donkey when he has lost it. If so
Exodus23:5 Perhaps you may see your enemy's donkey when it is carrying a heavy load. If the donkey has fallen down
Exodus23:6 Always do what is right for your poor people when they stand in front of a judge.
Exodus23:7 Do not help anyone to use lies to accuse another person. Never punish anyone with death if they are not guilty. I will never say that a wicked person is not guilty.
Exodus23:8 Do not accept a bribe. A bribe will hide the truth even from honest people. It can make good people tell lies.
Exodus23:9 Do not be cruel to a foreign person who lives among you. You yourselves know what that feels like. Remember that you lived in Egypt as foreigners.
Exodus23:10 You must plant seed in your fields for six years. Then you can bring your crops home at harvest time.
Exodus23:11 But in the seventh year
Exodus23:12 Do your work for six days each week
Exodus23:13 Be careful to obey everything that I have told you. Do not pray to other gods for help. Do not even speak about them.
Exodus23:14 Three times each year
Exodus23:15 Eat the Feast of Flat Bread every year. For seven days
Exodus23:16 Eat the Feast of Harvest every year. Offer to me the first crops that you bring from your fields. At the end of each year
Exodus23:17 Three times each year
Exodus23:18 When you kill an animal to offer it to me as a sacrifice
Exodus23:19 When you cut the first crops from your fields
Exodus23:20 Look! I will send an angel to lead you on your journey. He will keep you safe. He will take you to the place that I have prepared for you.
Exodus23:21 Be careful to obey him. Listen to what he says to you. Do not turn against him. If you do that
Exodus23:22 So be careful to obey him. Do everything that I tell you. Then I will be the enemy of your enemies. I will fight against anyone who fights against you.
Exodus23:23 My angel will go in front of you. He will bring you into the land of the Amorites
Exodus23:24 Do not worship their gods. Do not serve them. Do not copy the evil things that these people do. Destroy their idols and break their special stones into pieces.
Exodus23:25 You must serve only me
Exodus23:26 Your women will safely give birth to babies. They will all be able to have children. Everyone will live a long life.
Exodus23:27 I will cause all the nations that you meet to be afraid. All the people who attack you will become confused. I will cause all your enemies to turn round and run away from you.
Exodus23:28 I will send great fear on the people as you move into the land. That will chase out the Hivites
Exodus23:29 But I will not chase out those people in one year. If I did that
Exodus23:30 I will remove the people slowly
Exodus23:31 I will decide where the borders of your land will be. You will have the land from the Red Sea to the Mediterranean Sea
Exodus23:32 Do not make any agreement with them or with their gods.
Exodus23:33 Do not let them live in your land. They would cause you to do bad things against me. If you serve their gods
Exodus24:1 Then God said to Moses
Exodus24:2 Only Moses can come near to me
Exodus24:3 Moses went and he told the people all the Lord 's commands and his teaching. The people answered together
Exodus24:4 Then Moses wrote down everything that the Lord had said. Early the next morning
Exodus24:5 Moses sent young Israelite men to kill animals there
Exodus24:6 Moses took half of the blood of these animals and he put it into some bowls. He splashed the other half of the blood over the altar.
Exodus24:7 Then he took the Book of God's Covenant and he read it to the people. They replied
Exodus24:8 Then Moses used the blood in the bowls to splash on the people. He said
Exodus24:9 Then Moses and Aaron
Exodus24:10 They saw the God of Israel there. Under his feet was something like a jewel called sapphire. It was as bright as a clear blue sky.
Exodus24:11 God did not hurt those Israelite leaders. They saw God. They ate a meal and they drank together.
Exodus24:12 The Lord said to Moses
Exodus24:13 So Moses went up the mountain of God. His servant Joshua went with him.
Exodus24:14 Moses said to the leaders
Exodus24:15 When Moses went up the mountain
Exodus24:16 The Lord 's bright glory appeared on Mount Sinai. The cloud covered the mountain for six days. On the seventh day
Exodus24:17 The people could see the Lord 's bright glory. To them
Exodus24:18 Moses went up the mountain and he went into the cloud. He stayed on the mountain for 40 days and 40 nights.
Exodus25:1 The Lord said to Moses:
Exodus25:2 ‘Tell the Israelites to bring me an offering. Every person who wants to give can make a gift to me. You must receive their gifts.
Exodus25:3 These are the gifts that you must receive from the people as an offering: gold
Exodus25:4 blue
Exodus25:5 red leather from sheep's skins and other good leather; wood from acacia trees;
Exodus25:6 olive oil for the lamps; spices for the special oil and for incense with a nice smell;
Exodus25:7 onyx and other jewels to fix on the priest's ephod and his breastpiece.
Exodus25:8 You must tell the Israelites to make a special place to be my home. Then I will live among them.
Exodus25:9 I will show you how to make this tabernacle and everything that you will put inside it. But you must make everything in the way that I tell you.
Exodus25:10 The Israelites must use acacia wood to make a big box. It must be 1 metre long
Exodus25:11 Cover the wood with gold
Exodus25:12 Make four gold rings and fix them to the four feet of the box. Fix two rings on one side and two rings on the other side.
Exodus25:13 Then use acacia wood to make two poles. Cover them with gold too.
Exodus25:14 Push the poles through the rings on the sides of the box. You will use the poles to carry the box.
Exodus25:15 The poles must remain along the side of the box
Exodus25:16 Then put into the box the two flat stones with the words of the covenant on them. I will give those to you.
Exodus25:17 Use pure gold to make a lid for the box. Make the lid 1 metre long and 75 centimetres wide.
Exodus25:18 Use gold to make images of two cherubs. Use a hammer to make them the right shape. They will stand at the two ends of the lid.
Exodus25:19 One cherub will stand at one end of the lid
Exodus25:20 The cherubs must look towards each other and watch over the lid. Their wings must touch each other over the top of the lid.
Exodus25:21 Then put the lid on the top of the box. Put the flat stones with the words of the covenant into the box. I will give those to you.
Exodus25:22 I will meet with you there
Exodus25:23 Use acacia wood to make a table. It must be one metre long
Exodus25:24 Cover the wood with gold. Build up the edges of the table with gold.
Exodus25:25 Also
Exodus25:26 Make four gold rings for the table. Fix them to the four corners
Exodus25:27 The rings must be near to the extra piece of wood. They will hold the poles that you will use to carry the table.
Exodus25:28 Use acacia wood to make the poles. Cover them with gold. Use the poles to carry the table.
Exodus25:29 Use pure gold to make plates
Exodus25:30 Put the Lord 's special bread on this table. The bread must always be there for me.
Exodus25:31 Use one piece of pure gold to make a lampstand. Use a hammer to make it the right shape. Use the gold to make its base
Exodus25:32 Make six branches on the lampstand
Exodus25:33 Put three cups in the shape of almond flowers and their leaves on each branch. Each of the six branches of the lampstand will have three cups like that.
Exodus25:34 On the lampstand itself
Exodus25:35 Put one closed flower under the first pair of branches. Put a second closed flower under the second pair of branches. Put the third closed flower under the third pair of branches. So there will be three closed flowers for the six branches on the lampstand.
Exodus25:36 You must make the flowers and the branches from the same piece of pure gold as the lampstand. Use a hammer to make the gold into the right shape.
Exodus25:37 Then make seven lamps and fix them to the lampstand. Fix them so that they light the space in front of the lampstand.
Exodus25:38 Also use pure gold to make small tools and dishes to take care of the lamps.
Exodus25:39 Use 34 kilograms of pure gold to make the lampstand and all the tools to take care of it.
Exodus25:40 Be careful to make all these things like the plan that I have shown you on the mountain.
Exodus26:1 Make the tabernacle with ten curtains of very good linen. Also use blue
Exodus26:2 All the curtains must be the same size: 12 metres long and 2 metres wide.
Exodus26:3 Join five of the curtains together. Do the same thing with the other five curtains.
Exodus26:4 Fix rings of blue material along the edge of the last curtain in each set of five curtains.
Exodus26:5 Put 50 rings on the edge of the first set. Put 50 rings on the edge of the other set. Do this so that the rings on one curtain are opposite to the rings on the other curtain.
Exodus26:6 Then use 50 small gold hooks to join the curtains together. In this way
Exodus26:7 Use goats' hair to make 11 curtains. They will cover the tabernacle
Exodus26:8 All the curtains must be the same size: 13.5 metres long and 2 metres wide.
Exodus26:9 Join five of the curtains together to make one set. Fix the other six curtains together to make a second set. Turn the sixth curtain of this set over itself to cover the entrance of the tent.
Exodus26:10 Use material to make 50 rings along the edge of the end curtain in each set.
Exodus26:11 Then make 50 bronze hooks. Use them to join the two sets of curtains together so that it makes a single tent.
Exodus26:12 The curtains for the tent will be longer than the curtains for the tabernacle. The extra half of the curtain for the tent will hang at the back of the tabernacle.
Exodus26:13 The curtains of the tent will be half a metre longer on both sides. The extra material will hang over each side of the tabernacle to cover it completely.
Exodus26:14 Use red leather from sheep's skins to cover the tent. Then use another kind of good leather to cover the red leather.
Exodus26:15 Use acacia wood to make boards to hold up the tabernacle.
Exodus26:16 Each board must be 4 metres high and 65 centimetres wide.
Exodus26:17 Make two wooden pegs on each board
Exodus26:18 Make 20 boards for the south side of the tabernacle.
Exodus26:19 Then use silver to make 40 heavy bases to hold up the boards. Two bases will hold up each board. The pegs on the boards will fit into holes in the bases.
Exodus26:20 Also make 20 boards for the north side of the tabernacle.
Exodus26:21 Make 40 silver bases
Exodus26:22 Make six boards for the west side
Exodus26:23 Then make two boards for the corners at the back of the tabernacle.
Exodus26:24 You must fix the two boards together
Exodus26:25 So there will be eight boards at the back of the tabernacle and 16 silver bases
Exodus26:26 You must also use acacia wood to make bars to fix across the boards. Make five bars for the south side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:27 five bars for the north side
Exodus26:28 The bar that goes across the centre of the boards must reach from one end of the tabernacle to the other end.
Exodus26:29 Use gold to cover the boards and to make rings for the boards. These rings will hold the bars in place across the boards. Also cover the wooden bars with gold.
Exodus26:30 Make the tabernacle the same as the plan that I showed to you on the mountain.
Exodus26:31 Use blue
Exodus26:32 Use gold rings to hang the curtain from four poles of acacia wood. Cover these poles with gold. Then fix them on four silver bases.
Exodus26:33 Hang the top of the curtain from the rings. Put the Covenant Box behind the curtain
Exodus26:34 Put the special lid on the Covenant Box
Exodus26:35 Put the special table in the Holy Place
Exodus26:36 Make a curtain for the entrance of the tent. Use blue
Exodus26:37 Use acacia wood to make five poles. Cover them with gold. Fix gold rings to the poles. Use bronze to make five bases to hold up the poles. Then hang the curtain from the rings on the poles.
Exodus27:1 Use acacia wood to make an altar. It must be square
Exodus27:2 Make four horns
Exodus27:3 Make all the tools for the altar with bronze. Make pots to remove the ashes
Exodus27:4 Use bronze to make a square net and rings at each corner.
Exodus27:5 Fix this net inside the altar
Exodus27:6 Use acacia wood to make poles for the altar. Cover them with bronze.
Exodus27:7 You must put the poles through the rings when you carry the altar. There will be a pole on each of two sides of the altar.
Exodus27:8 Use boards to make the altar
Exodus27:9 Make a yard around the tent of the tabernacle. The south side must be 45 metres long. It will have curtains that are made of good linen.
Exodus27:10 Make 20 poles and 20 bronze bases to hold the poles. Use silver to make hooks and sticks to hold the curtains.
Exodus27:11 The north side of the yard must also be 45 metres long. It will also have 20 poles with their bronze bases
Exodus27:12 The west end of the yard must be 23 metres long. Make curtains for it
Exodus27:13 The entrance to the yard is at its east end. That end must also be 23 metres long.
Exodus27:14 There will be 7 metres of curtain on each side of the entrance. There will be three poles with their bases to hold the curtains on each side.
Exodus27:15 
Exodus27:16 Make a curtain 9 metres wide for the entrance to the yard. Make it out of blue
Exodus27:17 All the poles around the yard must have bronze bases. They will also have silver hooks and sticks to hold the curtains.
Exodus27:18 The yard will be 45 metres long and 23 metres wide. Use good linen to make the curtains. They must be 2¼ metres high on the bronze bases.
Exodus27:19 Use bronze to make all the tools that you will use in the tabernacle. Also use bronze to make the pegs that fix the tent of the tabernacle to the ground. And make bronze pegs to fix the curtain around the yard to the ground.
Exodus27:20 Command the Israelites to bring you pure oil from fresh olives. You will use this oil for the lamps. The lamps must give light all the time.
Exodus27:21 Aaron and his sons must make sure that the lamps always give light from evening until morning. The lampstand will be in the Tent of Meeting
Exodus28:1 Bring your brother Aaron and his sons from among the other Israelites. They will serve me as my priests. That will be Aaron and his sons
Exodus28:2 Make special clothes for Aaron. They will be beautiful clothes to show that he deserves honour.
Exodus28:3 I have given special skills to some workers so that they know how to make beautiful things. Tell them to make clothes for Aaron. They will show that I have chosen him to serve me as my priest.
Exodus28:4 These are the clothes that they must make: a breastpiece
Exodus28:5 The workers who have special skills must use beautiful material that is gold
Exodus28:6 Use gold
Exodus28:7 Fix pieces of cloth on its two edges. These will go over the priest's shoulders to join the front and the back parts of the ephod.
Exodus28:8 Make a beautiful belt to tie around the ephod. The belt and the ephod must be one piece of work. Use the same materials to make both of them: gold
Exodus28:9 Take two onyx stones and write on them with a sharp tool. Write the names of the sons of Israel.
Exodus28:10 Begin with the name of the oldest son and finish with the name of the youngest son. Write six names on one stone and six names on the other stone.
Exodus28:11 A worker who has skill to cut letters into metal must do this work. Then fix the stones into beautiful gold around their edges.
Exodus28:12 Fix the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod so that they show the names of the 12 sons of Israel. Aaron will carry the names on his shoulders as he serves the Lord. Then the Lord will remember to bless his people.
Exodus28:13 Use gold to make beautiful pieces to hold the stones.
Exodus28:14 And use pure gold to make two thin chains
Exodus28:15 A worker must use special skills to make a breastpiece so that it looks beautiful. The breastpiece will help the priest to know what God is saying. Make it like the ephod. Use gold
Exodus28:16 Bend a piece of cloth over to make a square pocket
Exodus28:17 Then fix four rows of jewels on it. These are the names of the jewels: a ruby
Exodus28:18 a turquoise
Exodus28:19 a jacinth
Exodus28:20 a chrysolite
Exodus28:21 There will be 12 jewels
Exodus28:22 Use pure gold to make two thin chains like strings. These will fix the breastpiece to the ephod.
Exodus28:23 Make two gold rings and fix them to the two top corners of the breastpiece.
Exodus28:24 Fix the two gold chains to the two rings on the corners of the breastpiece.
Exodus28:25 Fix the other ends of the two chains to the shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus28:26 Make two gold rings and fix them to the two bottom corners of the breastpiece. Put them on its inside edge
Exodus28:27 Make two more gold rings. Fix them to the bottom edge of the shoulder pieces
Exodus28:28 Use a string of blue material to tie the rings on the breastpiece to the rings on the ephod. Fix them together above the ephod's belt. Then the breastpiece and the belt will not become separate.
Exodus28:29 When Aaron goes into the Holy Place
Exodus28:30 Put the Urim and Thummim in the pocket of the breastpiece. They will also be over Aaron's heart when he goes in there to serve the Lord. The Urim and Thummim will always be there to show Aaron what the Lord is saying about the Israelites. He will carry this help over his heart all the time.
Exodus28:31 Use only blue cloth to make the robe for the ephod.
Exodus28:32 Make a hole in the top of the robe
Exodus28:33 Use blue
Exodus28:34 There will be a bell then a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 Aaron must wear the robe when he is serving the Lord. The bells will make a noise when he goes into the Holy Place to serve the Lord. They will also make a sound when he comes out. Then he will not die.
Exodus28:36 Use pure gold to make a thin plate and write on it carefully with a sharp tool. Write this sign on it: “ Holy to the Lord ”.
Exodus28:37 Fix a blue string to the plate and tie it to the front of the linen hat.
Exodus28:38 Aaron will always wear this sign on the front of his head. When the Israelites bring gifts to God
Exodus28:39 Use good linen to make the long shirt and the hat for the priest. A worker with special skills must make the long belt
Exodus28:40 Make shirts
Exodus28:41 Dress your brother Aaron and his sons with these clothes. Then pour oil on their heads to show that they have my authority. Make them separate from the other Israelites as my holy servants. They will serve me as my priests.
Exodus28:42 Use good linen to make trousers that will cover the lower parts of their bodies.
Exodus28:43 Aaron and his sons must wear them when they go into the Tent of Meeting. They must also wear them when they go near to the altar to serve me in the Holy Place. Then they will not be guilty. I will not punish them with death. This will always be a rule for Aaron and his descendants.
Exodus29:1 When you make the priests God's special servants
Exodus29:2 Use good flour from wheat to make three kinds of bread
Exodus29:3 Put the bread and the biscuits into a basket. Bring them to the Tent of Meeting with the bull and the two sheep.
Exodus29:4 Then bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Use water to wash them there and make them clean.
Exodus29:5 Then take the special clothes. Dress Aaron in the shirt
Exodus29:6 Put the linen hat on Aaron's head. Fix the special gold sign to the hat.
Exodus29:7 Take the special olive oil. Pour it on his head to show that God has chosen him to be his servant.
Exodus29:8 Bring Aaron's sons and dress them in their special shirts.
Exodus29:9 Put the cloth hats on their heads. Tie the long cloth belts around Aaron and on his sons. These men and their descendants will always be priests. That is a law that continues for ever. That is how you must make Aaron and his sons God's special servants.
Exodus29:10 Bring the bull to the front of the Tent of Meeting. Aaron and his sons must put their hands on the bull's head.
Exodus29:11 Then kill the animal as an offering to the Lord
Exodus29:12 Take some of the bull's blood. Use your finger to put some of it on the horns of the altar. Pour the blood that remains on the ground around the altar.
Exodus29:13 Take all the fat that is round the inside parts of the animal. Take the best part of the liver. Take both the kidneys with the fat that is round them. Burn all these things on the altar.
Exodus29:14 But burn the rest of the bull's body outside the camp
Exodus29:15 Take one of the male sheep. Aaron and his sons must put their hands on its head.
Exodus29:16 Kill the sheep and take its blood to the altar. Throw some of the blood onto every side of the altar.
Exodus29:17 Cut the sheep into pieces. Wash its inside parts and its legs. Then put them together with its head and the other pieces.
Exodus29:18 Completely burn the whole sheep on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the Lord. Its smell pleases him.
Exodus29:19 Take the second male sheep. Aaron and his sons must put their hands on its head.
Exodus29:20 Kill the sheep. Put some of its blood on Aaron's right ear
Exodus29:21 Mix together some of the blood on the altar and some of the special oil. Splash this onto Aaron and onto his clothes. Splash it also onto his sons and onto their clothes. Then Aaron and his sons and their clothes will be holy.
Exodus29:22 Take all the fat from this male sheep. Take its fat tail
Exodus29:23 Take one piece of each kind of flat bread out of the basket. Take some bread that has no oil in it
Exodus29:24 Put all these things in the hands of Aaron and his sons. Then lift these things up to the Lord as an offering to him.
Exodus29:25 Then take them from Aaron and his sons and burn them on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the Lord. Its smell pleases him.
Exodus29:26 Take the meat from the ribs of the sheep that makes Aaron God's special servant. Lift it up to the Lord as an offering to him. Then you may eat this meat from the sheep.
Exodus29:27 You must keep separate this meat from the sheep's ribs
Exodus29:28 In the future
Exodus29:29 Aaron's special clothes will belong to his descendants after he dies. When they too become God's special servants as his priests
Exodus29:30 After Aaron's death
Exodus29:31 Take the male sheep that makes Aaron God's special servant. Cook its meat in a holy place in the yard of the tabernacle.
Exodus29:32 Aaron and his sons must eat this meat and the bread that was in the basket. They must do this at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
Exodus29:33 That meat was part of the sacrifice which made them clean from their sins. It made them God's special servants as his priests. No other person may eat those things
Exodus29:34 But if any of the meat from the offerings remains until the morning
Exodus29:35 Do everything that I have commanded you
Exodus29:36 Kill a bull each day
Exodus29:37 Do this to the altar for seven days to make it pure. Then it will be completely holy. Anything that touches the altar will become holy too.
Exodus29:38 You must offer a regular gift on the altar each day. This offering must be two lambs that are one year old.
Exodus29:39 Offer one lamb in the morning and the other lamb in the evening.
Exodus29:40 With the first lamb
Exodus29:41 Kill the other lamb in the evening. Offer it with the same gifts of food and drink as in the morning. It is a burnt offering to the Lord. Its smell pleases him.
Exodus29:42 You and your descendants must continue to make this burnt offering as a regular offering to me
Exodus29:43 I will meet with the Israelites there. My bright glory will make it a holy place.
Exodus29:44 In that way I will make the Tent of Meeting and its altar a special
Exodus29:45 I will live among the Israelites and I will be their God.
Exodus29:46 They will know that I am the Lord their God. I brought them out of Egypt so that I could live among them. I am the Lord their God.
Exodus30:1 Use acacia wood to make an altar so that you can burn incense on it.
Exodus30:2 The altar must be square. Make it 45 centimetres long
Exodus30:3 Cover all the altar with gold: its top
Exodus30:4 Make two gold rings for the altar. Fix them below the gold edge on two opposite sides. They will hold the poles for you to carry the altar.
Exodus30:5 Use acacia wood to make the poles. Cover them with gold.
Exodus30:6 Put the altar for incense in the Holy Place in the tabernacle. Put it in front of the curtain that hides the Covenant Box. It will be near the special lid that covers the Covenant Box. That is the place where I will meet with you.
Exodus30:7 Every morning
Exodus30:8 He must burn incense again when he lights the lamps in the evening. Incense must burn there as an offering to the Lord every day from now on.
Exodus30:9 Only offer proper incense on this altar. Do not offer any burnt offerings or grain offerings on it. And do not pour any drink offerings on it.
Exodus30:10 Once every year
Exodus30:11 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:12 ‘When you count the number of the Israelites
Exodus30:13 Each person that you count must give six grams of silver. That is the proper weight that they use in the holy place. The silver is an offering to the Lord.
Exodus30:14 You must count every person who is 20 years old or more. Each person must offer his gift to the Lord.
Exodus30:15 Rich people must not give more than six grams of silver. Poor people must not give less than that. That is the offering that you must each give to the Lord. It is the price that everyone must pay for his life.
Exodus30:16 Receive these silver coins from the Israelites. Use the money to take care of the Tent of Meeting. Then the Lord will always remember that you belong to him. You have paid money to save your lives.’
Exodus30:17 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:18 ‘Use bronze to make a large bowl for water. Also use bronze to make a base for it to stand on. Put it between the Tent of Meeting and the altar. Fill it with water.
Exodus30:19 Aaron and his sons must use the water from the bowl to wash their hands and their feet.
Exodus30:20 Every time they go into the Tent of Meeting
Exodus30:21 If they wash their hands and their feet
Exodus30:22 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:23 ‘Mix together these special spices: 6 kilograms of pure myrrh
Exodus30:24 6 kilograms of cassia
Exodus30:25 Mix these spices to make a special oil. A worker should use special skills to make it
Exodus30:26 Then pour some of this special oil on these things: the Tent of Meeting
Exodus30:27 the special table and all its tools
Exodus30:28 the altar for burnt offerings and all its tools
Exodus30:29 You must make all these things special for me. They will be very holy things. Anything that touches them will also become holy.
Exodus30:30 Pour some of the special oil on Aaron's head and on the heads of his sons. Make them special for me. Then they can serve me as my priests.
Exodus30:31 Say to the Israelites
Exodus30:32 Do not put it on the bodies of anyone
Exodus30:33 Nobody must make a perfume that has a sweet smell like this oil. Nobody must put this oil on any person who is not a priest. Anyone who does not obey this rule will no longer belong to my people.” ’
Exodus30:34 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:35 Give them to a worker who can use special skills to make the incense. It must have a sweet smell
Exodus30:36 Beat some of the incense to make powder. Put this in front of the Covenant Box
Exodus30:37 Do not make any incense like this for yourselves. You must always think about it as very holy. It belongs to the Lord.
Exodus30:38 If anyone makes any incense like it for his own perfume
Exodus31:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus31:2 ‘Look! I have chosen Bezalel
Exodus31:3 I have filled him with my Spirit so that he has special skills. He knows how to make many kinds of things.
Exodus31:4 He can draw pictures of beautiful things that he wants to make. Then he uses gold
Exodus31:5 He knows how to cut valuable stones and make them look beautiful. He knows how to cut wood into different shapes. He is very clever at all kinds of work.
