Leviticus1:1 The Lord spoke to Moses from the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus1:2 He said to Moses
Leviticus1:3 A person may want to give a burnt offering to the Lord. That offering may be a bull that he takes from among his animals. It must be perfect. The person must bring the bull to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus1:4 Then he must put his hand on the animal's head. The Lord will accept the animal's death to pay for the person's sins.
Leviticus1:5 The person who brings the offering must kill the young bull in front of the Lord. Aaron's sons
Leviticus1:6 The person must remove the skin from the dead animal. Then he must cut the meat into pieces.
Leviticus1:7 Aaron's sons will light a fire on the altar. They will put pieces of wood on it.
Leviticus1:8 Then Aaron's sons will put the pieces of meat onto the wood that is burning. They will put the head and the fat on the fire with the other meat.
Leviticus1:9 The person who brings the offering must use water to wash the legs and the inside parts of the animal. Then the priest will burn the whole animal on the altar. The smell of the burnt offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus1:10 A person may want to give a sheep or a goat as a burnt offering to the Lord. His offering must be a perfect male animal.
Leviticus1:11 He must kill it in front of the Lord
Leviticus1:12 The person who brings the offering must cut the animal into pieces. The priests will take the pieces of meat
Leviticus1:13 The person who brings the offering must use water to wash the legs and the inside parts of the animal. Then the priest will burn the whole animal on the altar. The smell of the burnt offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus1:14 A person may want to give a bird as a burnt offering to the Lord. His offering must be a dove or a young pigeon.
Leviticus1:15 The priest will take the bird to the altar. He will tear off the head and he will burn it on the altar. He will pour the bird's blood onto the side of the altar.
Leviticus1:16 Then the priest will remove the part of the bird where it keeps its food
Leviticus1:17 He will take hold of the bird's wings and he will tear open the body. But he must not tear it completely into two pieces. He will burn all of it on the wood that is burning on the altar. The smell of the burnt offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus2:1 A person may want to give a grain offering to the Lord. He must choose the best flour for his offering. He must pour olive oil on the flour and he must put incense on it.
Leviticus2:2 He must take it to Aaron's sons
Leviticus2:3 The part of the grain offering that remains will belong to Aaron and his sons. It is a very holy part of the gifts that people offer to the Lord.
Leviticus2:4 If you bake your grain in an oven before you offer it
Leviticus2:5 You may choose to cook a cake on a flat plate. If so
Leviticus2:6 You must break the cake into pieces and then pour olive oil on it. It is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 If you cook the grain in a pot
Leviticus2:8 Use these things to make your grain offering for the Lord. Then you must take it to the priest. He will take it to the altar.
Leviticus2:9 He will take a part of the grain offering and he will burn it in the fire on the altar. The smell of this offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus2:10 The part of the grain offering that remains will belong to Aaron and his sons. It is a very holy part of the gifts that people offer to the Lord.
Leviticus2:11 You must not use yeast in any grain offering that you give to the Lord. You must not give any yeast or honey to the priests for them to burn as a gift to the Lord.
Leviticus2:12 You may offer yeast or honey to the Lord as the first part of your harvest. But you must not burn them on the altar to make a sweet smell that gives pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus2:13 You must put salt on all your grain offerings. Salt is a mark of God's covenant with you
Leviticus2:14 You may want to use the first crops of your harvest to make a grain offering to the Lord. If so
Leviticus2:15 It is a grain offering
Leviticus2:16 The priest will take a part of the grain and olive oil
Leviticus3:1 A person might want to give a friendship offering to the Lord. He may choose a bull or a cow from among his animals. It must be perfect.
Leviticus3:2 The person must put his hand on the animal's head. Then he must kill it at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Aaron's sons
Leviticus3:3 The person must offer some parts of the friendship offering as a gift to the Lord. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal.
Leviticus3:4 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver.
Leviticus3:5 Aaron's sons will take these parts of the animal. They will put them on top of the burnt offering that is on the wood that burns on the altar. The smell of them while they are burning will give pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus3:6 A person may want to give a sheep or a goat as a friendship offering to the Lord. It must be a perfect animal
Leviticus3:7 If it is a young sheep
Leviticus3:8 He must put his hand on its head. Then he must kill it at the front of the Tent of Meeting. Aaron's sons will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:9 The person must offer some parts of the friendship offering as a gift to the Lord. He must cut the fat part of the tail off the animal. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal.
Leviticus3:10 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver.
Leviticus3:11 The priest will burn these parts on the altar as a gift of food to the Lord.
Leviticus3:12 A person may give a goat as his offering to the Lord.
Leviticus3:13 The person must put his hand on the goat's head. Then he must kill it at the front of the Tent of Meeting. Then Aaron's sons will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:14 The person must offer some parts of his offering as a gift to the Lord. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal.
Leviticus3:15 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver.
Leviticus3:16 The priest will burn these parts on the altar as a gift of food. The smell of them while they are burning will give pleasure to the Lord. All the fat from these offerings belongs to the Lord.
Leviticus3:17 You must never eat any fat or any blood yourselves. This is a rule that you and your descendants must always obey
Leviticus4:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus4:2 ‘Say to Israel's people
Leviticus4:3 The person who does a wrong thing might be the leader of the priests. If that is so
Leviticus4:4 He must bring the young bull to the Lord at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must put his hand on the bull's head. Then he must kill it in front of the Lord.
Leviticus4:5 The leader of the priests must take some of the blood into the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus4:6 He must put his finger in the blood. Then he must shake the blood seven times towards the curtain of the Lord 's Most Holy Place.
Leviticus4:7 He must also put some of the blood on the horns of the incense altar. That altar is in the Lord 's Holy Place
Leviticus4:8 Then the leader of the priests must remove all the fat from the bull which is his sin offering. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal.
Leviticus4:9 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver.
Leviticus4:10 That is the same as the priest does with a bull which is a friendship offering. The priest must burn all the fat on the altar which is for burnt offerings.
Leviticus4:11 But this is what the priest must do with all the other parts of the bull. He must take its skin
Leviticus4:12 He must take all those parts to a clean place which is outside the camp. He must burn them on a fire of wood on the heap of ashes.
Leviticus4:13 The whole group of Israel's people might do a wrong thing when they did not want to do it. They might do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands. They are guilty even if they did not know about it.
Leviticus4:14 When they realize that they have done a sin
Leviticus4:15 The leaders of the people must put their hands on the bull's head. Then one of them must kill it in front of the Lord.
Leviticus4:16 The leader of the priests must take some of the bull's blood into the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus4:17 He must put his finger in the blood. Then he must shake the blood seven times towards the curtain of the Lord 's Most Holy Place.
Leviticus4:18 He must also put some of the blood on the horns of the incense altar. That altar is in the Lord 's Holy Place
Leviticus4:19 Then the priest must remove all the fat from the dead animal. He will burn all the fat on the altar for burnt offerings.
Leviticus4:20 He will do with the other parts of this bull the same things that he did with his own sin offering. This is how the priest will make the people clean from their sins. God will forgive them when the priest does this.
Leviticus4:21 The priest must take the other parts of the bull outside the camp. He must burn them as he did with the first bull. This is the sin offering for the whole group of Israel's people.
Leviticus4:22 A leader might do a wrong thing when he did not want to do it. He might do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands
Leviticus4:23 When he realizes that he has done a sin
Leviticus4:24 He must put his hand on the goat's head. Then he must kill it in the place where they kill animals for burnt offerings to the Lord. It is a sin offering for the leader.
Leviticus4:25 The priest must put his finger in the goat's blood. He will put some of it on the horns of the altar which is for burnt offerings. He must pour the blood that remains onto the ground in front of that altar.
Leviticus4:26 Then the priest must remove all the fat from the goat. He must burn the fat on the altar
Leviticus4:27 One of the ordinary people might do a wrong thing when he did not want to do it. He might do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands
Leviticus4:28 When he realizes that he has done a sin
Leviticus4:29 He must put his hand on the head of the goat which is his sin offering. Then he must kill it in the place where they kill animals for burnt offerings.
Leviticus4:30 The priest must put his finger in the goat's blood. He will put some of it on the horns of the altar which is for burnt offerings. He must pour the blood that remains onto the ground in front of that altar.
Leviticus4:31 Then the priest must remove all the fat from the goat
Leviticus4:32 If the person chooses to offer a sheep as his sin offering
Leviticus4:33 He must put his hand on the head of the sheep which is his sin offering. He must then kill it at the place where they kill animals for burnt offerings.
Leviticus4:34 The priest must put his finger in the sheep's blood. He will put some of it on the horns of the altar which is for burnt offerings. He must pour all the blood that remains onto the ground in front of the altar.
Leviticus4:35 He must remove all the fat from the sheep. This is the same as they do with fat from sheep which are sacrifices for friendship offerings. The priest must then burn the fat on the altar. He will burn it on top of the other offerings that people give to the Lord. This is how the priest will make the person clean from their sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this.
Leviticus5:1 Someone may have seen something happen
Leviticus5:2 Someone may touch something that is unclean. It may be the dead body of a wild animal or a farm animal that is not clean. Or he may touch the dead body of an unclean animal that moves near to the ground. Even if the person does not realize what he has done
Leviticus5:3 Someone may touch something that comes out from another person and it is unclean. The person may not know that he has touched it
Leviticus5:4 Someone may not think carefully before he makes a promise. He may promise to do something that is good or something that is bad. Even if he does not know that he has made a careless promise
Leviticus5:5 If someone becomes guilty for any of these reasons
Leviticus5:6 He must bring a female sheep or a female goat for his sin offering. That will be the price to pay for his sin. The priest will offer the animal to the Lord as a sin offering. The Lord will accept the animal's death to pay for the person's sin.
Leviticus5:7 The person may not have enough money to bring an animal as his sin offering. If so
Leviticus5:8 He must take his birds to the priest. The priest will first offer to the Lord the bird that is for a sin offering. He must break the bird's neck
Leviticus5:9 He must shake some of the blood onto the side of the altar. Then he must pour the blood that remains onto the ground in front of the altar. That bird is a sin offering.
Leviticus5:10 The priest must then offer the other bird as a burnt offering. He must do this in the way that the rules say he should. This is how the priest will make the person clean from their sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this.
Leviticus5:11 The person may not have enough money to bring two doves or two young pigeons. If so
Leviticus5:12 He must take it to the priest. The priest must take some of the flour in his hand. He will burn this on the altar as a gift to the Lord
Leviticus5:13 In this way the priest will make the person clean from any of these sins that he has done. God will forgive the person when the priest does this. The flour that remains will belong to the priest
Leviticus5:14 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus5:15 ‘Someone may not obey the rules about the Lord 's holy things. He may not realize that he has done anything wrong
Leviticus5:16 The person must also give money to pay for the holy thing which he spoiled. He must add one fifth more to its value. He must give the sheep and the money to the priest. The priest will offer the sheep as a guilt offering to make the person clean from their sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this.
Leviticus5:17 Someone may fail to obey one of the Lord 's commands. Even if he does not know that he has done this
Leviticus5:18 When he realizes what he has done
Leviticus5:19 The sheep is a guilt offering for that person. The person is guilty of a sin against the Lord.’
Leviticus6:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:2 ‘Someone may cheat another person
Leviticus6:3 He may find something that another person has lost but he keeps it for himself. He may tell lies about it. He may say that he does not have it. He may deceive people in other ways that people do.
Leviticus6:4 When someone does any of these sins
Leviticus6:5 He may have told lies about something so that he keeps it for himself. On the day when he knows that he is guilty
Leviticus6:6 He must bring a perfect male sheep as his guilt offering to the Lord. You must decide its value in shekels of silver. He must give this guilt offering to the priest.
Leviticus6:7 The priest will offer the sheep to the Lord to make the person clean from his sin. Whatever sin the person has done to make him guilty
Leviticus6:8 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:9 ‘Give this command to Aaron and his sons: “These are the rules about burnt offerings. The burnt offering must stay on the altar fire all night until the morning. The fire on the altar must continue to burn all night.
Leviticus6:10 When the morning comes
Leviticus6:11 Then he must dress himself in other clothes. He must carry the ashes outside the camp. He must put them in a special place that God has chosen.
Leviticus6:12 The fire that is on the altar must always continue to burn. Every morning the priest must put more wood on the fire. He will put the animal for the burnt offering on top of the fire. He will also burn the fat of the friendship offerings on the fire.
Leviticus6:13 The fire on the altar must always continue to burn. It must never stop burning.
Leviticus6:14 These are the rules about grain offerings. Aaron's sons must take them in front of the altar to give them to the Lord.
Leviticus6:15 A priest must take some of the pure flour and the olive oil in his hand. He must also take all the incense that is on the grain offering. He will burn this part of the offering on the altar as a gift to the Lord. The smell of the offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus6:16 Aaron and his sons may eat what remains of the friendship offerings. They must use it to make bread that has no yeast in it. Then they must eat it in a holy place
Leviticus6:17 The bread must not have any yeast in it. I have given this part of my offerings to the priests. It belongs to them. It is very holy
Leviticus6:18 Any male descendant of Aaron may eat this part of the Lord 's offerings. This rule will be true for all time. Anyone who touches these offerings will be holy.” ’
Leviticus6:19 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:20 ‘On the day when you anoint Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:21 They must mix it carefully with olive oil and they must cook it on a flat plate. Then they must break it into pieces and offer it to the Lord. The smell of it will give pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus6:22 The priest who must prepare the offering is Aaron's son that they anoint to take his father's place. This rule will continue for Aaron's descendants for all time. The priest must completely burn the whole offering as a gift to the Lord.
Leviticus6:23 Every grain offering that a priest gives belongs to the Lord completely. Nobody may eat any of it.’
Leviticus6:24 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:25 ‘Tell Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers this sacrifice will eat his part of the animal. He must do that in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 If anyone touches the meat
Leviticus6:28 If a person uses a clay pot to cook the meat
Leviticus6:29 The meat is very holy. Only a male person who belongs to a priest's family may eat it.
Leviticus6:30 But it is different if they take some blood from the sin offering into the Tent of Meeting. If they do that to make people clean from their sin
Leviticus7:1 Tell Aaron
Leviticus7:2 They must kill the animal for a guilt offering at the place where they kill the animals for burnt offerings. The priest must throw the blood onto the sides of the altar.
Leviticus7:3 The person who brings the offering must offer all the fat from the animal. That includes the fat part of its tail and the fat that is around its inside parts.
Leviticus7:4 He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is on them. He must also remove the bottom part of the liver. He must offer these to the Lord.
Leviticus7:5 Then the priest must burn all these parts of the animal on the altar as a gift to the Lord. It is a guilt offering.
Leviticus7:6 Any male person who belongs to a priest's family may eat the meat that remains. They must eat it in a holy place because it is very holy.
Leviticus7:7 These rules are the same for sin offerings and for guilt offerings. The meat belongs to the priest who offers the animal to make a person clean from their sin.
Leviticus7:8 But it is different with burnt offerings. The skin of the animal that the priest burns on the altar belongs to that priest.
Leviticus7:9 If people bring a grain offering that they have cooked in an oven or on a flat plate
Leviticus7:10 It is different if the grain offering has dry grain
Leviticus7:11 These are the rules about sacrifices for friendship offerings which people may offer to the Lord.
Leviticus7:12 If they do it to thank the Lord
Leviticus7:13 For their offering
Leviticus7:14 They must offer one of each kind of these grain offerings to the Lord. These belong to the priest who throws the blood on the side of the altar for the friendship offering.
Leviticus7:15 The people must eat the meat of the animal for the friendship offering on the same day that they offer it. They must not keep any of the meat until the next day.
Leviticus7:16 It is different if people bring a friendship offering because of a promise that they have made to the Lord. Or if they do it because they choose to make a gift to the Lord. These people may eat some of the meat on the day that they offer the sacrifice. They may also eat the meat that remains on the next day.
Leviticus7:17 But if any meat still remains on the third day
Leviticus7:18 If people eat any of the meat on the third day
Leviticus7:19 If any of the meat touches something that is unclean
Leviticus7:20 Anyone who has not made themselves clean must not eat the meat of the sacrifice for a friendship offering. If they do that
Leviticus7:21 You become unclean if you touch anything that is unclean from a human
Leviticus7:22 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus7:23 ‘Tell Israel's people
Leviticus7:24 You may find the body of an animal that has died by itself
Leviticus7:25 You must not eat the fat of an animal that you have offered to the Lord as a gift. Anyone who does that will no longer belong to Israel's people.
Leviticus7:26 Wherever you live
Leviticus7:27 Anyone who eats any blood will no longer belong to Israel's people.” ’
Leviticus7:28 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus7:29 ‘Tell Israel's people
Leviticus7:30 They must carry it in their own hands to burn on the altar. They must bring the animal's fat and the meat around its ribs. They must lift these up as a special offering to the Lord.
Leviticus7:31 The priest will then burn the fat on the altar. But the meat around the ribs will belong to Aaron and his sons.
Leviticus7:32 The top part of the back right leg of the animal is a gift to the priest.
Leviticus7:33 It will belong to the priest who offers the blood and the fat for the friendship offering.
Leviticus7:34 I have taken these parts of the animals that the Israelites bring as their friendship offerings. Those are the ribs that are lifted up to me
Leviticus7:35 The Lord gave this part of the Israelites' offerings to Aaron and to his descendants. This started on the day that they became priests to serve the Lord.
Leviticus7:36 The Lord commanded the Israelites to do this on the day that Moses anointed them as priests. This part of the offerings would always belong to the priests.
Leviticus7:37 These are the rules for burnt offerings
Leviticus7:38 The Lord gave these rules to Moses on Mount Sinai. On that day
Leviticus8:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus8:2 ‘Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Also bring their special clothes and the olive oil to anoint them as priests. Bring the bull for the sin offering and the two male sheep for sacrifices. Bring the basket of flat bread with no yeast in it.
Leviticus8:3 Tell all the people to come together in front of the Tent of Meeting.’
Leviticus8:4 Moses did what the Lord had commanded him to do. All the Israelite people met at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus8:5 Moses said to the people
Leviticus8:6 Moses told Aaron and his sons to stand in front of the people. He washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 Moses put the special shirt on Aaron. He tied the long belt around Aaron. He put the priest's robe on him. Then he put the ephod on him
Leviticus8:8 He put the breastpiece on Aaron
Leviticus8:9 Moses put the linen hat on Aaron's head. He fixed the thin gold plate on the front of the hat
Leviticus8:10 Moses took some of the special olive oil. He splashed it on the tabernacle and on everything in it. He did this to show that they belonged to God.
Leviticus8:11 He splashed oil on the altar seven times. He put oil on the altar's tools
Leviticus8:12 Then Moses poured some of the oil on Aaron's head to anoint him. This made Aaron ready to serve God as his priest.
Leviticus8:13 Then Moses told Aaron's sons to come forward. He put a robe on each of them. He tied long cloth belts around them. He put cloth hats on their heads. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do.
Leviticus8:14 Then Moses brought the bull for the sin offering. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the bull's head.
Leviticus8:15 He killed the bull. Moses took some of the blood on his fingers. He put the blood on the four horns of the altar to make the altar clean. He poured out the blood that remained on the ground in front of the altar. In this way
Leviticus8:16 Moses removed the fat that was around the inside parts of the bull. He removed the bottom part of its liver
Leviticus8:17 But Moses took outside the camp the parts of the bull that remained. There he completely burned its skin
Leviticus8:18 Moses then brought one of the male sheep to be a burnt offering. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the sheep's head.
Leviticus8:19 He killed the sheep. Moses splashed its blood onto the sides of the altar.
Leviticus8:20 He cut the sheep into pieces. He burned its head
Leviticus8:21 He used water to wash its inside parts and its legs. He burned the whole sheep on the altar as a burnt offering to the Lord. The smell of this gift while it was burning gave pleasure to the Lord. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do.
Leviticus8:22 Moses then brought the other male sheep to be a sacrifice for new priests. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the sheep's head.
Leviticus8:23 He killed the sheep. Moses took some of its blood. He put it on Aaron's right ear
Leviticus8:24 Then Moses told Aaron's sons to come forward. He put blood on their right ears
Leviticus8:25 Moses took the fat part of the sheep's tail and the fat around its inside parts. He also took the bottom part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat. With all the sheep's fat
Leviticus8:26 Moses took one round loaf of bread from the basket of flat bread that belonged to the Lord. He also took one loaf that had olive oil mixed in the flour. And he took one flat biscuit. He put them on top of the fat parts of the sheep and its right leg.
Leviticus8:27 Then Moses put all of them on the hands of Aaron and his sons. They lifted them up as a special offering to the Lord.
Leviticus8:28 Moses took them from their hands and he burned them on the altar
Leviticus8:29 Then Moses took the meat that was around the sheep's ribs. He lifted it up as a special offering to the Lord. This part belonged to Moses
Leviticus8:30 Moses took some of the oil and some of the blood from the altar. He shook them on Aaron and his clothes. He also shook it on Aaron's sons and on their clothes. In this way he made them holy and ready to serve the Lord.
Leviticus8:31 Moses said to Aaron and to his sons
Leviticus8:32 Completely burn any meat and bread that remain.
Leviticus8:33 You must stay at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting for seven days. That is the time to prepare you to serve God as priests. You must not leave until the seven days have finished.
Leviticus8:34 The things that we have done today are what the Lord has commanded us to do. We have done it to make you clean from your sins.
Leviticus8:35 Remember to stay at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting for seven days and seven nights. Do everything that the Lord has commanded
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did everything that the Lord had told Moses they must do.
Leviticus9:1 On the eighth day
Leviticus9:2 He said to Aaron
Leviticus9:3 Then tell Israel's people
Leviticus9:4 Also bring a bull and a male sheep for a friendship offering that you will offer as a sacrifice to the Lord. And bring a grain offering of flour that is mixed with olive oil. Do this because the Lord will appear to you today.” ’
Leviticus9:5 The people brought all the animals and the grain to the front of the Tent of Meeting as Moses had commanded. All the Israelite people stood together there to worship the Lord.
Leviticus9:6 Moses said to the people
Leviticus9:7 Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus9:8 So Aaron went to the altar. He killed the young bull that was a sin offering for himself.
Leviticus9:9 Aaron's sons brought the blood to him. He put his finger in the blood and he put it on the four horns of the altar. He poured the blood that remained onto the ground in front of the altar.
Leviticus9:10 Aaron took the bull's fat
Leviticus9:11 But Moses took the meat and the skin of the bull outside the camp. There he completely burned it.
Leviticus9:12 Then Aaron killed the male sheep for the burnt offering. His sons brought the blood to him. He splashed it onto the sides of the altar.
Leviticus9:13 His sons brought the pieces of the sheep to him
Leviticus9:14 He washed the sheep's inside parts and its legs. He burned them on top of the burnt offering on the altar.
Leviticus9:15 Then Aaron brought to the altar the male goat which was the sin offering for the people. He killed it as a sacrifice to make the people clean
Leviticus9:16 Then Aaron brought the animal for the burnt offering. He offered it as a sacrifice in the proper way.
Leviticus9:17 He also brought the grain offering. He took some of it in his hand. He burned it on the altar together with the burnt offering of that morning.
Leviticus9:18 Aaron killed the bull and the male sheep which were friendship offerings for the people. His sons brought the blood to him. He splashed it onto the sides of the altar.
Leviticus9:19 His sons also took the fat pieces from the bull and from the male sheep. They took the fat part of the tail
Leviticus9:20 They put those pieces on the meat of the animal's ribs. Aaron burned the fat pieces of the animals on the altar as an offering.
Leviticus9:21 Then Aaron lifted up the meat of the ribs and the top part of the right leg of each animal as a special offering to the Lord. He did this as Moses had commanded him to do.
Leviticus9:22 Aaron had now finished making the sin offering
Leviticus9:23 Moses and Aaron went into the Tent of Meeting. When they came out
Leviticus9:24 Then the Lord sent a fire to completely burn the burnt offering and the fat pieces that were on the altar. When the people saw this
Leviticus10:1 Aaron's sons
Leviticus10:2 The Lord suddenly sent fire to completely burn Nadab and Abihu. They died there in front of the Lord 's altar.
Leviticus10:3 Then Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus10:4 Then Moses told Mishael and Elzaphan to come to him. They were sons of Aaron's uncle
Leviticus10:5 Nadab and Abihu were still wearing their special robes. Mishael and Elzaphan carried them outside the camp
Leviticus10:6 Then Moses spoke to Aaron and to his other sons
Leviticus10:7 Do not go away from the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. If you do that
Leviticus10:8 Then the Lord said to Aaron
Leviticus10:9 ‘Do not drink any wine before you go into the Tent of Meeting. Do not drink anything that contains alcohol. If you do
Leviticus10:10 You must know the difference between things that are holy and things that are ordinary. You must know what is clean and what is unclean.
Leviticus10:11 You must teach the Israelites all the laws that I told Moses to give to them.’
Leviticus10:12 Moses said to Aaron and to his sons who still lived
Leviticus10:13 You must eat it in a holy place. It is the part of the grain offering that belongs to you and to your sons. The Lord has given it to you from his special gifts. These are the commands that the Lord has given to me.
Leviticus10:14 The Lord has also given you parts of the offerings that are lifted up to him. He has given you the meat around the ribs and the top part of the back leg. You may eat these parts. Your sons and your daughters may eat them too. You may only eat these parts in a place that is clean. They belong to you and to your sons. The Lord has given them to you from the friendship offerings that the Israelites give to him.
Leviticus10:15 Bring the top of the leg and the meat of the ribs
Leviticus10:16 After that
Leviticus10:17 Moses asked them
Leviticus10:18 Look! You did not bring the blood of the goat for the sacrifice into the holy place. So you should have eaten its meat near the holy tent
Leviticus10:19 Aaron said to Moses
Leviticus10:20 When Moses heard Aaron's reply
Leviticus11:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 ‘Tell the Israelites
Leviticus11:3 You may eat animals which have feet in two separate parts. They must also be animals which bring their food back into their mouths and eat it again.
Leviticus11:4 Some animals have feet in two separate parts but they do not eat their food twice. Other animals eat their food twice but their feet do not have two separate parts. You must not eat those animals. Camels eat their food twice but their feet are not in two separate parts. So camels are not clean for you to eat.
Leviticus11:5 Rock badgers also eat their food twice but their feet are not in two separate parts. So they are also unclean.
Leviticus11:6 Rabbits also eat their food twice but their feet are not in two separate parts. They are unclean too.
Leviticus11:7 Pigs have feet that are in two completely separate parts. But pigs do not bring their food back to eat it a second time. So pigs are not clean for you to eat.
Leviticus11:8 You must not eat the meat from these animals. You must not touch their dead bodies. They are unclean animals for you.
Leviticus11:9 These are the rules about animals that live in rivers or in the sea. If they have both fins and scales on their bodies
Leviticus11:10 But you must not eat anything that does not have both fins and scales. That includes fish or any other animals that live in water. They are disgusting for you.
Leviticus11:11 So do not eat their meat or touch their dead bodies.
Leviticus11:12 Any fish or other animals that live in water and do not have both fins and scales are unclean. Keep away from them.
Leviticus11:13 These are the rules about birds. These birds are disgusting so do not eat them: eagles
Leviticus11:14 buzzards
Leviticus11:15 
Leviticus11:16 
Leviticus11:17 
Leviticus11:18 
Leviticus11:19 
Leviticus11:20 These are the rules about insects that fly in the air and also walk on the ground. They are disgusting and you must not eat them.
Leviticus11:21 But some of these insects have legs that they can use to jump. You may eat those kinds of insects.
Leviticus11:22 You may eat any kinds of locusts
Leviticus11:23 But you must not eat any other insects that fly and they also walk on the ground.
Leviticus11:24 All these animals will make you unclean if you touch the dead body of one of them. If you do touch one
Leviticus11:25 If you carry a body of one of these animals
Leviticus11:26 Do not touch an animal that does not have feet in two completely separate parts. Do not touch an animal that does not bring food back into its mouth to eat it again. If you touch the dead body of any of these animals
Leviticus11:27 Do not touch the dead body of an animal that has paws to walk on. If you do touch one
Leviticus11:28 If you carry a body of one of these animals
Leviticus11:29 These are the rules about animals that move near to the ground. These animals are unclean for you: moles
Leviticus11:30 
Leviticus11:31 If you touch the dead body of one of them
Leviticus11:32 If one of them dies and it falls onto something
Leviticus11:33 If one of these dead animals falls into a clay pot
Leviticus11:34 If you pour water from the pot onto food
Leviticus11:35 If one of these dead animals falls onto something
Leviticus11:36 But if one of these dead animals falls into a spring of water
Leviticus11:37 If one of these dead animals falls onto some dry seeds
Leviticus11:38 But if the seeds are wet and the dead animal falls onto them
Leviticus11:39 An animal that is good for food might die. If anyone touches its dead body
Leviticus11:40 If anyone eats meat from it
Leviticus11:41 You must not eat small animals that move across the ground.
Leviticus11:42 This includes animals that move along the ground on their stomachs and animals that walk on four legs or on many legs. All those things that move near to the ground are disgusting. Do not eat them.
Leviticus11:43 Do not allow those small animals to make you unclean. Do not become disgusting yourselves
Leviticus11:44 I am the Lord your God. So keep yourselves separate from these unclean things. You must be holy
Leviticus11:45 I am the Lord. I brought you out of the land of Egypt so that I would be your God. So you must be holy
Leviticus11:46 These are the rules about animals which live on the land
Leviticus11:47 You must learn the difference between animals that are unclean and animals that are clean. You must know which animals you may eat and which animals you must not eat.” ’
Leviticus12:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus12:2 ‘Tell the Israelites
Leviticus12:3 Eight days after the boy is born
Leviticus12:4 His mother must wait for 33 days after the birth before she becomes clean again. During this time
Leviticus12:5 If a woman gives birth to a girl
Leviticus12:6 The mother must wait for the right number of days after she gives birth to a son or a daughter. When those days have finished
Leviticus12:7 The priest will offer the gifts to the Lord to make the woman clean again. These are the rules for a mother who gives birth to a boy or a girl.
Leviticus12:8 If the mother does not have enough money to buy a lamb
Leviticus13:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 ‘A person may have a mark on his skin like a sore or a boil. It might become a bad disease that he could give to other people. If he has a mark like that
Leviticus13:3 The priest must look at the mark on the person's skin. The hair on the mark may have become white
Leviticus13:4 But the mark may be a bright place on the person's skin. It may not go deep under the skin. The hair on the mark may not be white. If those things are true
Leviticus13:5 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:6 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 But after the person has shown his mark to the priest
Leviticus13:8 The priest must look carefully at the mark. If it has become bigger
Leviticus13:9 If a person has a bad disease in his skin
Leviticus13:10 The priest must look carefully at the mark on the person's skin. The mark may be white and it may grow up above the skin. The hair on it may have become white. It may be a sore that has pus in it.
Leviticus13:11 If those things are true
Leviticus13:12 It is different if the disease has grown completely across all the person's skin. The priest may see that it covers the whole body
Leviticus13:13 The priest must carefully look at the person's skin. If the skin has become white across the person's whole body
Leviticus13:14 But if there is a sore with pus anywhere on the person's skin
Leviticus13:15 The priest must look carefully at the sore and say that the person is unclean. He still has a disease in his skin.
Leviticus13:16 If the sore changes and it becomes white
Leviticus13:17 The priest must look carefully at the skin. If the skin has become white
Leviticus13:18 A person might have a boil on his skin. Then it might get better.
Leviticus13:19 There may be a white mark that grows up above the skin where the boil had been. Or there may be a bright mark on the skin that seems to be red. If this happens
Leviticus13:20 The priest will look carefully at the mark on the person's skin. The mark may seem to grow deep under the skin and the hair on it may be white. If this is true
Leviticus13:21 But the priest may see that the mark does not have white hair. It may not go deep under the skin and it may not show very clearly. If that is true
Leviticus13:22 If the mark continues to grow across the skin
Leviticus13:23 But the mark may not grow bigger across the person's skin. This shows that it is a mark from the boil and it is not a bad disease. The priest must say that the person is clean.
Leviticus13:24 A person may have burnt his skin. Because of that
Leviticus13:25 The priest must look carefully at the mark. The hair on the bright mark may have become white. The mark may seem to grow deep under the skin. This shows that the place where the person burnt himself has become a bad disease in his skin. The priest must say that the person is unclean. He might give the disease to other people.
Leviticus13:26 But the priest may see that the mark does not have white hair. It may not go deep under the skin and it may not show very clearly. If that is true
Leviticus13:27 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:28 But the mark may not have grown across the person's skin. It may not show so clearly. This shows that it is a mark from the place where the person burnt his skin. The priest must say that the person is clean.
Leviticus13:29 A man or a woman may have a sore on their head or on their chin.
Leviticus13:30 The priest must look carefully at the sore. The sore may seem to go deep under the skin. It may have hair in it that is thin and yellow. If this is true
Leviticus13:31 But the sore may not go deep below the skin. There may be no hair in it that is black. If the priest sees that
Leviticus13:32 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:33 If that is true
Leviticus13:34 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:35 But after that
Leviticus13:36 If that happens
Leviticus13:37 But the sore may not have changed. Black hair may have started to grow in it. If that is true
Leviticus13:38 A man or a woman may have white marks on their skin.
Leviticus13:39 The priest must look carefully at the marks. If the colour of the marks is not bright
Leviticus13:40 The hair may disappear from a man's head until he is bald. That man is clean.
Leviticus13:41 If the hair disappears from the front of the man's head until that part is bald
Leviticus13:42 But if there is a red sore on the bald place at the top or at the back of the man's head
Leviticus13:43 The priest must look carefully at the sore. The sore in the bald place may grow up above the skin and it may seem to be red. If it looks like a skin disease that appears in other places of a person's body
Leviticus13:44 the man has a bad disease. He is unclean. The priest must say that the man is unclean because of the sore on his head.
Leviticus13:45 Any person who has a bad disease in his skin must tear his clothes. He must not make his hair look nice. He must cover the lower part of his face. He must shout
Leviticus13:46 All the time that he has the bad skin disease he will continue to be unclean. He must live in a separate place from other people
Leviticus13:47 Mould may grow on clothes that are made of wool or linen.
Leviticus13:48 It may grow on any piece of linen or wool
Leviticus13:49 If the mould on any of these things seems to be green or red
Leviticus13:50 The priest must look carefully at the mould. He must keep the thing that has the mould on it separate from other things for seven days.
Leviticus13:51 On the seventh day
Leviticus13:52 The priest must burn the piece of cloth or the piece of leather which has the mould. Because it has a dangerous mould
Leviticus13:53 When the priest looks at the piece of cloth or leather on the seventh day
Leviticus13:54 If that is true
Leviticus13:55 The priest must again look carefully at the piece of cloth after the person has washed it. If the mould still looks the same
Leviticus13:56 When the priest looks at the piece of cloth after the person has washed it
Leviticus13:57 After that
Leviticus13:58 But if the mould disappears after you have washed it the first time
Leviticus13:59 These are the rules for mould that grows on cloth that is made of wool or linen. They are also the rules for mould that grows on leather things. They tell the priest how to decide if the things are clean or unclean.
Leviticus14:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus14:2 ‘These rules are for a person who has had a bad disease in his skin. When the disease has finished
Leviticus14:3 The priest must go outside the camp and he must look carefully at the person's skin. If the disease has gone away
Leviticus14:4 this is what the priest must do. He must tell someone to bring two birds that are alive. They must be birds that are good for food. They must also bring a piece of wood from a cedar tree
Leviticus14:5 The priest will command someone to kill one of the birds. He must do that over a clay pot that has fresh water in it. The blood of the dead bird will mix with the water.
Leviticus14:6 The priest will take the living bird
Leviticus14:7 Then the priest will shake the blood over the person who had the disease. He will do this seven times. The priest will then say that the person is clean. He will let the living bird go so that it flies away.
Leviticus14:8 The person who has become well from the disease must wash his clothes. He must cut off all his hair and wash his body with water. Then he will be clean. After that
Leviticus14:9 On the seventh day
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day
Leviticus14:11 He must take these to the priest who is making him clean. The priest must tell the person to stand at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must bring his offerings to the Lord there.
Leviticus14:12 The priest must take one of the male lambs and the cup of olive oil. He must lift them up to the Lord as a special gift.
Leviticus14:13 He must kill the male lamb in the holy place where they kill the animals for sin offerings and burnt offerings. The guilt offering belongs to the priest
Leviticus14:14 The priest must put some of the blood from the guilt offering on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He must put some blood on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he must put some blood on the big toe of the person's right foot.
Leviticus14:15 The priest must pour some of the olive oil from the cup into his own left hand.
Leviticus14:16 He must put one finger of his right hand into the oil that is in his left hand. He must shake the oil seven times in front of the Lord.
Leviticus14:17 The priest must then take the oil that remains in his left hand. He must put some of it on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He must put some of the oil on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he must put some of it on the big toe of the person's right foot. He will put the oil on top of the blood from the offering.
Leviticus14:18 The priest must take the oil that still remains in his left hand. He must put it on the person's head. In this way the priest will make the person clean from his disease in front of the Lord.
Leviticus14:19 Then the priest must kill the animal for the sin offering. This makes the person who had the disease clean from his sins. After that
Leviticus14:20 He will offer this as a sacrifice on the altar
Leviticus14:21 If the person is too poor to bring all these offerings
Leviticus14:22 The person must also bring two birds that he has enough money to buy. They must be doves or pigeons. One bird will be for a sin offering and the other one will be for a burnt offering.
Leviticus14:23 On the eighth day
Leviticus14:24 The priest will take the male lamb and the cup of oil. He will lift them up as a special gift to the Lord.
Leviticus14:25 Then he will kill the lamb which is the guilt offering. He will put some of its blood on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He will put some of the blood on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he will put some of it on the big toe of the person's right foot.
Leviticus14:26 The priest will pour some of the oil into his own left hand.
Leviticus14:27 He will put one finger of his right hand into the oil. He will shake the oil seven times in front of the Lord.
Leviticus14:28 He will put some oil from his hand on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He will also put some oil on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he will put some oil on the big toe of the person's right foot. He will put the oil on the same places that he put the blood from the guilt offering.
Leviticus14:29 The priest must take the oil that still remains in his left hand. He must put it on the person's head. In that way the priest will make the person clean from his disease in front of the Lord.
Leviticus14:30 Then the priest will offer the pigeons or the doves that the person has been able to buy.
Leviticus14:31 One bird will be for a sin offering. The other will be for a burnt offering. The priest will offer these together with the grain offering. In this way the priest will make the person clean in front of the Lord.
Leviticus14:32 These are the rules for a poor person who had a bad disease in his skin. These are the offerings he must bring to make himself clean if he is too poor to bring all the usual offerings.’
Leviticus14:33 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus14:34 ‘One day you will go into Canaan
Leviticus14:35 If a person finds mould in his house
Leviticus14:36 The priest will tell the person to take everything out of the house. If the person does not do that
Leviticus14:37 The priest will look carefully at the walls of the house. He will see if the mould has green or red marks in it. He will see if the mould goes deep into the wall.
Leviticus14:38 If the priest sees those things
Leviticus14:39 On the seventh day
Leviticus14:40 If that has happened
Leviticus14:41 The person must then remove the clay that covers all the inside walls of the house. He must put the clay outside the town in a place for unclean things.
Leviticus14:42 The person who lives in the house must get some other stones and some more clay. He will use those instead of the unclean stones and clay that he removed.
Leviticus14:43 After he has done all those things
Leviticus14:44 The priest will come to the house and look carefully at the mould. If the mould has grown again across the walls
Leviticus14:45 The person must completely knock down his house. He must remove all the stones
Leviticus14:46 A person might go into the house during the time that the priest has shut it because of the mould. That person will become unclean until evening.
Leviticus14:47 Anyone who sleeps in the house must wash their clothes. And anyone who eats in the house must also wash their clothes.
Leviticus14:48 After the person has mended his house
Leviticus14:49 He must take two birds
Leviticus14:50 He will kill one of the birds over a clay pot that has fresh water in it. The blood of the dead bird will mix with the water.
Leviticus14:51 The priest will then take the piece of cedar wood
Leviticus14:52 In this way
Leviticus14:53 Then the priest will take the living bird outside the town. He will let the bird fly away over the fields. In this way he will make the house clean from the mould.’
Leviticus14:54 These are the rules for all kinds of bad disease.
Leviticus14:55 They include rules for mould that grows on cloth or on the walls of a house.
Leviticus14:56 They include rules for sores and boils and marks on someone's skin.
Leviticus14:57 They show if a person has a dangerous disease in their skin. They show whether the person is clean or he is unclean.
Leviticus15:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus15:2 ‘Tell Israel's people
Leviticus15:3 It does not matter whether the pus continues to leave the man's body or it stays inside. It causes the man to be unclean.
Leviticus15:4 If the man lies on a bed
Leviticus15:5 Anyone who touches the bed must wash his clothes and his whole body. He will continue to be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:6 If anyone sits on anything that the man has sat on
Leviticus15:7 Someone might touch the man who has pus coming from his body. If that happens
Leviticus15:8 The man who is unclean might spit on another person. Then the other person must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:9 The man might sit on something to ride on a horse or a donkey. Whatever he sits on will become unclean.
Leviticus15:10 Someone might touch or carry any of the things that the man has sat on. Then that person must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:11 The unclean man might not wash his hands before he touches another person. The person that he touched then becomes unclean. That person must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:12 If the unclean man touches a clay pot
Leviticus15:13 The man who has had the pus coming from his body might become well again. He must count seven days after the pus has gone. Then he must wash his clothes. He must wash his whole body in clean water. Then he will be clean.
Leviticus15:14 On the eighth day
Leviticus15:15 The priest will use one of the birds for a sin offering. He will use the other bird for a burnt offering. In this way the priest will make the man clean before the Lord. The pus that came from his body no longer causes him to be unclean.
Leviticus15:16 When semen comes out of a man by mistake
Leviticus15:17 He must wash any clothes or leather things that has his semen on them. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:18 When a man has sex with a woman
Leviticus15:19 Blood comes out from a woman's body every month. When this happens
Leviticus15:20 During the time that the woman is bleeding
Leviticus15:21 If anyone touches her bed
Leviticus15:22 If anyone touches something that the woman has sat on
Leviticus15:23 This is true for anything that the woman has sat on
Leviticus15:24 A man might have sex with a woman during the time that she is bleeding. If her blood touches him
Leviticus15:25 A woman is unclean all the time that blood is coming out of her body. This is true if it happens at a different time in the month. It is also true if it continues at the usual time for more than seven days. It is all the same. She is unclean.
Leviticus15:26 The rule is the same as when she is bleeding at the usual time of the month. During any time that she is bleeding
Leviticus15:27 Anyone who touches those things will also be unclean. They must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus15:28 When the woman stops bleeding
Leviticus15:29 On the eighth day
Leviticus15:30 The priest will use one of the birds for a sin offering. He will use the other bird for a burnt offering. In this way the priest will make the woman who had been bleeding clean before the Lord.
Leviticus15:31 In this way you will keep Israel's people separate from things that make them unclean. They will not cause my tabernacle to become unclean. That is the place where I live among them. If they make that place unclean
Leviticus15:32 These are the rules for anyone who has any kind of pus that comes out of their body. Or it may be semen that comes from a man's sex part. These things cause him to be unclean.
Leviticus15:33 They are also the rules for a woman during the time when she bleeds each month. They are the rules for any man or woman who becomes unclean because of these things. They are also the rules for a man who has sex with a woman during the time that she is unclean.
Leviticus16:1 The Lord spoke to Moses after two of Aaron's sons had died. They had burned incense in front of the Lord in a way that was wrong. The Lord punished them with death.
Leviticus16:2 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus16:3 This is what Aaron must do when he goes into the holy place. He must bring a young bull for a sin offering. He must also bring a male sheep for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:4 He must wash his whole body before he puts on his special clothes
Leviticus16:5 Israel's people must also give him two male goats for a sin offering and one male sheep for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 Aaron must offer the bull as a sin offering. This will make him clean from his own sins. It will also make the people in his house clean from their sins.
Leviticus16:7 Then he must take the two goats to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. There he must offer them to the Lord.
Leviticus16:8 He must throw special stones to decide what he should do with each goat. One stone will have a mark for the Lord. The other stone will have a mark for Azazel.
Leviticus16:9 Aaron must take the goat that the stone showed is for the Lord. He must kill it as a sin offering.
Leviticus16:10 But he must bring the other goat in front of the Lord while it is alive. He will send it away to escape into the desert. In that way
Leviticus16:11 Aaron must offer the bull which is for a sin offering. This will make him clean from his own sins. It will also make the people in his house clean from their sins.
Leviticus16:12 He must take a pot of hot coals that burn on the altar in front of the Lord. He must fill both his hands with incense that has a sweet smell. He must take the hot coals and the incense with him behind the curtain.
Leviticus16:13 Then he must put the incense onto the hot coals in front of the Lord. The smoke from the incense will rise up over the lid of the Covenant Box. If he does this
Leviticus16:14 Then he must take the blood from the bull. He must use his finger to splash blood onto the lid of the Covenant Box. He must also splash some blood seven times with his finger in front of the Covenant Box.
Leviticus16:15 Aaron must then kill the goat which is the sin offering for the people. He must take the goat's blood behind the curtain. He must use it in the same way that he used the bull's blood. He must shake it on the lid of the Covenant Box and in front of the Covenant Box.
Leviticus16:16 In this way Aaron will make the Most Holy Place clean. He must do this because of the sins of Israel's people that make them unclean. He must do the same thing for the whole Tent of Meeting to make it clean. The tent stands among Israel's people who are unclean.
Leviticus16:17 While Aaron is doing this
Leviticus16:18 When he comes out
Leviticus16:19 Then he must splash some of the blood on the altar seven times with his finger. In this way he will make it clean for the Lord to use. He must do this because Israel's people are unclean.
Leviticus16:20 Aaron must then bring the goat that is alive in front of the Lord. He must do this after he has made clean the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:21 He must put his hands on the goat's head. He must speak aloud all the sins that Israel's people have done. He must say that they have been wicked and they have not obeyed the Lord. In this way he will put all their sins on the goat's head. Aaron must then tell a man that he has chosen to send the goat away into the desert.
Leviticus16:22 The goat will carry all the sins of Israel's people far away to an empty place. The man must send it away into the desert.
Leviticus16:23 After this
Leviticus16:24 He must wash his whole body in a holy place. He must put on his own clothes and go out of the tent. Then he will offer the animals for his own burnt offering and the burnt offering for the people. In this way
Leviticus16:25 Then he must burn on the altar the fat from the sin offering.
Leviticus16:26 The man who sent the goat away to escape must wash his clothes and his whole body. Then he may return to the camp.
Leviticus16:27 Someone must carry the dead bodies of the animals for the sin offerings outside the camp. That is the bull and the goat that Aaron had killed. He used their blood to make the Most Holy Place clean. The man who takes them outside the camp must completely burn them. That includes their skin
Leviticus16:28 After that
Leviticus16:29 On the tenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 It will be the Day of Atonement. On this day the priest will make you clean. The Lord will forgive you for all your sins.
Leviticus16:31 It is a very holy day of complete rest for you. You must not eat during that day. This is a rule that you must all obey every year.
Leviticus16:32 The descendant of Aaron who is chosen as leader of the priests will do all these things on the Day of Atonement. He will put on the linen clothes that are holy.
Leviticus16:33 He will make clean the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:34 Once every year
Leviticus17:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus17:2 ‘Speak to Aaron
Leviticus17:3 If any Israelite wants to kill a bull
Leviticus17:4 
Leviticus17:5 This rule will stop the people killing animals as sacrifices to the Lord in the fields. Instead
Leviticus17:6 The priest must splash the animal's blood on the Lord 's altar at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must also burn the animal's fat. It will make a smell that gives pleasure to the Lord.
Leviticus17:7 The people must be faithful to the Lord. They must no longer worship the idols of goats and offer sacrifices to them. The people and their descendants must always obey this rule.”
Leviticus17:8 You must also tell the people
Leviticus17:9 If so
Leviticus17:10 Any Israelite or a foreigner who lives among you must not eat any blood. If anyone does that
Leviticus17:11 Everything that lives has life because of its blood. I have told you to offer blood on the altar. That is how you become clean from your sins. The animal's blood is the life that makes you clean.
Leviticus17:12 For this reason I have told Israel's people that you must not eat blood. And no foreign person who lives among you may eat blood either.
Leviticus17:13 Israelites or foreigners who live among them may catch a wild animal or a bird. If it is clean to eat
Leviticus17:14 All living things have life because of their blood. So I have told the Israelites that they must not eat the blood of any living thing. Anyone who eats blood can no longer belong among my people.
Leviticus17:15 An Israelite or a foreigner may find the body of an animal that has died by itself. Or it may be the body of an animal that wild animals have killed. If he eats the meat of that animal
Leviticus17:16 But if he does not wash his clothes and his whole body
Leviticus18:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus18:2 ‘Speak to Israel's people. Say to them
Leviticus18:3 You have been living in Egypt
Leviticus18:4 Instead
Leviticus18:5 You must obey my laws and my teaching. Anyone who does that will continue to live. I am the Lord.”
Leviticus18:6 Tell them this: “You must not have sex with anyone who belongs to your own family. I am the Lord.
Leviticus18:7 Do not have sex with your mother. That would cause your father to be ashamed.
Leviticus18:8 Do not have sex with any wife of your father. Your father would be ashamed.
Leviticus18:9 Do not have sex with your sister. She may be your mother's daughter or your father's daughter. She may have been born in your house or in another house. It does not matter. You must not have sex with any sister.
Leviticus18:10 Do not have sex with any granddaughter. That would make you ashamed.
Leviticus18:11 Do not have sex with the daughter of your father and any of his wives. She is your sister so you must not have sex with her.
Leviticus18:12 Do not have sex with your father's sister. She belongs to your father's family.
Leviticus18:13 Do not have sex with your mother's sister. She belongs to your mother's family.
Leviticus18:14 Do not have sex with the wife of your father's brother. That would make your uncle ashamed. His wife is your aunt. You must not try to get near to her.
Leviticus18:15 Do not have sex with your son's wife.
Leviticus18:16 Do not have sex with your brother's wife. That would make your brother ashamed.
Leviticus18:17 Do not have sex with both a woman and her daughter. And do not take the woman's son's daughter or her daughter's daughter so that you can have sex with any of them. They belong to the same family. It would be a very wicked thing to do.
Leviticus18:18 If your wife is still alive
Leviticus18:19 Do not have sex with a woman at the time when she bleeds each month. She is unclean during that time.
Leviticus18:20 Do not have sex with the wife of another man. You would become unclean.
Leviticus18:21 Do not give any of your children as an offering to the god Molech. That would bring shame to the name of your God. I am the Lord!
Leviticus18:22 A man must not have sex with another man as he would have sex with a woman. That is a disgusting thing to do.
Leviticus18:23 Do not have sex with any animal. That would make you unclean. A woman must not offer herself to an animal to have sex with it. She would be doing a very bad thing.
Leviticus18:24 Do not do any of these things that would make you unclean. The nations who live in Canaan make themselves unclean with all these things. I will chase out those people so that you can live in their land.
Leviticus18:25 The land itself has become unclean because the people did these things. So I am punishing those people for their sins. The land has thrown out the people who lived on it.
Leviticus18:26 But you yourselves must obey my laws and my rules. Do not do any of these wicked things. That includes the foreign people who live among you.
Leviticus18:27 The people who lived in Canaan before you did these disgusting things. Because of that
Leviticus18:28 So do not make the land unclean
Leviticus18:29 If anyone does any of these disgusting things
Leviticus18:30 You must obey what I have commanded you to do. Do not live in the disgusting way that these other people have lived. Do not make yourselves unclean like that. I am the Lord your God.” ’
Leviticus19:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus19:2 ‘Speak to all Israel's people. Tell them
Leviticus19:3 Each of you must respect his mother and his father. You must keep the Sabbath day as a day of rest. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn away from me to worship idols. Do not use metal to make images of gods for yourselves. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:5 When you offer a friendship offering as a sacrifice to me
Leviticus19:6 When you kill an animal for the sacrifice
Leviticus19:7 If anyone eats the meat on the third day
Leviticus19:8 The person who has eaten the meat on the third day deserves punishment. He has caused a gift that is holy to the Lord to become unclean. That person can no longer belong to Israel's people.
Leviticus19:9 At harvest time
Leviticus19:10 Do not pick all the grapes off your vines. Do not pick up the grapes that have fallen onto the ground. Leave some of these for the poor people and for the foreign people who live among you. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:11 You must not take things that belong to other people. You must not deceive other people or tell them lies.
Leviticus19:12 Do not use my name to promise something that you will not do. That would bring shame to the name of your God. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:13 Do not cheat anyone. Do not rob anyone. If you are paying someone to work for you
Leviticus19:14 You must not curse a deaf person. Do not put something in front of a blind person
Leviticus19:15 When you judge other people
Leviticus19:16 Do not say bad things about anyone for other people to hear. If somebody is in danger of death
Leviticus19:17 You must not think of ways to hurt another person. If someone has done a wrong thing
Leviticus19:18 Do not try to punish another Israelite who has done something wrong against you. Do not try to hurt them. Instead
Leviticus19:19 You must obey my rules. You must not allow two different kinds of animals to have babies together. Do not put two different kinds of seeds in one field. Do not wear clothes that are made from two different kinds of material.
Leviticus19:20 If a man has chosen a slave girl to marry
Leviticus19:21 The man must bring a male sheep to the Lord at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. That will be his guilt offering.
Leviticus19:22 The priest will offer the sheep to the Lord to make the man clean from his sin. Then the Lord will forgive the man for the sin that he has done.
Leviticus19:23 When you go into Canaan
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year
Leviticus19:25 In the fifth year
Leviticus19:26 You must not eat any meat that still has blood in it. Do not use magic to learn about future times or other things.
Leviticus19:27 Do not cut the hair from the sides of your head or from the edges of your beard.
Leviticus19:28 Do not cut marks in your body to show that you are sad about a dead person. Do not cut pictures into your skin. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:29 Do not cause your daughter to be a prostitute. That would bring shame on her. It would also cause many people to turn away from me and do wicked things.
Leviticus19:30 You must obey my rules about Sabbath days. Respect the holy place where you meet with me. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:31 Do not try to speak to the spirits of dead people. Do not ask other people to do this for you. That will make you unclean. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:32 You must stand up to give honour to old people. Respect and obey me
Leviticus19:33 Do not be cruel to foreign people who live among you.
Leviticus19:34 Be kind to them
Leviticus19:35 Always be fair when you measure things. When you measure how long
Leviticus19:36 always use correct weights and measures. Use the correct size of bowl to measure grain or oil. I am the Lord your God. I led you out of the land of Egypt.
Leviticus19:37 You must always obey all my laws and my rules. I am the Lord.” ’

Leviticus20:4 The people of that man's town may decide not to punish him. He has offered some of his children to Molech
Leviticus20:5 If that happens
Leviticus20:6 You must not try to speak to the spirits of dead people. Do not ask anyone to do that for you. Anyone who does that is no longer faithful to me. I will turn against that person so that he no longer belongs among Israel's people.
Leviticus20:7 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus20:8 You must always be careful to obey my laws. I am the Lord and I cause you to be holy.
Leviticus20:9 If anyone curses his mother or his father
Leviticus20:10 If a man has sex with another man's wife you must punish both of them with death.
Leviticus20:11 If a man has sex with his father's wife
Leviticus20:12 If a man has sex with his son's wife
Leviticus20:13 If a man has sex with another man
Leviticus20:14 If a man marries a woman and her mother
Leviticus20:15 If a man has sex with an animal
Leviticus20:16 If a woman offers herself to an animal to have sex with it
Leviticus20:17 If a man takes his sister to have sex with her
Leviticus20:18 A man must not have sex with a woman during the time when she is bleeding. If he does that
Leviticus20:19 A man must not have sex with his mother's sister or with his father's sister. If he does that
Leviticus20:20 If a man has sex with his aunt
Leviticus20:21 If a man marries his brother's wife
Leviticus20:22 You must always obey all my laws and my rules. If you do that
Leviticus20:23 I will chase out the nations who live there now
Leviticus20:24 I have told you that the land will belong to you as your home. It is a land where there is plenty of food and drink
Leviticus20:25 So you must know the difference between animals that are clean and animals that are unclean. You must know the difference between birds that are clean and birds that are unclean. You must not eat anything that I have told you is unclean. That includes any small thing that moves across the ground.
Leviticus20:26 I am the Lord and I am holy. So you must be holy as my people. I have chosen you to be separate from other people to belong to me.
Leviticus20:27 Any man or woman who talks with the spirits of dead people must die. Anyone who uses magic to learn about future times must also die. You must throw stones at that person until they die. They deserve to die for their sin.” ’
Leviticus21:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus21:2 But if it is someone from his family who has died
Leviticus21:3 He may touch the dead body of his sister if she has no husband to take care of her. The priest may make himself unclean for her.
Leviticus21:4 But a priest must not touch the dead body of someone who belongs to his family because of marriage. He must not make himself unclean for that person.
Leviticus21:5 A priest must not cut the hair off his head. He must not cut the edges of his beard. He must not cut marks in his body.
Leviticus21:6 Priests must be holy to serve me. They must not bring shame to the name of their God. They are the people who offer gifts to the Lord. They bring food to their God. So they must be holy people.
Leviticus21:7 A priest must not marry a woman who has been a prostitute. He must not marry a woman who has become separate from her husband. A priest must be holy to serve his God.
Leviticus21:8 You must respect priests as holy people
Leviticus21:9 If a priest's daughter becomes a prostitute
Leviticus21:10 They have poured special oil on the head of the leader of the priests. He is able to wear the special clothes for priests. This shows that he is greater than his brothers. So he must always make his hair look nice. He must not tear his clothes.
Leviticus21:11 He must not go anywhere that is near a dead body. Even if it is his father or his mother who has died
Leviticus21:12 He must not leave the holy place to go to a funeral. That would cause the holy place to be unclean. The oil that they poured on his head shows that I have chosen him as my servant. I am the Lord.
Leviticus21:13 The leader of the priests must marry a woman who has not had sex before.
Leviticus21:14 He must not marry a widow or a woman who has become separate from her husband. He must not marry a woman who has been a prostitute. Instead

Leviticus21:17 ‘Tell Aaron
Leviticus21:18 No man may serve me if he has anything wrong with his body. He must not be blind. He must not have a weak leg or one leg that is shorter than the other leg. He must not have any part of his face that is the wrong shape.
Leviticus21:19 He must not have an arm or a leg that is broken.
Leviticus21:20 He must not have a back that is not straight. He must not be very small and thin. He must not have an eye disease or a skin disease. He must not have testicles that are spoiled.
Leviticus21:21 No descendant of Aaron who has something wrong with his body may serve me as a priest. He may not offer gifts to me
Leviticus21:22 He may eat the holy food offerings and the most holy food offerings.
Leviticus21:23 But he must not go near the curtain in the Holy Place. He must not go up to the altar. This is because he has something wrong with his body. He must not make the holy places unclean. I am the Lord. I cause the priests to be holy.” ’

Leviticus22:1 The Lord said to Moses

Leviticus22:3 No priest may come near to the holy offerings when he is unclean. Those are gifts that Israel's people have offered to me
Leviticus22:4 If any descendant of Aaron is unclean
Leviticus22:5 He may have touched an unclean animal that moves near to the ground. He may have touched a person who is unclean for any reason. All these things will cause the priest himself to become unclean.
Leviticus22:6 Any priest who touches any of these unclean things will remain unclean until evening. He must not eat any of the holy offerings until he has washed his whole body.
Leviticus22:7 When the sun goes down
Leviticus22:8 A priest may find the body of an animal that has died by itself
Leviticus22:9 Priests must obey my commands. If they do not obey them
Leviticus22:10 Only people who belong to a priest's family may eat any of the holy offerings. No visitor who is living in the priest's house may do that. No servant that the priest pays to work for him may do that.
Leviticus22:11 But a slave that the priest has bought for himself may eat the holy offerings. And a slave that was born in the priest's house may eat them.
Leviticus22:12 If a priest's daughter marries a man who is not a priest
Leviticus22:13 But if her husband dies or she becomes separate from him
Leviticus22:14 Someone might eat a holy offering by mistake. If that happens
Leviticus22:15 The priests must always respect the holy offerings as special food. They are gifts which Israel's people offer to the Lord.
Leviticus22:16 If the priests allow other people to eat the holy offerings
Leviticus22:17 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:18 ‘Speak to Aaron
Leviticus22:19 I will only accept your gift if it is a perfect male animal. It can be a bull
Leviticus22:20 You must not offer to me any animal that has something wrong with it. If it is not perfect
Leviticus22:21 This is also the rule if someone brings an animal as a sacrifice for a friendship offering. He may do that because of a special promise. Or he may do it because he chooses to give it. It may be a bull
Leviticus22:22 You must not offer to the Lord an animal that is blind. It must not have a broken bone or a body that has become spoiled. It must not have sores or any other disease on its skin. You must not offer on the altar any animal like that as a gift to the Lord.
Leviticus22:23 You may bring a bull or a sheep that has a leg which is longer or shorter than the other legs. I will accept that animal as a gift that you choose to bring to me. I will not accept that animal as an offering that you make because of a promise.
Leviticus22:24 You must not offer to the Lord an animal that has something wrong with its testicles. I will not accept the animal if anyone has hurt its testicles or removed them. You must never hurt the testicles of any animal in your land.
Leviticus22:25 You must not offer to me animals like that which you have bought from a foreign person. I will not accept them as a gift of food from you. Those animals have bodies which are spoiled. They are not perfect.” ’

Leviticus22:27 ‘When a calf
Leviticus22:28 You must not kill a cow or a sheep on the same day that you kill its baby.

Leviticus22:30 You must eat the animal's meat on the same day that you offer it. None of the meat may remain until the next morning. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:31 You must be careful to obey my commands. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:32 You must not bring shame to my holy name. You people of Israel must know that I am holy as I live among you. I am the Lord and I cause you to be holy.
