Leviticus23:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:2 ‘Tell this to Israel's people: These are the special days and festivals that I have chosen. At these times you must meet together to worship me.
Leviticus23:3 In each week
Leviticus23:4 There are also times that I have chosen for festivals. They are holy days when you must meet together to worship me.
Leviticus23:5 The Passover festival will be on the 14th day of the first month of each year. It will begin when the sun goes down on that day. It is a time to give honour to the Lord.
Leviticus23:6 On the next day
Leviticus23:7 On the first of those seven days
Leviticus23:8 For seven days you must offer sacrifices as a gift to the Lord. On the seventh day you must meet together again to worship the Lord. You must not do your usual work on that day.’
Leviticus23:9 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:10 ‘Tell Israel's people
Leviticus23:11 He will lift up the grain as a special offering to the Lord. The Lord will accept your gift. The priest will do this on the day after the Sabbath day.
Leviticus23:12 On the same day
Leviticus23:13 You must also bring two tenths of an ephah of the best flour that is mixed with olive oil. That will be your gift to the Lord. The smell of this offering will give pleasure to the Lord. You must also offer 1 litre of wine as a drink offering.
Leviticus23:14 You must not eat any of the new grain until you have done this. You must not use the grain to make bread. You must not cook it. Wait until the day that you bring the offering to your God. Then you may eat the grain. This is a rule that will continue for ever
Leviticus23:15 Count seven weeks from the day when the priest has made this offering of grain.
Leviticus23:16 You must count until the day after the seventh Sabbath day. That will be 50 days. Then you must bring another grain offering to the Lord.
Leviticus23:17 Each family must bring from their homes two loaves of bread as a special gift to the Lord. You must use two tenths of an ephah of good flour and yeast to make the bread. This is a gift to the Lord from the first crops of your harvest.
Leviticus23:18 As well as the bread
Leviticus23:19 You must also offer one male goat as a sin offering. And you must offer two lambs that are one year old as a friendship offering.
Leviticus23:20 The priest will lift up the two lambs and the bread as a special gift to the Lord. They are a holy offering to the Lord that belongs to the priests.
Leviticus23:21 On that day
Leviticus23:22 At harvest time
Leviticus23:23 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:24 ‘Tell Israel's people
Leviticus23:25 You must not do any of your usual work on that day. Instead
Leviticus23:26 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:27 ‘The tenth day of the seventh month is the Day of Atonement. On that day
Leviticus23:28 You must not do any work on that day. It is a day of atonement. The priests will offer sacrifices to the Lord your God to make you clean from your sins.
Leviticus23:29 You must punish anyone who does not obey the rules for this day. That person can no longer belong among God's people.
Leviticus23:30 I will destroy any person from among my people who does any work on that day.
Leviticus23:31 You must not do any kind of work. This rule will continue for ever in all the places where you live.
Leviticus23:32 It is a Sabbath day of complete rest for all of you. It is a day when you must not eat any food. You must rest from the evening of the ninth day until the evening of the tenth day.’
Leviticus23:33 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:34 ‘Tell Israel's people
Leviticus23:35 On the first day of the festival
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you must offer special gifts to the Lord. On the eighth day you must meet together to worship the Lord. You must offer a special gift to him. It is the last day of the festival and you must all meet together. You must not do any of your usual work.
Leviticus23:37 These are the rules for the special days that the Lord has chosen. They are times when you must meet together to worship the Lord. You will also offer special gifts to him. You will bring burnt offerings
Leviticus23:38 You must meet to worship the Lord on these festival days
Leviticus23:39 On the 15th day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 On the first day
Leviticus23:41 You must worship the Lord in this way for seven days. Do this in the seventh month of each year. This is a rule that will continue for all time.
Leviticus23:42 All of you who were born as Israelites must live in huts for seven days.
Leviticus23:43 Then your descendants in the future will know that Israel's people lived in tents for many years. I caused them to do that after I rescued them from the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.” ’
Leviticus23:44 Moses told the Israelites these rules about the festivals that the Lord had chosen. They were times for them to worship him.
Leviticus24:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:2 ‘ Command Israel's people to bring you pure oil from olives that they have beaten. Use this oil to burn in the lamps in the Tent of Meeting. This will cause the light to shine all the time.
Leviticus24:3 Aaron must take care of the lamps that are in front of the curtain in the tent. The Covenant Box stands behind the curtain. Aaron must make sure that the lamps burn all night in front of the Lord
Leviticus24:4 Aaron must take care of the lamps on the lampstand that is made of pure gold. He must make sure that the lamps always burn there
Leviticus24:5 Use the best flour to bake 12 round loaves of bread. Use two tenths of an ephah of flour for each loaf.
Leviticus24:6 Put the loaves on the gold table in front of the Lord. Put them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 Put pure frankincense along each row. This incense will burn as a special gift to the Lord instead of the bread.
Leviticus24:8 Aaron must put new bread on the table every Sabbath day. It shows the covenant that continues for ever between Israel's people and the Lord.
Leviticus24:9 The bread will belong to Aaron and his sons. They must eat it in a holy place because it is a very holy offering to the Lord. It will always be a part of the offerings that belongs to the priests.’

Leviticus25:1 When Moses was on Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 ‘Tell Israel's people this: You will go into the land that I will give to you. Then the land must have a rest every seven years. That will give me honour.
Leviticus25:3 For six years
Leviticus25:4 But in the seventh year
Leviticus25:5 You must not cut your crops or your grapes at harvest time. The land must have a year of complete rest.
Leviticus25:6 But some things will still grow on the land during that year. You may eat anything that grows by itself in the Sabbath year. That will be food for you and for your servants. It will be food for the people that you pay to work for you. And it will be food for the foreign people who are living with you.
Leviticus25:7 The land will also provide food for your farm animals and for the wild animals. You may eat any food that the land provides during that year.
Leviticus25:8 You must also count seven Sabbath years. That is seven times seven years
Leviticus25:9 In the 50th year you must make a loud noise with trumpets everywhere in your land. Do that on the tenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus25:10 You must make sure that the 50th year is a special holy year. It is a happy Jubilee year. It is a time for everyone in the land to become free from their debts. Anyone who has sold his family's land will receive it back again. Slaves will return to live with their own people.
Leviticus25:11 The 50th year is a Jubilee year for you. You must not plant any seeds in your fields. You must not cut your crops at harvest time. You must not pick any grapes from your vines.
Leviticus25:12 It is a Jubilee year for you. You must keep it as a holy year. You may only eat food that grows by itself in your fields.
Leviticus25:13 In the Jubilee year you will all return to the land that belongs to your family.
Leviticus25:14 At any time
Leviticus25:15 If you buy some land
Leviticus25:16 If there are many years until the Jubilee year
Leviticus25:17 Do not cheat each other. Respect and obey your God. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus25:18 You must obey my rules and my laws. If you are careful to do that
Leviticus25:19 The land will provide plenty of food for you to eat. You will live there safely.
Leviticus25:20 You may think
Leviticus25:21 This is what will happen. I will bless the land in the sixth year so that it grows a lot of food for you. It will provide enough crops to give you food for three years!
Leviticus25:22 When you plant seeds in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 You must never sell your land so that it completely belongs to someone else. The land always belongs to me
Leviticus25:24 Anybody who sells land must always have the chance to buy it back again.
Leviticus25:25 If an Israelite becomes poor
Leviticus25:26 There might not be anyone in the man's family who can do that. Later
Leviticus25:27 He must count how many years remain until the next Jubilee year. He must pay the value of those years to the man that he sold the land to. Then it will become his own land again.
Leviticus25:28 But the man may never have enough money to pay the value of the land. If so
Leviticus25:29 It is different for a house inside a city that has walls. If someone sells his house in a city
Leviticus25:30 If the man does not buy back the house in that year
Leviticus25:31 But a house may be in a village that has no walls around it. If someone sells the house
Leviticus25:32 Some cities belong to the Levites for them to live in. If a Levite sells his house
Leviticus25:33 When the Jubilee year comes
Leviticus25:34 But the Levites must never sell the fields that are around their cities. That land belongs to them for ever.
Leviticus25:35 If an Israelite becomes poor
Leviticus25:36 If you lend money to him
Leviticus25:37 If you lend money to him
Leviticus25:38 I am the Lord your God who rescued you from the land of Egypt. Now I am giving you the land of Canaan. I have done this to be your God.
Leviticus25:39 If a poor Israelite is in debt to you
Leviticus25:40 Instead
Leviticus25:41 In the Jubilee year
Leviticus25:42 Israel's people are my servants. I brought them out of Egypt. So nobody may buy them as slaves.
Leviticus25:43 You must not be cruel to poor people. Instead
Leviticus25:44 You are allowed to buy male and female slaves
Leviticus25:45 You may also buy people from the families of foreign people who live among you. The children that they give birth to in your land may also become your slaves.
Leviticus25:46 Foreign slaves may continue to belong to you for your whole life. After you die
Leviticus25:47 A foreign person who lives with you might become rich. A poor Israelite might be in debt to him. So the Israelite might sell himself to the foreigner or to someone from the foreigner's family.
Leviticus25:48 Someone from the family of the poor Israelite always has the chance to buy him back.
Leviticus25:49 It may be his brother
Leviticus25:50 The poor man and the foreign man who bought him must decide together the fair price. They must count the number of years that remain until the next Jubilee year. They must decide how much pay a worker would receive during that time. That is the right price for the poor Israelite man to become free.
Leviticus25:51 Many years may remain until the Jubilee year. If so
Leviticus25:52 If only a few years remain
Leviticus25:53 The foreign person who has bought the Israelite man must pay him each year
Leviticus25:54 When the Jubilee year comes
Leviticus25:55 Israel's people are my own servants. They belong to me because I rescued them from the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.’
Leviticus26:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus26:2 You must obey my rules about Sabbath days. You must respect my holy tabernacle. I am the Lord.
Leviticus26:3 You must carefully obey my laws and my commands.
Leviticus26:4 If you do
Leviticus26:5 You will continue to bring in grain from your fields until it is time to pick your grapes. And you will continue to pick your grapes until it is time to plant seeds for the next year. You will have all the food to eat that you need. You will live safely in your land.
Leviticus26:6 I will cause there to be peace in your land. You will not be afraid of trouble when you lie down to sleep. I will remove dangerous animals from your land. No enemies will come to attack you.
Leviticus26:7 You will be able to chase after your enemies and kill them.
Leviticus26:8 Five of your men will chase away 100 of your enemies. 100 of your men will chase away 10
Leviticus26:9 I will help you. I will cause you to have many children so that the number of your people grows. I will do what I have promised to do in my covenant with you.
Leviticus26:10 When harvest time comes each year
Leviticus26:11 I will have my home among you. I will never turn against you.
Leviticus26:12 I will walk among you. I will be your God and you will be my people.
Leviticus26:13 I am the Lord your God. I brought you out from Egypt so that you would no longer be slaves of the Egyptians. I destroyed the power that caused you to suffer. Then you could live as free people.
Leviticus26:14 But it will be different if you do not respect me and obey my commands.
Leviticus26:15 I will punish you if you do not accept my laws and you turn against my rules. I will punish you if you do not obey all my commands and you spoil the covenant that I made with you.
Leviticus26:16 This is how I will punish you: I will cause terrible things to happen to you. You will have diseases that make you weak and diseases that make your bodies very hot. You will have diseases that spoil your eyes and diseases that take away your strength. You will plant seeds in your fields but your enemies will eat the crops.
Leviticus26:17 I will turn against you. Your enemies will attack you and they will win against you. People who hate you will rule over you. You will run away to escape even if nobody is chasing you!
Leviticus26:18 Even after I have punished you like that
Leviticus26:19 I will stop you being proud of your strength. I will not send any rain from the sky. Your land will become hard and dry.
Leviticus26:20 You will use all your strength to work very hard
Leviticus26:21 If you continue to turn against me
Leviticus26:22 I will send wild animals to attack you. They will kill your children and your animals. Very few of your people will remain and nobody will travel on your roads.
Leviticus26:23 After all that punishment
Leviticus26:24 I myself will turn against you. I will punish you another seven times more strongly for your sins.
Leviticus26:25 I will send armies to attack you. They will punish you because you have not obeyed my covenant with you. If you escape into your cities
Leviticus26:26 I will stop you getting any food. Ten women will bake all the bread for their families in only one oven. They will weigh the bread for each person but nobody will have enough to eat.
Leviticus26:27 After this
Leviticus26:28 I will be angry and I will turn against you. I myself will punish you seven times more strongly because of your sins.
Leviticus26:29 Then you will be so hungry that you will eat your own children.
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your altars for sacrifices to false gods and your altars for incense. I will put your dead bodies in a heap on top of your dead idols. I will hate you.
Leviticus26:31 I will destroy your cities and the places where you worship. The sweet smell of your offerings will not give me pleasure.
Leviticus26:32 I myself will destroy the land where you live. Your enemies who come to live there will be very upset when they see it.
Leviticus26:33 I will chase you out to go and live among other nations. I will cause enemies to attack you. I will destroy your land. Your cities will become heaps of stones.
Leviticus26:34 While you are living in the countries of your enemies
Leviticus26:35 When you lived on the land
Leviticus26:36 Some of you will remain alive in the countries of your enemies. But you will live in great fear. The sound of a leaf that moves in the wind will cause you to run away. You will think that someone is chasing you to kill you. Nobody is chasing you
Leviticus26:37 You will fall over each other
Leviticus26:38 You will die among the nations where you are living. That is where you will come to an end.
Leviticus26:39 Those of you who still remain will slowly die in the countries of your enemies. You will become weak and you will die because of your sins and because of the sins of your ancestors.
Leviticus26:40 But one day
Leviticus26:41 That was why I myself turned against them and I took them to the countries of their enemies. When they know that
Leviticus26:42 When that happens
Leviticus26:43 It was necessary for my people to go away from the land for a time. The land needed to be empty so that it could have the Sabbath rest that it should have had. My people refused to obey my rules. They hated my laws. So they needed to receive the punishment that they deserved for their sins.
Leviticus26:44 But I am the Lord their God. So I will not forget them while they are living in the countries of their enemies. I will not turn away from them. I will not destroy them completely. I will not forget the covenant that I made with them.
Leviticus26:45 Yes
Leviticus26:46 These are the laws
Leviticus27:1 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus27:2 ‘Speak to Israel's people. Tell them
Leviticus27:3 He must pay the value of the offering that he has promised. For a man between 20 years and 60 years old
Leviticus27:4 For a woman
Leviticus27:5 For a male between 5 years and 20 years old
Leviticus27:6 For a boy between one month and five years old
Leviticus27:7 For a man who is older than 60 years
Leviticus27:8 The person who makes the promise might be too poor to pay the value of his offering. If so
Leviticus27:9 Someone might promise to offer an animal as a gift to the Lord. If it is the kind of animal that the Lord would accept as an offering
Leviticus27:10 The person must not then change his offering in any way. He must not change a good animal for a bad animal. He must not change a bad animal for a good animal. If the person does change one animal for another
Leviticus27:11 The animal that the person promises to give might be an unclean animal. The Lord does not accept that kind of animal as an offering. So the person must bring the animal to the priest.
Leviticus27:12 The priest will look at the animal to see whether it is good or bad. Then he will decide its value as an offering. The priest is the person who decides the value.
Leviticus27:13 The person who promised his animal as an offering might want to buy the animal back. If so
Leviticus27:14 Someone might offer his house as a holy gift to the Lord. The priest will look at the house to see whether it is good or bad. Then he will decide its value as an offering.
Leviticus27:15 If the person who offered the house wants to buy it back
Leviticus27:16 Someone might offer part of his family's land as a holy gift to the Lord. He must decide how much seed the land needs to grow crops. The value of the land will be 50 shekels of silver for each homer of seed that the land needs.
Leviticus27:17 If a person gives his land to the Lord in a Jubilee year
Leviticus27:18 But the land's value will be less if the person gives it after the Jubilee year. The priest will count the number of years that remain until the next Jubilee. Then he will decide the value of the land.
Leviticus27:19 If the person wants to buy the land back
Leviticus27:20 It is different if he does not buy the land back and he sells it to someone else. If he does that
Leviticus27:21 When the next Jubilee year comes
Leviticus27:22 Someone might offer to the Lord a field that is not part of his family's land. It might be a field that he has bought from someone.
Leviticus27:23 If so
Leviticus27:24 When the Jubilee year comes
Leviticus27:25 The priest is the person who must decide the value of all these offerings. He must use the shekel that they use in the holy place to decide the value. There are 20 gerahs in one shekel.
Leviticus27:26 The first baby that an animal gives birth to is holy. It belongs to the Lord. So nobody can offer it as a special gift to the Lord. If it is an ox or a sheep
Leviticus27:27 But if it is an unclean animal
Leviticus27:28 Someone might give to the Lord as a complete gift something that belongs to him. It might be a person
Leviticus27:29 If you give a person to the Lord as a complete gift
Leviticus27:30 One tenth of all the food that the land provides belongs to the Lord as a holy gift. It may be grain that grows in the fields
Leviticus27:31 If a person chooses to buy back any of his tithe
Leviticus27:32 Out of every ten farm animals
Leviticus27:33 The owner must not pick what animal he will give to the Lord. He must not change a good animal for a bad animal. He must not change a bad animal for a good animal. If anyone does that
Leviticus27:34 These are the commands that the Lord gave to Moses on Mount Sinai. He told Moses to teach them to Israel's people.
Numbers1:1 The Israelites were in the Sinai Desert. It was the first day of the second month of the second year after they left Egypt. Moses was in the Tent of Meeting. The Lord said to Moses
Numbers1:2 ‘Put the people into clans and families and count them. Make a list. Write the name of each man on the list.
Numbers1:3 You and Aaron must record the names of all the men in each group who can fight in the army. Record the names of men who are 20 years old or older.
Numbers1:4 A man from each tribe must help you. Each of them will be the leader of his family.
Numbers1:5 These are the men from each tribe who must help you: Reuben: Elizur
Numbers1:6 Simeon: Shelumiel
Numbers1:7 Judah: Nahshon
Numbers1:8 Issachar: Nethanel
Numbers1:9 Zebulun: Eliab
Numbers1:10 Ephraim (Joseph's son): Elishama
Numbers1:11 Benjamin: Abidan
Numbers1:12 Dan: Ahiezer
Numbers1:13 Asher: Pagiel
Numbers1:14 Gad: Eliasaph
Numbers1:15 Naphtali: Ahira
Numbers1:16 These were the men that they chose from among the people. They were leaders of their clans. Each of them worked on behalf of the tribe of their ancestor.
Numbers1:17 Moses and Aaron told these leaders to come and help.
Numbers1:18 On the first day of the second month
Numbers1:19 They did the things that the Lord had told Moses to do. So Moses recorded the names there in the Sinai Desert.
Numbers2:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers2:2 ‘The Israelites must put up their tents on each side of the Tent of Meeting. They must not be very near to it but they must be all around it. They must live together in their tribes. Each person must be with his own tribe. Each tribe must have its own flag.
Numbers2:3 The people in Judah's tribe must be on the east side of the Tent. They must have their flag with them
Numbers2:4 There will be 74
Numbers2:5 The tribe of Issachar will put up their tents on one side of Judah's camp. The leader of the tribe of Issachar is Nethanel
Numbers2:6 There will be 54
Numbers2:7 The tribe of Zebulun will be on the other side of Judah's camp. The leader of the tribe of Zebulun is Eliab
Numbers2:8 There will be 57
Numbers2:9 So there will be 186
Numbers2:10 The tribe of Reuben must be on the south side of the tent. They must have their flag with them
Numbers2:11 There will be 46
Numbers2:12 The tribe of Simeon will put up their tents on one side of Reuben's camp. The leader of the tribe of Simeon is Shelumiel
Numbers2:13 There will be 59
Numbers2:14 The tribe of Gad will be on the other side of Reuben's camp. The leader of the tribe of Gad is Eliasaph
Numbers2:15 There will be 45
Numbers2:16 So there will be 151
Numbers2:17 The Levites must march next. The Levites must carry the Tent of Meeting. They must march between the first two groups of tribes and the last two groups of tribes. Each man must be in his own place. They must march with their flag.
Numbers2:18 The tribe of Ephraim must be on the west side of the tent. They must have their flag with them and they must live in groups. The leader of the tribe of Ephraim is Elishama
Numbers2:19 There will be 40
Numbers2:20 The tribe of Manasseh will be on one side of Ephraim's camp. The leader of the tribe of Manasseh is Gamaliel
Numbers2:21 There will be 32
Numbers2:22 The tribe of Benjamin will be on the other side of Ephraim's camp. The leader of the tribe of Benjamin is Abidan
Numbers2:23 There will be 35
Numbers2:24 So there will be 108
Numbers2:25 The tribe of Dan must be on the north side of the tent. They must have their flag with them and they must live in groups. The leader of the tribe of Dan is Ahiezer
Numbers2:26 There will be 62
Numbers2:27 The tribe of Asher will put up their tents on one side of Dan's camp. The leader of the tribe of Asher is Pagiel
Numbers2:28 There will be 41
Numbers2:29 The tribe of Naphtali will be on the other side of Dan's camp. The leader of the tribe of Naphtali is Ahira
Numbers2:30 There will be 53
Numbers2:31 So there will be 157
Numbers2:32 In that way
Numbers2:33 They did not count the Levites with the other Israelites. That was what the Lord had told Moses.
Numbers2:34 The Israelites did everything that the Lord had told Moses. They put up their tents in groups
Numbers3:1 This is the list of the family of Aaron and Moses. This was at the time when the Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 The names of the sons of Aaron were Nadab
Numbers3:3 Aaron anointed them
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu fell down and died in front of the Lord. That happened when they offered strange fire to the Lord in the Sinai Desert. They had no children. So only Eleazar and Ithamar worked as priests while their father Aaron was alive.
Numbers3:5 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:6 ‘Bring the tribe of Levi to Aaron the priest. They must help him.
Numbers3:7 They must serve him and all the Israelites. They must work at the Tent of Meeting and take care of it.
Numbers3:8 They must work on behalf of the Israelites. They must take care of the things that are in the Tent of Meeting. That is how they will serve God in the tabernacle.
Numbers3:9 Give the Levites to Aaron and to his descendants. They will be the Israelites who help him. They do not have to do anything else.
Numbers3:10 But you must make a list of Aaron and his descendants. They will be the priests. If any other person comes near to the special tent
Numbers3:11 The Lord also said to Moses
Numbers3:12 ‘I will not take the firstborn son of every woman in Israel to belong to me. I will take the Levites instead of them.
Numbers3:13 I killed all the firstborn sons of the Egyptians. But I kept the firstborn sons of every person and animal among the Israelites for myself. They belong to me. I am the Lord!’
Numbers3:14 The Lord said to Moses in the Sinai Desert
Numbers3:15 ‘Put the Levites into clans and families and count them. Make a list of every male who is a month old or older.’
Numbers3:16 Moses obeyed the Lord. So Moses recorded them as the Lord said.
Numbers3:17 The names of the sons of Levi were Gershon
Numbers3:18 Gershon had two sons. Their names were Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 Kohath's sons were called Amram
Numbers3:20 Merari's sons were called Mahli and Mushi. Those were the sons and grandsons of Levi. They became the leaders of the clans of Levi's tribe.
Numbers3:21 There were two families in Gershon's clan. They were the families of Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:22 There were 7
Numbers3:23 Gershon's clan had to put up their tents on the west side of the special tent.
Numbers3:24 The leader of the clan of Gershon was Eliasaph the son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 Gershon's clan had to take care of the Tent of Meeting
Numbers3:26 There was a yard round the outside of the tabernacle and the altar. Gershon's family took care of the curtains that hung around the yard
Numbers3:27 There were four families in Kohath's clan. They were the families of Amram
Numbers3:28 There were 8
Numbers3:29 Kohath's clan had to put up their tents on the south side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:30 The leader of Kohath's descendants was Elizaphan
Numbers3:31 Kohath's family had to take care of the Covenant Box
Numbers3:32 Eleazar
Numbers3:33 There were two families in Merari's clan. They were the families of Mahli and Mushi.
Numbers3:34 There were 6
Numbers3:35 The leader of Merari's clan was Zuriel
Numbers3:36 Merari's family had to take care of all the wood parts of the tabernacle. They took care of the heavy things that stood on the ground to hold the poles.
Numbers3:37 They also took care of the poles that held the cloth around the yard
Numbers3:38 Moses
Numbers3:39 So Moses and Aaron obeyed the Lord. They counted the Levites by their clans. There were 22
Numbers3:40 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:41 The Israelites' firstborn sons belong to me. I am the Lord. But I will accept all the males among the Levites instead. The Israelites' firstborn animals also belong to me. But I will accept the animals of the Levites instead.’
Numbers3:42 So Moses counted all the firstborn males among the Israelites as the Lord had said.
Numbers3:43 He counted 22
Numbers3:44 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:45 ‘Do not take the firstborn males among the people to belong to me. Accept the Levites instead. Do not use the firstborn animals of the people. Accept the animals of the Levites instead. The Levites will belong to me. I am the Lord.
Numbers3:46 There are 273 more firstborn males of the people than there are Levites. So the people must pay for the extra firstborn sons.
Numbers3:47 You must accept five shekels for each extra firstborn male. The shekel must have the same weight as the shekel that the Levites use in the holy place. There are 20 gerahs in one shekel.
Numbers3:48 Give that money to Aaron and his sons.’
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the money to buy back the extra 273 firstborn sons. That was because the number of firstborn sons was more than the number of Levites.
Numbers3:50 He took 1
Numbers3:51 Moses did what the Lord had told him to do. He gave the money to Aaron and his sons.
Numbers4:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:2 ‘Write a list of the people in Kohath's clan in their families. That list must be separate from the list of the other Levites.
Numbers4:3 Record the men who can work in the Tent of Meeting. Record them if they are between 30 and 50 years old.
Numbers4:4 These men must take care of everything that is most holy in the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers4:5 When the people are ready to move
Numbers4:6 Then they must cover the curtain with good leather. On the top of that
Numbers4:7 They must put a blue cloth over the table for the special bread. The bread must remain on the table. They must put on the cloth the plates
Numbers4:8 Then they must cover all those things with a red cloth. On the top of the red cloth
Numbers4:9 They must cover the lampstand and its tools with a blue cloth. They must also cover the jars for the oil with the blue cloth.
Numbers4:10 Then they must cover all this with good leather. They must carry it on a board.
Numbers4:11 They must cover the gold altar with a blue cloth. They must cover the blue cloth with good leather. Then they must fix the poles to the altar
Numbers4:12 They must cover all the tools from God's holy tent with a blue cloth. They must cover the blue cloth with good leather. They must carry all this on a board.
Numbers4:13 They must remove the ashes from the bronze altar. They must cover the bronze altar with a purple cloth.
Numbers4:14 They must put all the tools on top of it. These include the plates for the fire
Numbers4:15 When the people are ready to leave
Numbers4:16 Eleazar
Numbers4:17 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:18 ‘You must not allow Kohath's clan to come to an end among the other Levites.
Numbers4:19 Do not let the men in that clan die. That will happen if they come near to the most holy things. You must do this: Aaron and his sons must go into the Tent of Meeting. They will tell each man the work that he must do. And they must tell each man the things that he must carry.
Numbers4:20 But the men from Kohath's clan must not look at any of the holy things
Numbers4:21 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers4:22 ‘Also write a list of the people in Gershon's clan in their families.
Numbers4:23 Record the men who can work in the Tent of Meeting. Record them if they are between 30 and 50 years old.
Numbers4:24 The work of the men in Gershon's clan is to carry these things:
Numbers4:25 They must carry the curtains of God's tent. They must carry the Tent of Meeting with the things that cover it. They must also carry the good leather skin that covers everything. And they must carry the curtain for the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers4:26 They must carry the curtains of the yard that is around the tabernacle. Also
Numbers4:27 The men in Gershon's clan must do everything that Aaron and his sons ask them to do. Aaron and his sons must tell them the things that they should carry. And they must tell them everything that they should do.
Numbers4:28 That is the work for the Tent of Meeting that the descendants of Gershon must do. Ithamar
Numbers4:29 Also God said to Moses
Numbers4:30 Record the men who can work in the Tent of Meeting. Record them if they are between 30 and 50 years old.

Numbers4:32 And they must carry the pillars
Numbers4:33 That is the work of the descendants of Merari in the Tent of Meeting. Ithamar
Numbers4:34 So Moses

Numbers4:36 They recorded them in clans. There were 2
Numbers4:37 Moses and Aaron recorded the descendants of Kohath who worked at the Tent of Meeting. The Lord had told Moses that they must do that.
Numbers4:38 Moses and Aaron also recorded the names of Gershon's descendants in their clans and in their families.

Numbers4:40 They recorded them in clans and families. There were 2

Numbers4:46 Moses

Numbers5:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:2 ‘Speak to the Israelites. They must send any person with a disease of the skin away from the camp. Or somebody may have a disease that causes pus to come out of his body. Any person like that is unclean. Anyone who has touched a dead person is also unclean. Send those people out of the camp too.
Numbers5:3 Do this with both men and women. Send them out of the camp. They must not make the camp unclean
Numbers5:4 The Israelites did what the Lord had said to Moses. They sent those people outside the camp.
Numbers5:5 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:6 ‘Tell these rules to the Israelites. A person might do a wrong thing against another person. If they do
Numbers5:7 That person must agree that they have done this sin. They must pay the right amount to the other person. They must also pay them one fifth extra.
Numbers5:8 But the other person may have died. If they have no relative who can accept the money
Numbers5:9 All the special gifts from the Israelites to God are for the priest.
Numbers5:10 The priest will keep all these gifts that he receives from people.’
Numbers5:11 The Lord said to Moses:
Numbers5:12 ‘Speak to the Israelites. Tell them to follow these rules. A woman may do wrong things against her husband.
Numbers5:13 She might have had sex with another man. The husband might not know about this. His wife might have kept it secret. Nobody saw what was happening. So there is nobody to say that it happened.
Numbers5:14 But the man may think that it happened. He will be jealous. But his wife may not really have made herself unclean in that way.
Numbers5:15 The man must take his wife to the priest. He must bring one kilogram of barley flour with him. This is a grain offering to discover if his wife did do those things. So he must not put oil or incense on the grain offering. It is an offering because the man does not trust his wife.
Numbers5:16 Then the priest will bring the woman to stand in front of the Lord.
Numbers5:17 He must pour some holy water into a bowl that is made from clay. He must take some dust from the floor of the tabernacle. And he must put the dust into the water.
Numbers5:18 Then the priest must bring the woman in front of the Lord. He must make the woman's hair free. He must put into her hands the grain offering. That is the offering because the man does not trust his wife. The priest must hold the bowl that contains bitter water. The bitter water brings a curse on guilty people.
Numbers5:19 Then the woman must agree to the strong promise that the priest tells her to say. The priest must say to her
Numbers5:20 But if another man has had sex with you
Numbers5:21 may the Lord bring the curse on you. The bitter water will cause your female parts to become smaller. Your stomach will become very big.
Numbers5:22 That will happen when you drink the bitter water.” The woman must say
Numbers5:23 The priest must write the curses on a scroll of paper. Then he must wash the words off the paper into the bitter water.
Numbers5:24 He must tell the woman that she must drink the bitter water. This water that brings a curse will go into her body. It may cause her to be very ill.
Numbers5:25 But first
Numbers5:26 He must burn a small part of it as a sacrifice. After that
Numbers5:27 If the woman has had sex with another man
Numbers5:28 But
Numbers5:29 Perhaps a man is jealous because his wife has had sex with another man. Or he may only think that his wife has had sex with another man. This is what the Law says that the priest must do: The man must bring her in front of the Lord.
Numbers5:30 
Numbers5:31 If the woman did not do those wrong things
Numbers6:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:2 ‘Tell the Israelites this: “A man or a woman may want to make a special promise to the Lord. That promise will make the person separate from other people
Numbers6:3 They must not drink wine. They must not drink any alcohol or vinegar. They must not drink the juice of grapes
Numbers6:4 They must not eat the seeds of grapes for all the time that they are Nazirites. They must not even eat the skins of grapes.
Numbers6:5 A person must not cut his hair during the time that he is a Nazirite. He must stay like this all the time that he is separate for the Lord. He must let the hair on his head grow long.
Numbers6:6 He must not go near a dead body while he belongs to the Lord as a Nazirite.
Numbers6:7 His hair shows that he has become separate for God. So he must not go near a dead body. He must not go near it even if the dead person is his father
Numbers6:8 During the time that the person is a Nazirite
Numbers6:9 The Nazirite may be near to a person when that person dies suddenly. Then his head becomes unclean. He must remove all the hair from his head seven days after the person dies. Then he will be clean.
Numbers6:10 On day 8
Numbers6:11 The priest must sacrifice one bird as a sin offering and the other bird as a burnt offering. The priest must do that because the Nazirite has done a wrong thing. He went too near to a dead body. So the priest must make him clean from his sin. The hair on the Nazirite's head will be clean again on that same day.
Numbers6:12 He must begin his time as a Nazirite again
Numbers6:13 He is a Nazirite for a certain time. When that time finishes
Numbers6:14 He must offer to the Lord three animals that have nothing wrong with them. He must offer a young male sheep that is one year old. That is a burnt offering. He must offer a young female sheep that is one year old. That is a sin offering. He must offer a male sheep. That is a friendship offering.
Numbers6:15 And he must bring a basket of bread that people have made without yeast. He must bring cakes that people have made with the best flour and oil. Also
Numbers6:16 The priest must offer those things to the Lord. And he must sacrifice the sin offering and the burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 Then the priest must sacrifice the male sheep as a friendship offering to the Lord. He must offer the basket of bread. He must give the grain offering and the drink offering with it.
Numbers6:18 Then the Nazirite must stand at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. He must remove all the hair from his head. He must put his hair into the same fire where the priest has cooked the friendship offering.
Numbers6:19 When the male sheep is cooked
Numbers6:20 The Nazirite must give those things back to the priest. The priest must lift them up to the Lord as a special gift. They are a special gift for the priest. Also
Numbers6:21 Those are the rules about Nazirites. Also
Numbers6:22 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:23 ‘Tell Aaron and his sons to use these words to bless the Israelites.
Numbers6:24 They must say: “The Lord will bless you and he will keep you safe.
Numbers6:25 The Lord will smile at you and he will be very kind to you.
Numbers6:26 The Lord will be pleased with you and he will give you peace in your minds.”
Numbers6:27 Aaron and his sons must use my name to bless the Israelites. If they do that
Numbers7:1 When Moses had put up the tabernacle
Numbers7:2 Then the 12 leaders of Israel's tribes brought their offerings. These men were the leaders who had helped to count the people.
Numbers7:3 They brought their gifts to the Lord. They brought six carts
Numbers7:4 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers7:5 ‘Accept these gifts from the leaders. Give them to the Levites. All the Levites can use them for their work at the Tent of Meeting.’
Numbers7:6 So Moses took the carts and the oxen and he gave them to the Levites.
Numbers7:7 He gave two carts and four oxen to the descendants of Gershon.

Numbers8:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:2 ‘Say to Aaron
Numbers8:3 So Aaron did that. He put its lamps at the front of the lampstand
Numbers8:4 A worker had made the lampstand from one piece of gold that he had made flat with a hammer. From its base to the flowers at the top
Numbers8:5 Again the Lord spoke to Moses. He said
Numbers8:6 ‘Take the Levites from among the Israelites and make them clean.
Numbers8:7 This is how you will do it: Splash on them the water that makes people clean. They must remove all the hair from their whole body
Numbers8:8 Then they must take a bull with the proper grain offering and oil. And you must take another bull for a sin offering.
Numbers8:9 Then you must bring the Levites in front of the Tent of Meeting. You must also bring all the Israelites together there.
Numbers8:10 You must bring the Levites in front of the Lord. Then the Israelites must put their hands on the Levites' heads.
Numbers8:11 Aaron must give the Levites to the Lord as a special offering from the Israelites. Then they will be ready to do the Lord 's work.
Numbers8:12 Then the Levites must put their hands on the heads of the bulls. Then offer one bull for a sin offering and the other one for a burnt offering to the Lord. That will make the Levites clean from their sins.
Numbers8:13 And you must cause the Levites to stand in front of Aaron and his sons. So they will be a special gift to the Lord.
Numbers8:14 That is how you will make the Levites separate from the other Israelites. And then the Levites will belong to me.
Numbers8:15 After that
Numbers8:16 That is because all the Levites among the Israelites are mine. All the Israelites' firstborn sons are mine. But I have taken the Levites for myself instead.
Numbers8:17 Every firstborn son among the Israelites is mine. And all the firstborn male animals are mine too. On the day that I killed all the firstborn sons in Egypt
Numbers8:18 But I have not taken all the firstborn sons among the Israelites. Instead
Numbers8:19 I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and to his sons from among the Israelites. They will work at the Tent of Meeting on behalf of the Israelites. They will make the Israelites clean from their sins. So there will not be a bad disease among the Israelites when they go near to God's holy tent.’
Numbers8:20 So Moses and Aaron and all the Israelites did to the Levites everything that the Lord had commanded Moses to do.
Numbers8:21 The Levites made themselves clean and they washed their clothes. Then Aaron brought them in front of Lord as a special gift. Aaron also burnt sacrifices to make them clean.
Numbers8:22 After that
Numbers8:23 Then the Lord spoke to Moses again. He said
Numbers8:24 ‘These are the rules about the Levites: The men who are 25 years old or older must go in to work in the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers8:25 But they must stop that work when they are 50 years old. They must not do this work any more.
Numbers8:26 But they can help the other Levites in the Tent of Meeting. They can help them to do their jobs. But they must not have any of their own jobs. Those are the rules about the Levites and their jobs.’

Numbers10:8 Only Aaron's sons

Numbers11:1 Then the people started to complain about their problems. The Lord heard them and he became very angry. The Lord sent his fire to burn among them. It destroyed some parts at the edge of the camp.
Numbers11:2 So the people asked Moses to help them. Moses prayed to the Lord
Numbers11:3 So that place was called Taberah
Numbers11:4 Some foreign people were travelling with the Israelites. These people wanted to eat better food. So the Israelites wept again. They said
Numbers11:5 We remember the fish that we ate in Egypt. We had as much as we wanted. And we remember all the nice vegetables that we ate
Numbers11:6 But now our mouths are dry and we do not want to eat this food. There is not even anything to look at
Numbers11:7 The manna was like small coriander seeds. Its colour was yellow and white.
Numbers11:8 The people walked about and they picked it up from the ground. They used heavy stones to make it into flour. Or they beat it with a stick. They cooked it in pots of water
Numbers11:9 At night
Numbers11:10 People in every family were crying at the doors of their tents. And Moses heard them. The Lord became very angry with the people. Moses was also not happy.

Numbers11:12 They are not my children! But you have said to me
Numbers11:13 They are complaining to me. They are saying
Numbers11:14 I cannot be the leader of all these people unless I receive help. This job is too difficult for me.
Numbers11:15 So if you do not want to help me

Numbers11:17 Then I will come down and I will speak with you there. I will take some of the authority of my Spirit that I gave to you. I will share it with them. Then they will help you to lead the people. So you will not be the only man who is doing that job.

Numbers11:19 You will not eat it only for one day

Numbers11:32 The people were picking up the birds all day and all night and all the next day. Each person picked up at least 1

Numbers11:35 From Kibroth-Hattaavah the people travelled to Hazeroth. They stayed at Hazeroth.

Numbers13:17 When Moses sent them to explore the land
Numbers13:18 Look at the land to see what it is like. See whether the people who live there are strong or weak. See whether few people or many people live there.
Numbers13:19 See whether their land is good or bad. Look at the towns where the people live. See if they have strong walls or if they are in the open.
Numbers13:20 See whether the land is able to make good food grow there
Numbers13:21 So the men went and they explored the land of Canaan. They started in the Zin Desert
Numbers13:22 When they had gone through the Negev Desert in the south of the land
Numbers13:23 Then the men came to the valley called Eshcol. They cut a branch which had one group of grapes on it. It was so large that two men carried it between them on a pole. They also brought some other fruits called pomegranates and figs.
Numbers13:24 People called that valley ‘Eshcol’ because of the large group of grapes that the Israelites cut down there.
Numbers13:25 The men returned after they had explored the land for 40 days.
Numbers13:26 The men returned to Kadesh in the Paran Desert. They brought their message to Moses and Aaron and to all the Israelites there. They told them all about the land of Canaan and they showed its fruit to them.

Numbers15:1 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:2 ‘Say to the Israelites
Numbers15:3 When you are living there
Numbers15:4 The person who brings the gift to the Lord must also bring a gift of flour. He must mix the flour with oil. It should be 1 kilogram of your best flour. Mix this flour with 1 litre of olive oil.
Numbers15:5 You must also give 1 litre of wine for each lamb. You must give that as a drink offering with each burnt offering or sacrifice.
Numbers15:6 If the animal is a male sheep
Numbers15:7 Also
Numbers15:8 A person can give a young bull as a burnt offering. This may be a special sacrifice that he has promised to give to God. Or it may be a friendship offering to the Lord.
Numbers15:9 With the bull
Numbers15:10 Also pour 2 litres of wine on the altar as a drink offering. The smell of this sacrifice will please the Lord.
Numbers15:11 You must do the same thing for each bull or for each male sheep. And you must do the same thing for each male lamb or for each young goat.
Numbers15:12 You must do this for every animal that you offer as a gift to God.
Numbers15:13 All the Israelites must do this when they burn sacrifices. The smell of the sacrifices as they burn will please the Lord.
Numbers15:14 A stranger who is living in your land may want to give a gift by fire to the Lord. He must do the same as you do now
Numbers15:15 These rules will be the same for the Israelites and for all foreign people who live among you. They are for now and for all future time. Both you and the foreign person must obey the Lord 's rules in the same way.
Numbers15:16 There is the same law for you and for the foreign person who is living among you. There is only one set of rules.” ’
Numbers15:17 The Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:18 ‘Tell the Israelites this: “I am bringing you into the land of Canaan. After you have arrived there
Numbers15:19 When you first eat the food from that land
Numbers15:20 You must give a cake as a special offering from the first part of your flour. Do this as you give your special gift from your threshing floor.
Numbers15:21 From the first part of your flour
Numbers15:22 Someone among you might not obey a command that the Lord gave to Moses. But you might not realize your mistake.
Numbers15:23 It might be any command that the Lord gave to Moses for you and your descendants to obey.
Numbers15:24 If it was a mistake that the whole group did not know about it
Numbers15:25 The priest will use these offerings to make the all the Israelites clean from their sin. The Lord will forgive them. Their sin was a mistake and they have brought the proper offerings to the Lord. They have brought to him their burnt offering and their sin offering. So the Lord will forgive them for their mistake.
Numbers15:26 He will forgive all the Israelites and the foreign people who live among you. The sin was a mistake that the whole group should not have made.
Numbers15:27 If one person makes a mistake like that
Numbers15:28 The priest will use this sacrifice to make the person clean from their sin. Then the Lord will forgive that person for their mistake. The person did not realize that it was a sin against the Lord.
Numbers15:29 The same rule is for all people who make a mistake like that. It is for the Israelites and for all the foreign people who live among you.
Numbers15:30 But someone might do something that he knows is a sin. He does it because he wants to do it
Numbers15:31 He has not respected the Lord 's teaching. He has not obeyed the Lord 's commands. So you must remove that person completely from among the people. He deserves his punishment because he is guilty.” ’

Numbers19:1 The Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 ‘This is another rule that the Lord has commanded in his Law. Tell the Israelites to bring you a young red cow that is perfect. It must not have anything wrong with it. Nobody must have put a yoke on it.
Numbers19:3 You must give the cow to Eleazar the priest. He must take it outside the camp. People must kill it there while Eleazar watches.
Numbers19:4 Eleazar must use his finger to take some of the blood from the cow. He must turn towards the Tent of Meeting and splash the blood seven times in that direction.
Numbers19:5 Then people must burn the whole cow while Eleazar is watching. They must burn its body completely
Numbers19:6 The priest must then take some wood
Numbers19:7 Then the priest must wash his clothes and wash his whole body with water. After that
Numbers19:8 The man who burns the cow must also wash his clothes in water
Numbers19:9 Then a man who is clean must pick up all the ashes of the red cow. He must put them in a clean place outside the camp. Keep them safe there for the Israelites to use. Mix the ashes with water. That water will cause unclean people to become clean again. It is water to make people clean from their sin.
Numbers19:10 The man who picks up the cow's ashes must also wash his clothes. He will continue to be unclean until evening. This rule will be for all time
Numbers19:11 Any person who touches a dead body will be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 He must use the water to wash himself on the third day and on the seventh day. Then he will be clean again. If he does not wash himself on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 If anyone touches the body of a dead person and he does not make himself clean again
Numbers19:14 This is another rule: When someone dies in a tent
Numbers19:15 Any pot or bowl in the tent that does not have a lid will also become unclean.
Numbers19:16 These people also become unclean: anyone who touches the dead body of a person out in the country. Somebody may have killed the dead person or they may have died by themselves. Also
Numbers19:17 When a person has become unclean
Numbers19:18 Then someone who is clean must take a branch of hyssop. He must put it into the water. He must use the hyssop to splash the water on the tent and on everything that is in it. He must also splash the water on all the people who have been in the tent. He must also splash it on anyone who has touched a human bone or a dead body or someone's grave.
Numbers19:19 The clean person must splash the water on the unclean person on the third day and on the seventh day. On the seventh day he will cause the person to be clean again. Then the unclean person must use water to wash his clothes and his whole body. In the evening
Numbers19:20 But if an unclean person does not make himself clean in this way
Numbers19:21 This is a rule for all time for the people. The man who splashes the special water on unclean people must wash his clothes. Anyone who touches the special water will be unclean until evening.
Numbers19:22 If an unclean person touches anything

Numbers28:6 It is a regular burnt offering that God told you about. He told you about it on Mount Sinai. It is an offering by fire to the Lord. The smell as it burns makes the Lord happy.

Numbers28:8 You must offer the other lamb when it begins to get dark. Offer it with a grain offering and a drink offering like the offerings in the morning. You must give it as an offering by fire. The smell of the smoke from these sacrifices will make the Lord happy.

Numbers30:10 A woman who is living with her husband may make a promise to the Lord.
Numbers30:11 If her husband hears about it but he does not say anything
Numbers30:12 But her husband may say clearly that she must not do it. Then she must obey him. The Lord will understand why she does not do it.

Numbers30:14 If the husband does not say anything for many days about the promise

Numbers30:16 Those are the rules that the Lord gave to Moses. They say what must happen when husbands

Numbers35:15 Those six towns will be safe places for Israelites and for foreign people who live among them. Anyone who has killed another person may run to one of these towns and be safe there. But they will only be safe if they did not mean to kill the other person.

Numbers35:17 Someone might hit another person with a big stone. If the person dies
Numbers35:18 A man might hit a person with a dangerous weapon made from wood. If the person dies
Numbers35:19 The dead person's relative himself must kill the murderer when he meets him.
Numbers35:20 These are other examples of murder: A man might push another person hard because he hates him. A man might throw something at another person to hurt him.
Numbers35:21 Or a man might hit someone with his hand because he hates him. If the person dies as a result
Numbers35:22 But when a man kills another person
Numbers35:23 Or he might drop a heavy stone on someone when he had not seen them. If he did not hate the other person
Numbers35:24 If that happens
Numbers35:25 If the man is a killer but not a murderer
Numbers35:26 But one day

Numbers35:32 Also
Numbers35:33 Murder causes the land where you live to become unclean. You have to make it clean again. You must kill anyone who murders another person. That is the only way to make the land clean again.

Deuteronomy10:3 So I used acacia wood to make a box. I also cut two new flat stones that were like the first stones. Then I carried the two new stones up the mountain in my hands.
Deuteronomy10:4 The Lord wrote on the stones the same words that he had written before. Those were the ten special commands that he had told you before. He spoke those commands from the middle of the fire on the mountain
Deuteronomy10:5 I came down from the mountain. I put the stones into the box that I had made
Deuteronomy10:6 (At that time
Deuteronomy10:7 Then they travelled to Gudgodah. From there they travelled to Jotbathah
Deuteronomy10:8 At that time
Deuteronomy10:9 That is why the Levites did not receive any land for themselves
Deuteronomy10:10 I stayed on the mountain for 40 days and nights
Deuteronomy10:11 Then the Lord said to me
Deuteronomy10:12 So
Deuteronomy10:13 Obey the Lord 's commands and rules that I am giving to you today. If you do these things
Deuteronomy10:14 The whole sky and the highest heavens belong to the Lord your God. The earth and everything that is in it also belong to him.
Deuteronomy10:15 But the Lord chose to love your ancestors with a faithful love. You are their descendants. He has chosen you from among all the people of the world. You can see that this is true today!
Deuteronomy10:16 So remember that you belong to the Lord. Live to please him! Do not refuse to obey him.
Deuteronomy10:17 For the Lord your God rules all other gods. He is the Lord over all other lords. He is great and powerful. We should respect him and obey him. He is fair to everyone. He will not accept any bribes!
Deuteronomy10:18 He brings justice to widows and to children who have no family. He loves the foreign people who live among you. He gives them food to eat and clothes to wear.
Deuteronomy10:19 You too must love the foreign people who live among you. Remember that you also lived as foreigners in Egypt at one time.
Deuteronomy10:20 Respect and obey the Lord your God. Serve him faithfully. If you make a promise
Deuteronomy10:21 He is the one that you should praise. He is the God that you worship. He has done great and powerful things to help you
Deuteronomy10:22 When your ancestors went to Egypt
Deuteronomy11:1 Love the Lord your God. Do what he tells you to do. Always obey his rules
Deuteronomy11:2 Remember that today I am speaking to you who have seen his powerful acts! Your children did not see the great things that the Lord your God did to teach you. You saw for yourselves how great
Deuteronomy11:3 But your children did not see the great miracles that he did in Egypt. He punished Pharaoh
Deuteronomy11:4 You saw how God destroyed Egypt's army
Deuteronomy11:5 Your children did not see all the things that the Lord did in the desert as he brought you here.
Deuteronomy11:6 They did not see how he punished Dathan and Abiram
Deuteronomy11:7 Remember that I am speaking to you
Deuteronomy11:8 So you must obey all the commands that I am giving to you today. If you do that
Deuteronomy11:9 Then you will enjoy a long life in that land. It is the land that the Lord promised to give to your ancestors and to their descendants. It is a land where there is plenty of food and drink
Deuteronomy11:10 The land that you are going into is not like the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy11:11 But now you are going across the Jordan River into a different kind of land. It has hills and valleys. Rain falls from the sky and makes the ground wet.
Deuteronomy11:12 The Lord your God takes care of the land there. He watches over it every day
Deuteronomy11:13 So listen carefully to the commands that I am giving to you today. Love the Lord your God and serve him in everything that you think and in everything that you do.
Deuteronomy11:14 If you do that
Deuteronomy11:15 I will make grass grow in your fields for your animals to eat. You will have as much food as you want to eat.’
Deuteronomy11:16 But be careful! Do not turn away from the Lord so that you worship other gods. Do not let those things deceive you.
Deuteronomy11:17 If you do that
Deuteronomy11:18 Continue to think carefully about these commands in everything that you do. Tie them as signs onto your hands so that you remember them. Also tie them around your heads.
Deuteronomy11:19 Teach them to your children. Talk about them when you sit together at home. Talk about them as you walk along the road
Deuteronomy11:20 Write them on the wood beside the doors of your houses and on your gates.
Deuteronomy11:21 Do these things so that you and your descendants will live long lives in the land. It is the land that the Lord promised to give to your ancestors. You will continue to live there while there is still a sky above the earth.
Deuteronomy11:22 Be careful to obey these commands that I am giving to you. Love the Lord your God. Live in a way that pleases him. Be faithful to him.
Deuteronomy11:23 If you do that
Deuteronomy11:24 Every piece of ground that you walk on will belong to you. The borders of your land will go from the desert in the south as far as Lebanon in the north. They will go from the Euphrates River in the east to the Mediterranean Sea in the west.
Deuteronomy11:25 Nobody will be able to stop you. The Lord your God will cause everyone in the other nations to be afraid of you. They will shake with fear. He has promised to do that
Deuteronomy11:26 Today I am telling you to choose. Do you want God to bless you? Or do you want God to curse you?
Deuteronomy11:27 The Lord your God will bless you if you obey his commands that I am giving to you today.
Deuteronomy11:28 But he will curse you if you refuse to obey his commands. If you turn away from him and you serve other gods that are new to you
Deuteronomy11:29 When the Lord brings you into the land where you will live
Deuteronomy11:30 You know where these mountains are. They are west of the Jordan River
Deuteronomy11:31 Soon you will go across the Jordan River. You will take for yourselves the land that the Lord your God is giving to you. It will belong to you as your home.
Deuteronomy11:32 When you are there
Deuteronomy12:1 These are the laws and the rules that you must obey. Be careful to obey them when you live in the land that the Lord your God has given to you. He is the God that your ancestors worshipped. Obey these laws all the time that you live in that land.
Deuteronomy12:2 You will chase out the nations who live there. Then you must destroy completely all the places where those people worship their gods. Those places are on the tops of mountains and hills
Deuteronomy12:3 You must break into pieces their altars and stone pillars. Burn their Asherah poles. Knock down the images of their gods. Destroy those places so that nobody remembers the false gods.
Deuteronomy12:4 Do not worship the Lord your God in the same way that those people worship their gods.
Deuteronomy12:5 The Lord will choose one place from the land where all your tribes live. That is the place where people must go to worship him. That is his special home.
Deuteronomy12:6 That is where you must take your burnt offerings and other sacrifices to offer to the Lord. Take your tithes and offerings to him there. Take the gifts that you have promised to give
Deuteronomy12:7 You and your families will go there to meet with the Lord your God. You will be happy as you eat together there. You will enjoy the results of all your work
Deuteronomy12:8 At this time
Deuteronomy12:9 You have not yet reached the place where you can rest. That is in the land that the Lord your God is giving to you as your home.
Deuteronomy12:10 But when you go across the Jordan River
Deuteronomy12:11 At that time
Deuteronomy12:12 Go there to enjoy time with the Lord your God. Take with you your children and your servants. Also take with you the Levites who live in your towns. Remember that they have not received any land for themselves
Deuteronomy12:13 So be careful! Do not offer your burnt offerings in any place that you choose for yourself.
Deuteronomy12:14 Only make offerings to the Lord in the place that he chooses. That will be in the land that belongs to one of your tribes. It is the place where you must do all the things that I am commanding you to do.
Deuteronomy12:15 But you may kill your animals and eat the meat in any of the towns where you live. When the Lord your God blesses you and he gives you animals
Deuteronomy12:16 But you must not eat the blood of any animal. You must pour it on the ground like water.
Deuteronomy12:17 Do not eat any of your offerings to the Lord in the places where you live. Do not eat the tithes that you have taken from your grain
Deuteronomy12:18 You may only eat these offerings in the place that the Lord will choose as his special home. That is true for you
Deuteronomy12:19 As long as you live in the land
Deuteronomy12:20 The Lord your God has promised to give you more and more land. When he does that
Deuteronomy12:21 The place that God chooses as his special home may be far away from you. If it is
Deuteronomy12:22 You should eat them in the same way that you eat deer. People who are unclean may eat them
Deuteronomy12:23 But never eat the animal's blood. The blood of an animal gives it life. You must not eat the life together with the meat!
Deuteronomy12:24 You must not eat the blood. You must pour it on the ground like water.
Deuteronomy12:25 Do not eat it! Then you will be doing what the Lord says is right. You will enjoy a happy life and so will your children.
Deuteronomy12:26 It is only the holy things that you must take to the place that the Lord will choose for himself. Those are the gifts and sacrifices that you are offering to him.
Deuteronomy12:27 Offer the meat and the blood of your burnt offerings on the altar of the Lord your God. For the other sacrifices
Deuteronomy12:28 Be careful to obey all these commands that I am giving to you. Then you will be doing things that the Lord your God says are good and right. You and your children will enjoy a happy life.
Deuteronomy12:29 When you go into the land where the other nations live
Deuteronomy12:30 But after the Lord has destroyed them
Deuteronomy12:31 No! You must not worship the Lord your God in the same way that those people worship their gods. When they worship
Deuteronomy12:32 Be careful to do everything that I am telling you. Do not add anything to these commands. Do not remove anything from them.
Deuteronomy13:1 One day
Deuteronomy13:2 He may say that you should start to worship other gods that you did not know about before. The miracles may happen
Deuteronomy13:3 but do not listen to that prophet's message. The Lord your God is using this person to test your thoughts. He wants to know if you really love him in everything that you think and everything that you do.
Deuteronomy13:4 You must serve only the Lord your God and respect him with fear. You must obey his commands and serve him faithfully.
Deuteronomy13:5 You must punish that false prophet with death. He has told you to turn against the Lord your God. The Lord rescued you from Egypt
Deuteronomy13:6 Even someone in your own family may try to turn you away from the Lord. It may be your own brother
Deuteronomy13:7 They are gods that people who live around you may worship. They may be gods of people who live near to you. They may be gods of people who live anywhere on the earth.
Deuteronomy13:8 But never agree with anyone who says that you should worship those gods. Do not even listen to him! Do not feel sorry for him. Do not try to protect him from punishment.
Deuteronomy13:9 Instead
Deuteronomy13:10 You must throw stones at him until he dies. He tried to turn you away from the Lord your God. It was the Lord who rescued you from Egypt
Deuteronomy13:11 When you punish the false prophet with death
Deuteronomy13:12 The Lord your God will give you towns to live in. When you are there
Deuteronomy13:13 They may be turning the other people in their town away from the Lord. They may be saying
Deuteronomy13:14 Then you must check carefully that the news is true. You must discover whether people really have done this wicked thing in one of your towns.
Deuteronomy13:15 If the news is true
Deuteronomy13:16 You must put all the valuable things in a heap in the town's public place. Then burn down the whole town as an offering to the Lord your God. Leave the town as a heap of stones. Nobody must ever build it again.
Deuteronomy13:17 You must not keep any of the valuable things for yourselves. They have been given to the Lord. If you destroy the whole town
Deuteronomy13:18 The Lord your God will bless you if you listen to what he says. You must obey all his commands that I am giving you today. You must do the things that he says are right.
Deuteronomy14:1 You are people who belong to the Lord your God. When someone dies
Deuteronomy14:2 You are the Lord 's own special people. He has chosen you to belong to him. You are much more valuable to him than any other people in the whole earth.
Deuteronomy14:3 Do not eat anything that God says is unclean.
Deuteronomy14:4 You may eat the meat of these animals: cows
Deuteronomy14:5 deer
Deuteronomy14:6 You may eat any animal with feet that have two separate parts. They must also bring their food back into their mouths and eat it again.
Deuteronomy14:7 But you must not eat these animals: camels
Deuteronomy14:8 You may not eat pigs
Deuteronomy14:9 You may eat any fish that has fins and scales.
Deuteronomy14:10 But other things that live in the water do not have fins or scales. Those are not clean food for you. Do not eat them.
Deuteronomy14:11 You may eat any clean bird.
Deuteronomy14:12 But these birds you must not eat: eagles

Deuteronomy14:14 
Deuteronomy14:15 
Deuteronomy14:16 
Deuteronomy14:17 
Deuteronomy14:18 
Deuteronomy14:19 Some insects have wings but they walk on the ground. Insects like that are unclean food for you. You must not eat them.
Deuteronomy14:20 But you may eat any clean insect that has wings.
Deuteronomy14:21 You must not eat anything that you find dead already. You may give it to a foreign person who lives in any of your towns. He may eat it. You may give it to him or sell it to him. But you yourselves are special people who belong to the Lord your God. You must not cook a young goat in the milk from its mother.
Deuteronomy14:22 You must keep safe a tenth part of all the crops that grow in your fields. You must be careful to do this every year.
Deuteronomy14:23 Then take your tithe to the place that the Lord your God will choose as his special home. It will be a tenth part of your grain
Deuteronomy14:24 But the Lord 's special place may be a long way from your home. If he has blessed you with a lot of things
Deuteronomy14:25 If that is true

Deuteronomy14:27 Do not forget to help the Levites who live in your towns. Remember that they have not received any land as you have.

Deuteronomy15:1 At the end of every seven years you must remove the debts of people that you have lent money to.
Deuteronomy15:2 This is how you must do it: Every person who has lent money to someone must remove that debt. He must not demand to get his money back from any other Israelite. It is the Lord 's time to remove debts.
Deuteronomy15:3 You can demand to get your money back from a foreign person. But if you have lent money to another Israelite
Deuteronomy15:4 The Lord will bless you in the land that he is giving you to live in. So there should be no poor people among you.
Deuteronomy15:5 But you must always obey him completely. So be careful to obey all the commands that I am giving to you today.
Deuteronomy15:6 For the Lord your God will bless you
Deuteronomy15:7 The Lord your God will bless you in the land that he is giving to you. But perhaps there will be a poor person among the Israelites in one of your towns. Then do not refuse to help him. Do not think only about yourself.
Deuteronomy15:8 Instead
Deuteronomy15:9 Be careful not to think this wicked thought: ‘The seventh year will happen soon. That is the year when I must forgive people's debts. So I will not lend money to people at this time.’ Do not be cruel like that to another Israelite who is poor. Do not refuse to lend him anything. He might complain about you to the Lord. Then the Lord would say that you are guilty of a sin.
Deuteronomy15:10 Instead
Deuteronomy15:11 There will always be some poor people among you in the land where you are going. So I command you to be generous to other Israelites who are poor. Help them with the things that they need.
Deuteronomy15:12 You may buy another Israelite to be your servant
Deuteronomy15:13 When you let him go
Deuteronomy15:14 Give him gifts of sheep
Deuteronomy15:15 Remember that you were once slaves in Egypt. But the Lord your God rescued you from there. This is why I am giving you this command today.
Deuteronomy15:16 But perhaps your servant will say to you
Deuteronomy15:17 If he says that
Deuteronomy15:18 Do not think that it will be difficult for you if he goes free. Remember that he has served you well for six years. He has been worth twice as much as a servant that you pay to do your work. The Lord your God will bless you in everything that you do.
Deuteronomy15:19 You must keep separate every firstborn male animal that is born from your cows
Deuteronomy15:20 Each year
Deuteronomy15:21 But if a firstborn animal is not perfect
Deuteronomy15:22 You may eat animals like that at home. It is the same as if you are eating meat from deer. Anyone may eat it. That includes people who are clean and people who are unclean.
Deuteronomy15:23 But you must not eat the animal's blood. You must pour it on the ground like water.
Deuteronomy16:1 The month Abib is a special month for you. In this month
Deuteronomy16:2 You must go to the special place that the Lord will choose for his people to worship him. There you must kill one of your animals as a sacrifice to remember the Passover. You may kill one of your cows or sheep.
Deuteronomy16:3 At this feast
Deuteronomy16:4 During those seven days
Deuteronomy16:5 You must not kill the animal for the Passover sacrifice in any of the towns that the Lord your God gives to you.
Deuteronomy16:6 You must go to the place that he will choose as his special home. You must kill the animal there
Deuteronomy16:7 Cook the meat and eat it in the place that the Lord your God will choose. The next morning
Deuteronomy16:8 For the next six days
Deuteronomy16:9 Count seven weeks from the first day that you bring in crops of grain from your fields.
Deuteronomy16:10 Then eat the Feast of Weeks to worship the Lord your God. Bring the gifts that you want to offer to him. Bring gifts that show how much he has blessed you.
Deuteronomy16:11 You and your family will be happy to worship the Lord your God
Deuteronomy16:12 Remember that you were once slaves in Egypt. So be careful to obey these commands.
Deuteronomy16:13 When you have prepared the grain and the grapes from your fields
Deuteronomy16:14 Enjoy the festival with your children
Deuteronomy16:15 Eat this feast to worship the Lord your God at the special place that he chooses. Do it for seven days. He will bless you in everything that you do
Deuteronomy16:16 Three times in every year
Deuteronomy16:17 Give to the Lord as much as you are able to. Your gifts may be large or small
Deuteronomy16:18 Each tribe must choose judges and officers in every town that the Lord gives you. They must judge the people in a way that is fair.
Deuteronomy16:19 Judges must do what is right for every person
Deuteronomy16:20 Make sure that there is justice for everyone in the land that the Lord your God is giving to you. Then the land will belong to you and you will enjoy life in it.
Deuteronomy16:21 When you build an altar to offer sacrifices to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy16:22 Do not put up any stone pillar as an idol. The Lord your God hates everything like that.
Deuteronomy17:1 When you offer an animal as a sacrifice to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy17:2 The Lord your God will give you towns to live in. You may hear that a man or a woman who lives in one of your towns has done a bad thing. He has done a sin that is against the covenant of the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy17:3 He may have started to worship false gods that I have commanded you not to worship. Perhaps he worships the sun
Deuteronomy17:4 If you hear news like that
Deuteronomy17:5 you must punish that person. Take the man or woman who has done that wicked thing to the gate of the town. Then you must throw stones at that person to kill them.
Deuteronomy17:6 But two or three people must agree about what the person has done. You must never kill anyone because of the words of only one person.
Deuteronomy17:7 The people who saw the wicked act must throw the first stones to kill the person. Then everyone else must also throw stones. In that way
Deuteronomy17:8 It may be difficult for the judges in your towns to decide what is true. This may happen when one person has attacked or killed another person. It may happen when people cannot agree about some land. It may be anything that people in your towns are arguing about. If you do not know who is right
Deuteronomy17:9 You must go to the Levite priests and to the judge who has authority at that time. Ask them to decide what is right and true.
Deuteronomy17:10 When they decide
Deuteronomy17:11 Accept their teaching. Agree with what they have decided is right. Obey their words completely. Do not try to do anything that is different.
Deuteronomy17:12 Someone might be too proud to obey the priest who has authority as the Lord 's servant. He may not agree with what the judge says is right. You must punish any proud person like that with death. In that way you will remove evil things from Israel.
Deuteronomy17:13 Then all the people will hear what has happened. They will be afraid. They will no longer be too proud to obey the judge.
Deuteronomy17:14 You are going into the land that the Lord your God is giving to you. You will take it for yourselves to be your home. Then you may say
Deuteronomy17:15 If that happens
Deuteronomy17:16 A king must not get a lot of horses for himself. He must not send his people back to Egypt to buy more horses. The Lord has said to you
Deuteronomy17:17 A king must not marry many wives. They may turn his thoughts away from the Lord. He must not store a lot of silver and gold for himself.
Deuteronomy17:18 When anyone starts to rule as king
Deuteronomy17:19 He must keep this scroll with him all the time. He must continue to read it every day of his life. If he does that
Deuteronomy17:20 If he does that
