Deuteronomy18:2 They will receive no land among the other Israelites. The Lord himself is the one who gives them everything that they need. He has promised to do that for them.
Deuteronomy18:3 When you bring a bull or a sheep as a sacrifice to the Lord
Deuteronomy18:4 You must also give them the first part of your grain
Deuteronomy18:5 Do this because the Lord your God has chosen the Levites to serve him from among all your tribes. He wants them and their descendants to be ready to serve him at all times
Deuteronomy18:6 Any Levite may choose to go to the place that the Lord will choose. He can leave the town where he lives
Deuteronomy18:7 He may go to serve the Lord his God. He may join the other Levites who are ready to serve the Lord at his special place.
Deuteronomy18:8 A Levite may have sold some of his family's valuable things. He may keep that money and he may receive the same amount of food as the other Levites.
Deuteronomy18:9 When you go into the land that the Lord your God is giving you
Deuteronomy18:10 Nobody among you must ever burn your child in a fire
Deuteronomy18:11 Never use magic to curse anyone. Never try to speak to the spirits of dead people or other spirits.
Deuteronomy18:12 The Lord hates anyone who does these things. It is because of these disgusting things that the Lord will chase the other nations out of the land as you move in.
Deuteronomy18:13 You must live in a completely right way that pleases the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy18:14 The people of the nations that you will chase away get help from magicians and diviners. But the Lord your God has commanded you not to do those things.

Deuteronomy18:19 He will speak on my behalf
Deuteronomy18:20 A false prophet may say that he speaks with my authority when that is not true. Or he may say that he has a message from another god. You must punish a false prophet with death.’

Deuteronomy21:2 Your leaders and your judges must then go to the field. They must measure how far the place is from the nearest towns.

Deuteronomy21:4 They must lead the cow down to a valley where no one has ploughed the ground or planted seed. There must be a stream of water in the valley. There they must break the cow's neck.
Deuteronomy21:5 The Levite priests must go there too. The Lord your God has chosen them to serve him and to ask him to bless his people. They also decide as judges what is right.

Deuteronomy21:11 You may see among them a beautiful woman that you want to take as your wife.
Deuteronomy21:12 Then you must bring her into your home. She must cut the hair off her head. She must cut her fingernails.
Deuteronomy21:13 She must change the clothes that she was wearing when you found her. Let her live in your house and weep for her parents for a whole month. Then you may have sex with her. You will be her husband and she will be your wife.
Deuteronomy21:14 Later
Deuteronomy21:15 A man may have two wives
Deuteronomy21:16 But when the man shares his things with his children
Deuteronomy21:17 He must recognize that his firstborn son is the son of the wife that he does not love. This son was the first son born to that man. So he must receive the double share of his father's things. That is what God's Law says must happen.
Deuteronomy21:18 Perhaps a man has a son who refuses to obey his parents. They punish him to teach him what is right

Deuteronomy28:22 The Lord will give you many kinds of disease. Your bodies will become weak and very hot. They will grow big and have a lot of pain. He will send great heat and there will be no rain. Your crops will all become spoiled. You will have all this pain until you die.
Deuteronomy28:23 There will be no clouds in the sky above to give any rain. The ground under your feet will be as hard as iron.
Deuteronomy28:24 There will be clouds of dust instead of rain. Dirt will come down from the sky until it has destroyed you.
Deuteronomy28:25 The Lord will let your enemies knock you down. You will attack them from one direction
Deuteronomy28:26 Your dead bodies will be food for all the birds and the wild animals. No one will be there to chase them away.
Deuteronomy28:27 The Lord will cause you to have boils on your bodies. That is what he did to the Egyptians. He will also cause you to have tumours and other bad diseases on your skin. You will not be able to get well again.
Deuteronomy28:28 The Lord will make you crazy and blind. He will confuse your thoughts.
Deuteronomy28:29 Even at midday

Deuteronomy28:46 They will show that God is very angry with you. You and your descendants will know that for ever.

Deuteronomy28:57 But now she will be so hungry that she will eat her new baby and the afterbirth. She will eat them secretly because there is nothing else for her to eat. She will not share anything with her family because your enemy will be too strong. The soldiers will stop any food from coming into your towns.

Deuteronomy32:1 Sky and earth
Deuteronomy32:2 My teaching will be like the rain that falls. My messages will help the people who hear them
Deuteronomy32:3 I will praise the Lord 's name. Everyone must know that our God is very great!
Deuteronomy32:4 He is the strong Rock where we can be safe. Everything that he does is perfect. He only does what is right and fair. We can trust him to be faithful.
Deuteronomy32:5 But you
Deuteronomy32:6 You are fools who do not understand what is right. You should not do these things against the Lord. He is your Father
Deuteronomy32:7 Remember what happened a long time ago. Think about the lives of your ancestors. Ask your father or the old men. They will tell you what happened in the past.
Deuteronomy32:8 The Most High God gave land to be the home of each nation. When he made people into separate groups
Deuteronomy32:9 But the Lord himself takes care of his own people. Jacob's descendants are his special people.
Deuteronomy32:10 The Lord found them in a desert place. It was a place where strong winds blew and nobody lived. He took care of them and he kept them safe. He protected them
Deuteronomy32:11 The Lord took care of his people
Deuteronomy32:12 The Lord alone was a guide for his people. He needed no foreign god to help him.
Deuteronomy32:13 The Lord helped them to travel over the high places in the land. He fed them with food from the fields. He made them strong with honey from the rocks. He gave them olive oil from trees among the rocks.
Deuteronomy32:14 Their cows and their goats gave them plenty of milk and butter. Their lambs
Deuteronomy32:15 The Israelites became rich and fat. They turned against God. They were full of good food
Deuteronomy32:16 They worshipped foreign gods. So the Lord became angry and jealous. They made idols which he hated.
Deuteronomy32:17 They offered sacrifices to demons
Deuteronomy32:18 You forgot about your strong Rock
Deuteronomy32:19 When the Lord saw this
Deuteronomy32:20 He said
Deuteronomy32:21 They have made me jealous as they serve false gods. They have made me angry as they worship useless idols. So I will make them jealous as I help a useless nation. I will make them angry as I bless a foolish nation.
Deuteronomy32:22 I will become so angry that I punish them very much. I will destroy them like a hot fire. It will be like a fire that reaches deep down into the place of dead people. It will destroy the ground and everything that grows on it. It will burn deep down to the foundations of the mountains.
Deuteronomy32:23 I will cause them to have many kinds of trouble. I will attack them like a soldier who shoots all his arrows.
Deuteronomy32:24 They will become weak and hungry. They will have bad diseases that give them much pain. I will cause wild animals to attack them. Snakes will bite them and they will die.
Deuteronomy32:25 If they go out of their homes
Deuteronomy32:26 I wanted to remove my people completely
Deuteronomy32:27 But then their enemies would boast. They would not understand. They would say
Deuteronomy32:28 Israel is a nation without wisdom. They do not understand what will happen to them.
Deuteronomy32:29 If they were wise

Deuteronomy32:31 The gods of our enemies are not strong like our Rock. Even our enemies know that their own gods are weak.
Deuteronomy32:32 Our enemies are like grapes full of poison. They are like grapes that have grown in the fields of Sodom and Gomorrah.
Deuteronomy32:33 The wine from their grapes kills people
Deuteronomy32:34 The Lord says
Deuteronomy32:35 When the right time comes
Deuteronomy32:36 At that time
Deuteronomy32:37 Then the Lord will ask his people
Deuteronomy32:38 You fed them with the best part of your sacrifices. You let them drink the wine from your offerings. So they should be there to help you! They should keep you safe!
Deuteronomy32:39 Understand the truth! I alone am God. There is no other god as well as me. I can kill and I can give life. I can hurt people and I can make them well again. Nobody can stop me doing what I choose to do.
Deuteronomy32:40 I lift up my hand to heaven and I make this strong promise: “I promise you this
Deuteronomy32:41 I will make my sword very sharp. It will move like lightning as I hold it in my hand. I will punish my enemies as they deserve. I will pay back everyone who hates me.
Deuteronomy32:42 My arrows will have blood all over them. My sword will cut into the bodies of my enemies. Everyone that I take hold of will die. I will cut off the heads of their leaders.” ’

Deuteronomy33:14 May the sun give them a good harvest of food
Deuteronomy33:15 May they have a harvest of good fruits from the mountains and hills that have been there since long ago.
Deuteronomy33:16 Please fill their land with many good things. Lord who appeared in the burning bush

Deuteronomy33:19 They will call other people to come to their mountain. They will offer the right sacrifices there. They will become rich from their work on the seas

Deuteronomy33:21 He has chosen the best part of the land for himself. When Israel's leaders met together
Deuteronomy33:22 I pray this for the tribe of Dan: Dan's tribe is like a young lion. They jump out from Bashan to attack their enemies.
Deuteronomy33:23 I pray this for the tribe of Naphtali: The Lord has blessed Naphtali's tribe very much. He has been very kind to them. Land in the west and in the south will belong to them.
Deuteronomy33:24 I pray this for the tribe of Asher: May the Lord bless Asher's tribe more than the other tribes. May their brothers be pleased with them. May Asher's people be rich enough to wash their feet in olive oil.
Deuteronomy33:25 They will use iron and bronze to make the gates of their towns strong. May they be strong for as long as they live.

Joshua5:5 All those men who came out of Egypt had already been circumcised. But all the sons who were born on the journey through the desert had not been circumcised.

Joshua10:10 The Lord caused the Amorites to become confused when they saw the Israelite army. So the Israelites won a great battle there at Gibeon. They chased their enemies along the road to Beth Horon. They continued to kill the Amorite soldiers as far as Azekah and Makkedah.
Joshua10:11 As the Amorites ran down the hill from Beth Horon
Joshua10:12 So the Lord caused the Israelites to win the fight against the Amorites. After the battle that day
Joshua10:13 So the sun stood still and the moon stopped moving across the sky. They stayed still while the Israelites punished their enemies. People wrote about this in the Scroll of Jashar. The sun stopped in the middle of the sky. It did not go down for about a whole day.
Joshua10:14 A day like that had never happened before
Joshua10:15 Then Joshua and all the Israelite army went back to their tents at Gilgal.
Joshua10:16 The five kings had run away from the Israelite soldiers. They had hidden in a cave at Makkedah.
Joshua10:17 Joshua heard that they were hiding there.
Joshua10:18 He said to his men
Joshua10:19 But you must continue to chase after your enemies. Do not stop now! Catch them! Do not let them reach their cities where they will be safe. Remember that the Lord your God has caused you to win against them.’
Joshua10:20 So Joshua and the Israelites destroyed nearly all the Amorite armies. But a few of the Amorites escaped and they reached their strong cities.
Joshua10:21 Then the whole Israelite army returned to Joshua at Makkedah. All the people in the land were too afraid to speak against the Israelites.
Joshua10:22 Then Joshua said to his men
Joshua10:23 So they brought the five kings to Joshua. They were the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:24 When they had brought the kings to Joshua
Joshua10:25 Joshua said to them
Joshua10:26 Then Joshua killed the five kings with his sword. He hung their bodies on five trees. They remained there until evening time.
Joshua10:27 At sunset
Joshua10:28 That day
Joshua10:29 After that
Joshua10:30 The Lord gave them power over that city and its king. Joshua destroyed the city and everyone who lived in it. He did not leave anyone alive. They did the same thing to Libnah's king as they had done to the king of Jericho.
Joshua10:31 After this
Joshua10:32 The Lord gave them power over the city. They fought for two days and then they took the city. They killed everyone who lived in it
Joshua10:33 Then Horam
Joshua10:34 Then Joshua and his army marched from Lachish to Eglon. They attacked Eglon city from all sides.

Joshua11:4 All these kings came with their big armies. They had many horses and chariots. The soldiers were too many to count.
Joshua11:5 These kings all joined together to fight against the Israelites. They put up their tents near the water at Merom.

Joshua11:11 The Israelites killed everyone who lived there. Nothing in the city was left alive. They burned the city and destroyed it.

Joshua11:13 But the Israelites did not burn the cities that were built on small hills. Hazor was the only city like that which Joshua did burn.
Joshua11:14 The Israelites took for themselves all the animals and the valuable things from those cities. But they killed all the people. They did not let any of them live.

Joshua20:4 The person who killed someone must go to the special city. When he arrives
Joshua20:5 The dead person's relative may chase after the killer. The leaders of the city must not let him hurt the killer. This is because the killer did not mean to kill.

Ruth1:1 There was a time when judges ruled Israel. During that time there was a famine in Judah. A man who lived in Bethlehem
Ruth1:2

Ruth1:5 After they had all lived in Moab for about ten years

1Samuel1:1 There was a man whose name was Elkanah. He lived in a town called Ramah. This town was in the hill country where Ephraim's tribe lived. Elkanah was the son of Jeroham. Jeroham was the son of Elihu. Elihu was the son of Tohu. Tohu was the son of Zuph. Zuph belonged to the tribe of Ephraim.
1Samuel1:2 Elkanah had two wives. One wife was called Hannah and the other wife was called Peninnah. Peninnah had children but Hannah did not have any children.
1Samuel1:3 Every year
1Samuel1:4 Every year
1Samuel1:5 Because Elkanah loved Hannah very much
1Samuel1:6 Peninnah was not kind to Hannah and she made Hannah very upset. She did this because the Lord had not let Hannah give birth to any children.
1Samuel1:7 This happened every year when they went to the Lord 's house in Shiloh. Peninnah would cause Hannah to be upset. Then Hannah would weep. She would refuse to eat anything.
1Samuel1:8 Hannah's husband

1Samuel1:10 Hannah was very upset as she prayed to the Lord. She could not stop weeping.
1Samuel1:11 She made a promise to God. She prayed

1Samuel2:1 Hannah prayed and she said
1Samuel2:2 Nobody else is holy like the Lord. There is no other God except you
1Samuel2:3 Do not boast or speak proud words! The Lord is a God who knows everything. He judges everything that people do.
1Samuel2:4 The weapons of brave soldiers may break. But weak people who fall become strong again.
1Samuel2:5 People who once had plenty of food to eat have to work hard for it now. But the people who were hungry now have enough food. Women who could not have any children have now given birth to seven children. But those who had many children are now alone.
1Samuel2:6 The Lord gives both life and death. He sends some people to their graves
1Samuel2:7 The Lord makes some people poor and he makes other people rich. He causes some people to be important and he puts other people low down.
1Samuel2:8 He raises up weak people from the ground. He lifts poor people up out of the dirt. He makes them become friends of princes. He puts them in places of honour. The deep places of the earth belong to the Lord. He built the world on a strong foundation.
1Samuel2:9 He takes care of his faithful people. But he puts wicked people in a dark place where they can do nothing. It is not a person's own strength that makes him great.
1Samuel2:10 The Lord will destroy his enemies. He will shout against them from heaven like loud thunder. The Lord will judge everyone on earth. He will make his king strong. He will give power to the king that he has chosen to rule.’

2Samuel22:5 Death was near enough to catch me! Danger was like a river that wanted to drown me.

1Kings4:1 King Solomon ruled over all Israel as king.
1Kings4:2 These were his important officers: Zadok's son Azariah was the priest.
1Kings4:3 Shisha's sons
1Kings4:4 Jehoiada's son Benaiah was the leader of the army. Zadok and Abiathar were priests.
1Kings4:5 Nathan's son Azariah had authority over the 12 officers for each region. Nathan's son Zabud was a priest who gave the king advice.
1Kings4:6 Ahishar had authority over work in the palace. Abda's son Adoniram had authority over the groups of workers.
1Kings4:7 Solomon had 12 officers with authority in the regions of Israel. They supplied food for the king and for the people who lived in his palace. Each officer had to supply food to the king for one month in each year.
1Kings4:8 These are their names: Ben-Hur had authority in Ephraim's hill country.
1Kings4:9 Ben-Deker had authority in Makaz
1Kings4:10 Ben-Hesed had authority in Arubboth. That included Sokoh and all the fields around Hepher.
1Kings4:11 Ben-Abinadab had authority in Naphoth Dor. He had married Solomon's daughter
1Kings4:12 Ahilud's son Baana had authority in Taanach and Megiddo
1Kings4:13 Ben-Geber had authority in Ramoth Gilead. That included the villages of Manasseh's son Jair in Gilead. It also included the region of Argob in Bashan with 60 large cities which had strong walls and gates with bronze bars.
1Kings4:14 Iddo's son had authority in Mahanaim.
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz had authority in Naphtali. He had married Solomon's daughter
1Kings4:16 Hushai's son Baana had authority in Asher and Aloth.
1Kings4:17 Paruah's son Jehoshaphat had authority in Issachar.
1Kings4:18 Ela's son Shimei had authority in Benjamin.
1Kings4:19 Uri's son Geber had authority in Gilead. Gilead was the region where Sihon
1Kings4:20 The people in Judah and Israel were too many to count. There were as many of them as the sand on the shore of the sea. They had enough to eat and to drink and they were happy.
1Kings4:21 Solomon ruled over all the nations from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines
1Kings4:22 This is the food that Solomon and the people in his palace ate each day: 5
1Kings4:23 10 fat calves that they fed with grain. 20 calves that ate grass in the fields. 100 sheep. Many kinds of deer and fat birds.

1Kings4:25 While Solomon was alive
1Kings4:26 Solomon had places to keep 4
1Kings4:27 The 12 officers in each region supplied food for King Solomon and for everybody that ate in his palace. Each region supplied food for one month each year so that there was enough for everyone.
1Kings4:28 Each officer also supplied barley and straw for the king's horses. They took the right amount to the different places where the horses lived.

1Kings4:31 He was wiser than any other man. This includes Ethan the Ezrahite
1Kings4:32 He wrote down 3
1Kings4:33 He could describe many plants. These included large trees like cedar trees that grow in Lebanon and small plants like hyssop that grows on walls. He also taught people about all kinds of animals

1Kings5:1 Hiram was the king of Tyre. He heard that Solomon had become king of Israel after his father David. Hiram had always been a friend of King David
1Kings5:2 Solomon then sent this message to Hiram:
1Kings5:3 ‘You know that my father David could not build a temple for the Lord his God. This was because he had to fight battles against enemies that were all around Israel. He did that until the Lord helped him to win against all of them.
1Kings5:4 But now the Lord my God has made us safe from danger. No enemy tries to attack us on any of our borders.
1Kings5:5 So I have decided to build a temple to give honour to the Lord my God. The Lord said to my father David
1Kings5:6 So please tell your men to cut down cedar trees in Lebanon for me. We ourselves do not have people who know how to cut wood from trees as well as your men from Sidon. So our men will help your men with the work. And I will pay your workers whatever you decide is right.’
1Kings5:7 Hiram was very happy when he received Solomon's message. He said
1Kings5:8 Then Hiram sent this message to Solomon: ‘I have received the message that you sent to me. I will give you the wood that you have asked for. I will send you cedar wood and pine wood
1Kings5:9 My men will take the trees from the hills of Lebanon down to the sea. Then we will tie several trees together like a boat. We will take them on the sea to the place where you want them. There we will undo the ropes so that your men can carry the trees away. You will pay me with enough food to feed the people of my palace.’
1Kings5:10 So Hiram supplied Solomon with all the cedar trees and pine trees that Solomon asked for.
1Kings5:11 Then Solomon supplied Hiram with 2
1Kings5:12 The Lord gave wisdom to Solomon

1Kings5:14 He sent them to Lebanon in groups of 10
1Kings5:15 Solomon also had 80
1Kings5:16 He also had 3
1Kings5:17 The king commanded the workers to cut large pieces of stone from the rocks. They cut the best stones into the right shape to build the foundation of the temple.
1Kings5:18 Solomon's men and Hiram's men worked together with men from Gebal. They cut the stones and the wood. They prepared them to build the temple.
1Kings6:1 Solomon began to build the Lord 's temple 480 years after the Israelites had left Egypt. It was in the fourth year that he had ruled Israel as king. He started the work in the month called Ziv
1Kings6:2 The temple that King Solomon built for the Lord was 27 metres long. It was 9 metres wide. It was 13.5 metres high.
1Kings6:3 The temple had an entrance room in front of its big hall. The entrance room was as wide as the temple
1Kings6:4 Solomon made narrow windows near the tops of the temple walls.
1Kings6:5 Solomon built rooms around the outside of the temple walls. These rooms continued outside the big hall and the inside room. The side rooms were on three levels.
1Kings6:6 The lowest level of rooms was 2.3 metres wide. The middle level was 2.7 metres wide and the top level was 3.2 metres wide. Each floor was built on wooden beams. The beams rested on stones in the temple walls
1Kings6:7 When they built the temple
1Kings6:8 The door to the lowest level of rooms on the sides of the temple was on the south side of the temple. Stairs went up to the middle level and to the top level.
1Kings6:9 Solomon finished building the temple. He made the roof with wooden beams and with cedar boards.
1Kings6:10 He also built the rooms around the sides of the temple. Each room was 2.3 metres high. Cedar beams fixed the rooms to the temple building.
1Kings6:11 Then the Lord gave this message to Solomon:
1Kings6:12 ‘I will make my home in this temple that you are building. I will do everything for you that I promised to your father David. But you must obey my laws
1Kings6:13 Then I will live among my people
1Kings6:14 So Solomon finished building the temple.
1Kings6:15 He used cedar boards to cover the walls on the inside of the temple. The boards went from the floor of the temple up to its ceiling. He covered the floor of the temple with wood from pine trees.
1Kings6:16 He built a wall across the inside of the temple to make a separate room. That wall was 9 metres from the back wall of the temple. The inside room was the Most Holy Place. The walls were covered with cedar boards from the floor to the ceiling.
1Kings6:17 The big hall in front of the Most Holy Place was 18 metres long.
1Kings6:18 The inside of the temple was covered with boards made of cedar wood. The workers cut pictures of fruits and flowers on the wood. The cedar boards completely covered the stone walls
1Kings6:19 Solomon prepared the inside room to be the Most Holy Place where they would put the Lord 's Covenant Box.
1Kings6:20 The inside room was 9 metres long
1Kings6:21 Solomon covered all the walls inside the temple with gold. He also hung gold chains across the entrance to the Most Holy Place. He covered everything there with gold.
1Kings6:22 So he covered everything inside the whole temple with gold. That included the altar that was inside the Most Holy Place.
1Kings6:23 Solomon used olive wood to make models of two cherubs to stand in the Most Holy Place. Each cherub was 4.4 metres tall.
1Kings6:24 The first cherub had two wings that were 2.2 metres long each. So it was 4.4 metres from the end of one wing to the end of the other wing.
1Kings6:25 The second cherub was also 4.4 metres across its wings. The two cherubs were the same size and the same shape.
1Kings6:26 Each cherub was 4.4 metres high.
1Kings6:27 Solomon put the cherubs in the inside room of the temple. Their wings went from one wall of the temple to the other wall. One cherub's wing touched one wall of the temple. One of the other cherub's wings touched the other wall. And their wings touched each other in the middle of the room.
1Kings6:28 Solomon covered the cherubs with gold.
1Kings6:29 Solomon's workers cut pictures in the wood on the walls of the big hall and the inside room. They were pictures of cherubs
1Kings6:30 They also covered the floors of both the rooms with gold.
1Kings6:31 They used olive wood to make doors for the entrance to the Most Holy Place. The doors were fixed to wooden pillars which had five sides.
1Kings6:32 The wooden doors had pictures of cherubs
1Kings6:33 The doors to the big hall of the temple were fixed to pillars made of olive wood. Those pillars had four sides.
1Kings6:34 Solomon also made two doors from pine wood. Each door had two separate parts that could turn.
1Kings6:35 He cut pictures of cherubs
1Kings6:36 Solomon also built a yard around the temple building. The wall around the yard had three rows of special stones
1Kings6:37 They built the foundation of the Lord 's temple in the fourth year that Solomon was king. It was in the month called Ziv.
1Kings6:38 They finished building the temple in the 11th year that Solomon was king. It was the eighth month
1Kings7:1 Solomon was also building a palace for himself. After 13 years
1Kings7:2 He built the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon. It was 46 metres long
1Kings7:3 They also used cedar wood to make the roof. They put the roof on top of the pillars and the beams. There were 45 beams
1Kings7:4 There were three rows of windows on each side of the room. They were opposite each other
1Kings7:5 All the doors had square corners and four sides. There were three doors in each group.
1Kings7:6 Solomon also built a Hall of Pillars. It was 23 metres long and 14 metres wide. There was an entrance room at the front of the hall. The entrance room also had pillars and a roof.
1Kings7:7 Solomon also built a Throne Room. He covered the walls with cedar boards
1Kings7:8 Solomon also built a house for himself to live in. It was in a yard behind the Hall of Justice. It was like the other buildings. He also built a house like it for his wife who was the king of Egypt's daughter.
1Kings7:9 Solomon's workers used valuable
1Kings7:10 They made the foundations with very large
1Kings7:11 On top of the foundation they used the best stones that they cut to the right size. They also used beams of cedar wood.
1Kings7:12 There was a wall around the great yard. This had three rows of special stones
1Kings7:13 King Solomon sent men to Tyre to bring a man called Hiram.
1Kings7:14 Hiram was the son of a widow from the tribe of Naphtali. His father was a worker who knew how to use bronze to make things. He lived in Tyre. Hiram also had special skills. He knew how to use bronze to make many kinds of things. So he came to work for King Solomon. He did all the work that Solomon asked him to do.
1Kings7:15 Hiram made two bronze pillars. Each pillar was 8.2 metres high and 5.5 metres around the outside. The metal itself was about 7 centimetres thick.
1Kings7:16 He also used bronze to make a top for each pillar. Each piece was 2.3 metres high.
1Kings7:17 Each piece had pictures like rows of chains that joined together. There were seven pictures like this on the top of each pillar.
1Kings7:18 Hiram also made pictures of two rows of pomegranates around the chains. They covered the tops of the pillars.
1Kings7:19 The tops of the two pillars were in the shape of flowers called lilies. Each one was 1.8 metres high.
1Kings7:20 There were pictures of 200 pomegranates in two rows all around the top of each pillar. They were next to the chains above the round shape at the top of the pillar.
1Kings7:21 Hiram put these two pillars at the entrance room of the temple
1Kings7:22 The tops of the pillars were in the shape of flowers called lilies. Hiram finished the work on the two bronze pillars.
1Kings7:23 Hiram also used bronze to make a big bath which they called ‘the Sea’. It was in the shape of a circle 4.5 metres across. It was 2.3 metres deep. It was 14 metres around the outside.
1Kings7:24 All around its edge
1Kings7:25 Hiram fixed ‘the Sea’ on top of 12 bronze bulls. Three pointed north
1Kings7:26 The walls of ‘the Sea’ were 7½ centimetres thick. Its top edge was like a cup in the shape of a lily flower. ‘The Sea’ contained about 40
1Kings7:27 Hiram also made ten bronze carts to carry water. Each one was 1.8 metres long
1Kings7:28 He made the water carts with bronze sides
1Kings7:29 There were pictures of lions
1Kings7:30 Each cart had four bronze wheels fixed to bronze axles. The axles were fixed under each cart at four places. These places had shapes like leaves on each side.
1Kings7:31 On the top of the cart there was a round piece which held a bowl. This piece was 50 centimetres deep and 75 centimetres across. Hiram cut pictures into the metal all around it. The bronze sides of the carts were square. They were not round.
1Kings7:32 There were four wheels under each cart. They were fixed to axles. The axles and the cart joined together in one piece. Each wheel was 70 centimetres high.
1Kings7:33 The wheels were like the wheels of a chariot. Hiram used bronze to make the axles and all the parts of the wheels.
1Kings7:34 Each cart had four handles. There was one handle on each side
1Kings7:35 There was a piece of metal round the top of each cart. It was 23 centimetres deep. It was fixed at each corner of the cart with pieces of bronze. These pieces and the sides of each cart were all joined together.
1Kings7:36 Hiram cut pictures of cherubs
1Kings7:37 Hiram used bronze to make the ten carts so that they all had the same size and shape.
1Kings7:38 And Hiram also made ten bronze buckets. Each bucket contained about 800 litres. Each bucket was 1.8 metres across. There was one bucket for each of the ten carts.
1Kings7:39 Hiram put five of the carts on the south side of the temple. He put the other five carts on the north side of the temple. He put ‘the Sea’ on the south side of the temple
1Kings7:40 Hiram also made dishes to carry ashes
1Kings7:41 Two pillars. Two pieces for the top of each pillar
1Kings7:42 400 images of pomegranates for the two groups of chains. (There were two rows of these images around the piece at the top of each pillar
1Kings7:43 Ten carts with the ten buckets that were on them.
1Kings7:44 The big bath called ‘the Sea’ and the 12 bulls under it.
1Kings7:45 The dishes
1Kings7:46 The king told his workers to pour the hot bronze into shapes in the ground. They did that at a special place in the region of the Jordan Valley
1Kings7:47 Solomon did not weigh any of these things
1Kings7:48 Solomon also made all these things for the Lord 's temple: The gold altar. The gold table which had the special bread on it.
1Kings7:49 The pure gold lampstands. There were five lampstands on one side of the door to the Most Holy Place and five on the other side. The gold images of flowers. The lamps. The small tools that held things for the altar.
1Kings7:50 The pure gold bowls. The small tools that they used for the lamps. The bowls for water. The dishes for ashes. The baskets that carried hot coals. The gold pieces that held the doors of the Most Holy Place. The gold pieces that held the doors of the temple's big hall.
1Kings7:51 King Solomon finished all the work for the Lord 's temple. Then he brought into it all the holy things that belonged to his father
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon told all the leaders of Israel to come to him in Jerusalem. They were all the leaders of the Israelite tribes and families. He wanted them to bring the Lord 's Covenant Box from Mount Zion
1Kings8:2 So all the Israelite leaders came together to meet with King Solomon. This happened during the Festival of Huts in the seventh month of the year
1Kings8:3 When all Israel's leaders had arrived
1Kings8:4 The priests and the Levites carried the Lord 's Covenant Box
1Kings8:5 King Solomon and all the Israelites who were with him walked in front of the Covenant Box. They offered many sheep and bulls as sacrifices. There were more animals than anyone could count.
1Kings8:6 Then the priests brought the Lord 's Covenant Box to its proper place in the inside room of the temple. That was the Most Holy Place. They put it under the wings of the cherubs.
1Kings8:7 The wings of the cherubs touched each other above the place where the Covenant Box was. The cherubs covered the Covenant Box and the poles that the Levites used to carry it.
1Kings8:8 The poles were very long. The priests could see their ends from the Holy Place
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the Covenant Box

1Kings10:1 The Queen of Sheba heard news that Solomon was a great king who served the Lord. So she came to ask Solomon some difficult questions to see how wise he was.
1Kings10:2 She arrived at Jerusalem with a big group of servants. She had many camels that carried spices. They also carried a lot of gold and valuable jewels. When she came to Solomon
1Kings10:3 Solomon answered all her questions. There was nothing that was too difficult for the king to explain to her.
1Kings10:4 The Queen of Sheba saw that Solomon was very wise. She saw the palace that he had built.
1Kings10:5 She saw all the food that he ate in his palace. She saw all his servants and officers and their beautiful clothes. She saw the servants who prepared his food and wine. She saw the burnt offerings that he offered in the Lord 's temple. All these things caused her to hold her breath in surprise.
1Kings10:6 She said to the king

1Kings10:11 Hiram's ships had brought gold from Ophir. They also brought from there large loads of good wood
1Kings10:12 The king used the wood to make steps for the Lord 's temple and for the king's palace. He also used it to make harps and lyres for the musicians. Nobody has ever seen such valuable wood
1Kings10:13 So King Solomon gave the Queen of Sheba all the gifts that he chose for her. He gave her everything that she wanted. Then she left Solomon and she returned to her own country with her servants.
1Kings10:14 Solomon received 25 tons of gold each year.
1Kings10:15 He also received money from traders
1Kings10:16 King Solomon's workers used gold to make 200 large shields. They hit the gold with hammers to make it flat. They used about four kilograms of gold to cover each shield.
1Kings10:17 They also made 300 small shields in the same way. They used about 2 kilograms of gold to cover each shield. He put these shields in the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 The king used ivory to make a large throne. He covered it with gold.
1Kings10:19 There were six steps up to the throne. The back of the throne was a round shape at the top. On both sides of the seat there were places for the king to put his arms. An image of a lion stood on each side of the throne.
1Kings10:20 There were 12 more images of lions on the six steps. There was one lion at each end of every step. There was no throne like it in any other kingdom.
1Kings10:21 They used gold to make all King Solomon's cups that he drank from. In the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon
1Kings10:22 The king had many large ships that could sail across the seas. They sailed with King Hiram's ships. Every three years they returned to Solomon with their loads. They brought to him gold
1Kings10:23 King Solomon was richer and wiser than any other king in the world.
1Kings10:24 People from every nation in the world wanted to talk to Solomon. They wanted to listen to the wisdom that God had given to him.
1Kings10:25 Every year

1Kings10:27 While Solomon ruled as king

1Kings10:29 Each chariot that they bought in Egypt cost 600 pieces of silver. Each horse cost 150 pieces of silver. They also sold chariots and horses to all the kings of the Hittites and to the kings of Syria.

1Kings15:1 Abijah became king of Judah after Nebat's son Jeroboam had been king of Israel for 18 years.
1Kings15:2 Abijah ruled in Jerusalem as king for three years. His mother's name was Maakah. She was the daughter of Abishalom.
1Kings15:3 Abijah did the same bad things that his father had done before him. He did not serve the Lord his God faithfully
1Kings15:4 But the Lord his God gave Abijah a son to rule after him in Jerusalem. He also made Jerusalem a strong city. The Lord did that because of his promise to David.
1Kings15:5 David had always done things that pleased the Lord. David had obeyed the Lord 's commands in his whole life
1Kings15:6 During Abijah's whole life

1Kings15:8 Abijah died and they buried him beside his ancestors in the City of David. Abijah's son Asa became king after him.
1Kings15:9 Asa became king of Judah when Jeroboam had been king of Israel for 20 years.
1Kings15:10 Asa ruled in Jerusalem as king for 41 years. His grandmother was Maakah. She was the daughter of Abishalom.
1Kings15:11 Asa did things that pleased the Lord
1Kings15:12 He removed the male prostitutes from the altars on the hills. He sent them out of Judah. He removed all the useless idols that his ancestors had made.
1Kings15:13 He also told his grandmother Maakah that she could no longer have authority as the Queen Mother. This was because she had made a disgusting Asherah pole to worship. Asa cut down the Asherah pole and he burned it in the Kidron Valley.
1Kings15:14 Asa did not remove the altars on all the hills
1Kings15:15 He brought into the Lord 's temple the things that he and his father had made as gifts to the Lord. They used silver and gold to make some of these things.
1Kings15:16 There was always a war between King Asa of Judah and King Baasha of Israel while they ruled.
1Kings15:17 One time

1Kings15:20 Ben-Hadad agreed to do what King Asa asked him to do. He sent his army with its leaders to attack towns in Israel. They won the battles at Ijon
1Kings15:21 When King Baasha heard this news
1Kings15:22 Then King Asa commanded all the men in Judah to do some hard work. Everyone had to carry away from Ramah all the big stones and the wood that Baasha had been using there. Then King Asa used those things to make Geba (in Benjamin's land) and Mizpah strong towns again.
1Kings15:23 The other things that happened while Asa was king are written in a book. The book is called ‘The history of Judah's kings’. It tells about Asa's power and all the great things that he did. It also tells about the cities that he built. When Asa became an old man he had a disease in his feet.

1Kings15:25 When Asa had been king of Judah for two years
1Kings15:26 He did things that the Lord said were evil. He did the same bad things that his father had done. He also caused the Israelites to do bad things.

1Kings15:29 When Baasha became king

2Kings3:27 So the king of Moab took his oldest son who would have become king after him. He killed him on the wall of the city as a burnt offering to their god. This made the Israelite soldiers become very upset. So they went away from the city and they returned to their own land.

2Kings23:1 King Josiah told all the leaders of Judah and Jerusalem to come and meet with him.
2Kings23:2 He went up to the Lord 's temple. All the people who lived in Jerusalem and in the rest of Judah went with him. They included the priests
2Kings23:3 Then the king stood in his place beside the pillar in the temple. He promised the Lord that he would obey the covenant. He agreed to serve the Lord faithfully and to obey his commands
2Kings23:4 Then the king gave a command to Hilkiah
2Kings23:5 He removed the priests who served false gods. The kings of Judah had chosen those priests to make offerings at the altars on the hills. They were on the hills in Judah's cities and all around Jerusalem. These priests offered sacrifices to Baal
2Kings23:6 He also removed the Asherah pole from the Lord 's temple. He took it outside Jerusalem
2Kings23:7 King Josiah also destroyed the rooms in the Lord 's temple where the male prostitutes lived. Women also made clothes there for the idol of Asherah.
2Kings23:8 He brought from the towns of Judah all the priests who served false gods. He destroyed the altars on the hills where those priests offered sacrifices. He did that everywhere in Judah
2Kings23:9 The priests who served at those altars did not have authority to serve at the altar in the Lord 's temple in Jerusalem. But they could eat the same flat bread that the other priests ate.
2Kings23:10 King Josiah destroyed the place called Topheth
2Kings23:11 He removed the images of horses that the kings of Judah had put beside the entrance to the Lord 's temple. He also burned the chariots that were there. The kings had put the horses and chariots there to give honour to the sun god. They were in the temple yard near the room of Nathan Melech
2Kings23:12 King Josiah knocked down the altars that were on the roof of the palace. The kings of Judah had built the altars there
2Kings23:13 King Josiah also destroyed the altars that were on the hills east of Jerusalem. Those altars were on the south side of Mount Trouble. King Solomon had built them to worship these false gods: Ashtoreth
2Kings23:14 Josiah broke the stone pillars that people worshipped into small pieces. He cut down the Asherah poles. He covered the ground where they had been with human bones.
2Kings23:15 Josiah also destroyed the altar at Bethel. It was an altar for false gods that Nebat's son
2Kings23:16 Then Josiah looked around and he saw graves on the hill. He sent someone to bring the bones from them. He burned them on the altar
2Kings23:17 He asked
2Kings23:18 King Josiah said
2Kings23:19 King Josiah removed the altars on the hills in all the towns of Samaria. The kings of Israel had built those altars and that had made the Lord angry. Josiah destroyed all of them
2Kings23:20 He punished with death all the priests who served false gods at those altars. He killed them on their own altars. He burned human bones on all the altars. After he had done that
2Kings23:21 Then the king commanded all the people
2Kings23:22 Since the time when the judges ruled Israel
2Kings23:23 But in the 18th year that Josiah ruled Judah as king
2Kings23:24 Josiah did other things to obey the rules that were written in the Book of God's Law. That was the book that Hilkiah the priest had found in the Lord 's temple. Josiah removed the people who spoke to the spirits of dead people and the other magicians. He destroyed the images that people worshipped in their homes. He destroyed all the other idols that people had started to worship in Jerusalem and in all Judah.
2Kings23:25 Josiah turned to the Lord and he served the Lord faithfully with all his strength. He obeyed all the Law of Moses. No other king was like Josiah
2Kings23:26 But the Lord continued to be angry with the people of Judah. The wicked things that King Manasseh did had made him very angry.
2Kings23:27 So the Lord said
2Kings23:28 The other things that happened while Josiah was king are written in a book. The book is called ‘The history of Judah's kings’. It tells about the things that Josiah did.
2Kings23:29 While Josiah was king
2Kings23:30 Josiah's servants put his dead body in a chariot. They took it from Megiddo to Jerusalem. They buried him in his own grave. Then the people of Judah poured olive oil on the head of Josiah's son Jehoahaz. So he became king after his father.
2Kings23:31 Jehoahaz was 23 years old when he became king. He ruled as king in Jerusalem for three months. His mother's name was Hamutal. She was the daughter of Jeremiah
2Kings23:32 Jehoahaz did things that the Lord said were evil.
2Kings23:33 Pharaoh Necho kept him in a prison at Riblah
2Kings23:34 Pharaoh Necho made Josiah's son Eliakim become the new king of Judah. Necho changed Eliakim's name to Jehoiakim. Then he took Jehoahaz away to Egypt. Later
2Kings23:35 King Jehoiakim paid Pharaoh Necho all the silver and gold that he asked for. But King Jehoiakim had to make the people of Judah pay taxes so that he could pay Pharaoh Necho. Each person in Judah had to pay what was right
2Kings23:36 Jehoiakim was 25 years old when he became king. He ruled for 11 years as king in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Zebidah. She was the daughter of Pedaiah
2Kings23:37 Jehoiakim did things that the Lord said were evil
2Kings24:1 While Jehoiakim was king
2Kings24:2 The Lord sent groups of soldiers to attack towns in Judah. The soldiers came from Babylon
2Kings24:3 The Lord had said that these things must happen. He had decided to send Judah away from himself because of the sins that King Manasseh had done.
2Kings24:4 Manasseh had murdered people who had not done anything wrong. Their blood was in the streets of Jerusalem. The Lord would not agree to forgive Manasseh for his sins.
2Kings24:5 The other things that happened while Jehoiakim was king are written in a book. The book is called ‘The history of Judah's kings’. It tells about the things that Jehoiakim did.
2Kings24:6 Jehoiakim died and they buried him beside his ancestors. His son Jehoiachin became king after him.
2Kings24:7 The king of Egypt did not take his army out of his own country again. That was because the king of Babylon had taken a lot of Egypt's land for himself. He had taken all the land between the Stream of Egypt and the Euphrates River. Before that
2Kings24:8 Jehoiachin was 18 years old when he became king. He ruled as king in Jerusalem for three months. His mother's name was Nehushta. She was the daughter of Elnathan
2Kings24:9 Jehoiachin did things that the Lord said were evil
2Kings24:10 King Nebuchadnezzar's officers took their army to attack Jerusalem. They made their camp all around the city.

2Kings24:12 Jehoiachin
2Kings24:13 King Nebuchadnezzar took all the valuable things from the Lord 's temple and from the king's palace. He cut away all the gold things that King Solomon had made for the Lord 's temple. The Lord had already warned that this would happen.
2Kings24:14 Nebuchadnezzar took away as his prisoners all the people who lived in Jerusalem. There were 10
2Kings24:15 Nebuchadnezzar took Jehoiachin away from Jerusalem as his prisoner to Babylon. He also took the king's mother

2Kings24:17 Nebuchadnezzar chose Jehoiachin's uncle

2Kings25:1 Nebuchadnezzar
2Kings25:2 Babylon's army stayed around the city until the 11th year that Zedekiah had been king.
2Kings25:3 By the 9th day of the 4th month
2Kings25:4 Then Babylon's army broke down Jerusalem's wall so that they could go into the city. Their soldiers were all around the city. So the king of Judah and all his army tried to escape in the night. They went through the gate that was near the king's garden. The path went between the two walls of the city. The king and his soldiers ran towards the Jordan Valley.
2Kings25:5 But the soldiers of Babylon's army chased after the king. They caught him on the flat land near Jericho. All King Zedekiah's soldiers ran away from him in many directions.
2Kings25:6 Babylon's soldiers took hold of King Zedekiah. They took him to King Nebuchadnezzar at Riblah. Nebuchadnezzar decided how to punish Zedekiah.
2Kings25:7 They killed all Zedekiah's sons as their punishment
2Kings25:8 King Nebuchadnezzar had an officer whose name was Nebuzaradan. He was the captain of the king's royal guards. Nebuzaradan came to Jerusalem when Nebuchadnezzar had ruled Babylon for 19 years. It was on the 7th day of the 5th month.
2Kings25:9 Nebuzaradan destroyed the Lord 's temple
2Kings25:10 Then Nebuzaradan commanded his whole army to knock down the walls around Jerusalem.
2Kings25:11 Captain Nebuzaradan sent away as prisoners all the people who remained in Jerusalem. He also sent away those people who had agreed to serve the king of Babylon and the workers who were still there.
2Kings25:12 But Nebuzaradan let some of the poorest people stay there. He gave them vineyards and fields to work in.
2Kings25:13 The soldiers from Babylon broke the two bronze pillars that were in the Lord 's temple. They also broke the carts which carried the buckets for water and the large bath called ‘the Sea’. They carried all the bronze pieces away to Babylon.
2Kings25:14 They also took away the pots
2Kings25:15 Nebuzaradan also took away the baskets that carried hot coals and the bowls for water. He took away everything that was made from gold or silver.
2Kings25:16 The bronze from the things that King Solomon had made for the Lord 's temple was very heavy. They included the two bronze pillars
2Kings25:17 Each pillar was 8 metres high. The bronze piece on the top of one pillar was more than 1 metre high. It had rows of chains with images of pomegranates made from bronze all around it. The other pillar
2Kings25:18 Captain Nebuzaradan took hold of these people: Seraiah
2Kings25:19 He also took hold of these people who remained in Jerusalem: The palace officer with authority over the soldiers
2Kings25:20 Nebuzaradan took hold of all those people. He brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah
2Kings25:21 There

1Chronicles10:2 The Philistines chased after Saul and his sons to catch them. They killed Saul's sons Jonathan
1Chronicles10:3 The Philistines were fighting the battle all around Saul. Some of their soldiers saw Saul and they shot their arrows at him. The arrows hurt Saul very much.
1Chronicles10:4 Saul said to the young man who carried his armour
1Chronicles10:5 The young man saw that Saul was dead. So he threw himself onto his own sword and he died too.
1Chronicles10:6 So Saul died there
1Chronicles10:7 All the Israelites who lived in Jezreel Valley saw that their army had run away from the Philistines. They also saw that Saul and his sons were dead. So they left their towns and they ran away. Then the Philistines came to live in those towns.
1Chronicles10:8 On the day after the battle
1Chronicles10:9 They removed everything from Saul's dead body. They took away his head and his weapons. Then they sent men through all the country of the Philistines with the news of Saul's death. These men told the news to all their people and to their idols as well.
1Chronicles10:10 They put Saul's weapons in the temple of their gods. They hung his head in the temple of their god
1Chronicles10:11 All the Israelites who lived in Jabesh Gilead heard about what the Philistines had done to Saul's body.
1Chronicles10:12 So all their brave soldiers went to get the dead bodies of Saul and his sons. They took them to Jabesh Gilead. They buried the bones under the large tree there. The people in Jabesh Gilead did not eat any food for seven days because they were so sad.
1Chronicles10:13 Saul died because he turned away from the Lord. He did not obey the Lord 's commands. He even tried to get advice from the spirits of dead people.
1Chronicles10:14 He did not ask the Lord to tell him what he should do. So the Lord punished him with death. The Lord gave the kingdom of Israel to Jesse's son

2Chronicles3:1 Then Solomon began to build the Lord 's temple in Jerusalem. He built it on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 Solomon began to build the temple on the second day of the second month of the fourth year that he ruled Israel as king.
2Chronicles3:3 The foundation for God's temple was 27 metres long and 9 metres wide. (They measured it in cubits.)
2Chronicles3:4 There was an entrance room at the front of the temple's big hall. It was as wide as the temple
2Chronicles3:5 They covered the walls inside the big hall with boards of cypress wood. Then they covered the boards with pure gold. They drew pictures of palm trees and chains on the walls.
2Chronicles3:6 They used valuable stones to make the temple beautiful. The gold that they used came from Parvaim.
2Chronicles3:7 They used gold to cover all parts of the temple: the beams for its roof
2Chronicles3:8 Solomon's workers built the Most Holy Place in the temple. It was 9 metres long and 9 metres wide. That was how wide the temple itself was. They used about 20
2Chronicles3:9 The gold nails weighed the same as 50 gold coins. They also covered the walls of the upstairs rooms with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 In the Most Holy Place
2Chronicles3:11 The cherubs stood side by side in the Most Holy Place. Their faces looked towards the big hall. Each cherub had two wings. Each wing was 2.2 metres long. They held their wings out so that one wing of each cherub touched a wing of the other cherub. The other wing of each cherub touched a wall of the Most Holy Place. The four wings of the two cherubs reached across 9 metres.

2Chronicles3:15 Solomon's workers made two pillars to stand at the front of the temple. They were 8 metres high. There was a piece on the top of each pillar that was 2.2 metres high.
2Chronicles3:16 They made images of chains around the top pieces of the pillars. They also made 100 images of pomegranates among the chains.
2Chronicles3:17 Then they put the two pillars at the entrance of the temple. One pillar stood on the south side of the entrance. The other pillar stood on the north side. Solomon called the pillar on the south side ‘Jakin’. He called the pillar on the north side ‘Boaz’.

2Chronicles8:10 King Solomon had 250 Israelite officers who had authority over the workers.

2Chronicles8:12 Solomon offered burnt offerings to the Lord on the altar that he had built. The altar was outside the entrance of the temple building.
2Chronicles8:13 He offered the sacrifices every day that Moses had told them to do. There were rules about sacrifices on Sabbath days
2Chronicles8:14 Solomon gave jobs to each group of priests as his father David had commanded. He also gave the Levites their jobs to do each day. They led the music to worship God and they helped the priests with their work. He gave each group of guards a job to watch the gates of the temple. He told them all to do this in the way that God's servant David had commanded.
2Chronicles8:15 All the priests and the Levites were careful to do exactly what the king had commanded. That included the rooms where they stored valuable things.
2Chronicles8:16 Solomon's workers did all the jobs that he told them to do. They started on the day when they built the foundation of the Lord 's temple. They continued to work until they had finished everything. In that way
2Chronicles8:17 Then Solomon went to Ezion-Geber and to Elath. They were towns on the coast of the sea
2Chronicles8:18 King Hiram had sailors who knew how to sail ships on the sea. He sent some of these men to work with Solomon's sailors. They sailed to Ophir and they brought back about 15 tons of gold. They gave it to King Solomon.
2Chronicles9:1 The Queen of Sheba heard news that Solomon was a great king. So she came to ask Solomon some difficult questions to see how wise he was. She arrived at Jerusalem with a big group of servants. She had many camels that carried spices. They also carried a lot of gold and valuable jewels. When she came to Solomon
2Chronicles9:2 Solomon answered all her questions. There was nothing that was too difficult for him to explain to her.
2Chronicles9:3 The Queen of Sheba saw that Solomon was very wise. She saw the palace that he had built.
2Chronicles9:4 She saw all the food that he ate in his palace. She saw all his servants and officers and their beautiful clothes. She saw the servants who prepared his food and wine. She saw the burnt offerings that he offered in the Lord 's temple. All these things caused her to hold her breath in surprise.
2Chronicles9:5 She said to the king
2Chronicles9:6 But I did not believe those things until I came here. Now I have seen everything with my own eyes
2Chronicles9:7 God has blessed your people and your officers! They are always with you
2Chronicles9:8 So we should praise the Lord your God! He is happy with you and he has chosen you to rule Israel on his behalf. Your God will continue to love Israel for ever
2Chronicles9:9 The Queen of Sheba gave to King Solomon more than four tons of gold
2Chronicles9:10 King Hiram's men had brought gold from Ophir. King Solomon's men had helped them to do this. They also brought very good wood
2Chronicles9:11 The king used the wood to make steps for the Lord 's temple and for the king's palace. He also used it to make harps and lyres for the musicians. Nobody had ever seen things like those in Judah.
2Chronicles9:12 King Solomon gave to the Queen of Sheba everything that she wanted. He gave more things to her than she had brought to him. Then she left with all her servants and she returned to her own country.
2Chronicles9:13 Solomon received 25 tons of gold each year.
2Chronicles9:14 He also received money from traders and from people who travelled to buy things. The kings of Arabia and the rulers of each region in Israel also brought gold and silver to Solomon.
2Chronicles9:15 King Solomon's workers used gold to make 200 large shields. They hit the gold with hammers to make it flat. They used about four kilograms of gold to cover each shield.
2Chronicles9:16 They also made 300 small shields in the same way. They used about 2 kilograms of gold to cover each shield. He put these shields in the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon.
2Chronicles9:17 The king used ivory to make a large throne. He covered it with pure gold.
2Chronicles9:18 There were six steps up to the throne. They fixed a gold box to the throne for the king to put his feet on. On both sides of the seat there were places to put his arms. An image of a lion stood on each side of the throne.
2Chronicles9:19 There were 12 more images of lions on the six steps. There was one lion at each end of every step. There was no throne like it in any other kingdom.
2Chronicles9:20 They used gold to make all King Solomon's cups that he drank from. In the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon
2Chronicles9:21 The king had many large ships that King Hiram's men could sail across the seas. Every three years they returned to Solomon with their loads. They brought to him gold
2Chronicles9:22 King Solomon was richer and wiser than any other king in the world.
2Chronicles9:23 The kings of every nation in the world wanted to talk to Solomon. They wanted to listen to the wisdom that God had given to him.
2Chronicles9:24 Every year
2Chronicles9:25 Solomon had places to keep 4
2Chronicles9:26 He ruled over all the kings around Israel
2Chronicles9:27 While Solomon ruled as king
2Chronicles9:28 People brought horses to Solomon from Egypt and from all the other countries.
2Chronicles9:29 People wrote down everything else that Solomon did while he ruled as king
2Chronicles9:30 Solomon ruled over all Israel for 40 years while he lived in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles9:31 Then he died. They buried him with his ancestors

2Chronicles32:1 After King Hezekiah had done all these good things
2Chronicles32:2 Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come to attack Judah. He realized that Sennacherib had decided to attack Jerusalem.
2Chronicles32:3 So Hezekiah talked with his advisors and his army officers about what they should do. They decided to stop all the water that came from springs around the city. They all agreed that this was a good idea.
2Chronicles32:4 A big group of people came to help. They stopped the water that came from the springs and from the stream that went through that region. They said

2Chronicles32:14 Look at all the nations that my ancestors completely destroyed. None of their gods could rescue any of them. So do not think that your God can save you from my power.
2Chronicles32:15 Do not let Hezekiah deceive you with his lies. Do not believe him. No god of any nation or kingdom has been able to save his people from me or from my ancestors. So your God certainly cannot save you from my power! ’

2Chronicles32:18 Then Sennacherib's men shouted loudly to the people who were standing on Jerusalem's walls. They spoke in the language of Judah's people. They wanted to make the people very afraid so that they could take the city for themselves.
2Chronicles32:19 They insulted the God of Jerusalem's people
2Chronicles32:20 King Hezekiah and Amoz's son
2Chronicles32:21 The Lord God sent an angel to destroy Assyria's army. The angel killed all the soldiers and the army officers in their camp. So the king of Assyria had to return home to his own country. He was very ashamed. He went into the temple of his god. There some of his own sons used their swords to kill him.
2Chronicles32:22 That is how the Lord saved Hezekiah and Jerusalem's people from King Sennacherib of Assyria. The Lord also saved them from all their other enemies. So all Judah had a time of peace.

2Chronicles33:1 Manasseh was 12 years old when he became king. He ruled as king in Jerusalem for 55 years.
2Chronicles33:2 Manasseh did things that the Lord said were evil. He did the same terrible sins that the other nations in Canaan had done. Those were the nations that the Lord had chased out so that the Israelites could live there.
2Chronicles33:3 Manasseh built again the altars on the hills that his father Hezekiah had knocked down. He built altars for people to worship idols of Baal. He also made Asherah poles. He bent down low to worship all the stars in the sky.
2Chronicles33:4 He built altars in the Lord 's temple. The Lord had said about his temple
2Chronicles33:5 Manasseh built altars to give honour to the stars in both yards of the Lord 's temple.
2Chronicles33:6 He burnt his own sons with fire as a sacrifice in Ben Hinnom Valley. He used magic and false gods to find out what would happen in the future. He took advice from magicians and people who talked to the spirits of dead people. He did many things that the Lord said were very evil. This made the Lord very angry.
2Chronicles33:7 Manasseh made an image of a false god. He put this idol in God's temple. God had said this about his temple to King David and to his son King Solomon: ‘My people will worship me in my temple here in Jerusalem. That is the place that I have chosen to be my home for ever. I have chosen it from among all the tribes of Israel.
2Chronicles33:8 I will never cause the Israelite people to leave this land that I gave to their ancestors. But they must be careful to obey all my commands
2Chronicles33:9 But Manasseh caused Judah's people and the people of Jerusalem to turn away from God. So they did more evil things than the people who lived in Canaan before them. The Lord had destroyed those nations so that the Israelites could live there.
2Chronicles33:10 The Lord warned Manasseh and his people. But they would not listen to him.
2Chronicles33:11 So the Lord brought the officers of the king of Assyria's army to attack them. They took hold of Manasseh. They put metal hooks in his nose and they tied bronze chains around him. Then they took him away to Babylon.
2Chronicles33:12 This gave Manasseh a lot of pain. He asked the Lord his God to be kind to him. He made himself humble and he prayed for help to the Lord
2Chronicles33:13 When Manasseh prayed to the Lord
2Chronicles33:14 After that
2Chronicles33:15 Manasseh removed from the Lord 's temple the foreign gods and the idol that he had put there. He also removed the altars that he had built on the hill of the temple and in other places in Jerusalem. He threw them away
2Chronicles33:16 Then he repaired the Lord 's altar in the temple. He brought friendship offerings and thank offerings as sacrifices on the altar. He told Judah's people to worship the Lord
2Chronicles33:17 The people still offered sacrifices at the other altars in the country. But they only offered those sacrifices to the Lord their God.
2Chronicles33:18 The other things that happened while Manasseh was king are written in a book. The book is called ‘The history of Israel's kings’. It includes Manasseh's prayer to God. It also includes the messages that the Lord
2Chronicles33:19 The book of the prophets' messages also includes Manasseh's prayer and how God answered him. It tells about all Manasseh's sins and how he turned away from God. It includes a list of all the places where he built altars and where he put Asherah poles and idols. He did those things before he made himself humble and he turned back to God.
2Chronicles33:20 Manasseh died and his people buried him in his palace. His son Amon became king after him.
2Chronicles33:21 Amon was 22 years old when he became king. He ruled as king in Jerusalem for two years.
2Chronicles33:22 Amon did things that the Lord said were evil
2Chronicles33:23 But he did not make himself humble and turn back to the Lord
2Chronicles33:24 Amon's own officers decided to kill him. They killed him in his palace.
2Chronicles33:25 Then the people of Judah punished all Amon's murderers with death. They chose his son Josiah to be king after him.
2Chronicles34:1 Josiah was eight years old when he became king. He ruled as king in Jerusalem for 31 years.

Ezra4:14 We serve you as our king. We do not want you to lose honour. So we have sent this letter to you.

Ezra4:21 Now you must command these people to stop their work. They must not build the city again until I decide to let them do it.
Ezra4:22 You must be very careful to make sure that they stop. We do not want the danger to our kingdom to become any greater. Our authority to rule them as kings must continue.’

Ezra5:5 But God was watching over the Jewish leaders. Nobody was able to stop them building at that time. The city's officers had to wait until they could send a letter to King Darius. They waited for him to reply.
Ezra5:6 Tattenai
Ezra5:7 This is what they said: ‘King Darius
Ezra5:8 We went to Judah to visit the temple of the great God. The king should know that the Jews are building it with big stones. They are putting wooden beams in its walls. They are working hard and very carefully. The building is growing fast.
Ezra5:9 We spoke to their leaders and we asked them
Ezra5:10 We also asked them to tell us their names. Then we could tell you who the leaders are.
Ezra5:11 This is the answer that they gave to us: “We are servants of the God who rules heaven and earth. A great king of Israel built this temple and finished it many years ago. Now we are building it again.
Ezra5:12 Our ancestors made the God of heaven angry. So he let King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon have power over them. He destroyed this temple. Then he took our people as prisoners to Babylon.
Ezra5:13 But later
Ezra5:14 He gave back to us the gold and silver things that belong in our God's temple. King Nebuchadnezzar had taken those things away from the temple in Jerusalem. He took them to Babylon and he put them in his own temple. But King Cyrus gave our valuable things to Sheshbazzar. He had given authority to this man to rule over Judah.
Ezra5:15 He said to Sheshbazzar
Ezra5:16 So this man
Ezra5:17 If the king should choose
Ezra6:1 King Darius told his officers to look in the books in the royal library. That was in Babylon
Ezra6:2 But it was in the region of Media that they found a scroll about the temple in Jerusalem. It was stored in a strong building in Ecbatana. This is what the scroll said: ‘This is an important note.
Ezra6:3 King Cyrus gave a command about God's temple in Jerusalem
Ezra6:4 They must build the walls with three rows of big stones
Ezra6:5 We will give back to them the silver and gold things that belong in their God's temple. King Nebuchadnezzar took them from the temple in Jerusalem and he brought them here to Babylon. Now they must return to the place where they belong
Ezra6:6 So listen to me
Ezra6:7 You must not do anything to stop the work on God's temple. The Jewish ruler and their leaders must continue to build this house of God. They must build it in its proper place.
Ezra6:8 So I will now tell you what you must do for the Jewish leaders. You must help them to build this temple again. Use the king's money to pay for everything that they need to continue the work. Use the taxes that we receive from people in the region west of the Euphrates River. The work on the building must not stop.
Ezra6:9 Also give to the priests the things that they need each day for their sacrifices to the God of heaven. Give them young bulls
Ezra6:10 Then their offerings will make the God of heaven happy. They will pray for God to bless the king and his family.
Ezra6:11 Nobody may change what I have commanded in this letter. If anyone does not obey this command
Ezra6:12 No king or nation should try to change my command. They must never destroy God's temple in Jerusalem. That is the place that he has chosen for people to worship him. I pray that he will remove anyone who tries to attack that place. I
Ezra6:13 Tattenai
Ezra6:14 So the leaders of the Jews continued to build the temple. The work went very well. They listened to the messages from God that the prophet Haggai and Iddo's grandson
Ezra6:15 They finished building the temple on the third day of Adar month. King Darius had then been king for six years.
Ezra6:16 Then the Israelite people offered the temple to God
Ezra6:17 They offered 100 bulls
Ezra6:18 They decided how each group of priests and Levites should work in God's temple in Jerusalem. Moses had written in his book how they should do this.
Ezra6:19 On the 14th day of the first month
Ezra6:20 The priests and Levites had all made themselves clean for their special work. The Levites killed the lambs for the Passover sacrifice. They did that on behalf of all the people
Ezra6:21 So all the people who had returned from Babylon ate the feast. Other Israelites in Jerusalem had turned away from the bad things that other nations who lived there did. They had decided to worship the Lord
Ezra6:22 Then they ate the Feast of Flat Bread for seven days. The Lord had caused them to be very happy
Ezra7:1 After these things
Ezra7:2 Hilkiah was the son of Shallum
Ezra7:3 Ahitub was the son of Amariah
Ezra7:4 Meraioth was the son of Zerahiah
Ezra7:5 Bukki was the son of Abishua
Ezra7:6 This man Ezra arrived from Babylon. He was a teacher who knew the Law of Moses very well. The Lord
Ezra7:7 In the seventh year that Artaxerxes was king
Ezra7:8 Ezra arrived in Jerusalem in the fifth month of Artaxerxes' seventh year as king.
Ezra7:9 He left Babylon on the first day of the first month and he arrived in Jerusalem on the first day of the fifth month. God had helped him with his journey.
Ezra7:10 God was kind to Ezra because Ezra had decided to study the Law of the Lord. Ezra always obeyed its rules. He also taught the Law and its rules to the Israelite people.
Ezra7:11 This is a copy of the letter that King Artaxerxes had given to Ezra
Ezra7:12 ‘From Artaxerxes
Ezra7:13 I have given this command: When you travel to Jerusalem
Ezra7:14 The king and his seven officers are sending you with their authority. You have the Law of your God that he has given to you. Go and see if your people in Judah and Jerusalem are obeying his Law.
Ezra7:15 Take with you the gold and silver that I and my officers have sent. They are a free gift for Israel's God
Ezra7:16 Also take the gold and silver that other people in Babylonia give to you. And take the gifts that the people and the priests have given for their God's temple in Jerusalem.
Ezra7:17 You must use the money to buy bulls
Ezra7:18 You and your friends may then use the rest of the silver and gold in the way that you choose. Decide what your God wants you to do.
Ezra7:19 Also take the valuable things that you use to worship your God in his temple. Take them safely to him in Jerusalem.
Ezra7:20 You may need other things to worship your God in his temple. You may take what you need from the royal store rooms.
Ezra7:21 I
Ezra7:22 This is how much you may give to him: 3
Ezra7:23 Be careful to give Ezra everything that the God of heaven wants for his temple. I do not want the God of heaven to be angry with the kingdom that I and my sons rule.
Ezra7:24 You must not take any kinds of taxes from the men who serve this God in his house. You have no authority to do this. Take no taxes from the priests
Ezra7:25 Ezra
Ezra7:26 Everyone must obey the laws of your God and also the laws of the king. If they do not obey
Ezra7:27 I praise the Lord
Ezra7:28 The Lord has been kind to me

Ezra8:17 I sent them all to Iddo
Ezra8:18 God was kind to us and he helped us. So they brought Sherebiah to us. He was a worker who had special skills to make things. He was a descendant of Israel's son Levi. He belonged to the clan of Levi's son
Ezra8:19 Hashabiah and Jeshaiah also came. They belonged to the clan of Levi's son
Ezra8:20 220 temple servants also came. King David had first chosen this group to help the Levites with their work. We made a list of all their names.
Ezra8:21 We were all waiting by the Ahava River. I told the people not to eat any food
Ezra8:22 I was ashamed to ask the king to send his soldiers with us to keep us safe. He could have sent soldiers who rode on horses with us
Ezra8:23 So we ate no food and we prayed to our God about this. He answered our prayers.
Ezra8:24 I chose 12 of the most important priests to do an important job
Ezra8:25 I told them to take care of the valuable things that we had received for the temple of our God. These were silver and gold things and other special things that the king
Ezra8:26 I weighed 20
Ezra8:27 and 20 gold bowls that weighed 8.5 kilograms. I also gave to them two beautiful bowls made from bronze. They shone brightly and they were as valuable as gold.
Ezra8:28 I said to the men
Ezra8:29 So you must be careful to keep them safe until you reach Jerusalem. When you get there
Ezra8:30 Then they gave the silver
Ezra8:31 We began our journey from the Ahava River on the 12th day of the first month. We left there to go to Jerusalem. Our God kept us safe on the journey. No enemies attacked us and nobody robbed us on the way.
Ezra8:32 So we arrived safely in Jerusalem. We rested there for three days.
Ezra8:33 On the fourth day
Ezra8:34 They weighed and counted everything carefully. They wrote everything in a list as they weighed it.
Ezra8:35 All the people who had returned from Babylon made burnt offerings as sacrifices to Israel's God. They offered to the Lord 12 bulls on behalf of all Israel. They also offered 96 male sheep and 77 lambs. They killed 12 male goats as a sin offering. They gave all these to the Lord as burnt offerings.
Ezra8:36 They also gave the king's letter with its commands to his officers and rulers in the region west of the Euphrates River. The king's officers helped the Israelite people and the work of God's temple.

Ezra10:8 If they did not arrive in three days
Ezra10:9 All the men from the tribes of Judah and Benjamin reached Jerusalem in three days. It was the 20th day of the ninth month. All the people sat in the yard in front of God's temple. Their bodies were shaking because they were afraid. It was also raining.

Ezra10:14 So please let our officers decide what to do on behalf of us all. People who have married foreign wives should come here on certain days. People from each town should come on the right day
Ezra10:15 Only four men did not agree with this. They were Asahel's son
Ezra10:16 So the Jews who had returned from Babylon did what they had agreed to do. Ezra the priest chose family leaders for each clan. He wrote down their names. The leaders met together to decide what to do. They started on the first day of the tenth month.
Ezra10:17 They finished on the first day of the first month of the new year.

Nehemiah2:1 It was the month of Nisan

Nehemiah2:9 The king sent officers of his army to keep me safe on the journey. He also sent soldiers who rode on horses. I went to the king's officers on the west side of the Euphrates River. I gave them the letters which the king had written.
Nehemiah2:10 But two important men were not happy when they heard that I had come to help the Israelite people. They were Sanballat
Nehemiah2:11 I arrived in the city of Jerusalem. After three days
Nehemiah2:12 I went out at night. I took a few of my friends with me. I was riding on a donkey. That was the only animal that we took with us. I did not tell anyone the idea that God had put in my mind about Jerusalem.
Nehemiah2:13 I went through the Valley Gate to the Jackal Well and the Dung Gate. As I went
Nehemiah2:14 I went to the Fountain Gate and then to the King's Pool. The path there was too narrow for my donkey to pass through.
Nehemiah2:15 So I went along the path in the valley while it was still night. I continued to look at the wall. I reached the Valley Gate again and I went back into the city.
Nehemiah2:16 The city's officers did not know that I had done this. I had not told anyone what I had decided to do. I had not told any of the other Jews

Nehemiah4:7 Sanballat

Nehemiah4:11 At the same time

Nehemiah6:1 We had finished building the city's wall. There were no holes in it now

Nehemiah6:3 So I sent this message back to them: ‘I am too busy to come and meet you. The work here is too important. I cannot stop the work here to come and meet you.’
Nehemiah6:4 They sent me the same message four times. Each time
Nehemiah6:5 Then Sanballat sent his officer to me with a fifth message. He carried a letter that was open for anyone to read.
Nehemiah6:6 This is what the letter said: ‘To Nehemiah. Other nations have heard the news that you and the other Jews have decided to turn against the king. Geshem also says that this is true. This is why you are building the city's wall. People are saying that you want to rule the Jews as their king.
Nehemiah6:7 You have chosen some prophets to speak your message in Jerusalem. You have told them to say

Nehemiah8:1 All the people met together in the open place near the Water Gate. They asked Ezra to bring the book of the Law of Moses. The Lord had given those laws to the Israelites so that they would obey them.

Nehemiah9:1 On the 24th day of the same month
Nehemiah9:2 The Israelite people made themselves separate from foreign people. They stood up and they told God that they were sorry for their sins. They agreed that their ancestors had also done many bad things.
