Nehemiah9:4 The Levites stood on some high steps. They called out in a loud voice to the Lord their God. Those Levites were Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 Then the Levites (Jeshua
Nehemiah9:6 Only you are the Lord. You made the sky and the heavens above the sky. You made all the stars
Nehemiah9:7 You are the Lord God who chose Abram. You brought him out of Ur
Nehemiah9:8 You knew that Abraham trusted you
Nehemiah9:9 You saw that our ancestors suffered very much in Egypt. You heard them cry to you for help at the Red Sea.
Nehemiah9:10 You did great miracles to show your power against Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 You divided the sea into two parts so that your people could go across it on a dry path. When Pharaoh's soldiers chased after them
Nehemiah9:12 During the day
Nehemiah9:13 At Mount Sinai
Nehemiah9:14 You told them about the Sabbath and how to make it a special day. You gave all your laws to Moses so that he would teach them to your people.
Nehemiah9:15 You gave your people bread from heaven when they were hungry. You gave them water from a rock to drink when they were thirsty. You commanded them to go to the land that you had promised to give them. You told them to take it for themselves.
Nehemiah9:16 But our ancestors turned away from you. They would not agree to obey your commands.
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to obey you. They forgot about all the miracles that you had done to help them. Instead
Nehemiah9:18 Our ancestors made an image of a young cow to be their god. They said to each other
Nehemiah9:19 You were kind to them while they travelled in the desert. You continued to lead them with a pillar of cloud in the day. At night
Nehemiah9:20 You gave them your good Spirit to teach them what to do. You continued to give them manna from heaven to eat. You gave them water from a rock to drink.
Nehemiah9:21 During 40 years you took care of them in the desert. Their clothes did not spoil. Their feet did not become big with pain.
Nehemiah9:22 You gave kingdoms and nations to your people. The borders of their land reached a long way. The land of King Sihon of Heshbon became their own land. So did the land of King Og of Bashan.
Nehemiah9:23 You caused your people to have many descendants
Nehemiah9:24 Their descendants went there and they took the land for themselves. You gave them power over the Canaanites and the other kings and nations who lived there. They could do whatever they wanted with those people.
Nehemiah9:25 They took their strong cities and their good land for themselves. The houses of those people were full of many good things. Your people could use the things that were already there. They had their wells
Nehemiah9:26 But your people did not obey you. They turned against you. They stopped obeying your laws. You sent your prophets to warn your people to return to you. But they killed the prophets that you sent to them. Your people insulted you in many bad ways.
Nehemiah9:27 So you put them under the power of their enemies. And their enemies were cruel to them. Then your people called to you for help because they were in trouble. You heard them from your home in heaven. Because you are so kind
Nehemiah9:28 But when your people were living safely again
Nehemiah9:29 You warned them to obey your laws. But they refused to listen to you. They did not obey your commands. You had given them rules which would help them to have true life if they obeyed them. But they would not obey your laws. They turned away from you and they continued to do bad things.
Nehemiah9:30 You continued to help your people for many years. You sent your prophets to tell them messages from your Spirit. You warned them
Nehemiah9:31 But because you are very kind
Nehemiah9:32 So we pray to you
Nehemiah9:33 But when you punished us like that
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 When they had their own kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 So today we live like slaves. Yes
Nehemiah9:37 But because of our sins you have caused foreign kings to rule over us. They take the food that grows in our fields. They use us and our animals in any way that they like. So we are very upset!’

Esther1:1 These things happened when King Xerxes ruled. He was the king who ruled over 127 regions
Esther1:2 King Xerxes lived in the capital
Esther1:3 During the third year that he ruled
Esther1:4 The feast continued for 180 days. King Xerxes wanted to show everyone how rich and powerful he was. He wanted to show that he was a great king.
Esther1:5 When this feast finished
Esther1:6 The gardens had curtains made of expensive white linen and purple material. They hung from silver rings on white stone pillars. White and purple strings held the curtains. There were seats made from gold and silver. The floor had many kinds of valuable stones to make it beautiful.
Esther1:7 People at the feast drank from gold cups. Each cup was different. The king gave plenty of his own wine to everyone to drink.
Esther1:8 Everyone could drink as much wine as they wanted. The king told his officers to give each person what they wanted
Esther1:9 At the same time
Esther1:10 On the seventh day of the feast
Esther1:11 He told them to bring Queen Vashti to him. He said that she must wear her royal crown. He wanted to show everyone at his feast how beautiful his queen was. She was a very beautiful woman.
Esther1:12 The eunuchs told Queen Vashti what the king had commanded. But she refused to go to him. This made the king very angry.
Esther1:13 The king spoke to his wise advisors about what had happened. These men understood the laws and customs of Persia. When something like this happened
Esther1:14 The seven advisors who were often with the king were Carshena
Esther1:15 The king asked them
Esther1:16 One of the advisors
Esther1:17 When other women hear about this
Esther1:18 Everywhere in Persia and Media
Esther1:19 So we suggest this
Esther1:20 You must send this command to everyone in your great kingdom. When people know about this law
Esther1:21 The king and his officers liked this idea. So the king did as Memukan had said.
Esther1:22 He sent letters to all the regions of his kingdom. The letters were in the language of each region
Esther2:1 Some time later
Esther2:2 The king's servants who helped him in the palace said
Esther2:3 The king could choose officers from every region of his kingdom to do this. They will bring all the beautiful young women here to Susa. Hegai will take care of them in the royal harem. He is the eunuch who has that job. The young women will receive special oils and perfumes to make them more beautiful.
Esther2:4 The king may then choose the young woman who pleases him most. She could take the place of Vashti as queen.’ King Xerxes thought that this was a good idea. He did everything that the servants said.
Esther2:5 A Jewish man called Mordecai lived in Susa. He was the son of Jair. Jair was the son of Shimei. Shimei was the son of Kish. Kish was a descendant of Benjamin.
Esther2:6 When Nebuchadnezzar was king of Babylon
Esther2:7 Mordecai was taking care of a young cousin called Hadassah. She was also called Esther. Esther's mother and father were no longer alive. She was the daughter of Mordecai's uncle. When her parents died
Esther2:8 Esther was one of the young women that the king's officers brought to Susa. That happened because of the king's command. The beautiful young women lived in the royal harem. Hegai had the authority to take care of them.
Esther2:9 Hegai liked Esther and he was kind to her. As soon as Esther arrived
Esther2:10 Esther did not tell anyone that she was a Jew. That is what Mordecai had told her.
Esther2:11 Mordecai wanted to know what was happening to Esther. So every day he walked in the palace yard that was near the harem. He wanted to hear some news about her.
Esther2:12 All the young women stayed in the harem for twelve months. For six months they received myrrh oil for their bodies. Then they received perfume and different kinds of oil for another six months. After that time they could each go to the king's bedroom.
Esther2:13 When the time came for a young woman to go to the king
Esther2:14 She would go to be with the king in the evening. She stayed with him during that night. In the morning
Esther2:15 One evening
Esther2:16 Esther went to King Xerxes in his royal rooms in the palace. That happened in the tenth month of the year
Esther2:17 The king loved Esther more than he loved any of the other women. He was more pleased with her than all the other young women. He put the royal crown on her head. He chose Esther to be queen
Esther2:18 The king gave a great feast for all the leaders and officers of his kingdom. He did it to give honour to Esther. He said that there would be a holiday for people in all the regions. He also gave expensive gifts to people.
Esther2:19 Some time later
Esther2:20 Esther still had not told anyone that she was a Jew. Mordecai had told her that she should not tell anyone. She continued to obey Mordecai
Esther2:21 One day
Esther2:22 Mordecai heard about this. So he told Queen Esther about it. Queen Esther told the king what Mordecai had said.
Esther2:23 The king sent his men to see if it was true. He discovered that Mordecai had told the truth. So he said that the two eunuchs must die. The king's officers hanged them from a wooden tower until they were dead. They wrote about this event in the history book
Esther3:1 After some time
Esther3:2 The king commanded that all the royal officers at the king's gate must bend down low down to Haman. So they all gave Haman honour when he passed them. But Mordecai refused to bend down and give honour to Haman.
Esther3:3 The officers at the king's gate asked Mordecai
Esther3:4 They warned Mordecai every day. But he would not agree to bend down low to Haman. So the officers told Haman about this. Mordecai had told them that he was a Jew. So they wanted to know if Haman would allow Mordecai to do this.
Esther3:5 When Haman realized that Mordecai would not give him honour
Esther3:6 But he did not want to destroy only Mordecai. Haman had discovered that Mordecai was a Jew. So he decided to destroy all the Jews who lived in Xerxes' whole kingdom.
Esther3:7 Haman asked his advisors when would be the right time to make this happen. He asked them in the first month of the year (Nisan)
Esther3:8 Then Haman said to King Xerxes
Esther3:9 If you agree
Esther3:10 The king took his special ring from his finger and he gave it to Hammedatha's son
Esther3:11 He said to Haman
Esther3:12 On the 13th day of the first month
Esther3:13 Men delivered the letters to all the regions of the kingdom. Haman's command said that
Esther3:14 Haman sent a copy of his command to every region
Esther3:15 King Xerxes told his men to take the letters quickly to all the people. They also read the law to the people in Susa city. Then Haman and the king sat down to drink wine together. But the people in the city could not understand what was happening.
Esther4:1 Mordecai discovered what Haman had done. He was very upset. He tore his clothes and he dressed in sackcloth and ashes. He went into the city. He cried and he wept loudly as he went.
Esther4:2 Nobody who was wearing sackcloth could go into the yard of the palace. So Mordecai stopped at the king's gate.
Esther4:3 Jews who lived in all the regions of Xerxes' kingdom heard the king's command. So they were very sad. They were weeping and they were fasting. Many of them lay on sackcloth and ashes.
Esther4:4 Esther's female servants and the eunuchs who took care of her told her what Mordecai was doing. So Esther was very upset. She sent clothes for Mordecai to wear instead of the sackcloth. But Mordecai refused to wear them.
Esther4:5 Hathach was one of the king's eunuchs who served Esther. Esther asked him to discover what was the matter with Mordecai.
Esther4:6 So Hathach went to speak to Mordecai. Mordecai was outside the king's gate
Esther4:7 Mordecai told Hathach everything that had happened. He told Hathach how much money Haman would pay to the king when people destroyed all the Jews.
Esther4:8 Mordecai gave Hathach a copy of the law that they had read aloud to the people in Susa. He told Hathach to show the law to Esther and explain it to her. Mordecai also said that Esther must go and speak to the king. She must ask the king to let her people live.
Esther4:9 Hathach went back into the palace. He told Esther what Mordecai had said.
Esther4:10 Then Esther sent Hathach back to Mordecai with this message:
Esther4:11 ‘Everybody knows the law. People cannot go to meet the king in his own part of the palace unless the king asks them to come. All the king's officers and all his people know that that is the law. Nobody can go to the king when the king has not asked to see them. Anyone who does that must die. They will only stay alive if the king holds out his gold sceptre towards them. As for me
Esther4:12 When Mordecai heard this message from Esther
Esther4:13 he sent this answer back to her: ‘Do not think that you will be safe because you live in the king's palace. You will not escape when they kill all the other Jews.
Esther4:14 You must speak now on behalf of all the Jewish people. If you do not
Esther4:15 Then Esther sent this answer to Mordecai:
Esther4:16 ‘Tell all the Jews in Susa to meet together. Tell them to fast and to pray for me. They must not eat or drink for three days
Esther4:17 Then Mordecai left the king's gate. He did everything that Esther had told him to do.

Esther5:3 The king asked her
Esther5:4 Esther replied

Esther5:7 Esther replied

Esther6:1 That night the king could not sleep. He asked a servant to bring the kingdom's history book. He asked the servant to read the book to him.
Esther6:2 The history book included a report of how two of the king's eunuchs wanted to kill King Xerxes. Their names were Bigthana and Teresh. The report said that Mordecai had told the king about this.
Esther6:3 When the king heard this
Esther6:4 The king said
Esther6:5 The king's servants said to the king
Esther6:6 Haman came in to the king's room. The king asked him
Esther6:7 So Haman replied to the king
Esther6:8 Your servants should bring some royal clothes that you yourself have worn. They should bring a horse that you yourself have ridden on as king. They should put a crown on the horse's head to show that it belongs to you.
Esther6:9 Then give the clothes and the horse to one of your royal officers. Tell the officer to put the king's clothes on the man that you want to give honour to. Then the officer must put the man on the king's horse. He must lead him through all the streets of the city. As he goes
Esther6:10 The king said to Haman
Esther6:11 So Haman took the king's clothes and the king's horse to Mordecai. He put the king's clothes on Mordecai. Then he led Mordecai on the horse through all the streets of the city. Haman went in front and he shouted

Esther6:13 He told his wife
Esther6:14 While they were speaking
Esther7:1 King Xerxes and Haman went to the meal that Queen Esther had prepared.
Esther7:2 This was the second day that they ate a special meal together. While they were drinking wine
Esther7:3 Queen Esther replied
Esther7:4 Someone has sold me and my people as if we are animals. Now they will kill us and completely destroy us all. If we had been sold to become slaves
Esther7:5 Then King Xerxes asked Queen Esther
Esther7:6 Esther replied
Esther7:7 The king was very angry and he stood up. He left his wine and he went into the palace garden. Haman realized that the king wanted to destroy him. So he stayed with Queen Esther and he asked her to save his life.
Esther7:8 Queen Esther was lying down on a bed. Haman threw himself down beside her. At that moment
Esther7:9 One of the king's eunuchs
Esther7:10 So they took Haman and they hanged him on the tower until he was dead. It was the same tower that Haman had built to kill Mordecai on. After that
Esther8:1 That day
Esther8:2 The king had taken back his special ring from Haman. Now he took it off his own finger and he gave it to Mordecai. And Esther gave Mordecai authority over all the things that had belonged to Haman.
Esther8:3 Then Esther went again to speak to the king. She threw herself down at his feet and she wept. She wanted him to stop Haman's evil idea to kill all the Jews.
Esther8:4 The king held out his gold sceptre towards Esther. So she stood up to speak to the king.
Esther8:5 Esther said to the king
Esther8:6 I cannot let this evil thing happen to my relatives. I have to do something to stop it.’
Esther8:7 King Xerxes replied to Queen Esther and to Mordecai
Esther8:8 Now you must use my authority to write another law. Write a law that seems right to you. Write a law that will save your people. Then mark the letters with my special ring. Any letter that has my name and the mark of my ring on it has the king's authority. Nobody can ever change it.’
Esther8:9 They called the king's secretaries to come and write the new law. They did that on the 23rd day of the third month (Sivan). Mordecai told them what to write. They sent the letters to the Jews
Esther8:10 Mordecai used King Xerxes' authority to write the letters. He made a mark on the letters with the king's special ring. Men who rode on the king's fastest horses quickly took the letters everywhere in the kingdom.
Esther8:11 The king's new law said that the Jewish people could join together to keep themselves safe. Jews who lived in every city could fight against anyone who attacked them. If an army of any kind attacked them
Esther8:12 The law said that the Jews in all the regions of the kingdom could do this on one day. That day was the 13th day of the 12th month (Adar).
Esther8:13 A copy of the law would go to every region of the kingdom
Esther8:14 The king's officers rode quickly on the royal horses to take the letters to every region. His officers also read the law aloud to the people in Susa city.
Esther8:15 Then Mordecai left the king's palace. He was wearing royal clothes that were blue and white. He had a large gold crown on his head. He wore a beautiful coat made from purple linen. The people in Susa shouted with joy because of the new law.
Esther8:16 All the Jews were very happy. Everyone respected them and gave them honour.
Esther8:17 When the new law reached every region and every city
Esther9:1 The 13th day of the 12th month (Adar) was the day when the enemies of the Jews had hoped to attack them. But now the king's new law said that the Jews could fight to keep themselves safe on that day. So the Jews won against their enemies.
Esther9:2 In all the cities in each region of the kingdom where the Jews lived
Esther9:3 All the king's officers and the rulers and leaders of each region also helped the Jews. They did that because they were afraid of Mordecai's power.
Esther9:4 He was now a very important officer in the king's palace. News about him reached all the regions as he became more and more powerful.
Esther9:5 So when the day arrived
Esther9:6 In Susa

Esther9:9 Parmashta

Esther9:13 Esther replied

Esther9:15 On the 14th day of the month called Adar
Esther9:16 In the other regions of the kingdom
Esther9:17 
Esther9:18 But the Jews in Susa did not rest until the 15th day of the month. That was because they killed their enemies on both the 13th day and the 14th day. On the 15th day
Esther9:19 That is why the Jews who live in towns and villages in the country have a holiday on the 14th day of Adar. On that day
Esther9:20 Mordecai wrote down all these events. He sent letters to all the Jews who were living in all the regions of King Xerxes' kingdom.
Esther9:21 He told them that they should have a holiday on the 14th and 15th days of Adar every year.
Esther9:22 They should do that to remember the time when they became safe from their enemies. That was the month when their trouble finished and they could be happy again. They should enjoy a feast together. They should send gifts of food to each other to show their joy. They should also give gifts to poor people.
Esther9:23 The Jews agreed to do everything that Mordecai had written in the letters. They had a festival every year to remember what had happened.
Esther9:24 They remembered what Hammedatha's son
Esther9:25 But Esther had told the king what Haman was doing. Then the king had stopped the evil thing that Haman wanted to do against the Jews. The king had written a command to say that Haman should die. The same thing that Haman wanted to do to the Jews should happen to him instead. The bodies of Haman and his sons were hung on the wooden tower that his men had built.
Esther9:26 So the Jews call the days of their festival ‘Purim’. That was the name of the dice that Haman used. They did this because of what Mordecai wrote in his letter. They wanted to remember everything that had happened to them.
Esther9:27 They made a rule for all the Jews
Esther9:28 From that time

Esther9:31 It told them how to have the days of Purim as a holiday at the right time each year. This was a rule that had the authority of the Jewish man
Esther9:32 Queen Esther's command made these rules about the Festival of Purim. The palace secretaries wrote them down in a book.

Esther10:2 Everything that King Xerxes did was written down in ‘The history book of the kings of Media and Persia’. The book tells about all the great things that King Xerxes did with his power and authority. It also tells how the king gave more authority to Mordecai

Job1:1 There was a man who lived in a place called Uz. His name was Job. He was a good and honest man. He was afraid to make God angry. He refused to do evil things.
Job1:2 Job had seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 He had 7
Job1:4 Each of Job's sons would give parties for each other in their homes. They sent messages to their three sisters to come and eat the feast with them.

Job1:18 That servant was still speaking
Job1:19 Suddenly there was a strong storm that came from the desert. The wind destroyed the house where your children were. The house fell on them and it killed them. I was the only one who escaped. I ran here to tell you what has happened.’
Job1:20 When Job heard this news he was very upset. He tore his clothes and he cut off all his hair. Then he bent down low on the ground to worship God.

Job3:1 Finally
Job3:2 This is what Job said:
Job3:3 ‘Forget the day of my birth. Forget the night when a son was born to my parents.
Job3:4 That day should be completely dark
Job3:5 That day should remain in a dark shadow. Clouds should cover it so that everywhere is black.
Job3:6 Remove that night completely! It should no longer be a date among the days of the year. Remove that day from the months of the year.
Job3:7 Do not let women become pregnant on that night. Nobody should be happy!
Job3:8 People who know how to curse days should curse that day. They should wake up Leviathan
Job3:9 The stars of early morning should be dark on that day. Do not let the sun shine at dawn
Job3:10 I curse that day because it allowed me to be born. It brought me into a life of trouble.
Job3:11 It would have been better if I had died at birth. I should have been born dead.
Job3:12 Instead
Job3:13 If I had died at birth
Job3:14 I would be resting with kings and rulers. They lived in beautiful houses which are now heaps of stones.

Job3:16 My parents should have buried me
Job3:17 After death

Job3:19 Great people and ordinary people are all there together. Slaves have no master that they must obey.

Job3:21 People like me only want to die

Job4:1 Eliphaz
Job4:2 ‘Please be patient and listen to what I say. Allow me to speak
Job4:3 Listen! You have taught many people. You have helped weak people to be strong.
Job4:4 With your words
Job4:5 But now you are the person who has trouble. You yourself are not patient. Trouble has come to you and you feel afraid.
Job4:6 But you respect God's authority
Job4:7 Think about this: People who are not guilty of sin do not suddenly die. God does not destroy people who do good things.
Job4:8 I will tell you what I have seen. Trouble comes to people who do wicked things. They receive the punishment that they deserve.
Job4:9 God destroys them with his breath. His anger is like a storm that blows them away.
Job4:10 Lions are strong animals that roar loudly. But if God breaks a lion's teeth
Job4:11 Even the strongest lion will die if it has nothing to eat. The young lions will run away from their mother to find food.
Job4:12 One night I heard a quiet voice. Someone spoke to me and told me a secret.
Job4:13 I was asleep
Job4:14 I was very afraid. Even my bones were shaking.
Job4:15 A wind was blowing over my face. The hair on my skin stood up.
Job4:16 Something stopped and it stood in front of me. I could not recognize its shape. Then I heard a quiet voice say:
Job4:17 “No human is righteous when he stands in front of God. He cannot be pure in front of his Maker.
Job4:18 God knows that even his angels make mistakes. They may do foolish things.
Job4:19 God used clay to make bodies for us who are humans. We came from the dust of the earth. We are as weak as moths.
Job4:20 We are alive in the morning
Job4:21 They no longer have their valuable things. They suddenly die and they are still not wise.” ’
Job5:1 ‘Shout for help
Job5:2 Foolish people may be angry and jealous. That is what kills them.
Job5:3 I have seen fools who seemed to be successful. But suddenly a curse destroyed their homes.
Job5:4 Their children are never truly safe. The judge says that they are guilty
Job5:5 Hungry people take their crops to eat. They even take them from among the weeds. Thirsty people take the fool's money for themselves.
Job5:6 Problems do not grow like plants in our fields. Trouble does not come up from the ground.
Job5:7 But trouble comes to everyone
Job5:8 I suggest that you ask God to help you. You should tell him about your problems.
Job5:9 He does great things that nobody can understand. He does more miracles than anyone can count.
Job5:10 He gives rain for the earth. He sends water for the fields.
Job5:11 He gives honour to humble people. When people are suffering

Job5:13 When wise people try to do clever things
Job5:14 In the middle of the day

Job6:1 Then Job replied:
Job6:2 ‘I am so upset that nobody could measure it! My problems are too many to weigh!
Job6:3 They are heavier than all the sand on the shore of the sea. That is why I did not think carefully before I spoke.
Job6:4 Almighty God has shot his arrows into me. Their poison is now deep inside me. God has sent me many troubles that make me afraid.
Job6:5 A wild donkey does not complain when it has grass to eat. An ox is quiet when it has its food.
Job6:6 You cannot enjoy food that has no salt in it. The white part of an egg is not nice to eat on its own.
Job6:7 I cannot eat that kind of food. It makes me ill.
Job6:8 I want God to give me what I ask him for. I want him to answer my prayer.
Job6:9 I would like him to destroy me. He should kill me himself.
Job6:10 Then I would have peace. I would be happy. I have received much cruel pain. But I have always obeyed the words of the holy God.
Job6:11 I am not strong enough to hope for my life to become any better. There is no reason for me to continue my life.
Job6:12 I am not as strong as stones. My body is not strong like bronze.
Job6:13 I no longer have strength to help myself. There is nowhere that I can go to get help.

Job11:7 You cannot discover everything about God. You cannot know how powerful he is.
Job11:8 His power goes higher than the sky. You cannot do anything about that. His power goes deeper than the place of death. You cannot know all about that.
Job11:9 The truth about God is wider than the whole earth. It goes far beyond the sea.
Job11:10 God may catch you and put you in prison. He may accuse you in a court. Nobody can stop him doing that.

Job12:1 Then Job replied. This is what he said:
Job12:2 ‘You people think that you know everything. You think that there will be no wisdom after you die.
Job12:3 But I understand things as well as you. You are no better than I am. Everyone knows the things that you have been saying.
Job12:4 I am a person who prayed to God and he answered me. But my friends now laugh at me. I have always lived completely in the right way. But still my friends laugh at me.
Job12:5 Some people never have any troubles. They think that people who suffer are fools. If people are in trouble
Job12:6 But robbers seem to live in peace. They cause God to be angry but they think that they are safe. They trust their own strength as their god.
Job12:7 Ask the animals and they will teach you. The birds in the sky would tell you the truth.
Job12:8 Speak to the earth and it will teach you. The fish in the sea would tell you what is right.
Job12:9 Any of them could tell you that the Lord has done this.
Job12:10 He has authority over the life of all the animals. He gives breath to all people so that they can live.
Job12:11 We use our mouths to taste food. We use our ears to test what we hear.
Job12:12 Old people are often wise. People who have lived for a long time understand things better.
Job12:13 God is wise and he is also powerful. He understands things and he gives good advice.
Job12:14 If God destroys something
Job12:15 If God stops the rain
Job12:16 God has strength and wisdom. He has power over people who tell lies
Job12:17 He causes clever people to become ashamed. He causes leaders to do foolish things.
Job12:18 He takes authority away from kings. He dresses them like slaves.
Job12:19 He causes priests to become ashamed. He removes the power of important people.
Job12:20 He stops faithful men from giving advice. He removes the wisdom of old men.
Job12:21 He causes rulers to be ashamed. He destroys the power of strong men.
Job12:22 He shows people things that have been secrets. He shines light into dark places.
Job12:23 He can make nations become great. He can also destroy them. He can make their land become bigger. He can also send their people away to other places.
Job12:24 He stops the world's leaders from understanding things properly. He sends them to travel through empty deserts.
Job12:25 They try to walk in the dark
Job13:1 I have seen all these things with my own eyes. I have heard everything that you have said. I have understood it.
Job13:2 I know as much as you do. You are no better than I am.
Job13:3 But I want to speak to the Almighty God. I need to show him that I am right.
Job13:4 What you say about me is only lies. You would be useless as doctors.
Job13:5 It would be better if you said nothing. That would be a wise thing for you to do.
Job13:6 Listen now to what I have to say. Listen carefully to the words that I speak.
Job13:7 You think that you can tell lies to speak on behalf of God! Your lies are not helping God.
Job13:8 You are trying to win the argument for God. But you are not being honest when you do that.
Job13:9 If God asks you questions
Job13:10 When you speak on God's behalf
Job13:11 His great power will frighten you. You will be very afraid of him.
Job13:12 Your wise teaching is worth no more than ashes. Your arguments are weak and useless.
Job13:13 So be quiet now and let me speak. Then we will see what happens to me.
Job13:14 I am ready for my life to be in danger. I must take that risk.
Job13:15 Even if God kills me
Job13:16 I am brave enough to stand in front of God. No wicked person would be able to do that. So it shows that I am not guilty.
Job13:17 Listen carefully to my words. Hear what I have to say.
Job13:18 I know what I want to say. I am ready to explain why I am not guilty.
Job13:19 Nobody can show that I have done wrong things. If anyone can do that
Job13:20 God
Job13:21 Please stop punishing me. And stop causing me to be so afraid of you.
Job13:22 Tell me to come and stand in front of you. Then I will answer you. Or allow me to speak first. Then you will reply to me.
Job13:23 Tell me how many wrong things I have done. Show me what my sins are.
Job13:24 Why do you hide yourself from me? Why do you think that I am your enemy?
Job13:25 I am as weak as a leaf that the wind can blow away
Job13:26 You write down things to accuse me. You punish me for the sins I did when I was young.
Job13:27 You tie my feet with chains. You watch me carefully wherever I go. You put marks on my feet.
Job13:28 So my life is slowly disappearing. It is like a coat that moths are eating.
Job14:1 Everyone that a woman gives birth to lives only for a short time. The days of his life are full of trouble.
Job14:2 He is like a flower that grows and then it soon dies. His life quickly disappears like a shadow.
Job14:3 I am only a human like that
Job14:4 People are not clean and pure. Nobody can change them to become pure.
Job14:5 You have decided how long people should live. You control the number of months that each person will live. Nobody can live any longer than that.
Job14:6 So do not watch us! Leave us alone! We must work for the time that you have decided. Please leave us to work in peace.
Job14:7 If someone cuts down a tree
Job14:8 Its roots in the ground may be old. The part of the tree that remains may be nearly dead.
Job14:9 But if only a little water comes near to it
Job14:10 But when people lose their strength and they die
Job14:11 When the water disappears from a lake or a river
Job14:12 It is the same with people when they die. They will never rise to live again. As long as the sky remains above the earth
Job14:13 Please hide me in the place where dead people go! Hide me there until you are no longer angry. Decide how long I must be there
Job14:14 If a man dies
Job14:15 You would call me and I would answer you. You would want to see me again
Job14:16 You would watch me carefully
Job14:17 You would forgive me for my sins. You would hide them away.
Job14:18 But mountains fall down and they break into pieces
Job14:19 Stones become sand when water pours over them. Floods wash away soil. In the same way
Job14:20 Finally
Job14:21 If his sons become famous
Job14:22 A dead person thinks only about himself. He feels his own pain and he is very sad.’
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz
Job15:2 ‘If you were wise
Job15:3 No wise man would argue in such a useless way. The things that you say do not mean anything.
Job15:4 You show that you do not respect God. You turn other people away from him.
Job15:5 It is your sin that causes you to speak like that. You try to say clever things that will deceive people.
Job15:6 I do not need to show that you are guilty. Your own words show that you are wrong.
Job15:7 You were not the first human to be born. You were not alive when God made the hills.
Job15:8 You do not know God's secret plans. You are not the only wise person in the world.
Job15:9 You do not know any more than we know. We understand as much as you do.
Job15:10 The old men agree with us. They are older and wiser even than your father.
Job15:11 You think that God's kind words are not important. But they should be enough for you.
Job15:12 You have allowed your thoughts to confuse you. We can see in your eyes that you are very angry.
Job15:13 Why are you so angry against God? You should not say such wicked things.
Job15:14 No one who is human can be truly pure and righteous.
Job15:15 God does not even trust his own angels. He knows that they make mistakes.
Job15:16 So he certainly will not trust people. People are wicked and cannot think true thoughts. They like to do evil things
Job15:17 So listen to me. I will explain something to you. I will tell you what I have seen.
Job15:18 These are things that wise men have taught me. They did not hide the truth. They learned these things from their ancestors.
Job15:19 They were the people who received the land. No foreigners were there to deceive them.
Job15:20 They knew this: A wicked person will have trouble all his life. Anyone who is cruel will suffer every year that he lives.
Job15:21 He will hear things that frighten him. He may think that he is safe
Job15:22 He is afraid to go out in the dark. He thinks that someone may kill him.
Job15:23 The wicked person walks about to look for food. He says
Job15:24 He is afraid and confused. Trouble is ready to attack him
Job15:25 That happens to the person who turns against God. He thinks that he is greater than Almighty God.
Job15:26 He is proud enough to attack God. He holds a big shield as he runs to attack God!
Job15:27 He eats too much and his whole body is very fat.
Job15:28 He lives in a town that is empty. No one else lives in the houses there. The buildings will soon fall down and they will become heaps of stones.
Job15:29 He will not be rich. He will lose his valuable things. Everything that he has will disappear.
Job15:30 When it becomes dark
Job15:31 He may trust in useless things to save him
Job15:32 Before he is old
Job15:33 He will be like a vine that loses its grapes. They fall off before they are ready to eat. He will be like an olive tree that loses its flowers
Job15:34 People who turn away from God are like that. They have no family to live after them. If they use the money from bribes to build their homes
Job15:35 They make plans to cause trouble
Job16:1 Then Job replied. This is what he said:
Job16:2 ‘I have heard all these things before. You
Job16:3 Please stop speaking useless words. Why do you talk to me like that?
Job16:4 If you were suffering like I am
Job16:5 But
Job16:6 But now
Job16:7 God
Job16:8 You have taken hold of me. Because of my troubles
Job16:9 God attacks me and he punishes me because he is angry. He shows his anger like a wild animal. He has become my enemy.
Job16:10 People insult me and they laugh at me. They hit me on my face. They all join together to attack me.
Job16:11 God has allowed evil men to take hold of me. He has put me under the power of wicked men.
Job16:12 I was living in peace
Job16:13 His arrows come from all around me. They go deep into my body and my blood pours out on the ground. But God still does not feel sorry for me.
Job16:14 He attacks me again and again. He rushes to attack me like a strong fighter.
Job16:15 I have used sackcloth to make my clothes. I am no longer proud of my strength
Job16:16 I have cried so much that my face is red. There are dark circles around my eyes.
Job16:17 But I have not been cruel. And when I pray
Job16:18 The earth should not hide my blood! People should remember how I have suffered. They should not forget that I asked for justice.
Job16:19 But even now
Job16:20 My friends have turned against me. I cry with many tears as I pray to God.
Job16:21 I need someone to speak to God on my behalf. He should ask God not to punish me
Job16:22 I only have a few more years to live. I will die and I will never again return to this earth.

Job18:1 Then Bildad
Job18:2 ‘You should not continue to talk like that! Stop and think carefully. Then we can talk.
Job18:3 You should not think that we are as foolish as cows.
Job18:4 You are so angry that you are hurting yourself. You will never change the way that things happen in the world. Your anger will not cause the earth to shake
Job18:5 The light of a wicked person's life will stop shining. His fire will no longer burn brightly.
Job18:6 His tent will be dark. The lamp that gives him light will stop burning.
Job18:7 Once he was strong but now his legs are weak. His own ideas cause him to fall down.
Job18:8 He does not know where he is going and he walks into a dangerous trap.
Job18:9 A trap catches his foot and he cannot move.
Job18:10 Someone has hidden a rope on the ground. It catches him as he walks along.
Job18:11 Everywhere a wicked person goes
Job18:12 Now he is hungry and his strength has disappeared. Terrible trouble is ready to take hold of him whenever it has the chance.
Job18:13 A bad disease eats his skin. It causes his arms and his legs to become useless and he dies.
Job18:14 Then he has to leave his tent where he had lived safely. He will have to meet the terrible king of death.
Job18:15 Other people will live in his tent. They have used sulphur to burn all his things.
Job18:16 He is like a tree whose roots have become dry
Job18:17 Nobody remembers that he ever lived. They have forgotten his name.
Job18:18 The wicked person has had to leave this world. He has gone from a place where there is light to a place where it is dark.
Job18:19 He has no children or grandchildren. He has no family to live in his home.
Job18:20 All people are very upset when they see what has happened to him. Wherever they live
Job18:21 I know that this is what happens to the homes of evil people. It happens to people who turn away from God.’

Job19:2 ‘Please stop speaking to me like that! Your words continue to make me suffer.
Job19:3 You have insulted me many
Job19:4 I might have done things that are wrong. But even if that might be true
Job19:5 You think that you are better people than I am. Because I have all these troubles
Job19:6 But you should realize that God has caused all my troubles. He is the one who has caught me in his trap.
Job19:7 If I shout
Job19:8 God has stopped me so that I cannot move forward. He has made my path dark so that I cannot see the way to go.
Job19:9 He has taken away my honour so that nobody respects me.
Job19:10 Everywhere that I go
Job19:11 He is very angry against me. He attacks me as if I am his enemy.
Job19:12 His whole army is coming to attack me! They build a road so that they can reach me. They are all around me.
Job19:13 God has caused my brothers to stay away from me. My friends do not want to meet me.
Job19:14 My relatives have left me. My best friends have forgotten me.
Job19:15 Visitors who stayed in my house now think that I am a stranger. Even my servants think that I am a foreigner.
Job19:16 I tell my servant to come to me
Job19:17 My wife moves away from the smell of my breath. My brothers stay away from me.
Job19:18 Even young children insult me. When I stand up
Job19:19 My best friends hate me. People that I love have turned against me.
Job19:20 My body is now only skin and bones. I still breathe but I am only just alive.
Job19:21 Please be kind to me
Job19:22 Do not punish me as God is punishing me. You have already caused me to suffer enough.

Job19:25 As for me
Job19:26 Illness may completely destroy my skin. But after that happens
Job19:27 I will see him for myself

Job20:1 Then Zophar replied to Job. This is what he said:
Job20:2 ‘I am very upset by the things that you have said. So I must say more to answer you.
Job20:3 You have said things that insult me. But now I know how to reply to you.
Job20:4 I am sure that you know this fact. It has been true from long ago
Job20:5 You know that a wicked person is only happy for a short time. His joy continues only for a moment.
Job20:6 He may think that he is more important than other people. He may be proud of the honour that he receives.
Job20:7 But he will disappear for ever
Job20:8 He will disappear like a dream that quickly goes away. Nobody can find him any more.
Job20:9 People who knew him will never see him again. His family will no longer recognize him.
Job20:10 His children will have to give things back to poor people. Those are the valuable things that he took from them.
Job20:11 He still had the strength of a young man. But his strength will die with him when he lies in his grave.
Job20:12 He enjoys the wicked things that he does. They are like something sweet in his mouth.
Job20:13 He continues to enjoy wicked things
Job20:14 But those things will become poison that hurts him
Job20:15 Like a person who is sick and loses the food from his stomach
Job20:16 He will drink the poison of snakes. A dangerous snake will bite him and it will kill him.
Job20:17 He will never enjoy the good things that the earth provides. He will not enjoy the honey and milk which pours out like rivers of water.
Job20:18 He will have to give back everything that he owned. He will not enjoy the things that he has worked for.
Job20:19 This will happen because he has been cruel to poor people. He has refused to help them. He has taken houses for himself that do not belong to him.
Job20:20 He will always want to have more things for himself. He takes anything that he wants to have.
Job20:21 Now nothing remains for him to take. So his riches will come to an end.
Job20:22 When he has everything that he wants
Job20:23 When he is filling himself with good food
Job20:24 He may escape from a soldier who has an iron sword. But then a soldier will shoot a bronze arrow into him.
Job20:25 He pulls the arrow out of his back. His blood makes its sharp point shine brightly. He is very afraid.
Job20:26 All his riches disappear in the dark. God will send a fire to destroy him. It will destroy all his things that remain.
Job20:27 The heavens will show that he is guilty. People on earth will speak against him.
Job20:28 A flood will destroy his house and everything that he has. That will happen on the day when God punishes people because he is angry with them.
Job20:29 That is how God punishes wicked people. God has decided to punish them as they deserve.’
Job21:1 Then Job replied. This is what he said:
Job21:2 ‘Listen carefully to the things that I am saying. You can only help me if you do that.
Job21:3 Be patient with me so that I can speak. After you have heard what I say
Job21:4 It is not people that I want to argue with. So I have good reason to be upset.
Job21:5 When you look at me
Job21:6 When I think about what has happened to me
Job21:7 Why do wicked people continue to live for a long time? The older they become
Job21:8 They see their own children grow and become strong. Their children also have strong families.
Job21:9 They are safe in their homes and they are not afraid. God does not punish them.
Job21:10 Their cows give birth safely to many calves.
Job21:11 Their children run and play. They jump like young lambs.
Job21:12 They enjoy the music of tambourines and harps. They sing with joy to the sound of flutes.
Job21:13 Wicked people have plenty of money through their whole lives. Even when they die
Job21:14 They say to God
Job21:15 We do not need to serve the Almighty God. If we pray to him
Job21:16 They think that they have made themselves rich. But it is not true. So I do not listen to the things that wicked people say.
Job21:17 It seems that wicked people enjoy a long life. They do not often have many troubles. When God is angry with them
Job21:18 He does not blow them away
Job21:19 You may say
Job21:20 Wicked people should receive their own punishment. They should see that Almighty God is angry with them.
Job21:21 After wicked people have died
Job21:22 No one can teach God what he should do. He himself judges the most powerful rulers.
Job21:23 One person has good health all his life. Even when he is ready to die
Job21:24 He eats good food and his body is strong.
Job21:25 But another person is sad all his life
Job21:26 But they all die one day and their bodies lie under the ground. Worms cover their bodies
Job21:27 I know what you are thinking. You want to show that I am guilty.
Job21:28 You say
Job21:29 But you should ask people who travel to different places. You should listen to the things that they say.
Job21:30 They would tell you that wicked people do not always suffer. When trouble comes
Job21:31 No one speaks against them to say that they are guilty. No one punishes them for the bad things that they have done.
Job21:32 When a wicked person dies
Job21:33 A big crowd of people come to his funeral. They make a heap of earth over his body
Job21:34 So your useless words do not comfort me. Your answers are all lies!’
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz
Job22:2 ‘Nothing that a man can do will help God. Even a wise man cannot help God.
Job22:3 If you are a righteous person
Job22:4 It is not because you respect and obey God that he punishes you. It is not because you are a good person that he accuses you.
Job22:5 No! It is because you are a very wicked person. You have done too many evil things for anyone to count them. That is why he is punishing you.
Job22:6 You took things from your friends when you lent money to them. You had no reason to do that. You even took away the only clothes that poor people were wearing.
Job22:7 When you met people who were weak and tired
Job22:8 You were a powerful man
Job22:9 But you refused to help widows. You were cruel to children who have no family.
Job22:10 That is why you have so many troubles. That is why you shake with fear.
Job22:11 It seems to you that you are in the dark and you cannot see. You feel as if a flood of deep water covers you.
Job22:12 God lives high above us in heaven. Look up at the stars and see how high they are!
Job22:13 So you say

Job23:1 Then Job replied. This is what he said:
Job23:2 ‘I continue to complain against God because I am still angry today. God continues to punish me even when I cry in pain.

Job23:4 I would tell him why I am not guilty. I would say many things to explain this to him.
Job23:5 Then I would know how he would answer me. I would understand what he would say to me.
Job23:6 Would God use his great power to argue against me? No! He would listen carefully to me.
Job23:7 Honest people can explain things to him. So he would judge me in a fair way. He would let me go free.

Job23:9 When he does his work in the north

Job24:1 Why has Almighty God not yet decided the time when he will judge wicked people? People who serve him faithfully continue to wait for justice.
Job24:2 Wicked men move the stones that show the edge of their neighbour's land. They take their neighbour's land and his sheep for themselves.
Job24:3 They take away a donkey that belongs to a child who has no family. They take an ox away from a widow
Job24:4 They stop poor people from receiving justice. Poor people have to run away from wicked people and hide.
Job24:5 Poor people have to look for food to eat
Job24:6 They find grain in the fields of other people. They pick grapes that remain in the vineyards of wicked people.
Job24:7 They have no clothes to wear during the cold nights. They sleep without anything to cover themselves.
Job24:8 The rain that falls in the mountains makes them very wet. So they hide among the rocks where they try to keep dry.
Job24:9 When wicked people lend money to poor people
Job24:10 Poor people have to go out with no clothes to wear. They are hungry while they carry the crops of other people.
Job24:11 They squeeze oil from olives that grow in the fields of other people. They also make wine from other people's grapes. But the poor people continue to be thirsty.
Job24:12 In the cities
Job24:13 Some people refuse to live in the light. They do not understand it. They do not go along the good way that it shows to them.
Job24:14 Murderers rob people when it is still dark. They kill poor
Job24:15 Adulterers wait until it is becoming dark. They think that no one will see them. They cover their faces so that no one will recognize them.
Job24:16 Robbers go into people's homes in the dark to take away their things. But they stay inside during the day. They never go out in the light.
Job24:17 They all like to do evil things at night
Job24:18 You may say
Job24:19 Snow soon disappears when the weather is very hot or very dry. In the same way
Job24:20 Their mothers soon forget them. Worms eat their bodies. No one remembers those wicked people. They are like dead trees that people have cut down.
Job24:21 Those wicked people are cruel to women who have no children. They are not kind to widows.
Job24:22 But God uses his strength to remove powerful people. When he attacks wicked people
Job24:23 God allows them to feel safe. But he is always watching everything that they do.
Job24:24 Wicked people may have success for a short time
Job24:25 What I have said is true. Nobody can say that I am telling lies. You cannot think that my words are useless.’

Job25:4 No human is righteous when he stands in front of God. He cannot be pure.

Job26:6 God sees everything that happens in the place of dead people. Nothing can hide that terrible place from him.
Job26:7 He puts the skies in their place in the north
Job26:8 He fills the clouds with water. They become heavy but they do not break.
Job26:9 When the moon is big and round
Job26:10 He marks the border between the sea and the sky. It is the place where the night finishes and the day begins.

Job26:12 He used his power to control the sea. He used his wisdom to destroy Rahab
Job26:13 He used his breath to make the sky become bright. With his hand he cut the great sea snake as it ran away.
Job26:14 These are only a few of the things that God does. We only know about a few small things. If he used all his great power
Job27:1 Job continued to speak. This is what he said:
Job27:2 ‘ Almighty God has not been fair to me. He has caused my life to be very sad. As surely as he lives
Job27:3 As long as God's Spirit gives me life
Job27:4 I will never speak any lies. I will always be honest.
Job27:5 I will never agree that you
Job27:6 I will never agree that I am guilty. During all of my life
Job27:7 I pray that God will punish my enemies as he punishes all wicked people. They do bad things to me
Job27:8 When God chooses to remove wicked people
Job27:9 When they are in trouble and they pray for help
Job27:10 People like that are not happy that Almighty God is their friend. They do not like to pray to him all the time.
Job27:11 I will teach you about the power of Almighty God. I will explain to you what he is thinking.
Job27:12 You yourselves have all seen what God has done. So you should stop saying all these useless things!
Job27:13 I will tell you what Almighty God does to wicked people. He punishes cruel people as they deserve.
Job27:14 Wicked people may have many children
Job27:15 Disease will kill any children who continue to live. When wicked men die
Job27:16 Wicked people may have lots of money. They may have silver like heaps of sand. They may have more clothes than they could ever wear.
Job27:17 But one day righteous people will wear those clothes. Honest people will share the silver among themselves.
Job27:18 The houses that wicked people build are not strong. They are as weak as a spider's web
Job27:19 Their riches quickly disappear. They are rich when they go to sleep
Job27:20 Troubles come to them like a flood of water
Job27:21 The east wind picks them up and takes them far away. It removes them from their homes.
Job27:22 It is a strong wind that hits wicked people and it never stops. It continues to beat them as they try to escape.
Job27:23 The cruel wind laughs at them as they run away in fear.’
Job28:1 ‘If people want to find silver
Job28:2 They know how to take iron from under the ground. They can also use heat to get copper from rocks.
Job28:3 People take light into dark places under the ground. They look carefully for valuable metals.
Job28:4 They dig deep holes in the ground far away from places where people live. Other people do not know about those places. People hang on ropes as they go deep down into the earth.
Job28:5 We grow our food on the earth above. But deep under the ground
Job28:6 Sapphires come from the rocks in those places. The dust there contains gold.
Job28:7 Even eagles or other birds with good eyes do not see those places.
Job28:8 Lions and other wild animals do not walk there.
Job28:9 People break into the hard rocks. They turn over rocks under the mountains.
Job28:10 They cut holes through the rocks. They discover stones that are very valuable.
Job28:11 They look carefully in the springs of water where rivers start. They bring into the light things that were hidden away.
Job28:12 But as for wisdom
Job28:13 Humans do not understand the value of wisdom. They cannot find it in this world where people live.
Job28:14 Wisdom is not in the sea. Even in the deepest sea
Job28:15 Nobody can buy wisdom. You may have lots of gold and silver
Job28:16 All the pure gold from Ophir will not be enough to buy it. It is worth more than valuable onyx or sapphires.
Job28:17 Neither gold nor beautiful glass is as valuable as wisdom. A whole pot full of gold could not buy it.
Job28:18 As for coral and jasper
Job28:19 Topaz from Ethiopia is not as valuable as wisdom. No one can buy it
Job28:20 So where can we find wisdom? Where can we learn to understand things properly?
Job28:21 We cannot see where it is. No person or animal can see it. No bird that flies in the sky can see it.
Job28:22 In the place of death
Job28:23 Only God knows the way to find wisdom. Only he knows where it is.
Job28:24 He can see to the ends of the earth. He knows what happens everywhere in the world.
Job28:25 He decided how strong the wind must be. He decided the size of the seas.
Job28:26 He made a command for the rain to decide where it should fall. He chose where storms should go and make thunder.
Job28:27 Then he thought about wisdom and its value. He prepared it and he tested it.
Job28:28 God said this to us humans: “If you want to be wise
Job29:1 Job continued to speak. This is what he said:
Job29:2 ‘I would like my life to be as it was before
Job29:3 He was my guide
Job29:4 I was strong and well. God was my friend and he protected my home.
Job29:5 At that time
Job29:6 I got plenty of milk from my cows
Job29:7 I would go to the gate of the city
Job29:8 When young men saw me
Job29:9 The city's rulers would stop speaking. They would cover their mouths with their hands.
Job29:10 The important leaders would be quiet. They would not want to say anything.
Job29:11 Everyone was very pleased to listen to the things that I said. They respected me when they saw the things that I did.
Job29:12 When poor people cried for help
Job29:13 People who were near to death praised me for my help. Widows were happy when I helped them.
Job29:14 I always did what was right
Job29:15 If people were blind
Job29:16 If poor people needed help
Job29:17 I destroyed the power of wicked people. I rescued the people that they had attacked.
Job29:18 At that time
Job29:19 My body is strong and well
Job29:20 People will continue to give me honour. Each day I will have new strength.”
Job29:21 People waited patiently to hear my advice. They listened carefully to what I said.
Job29:22 After I had spoken
Job29:23 They would wait for me to speak to them
Job29:24 They were surprised when I smiled at them. My smile made them happy.
Job29:25 As their leader
Job30:1 ‘But now it is different. Men who are younger than I am laugh at me. I would not even trust their fathers to take care of my sheep.
Job30:2 The work that those old men could do was no help to me. They no longer were strong.
Job30:3 They were weak and thin because they were hungry. They had to look for food in the desert at night. They found roots to eat in dry places.
Job30:4 They pulled up plants in the desert as their food. They burned the roots of bushes to keep themselves warm.
Job30:5 People chased them away from their towns
Job30:6 They had to live in dry valleys
Job30:7 They cried aloud like animals among the bushes. They hid together under the thorn bushes.
Job30:8 They were foolish people that nobody respected. People chased them away from their land.
Job30:9 But now their sons sing songs that insult me. They tell jokes about me.
Job30:10 They hate me and they do not come near me. When they see me
Job30:11 God has made me weak and helpless. People do to me anything that they want to do.
Job30:12 From one side
Job30:13 They stop me from escaping. They are ready to destroy me. They do not need anyone to help them.
Job30:14 I cannot stop them when they attack me. They rush forward to knock me down.
Job30:15 So I am always afraid. My honour has disappeared as if a strong wind has blown it away. My riches have also gone
Job30:16 Now I know that my life will soon end. Every day I continue to suffer.
Job30:17 At night
Job30:18 God has used his power to take hold of me. He has held my shirt around my neck.
Job30:19 He has thrown me down into the mud. I am no better than dust and ashes on the ground.
Job30:20 I call aloud to you
Job30:21 You have become cruel to me. You have used your strength to attack me.
Job30:22 You take hold of me and you carry me away
Job30:23 I know that you are already taking me to my death. Everyone who lives has to go to that place.
Job30:24 When someone is weak and in trouble
Job30:25 When other people were in trouble
Job30:26 But when I hoped for good things to happen
Job30:27 I hurt inside me all the time. Every day pain causes me to suffer.
Job30:28 Illness has caused my skin to become black. It is not the sun that has made that happen. I stand up in the public meeting place and I call aloud for help.
Job30:29 I have become like the jackals and the ostriches. My sad voice sounds like them.
Job30:30 My skin has become black
Job30:31 Now
Job31:1 ‘I have made a promise with myself about how I look at young women. I will never think that I want to have sex with them.
Job31:2 Anyone who does that should expect God to punish them. Almighty God in heaven above punishes people who do wrong things.
Job31:3 He sends trouble to people who do wicked things. He punishes those people who do evil things.
Job31:4 God sees all the things that I do. He carefully watches how I live my life.
Job31:5 He must know that I am an honest man. I have never tried to deceive people.
Job31:6 So he should judge me in a fair way. He should realize that I am not guilty.
Job31:7 God seems to think that I have not lived in the right way. He may think that I have wanted bad things when they looked nice to me. Or perhaps I have done something else that was wrong.
Job31:8 If I am really guilty of these things
Job31:9 I have not wanted to love any woman except my wife. I have not hidden outside my friend's house to wait for his wife to be alone.
Job31:10 If I had done those things
Job31:11 I would not have been faithful to my wife. I would have done a wicked thing and I would deserve punishment.
Job31:12 If I loved another woman
Job31:13 When my servants complain against me
Job31:14 If I had been cruel to them
Job31:15 God made me and he made my servants too. God brought us all to birth.
Job31:16 I have never refused to help people who are poor. I have always helped widows to hope for good things.
Job31:17 I have never kept all my food for myself. I have always shared it with children who have no family.
Job31:18 Since I was a young man
Job31:19 I have seen people without enough clothes to keep them warm
Job31:20 Then I would give them clothes. The wool from my sheep would keep them warm. And they all thanked me.
Job31:21 I have not turned against children who have no family
Job31:22 God should punish me if these things are not true. I would accept the punishment that I deserve.
Job31:23 I was afraid of the trouble that God might bring to me. So I could not do these bad things
Job31:24 I have not trusted in money or in gold. I have never thought that they would make me strong.
Job31:25 When I was very rich
Job31:26 I have looked at the sun when it shines brightly. And I have seen the moon when it is very beautiful.
Job31:27 But I have never wanted to worship them or to give them honour.
Job31:28 If I had done that
Job31:29 I do not laugh at my enemies when they suffer. If my enemy has troubles
Job31:30 I have not cursed my enemies
Job31:31 None of the people who work in my house have ever said
Job31:32 Travellers may come to sleep in my house. They do not have to sleep outside in the street.
Job31:33 I do not try to hide my sins
Job31:34 I am not afraid of what people think about me. Even if a great crowd of people speak against me
Job31:35 I want someone to listen to me! All the things that I say are true. Almighty God should give me an answer! I need to know what I have done that is wrong. He should write down the reason why I am guilty!
Job31:36 Then I would wear that paper clearly on my shoulders. I would be proud to wear it like a crown on my head.
Job31:37 I would explain to God everything that I have done. I would stand in front of him without fear
Job31:38 I have not grown crops on land that does not belong to me. The land itself would accuse me if that were true.
Job31:39 I have always paid for the food that other people have worked to grow. I have never cheated the farmers so that they suffered.
Job31:40 If I have ever done any of those things
Job32:1 Job would not agree that he had done anything that was wrong. So his three friends refused to argue with him any more.
Job32:2 Another man was also there. His name was Elihu
Job32:3 Elihu was also angry with Job's three friends. They had not been able to find an answer to Job's problem. But they still said that Job was guilty.
Job32:4 Elihu had waited for Job's three friends to speak before he spoke to Job himself. He waited because he was younger than them.
Job32:5 But he became angry when Job's three friends had nothing more to say. So he finally decided to speak.
Job32:6 Barakel's son
Job32:7 I thought
Job32:8 But it is a person's spirit that helps them to understand things. Almighty God has to put his breath into them.
Job32:9 Not all old people are wise. Old people do not always understand what is right.
Job32:10 So I say
Job32:11 Yes
Job32:12 I listened carefully to you
Job32:13 So do not say
Job32:14 Job was not speaking to me about his problems. So I will not answer him in the way that you did.

Job32:16 I have waited for them
Job32:17 So now I will say what I think. I
Job32:18 There are many things that I want to say. The spirit inside me causes me to speak.
Job32:19 I cannot keep quiet any longer! I am like a bag that is full of new wine. I am ready to break so that everything pours out!
Job32:20 So I have to speak or I will become ill. I must open my mouth and reply to you.
Job32:21 I will be fair in what I say. I will not give honour to anyone to get their help.
Job32:22 I am too honest to do that. If I am not fair

Job33:2 I will speak to you now. I am ready to tell you what I think.
Job33:3 I am an honest man
Job33:4 The Spirit of Almighty God has made me. His breath gives me life.

Job33:6 Listen! When God looks at us
Job33:7 So you should not be afraid of me. I will not do anything to hurt you.
Job33:8 I have heard the things that you have said
Job33:9 “I am not guilty. I have not done anything that is wrong. I am pure and clean.
Job33:10 But God has decided to punish me! He attacks me as if I am his enemy.
Job33:11 He has tied my feet with chains. He always watches me to see where I go.”
Job33:12 But now I tell you this
Job33:13 So you should not argue against him. You should not say
Job33:14 God does speak to people. He speaks to them in many different ways. But often people do not recognize his voice.
Job33:15 Sometimes God speaks to people in dreams and visions. He speaks to them at night
Job33:16 He tells them about things that will happen. He warns them about terrible things
Job33:17 He tells them to turn away from their sins. He tells them not to be proud.
Job33:18 He keeps people safe from death
Job33:19 Or God may warn people with a painful illness. He may make their bodies hurt all the time.
Job33:20 Then they do not want to eat anything. They cannot eat even the best food.
Job33:21 Their bodies become very thin. People can even see their bones.
Job33:22 They will soon die and go to their graves. Death is waiting to take them away.
Job33:23 But perhaps an angel may come to help one of those people. He may speak on behalf of God
Job33:24 Then God will be kind to that person. God will say
Job33:25 Then that person's body will become healthy again. He will be strong
Job33:26 When he prays to God
Job33:27 That person will say clearly to everyone
Job33:28 God rescued me from death
Job33:29 God does all these things for people. He does them many times.
Job33:30 He saves a person from death

Job33:32 After that

Job34:3 You recognize nice food when you eat it. So you should recognize the value of my words.

Job34:5 Job has said
Job34:6 I am right
Job34:7 There has never been a man like Job! He laughs at God as easily as he drinks water.
Job34:8 He is a friend of wicked people
Job34:9 Job says
Job34:10 So listen to me
Job34:11 He judges people fairly as he sees the things that they do. He gives people what they deserve.
Job34:12 It is true! Almighty God does not do wicked things. He never refuses to judge people in a fair way.
Job34:13 God's great power did not come from anyone else! He himself has authority over the whole world.
Job34:14 God could decide to remove life from the world. He could take back his spirit and his breath.
Job34:15 If he did that
Job34:16 If you really understand things

Job34:20 Anyone may die suddenly
Job34:21 He watches the way that each person lives. He sees what they do every day.
Job34:22 People who do wicked things cannot hide themselves. There is no place dark enough for them to hide from God.
Job34:23 God does not have to tell people when they must come to him
Job34:24 He destroys powerful people
Job34:25 He already knows the things that they have done. In the night
Job34:26 He punishes them because they have done wicked things. He does that where everyone can see.

Job34:28 They were cruel to poor people
Job34:29 But if God decides to do nothing
