Job36:1 Elihu continued to speak to Job. This is what he said:
Job36:2 ‘Be patient a little bit longer
Job36:3 I will tell you the many different things that I know. I will show you that God
Job36:4 Everything that I say to you is true. I am speaking to you as someone who knows everything.
Job36:5 God is very powerful but he does not turn anyone away. When he decides to do something
Job36:6 He does not allow wicked people to continue to live. But he is always fair to those who are poor and weak.
Job36:7 He takes care of righteous people. He gives them authority to rule like kings. He gives them honour for ever.
Job36:8 People who do wrong things may suffer in prison. Chains may hold their legs so that they cannot move.
Job36:9 When that happens
Job36:10 He tells them this to warn them. They must turn away from wicked things.
Job36:11 They must obey him and agree to serve him. If they do that
Job36:12 But if they refuse to listen to God
Job36:13 People who turn away from God are always angry. Even when God punishes them
Job36:14 They die while they are still young. Their lives finish in shame
Job36:15 But God rescues people who suffer. He uses their troubles to teach them about himself.
Job36:16 God has called you out of your troubles
Job36:17 But now you have a lot of trouble
Job36:18 Be very careful! Do not let anyone use their riches to deceive you. Do not let them pay you a lot of money to do something that is wrong.
Job36:19 Even if you become rich
Job36:20 Do not wait for the night to hide what you want to do. That is the time when people disappear from their homes.
Job36:21 Be careful! Do not try to do wicked things. God has sent these troubles to test you. He wants to stop you doing evil things.
Job36:22 Yes
Job36:23 Nobody can tell him what he should do. No one can say to him
Job36:24 People sing songs to praise God for the things that he has done. You also should remember to praise him.
Job36:25 People everywhere have seen the things that God has done. But we look at them from far away.
Job36:26 Yes
Job36:27 It is God who pulls up water from the earth to make the clouds. Then he causes it to become rain.
Job36:28 The rain pours down from the clouds. There is plenty for the people on the earth.
Job36:29 Who understands how the clouds move across the sky? How does the noise of thunder come from God's home?
Job36:30 Look at the lightning that God sends all over the sky! But he causes the bottom of the sea to remain dark.
Job36:31 This is the way that God rules the people of the world. He sends the rain to give them plenty of food.
Job36:32 He sends his lightning like an arrow. He commands it to hit the right place.
Job36:33 He sends thunder to show that a storm is coming. Even the cows know what is happening.’
Job37:1 ‘When I hear the noise of a storm like that
Job37:2 Listen carefully to the sound of God's voice
Job37:3 He sends lightning across the whole sky. Its light reaches everywhere on the earth.
Job37:4 After the lightning comes the sound of his voice. God roars like the great sound of thunder. When he speaks like that
Job37:5 When God's voice speaks
Job37:6 He tells the snow to fall to the earth. He tells the rain to pour down and make the ground wet.
Job37:7 He does that to show people what he can do. He causes everyone to stop their own work.
Job37:8 The wild animals have to hide in a safe place. They have to stay in their homes.
Job37:9 The strong wind of a storm comes from the place where it starts. Cold winds blow strongly from the north.
Job37:10 The cold wind is like God's breath. He causes ice to cover the rivers and the lakes.
Job37:11 He fills the clouds with lots of water. He sends the lightning through the clouds.
Job37:12 The clouds move around across the whole earth. They go wherever God sends them. He tells them what they must do.
Job37:13 He may send a storm as punishment for his land. Or he may send it to show his love for his people.
Job37:14 Listen carefully to what I am telling you
Job37:15 Do you know how God commands the storms? Do you know how he sends the bright lightning from the clouds?
Job37:16 Can you explain how the clouds hang in the sky? God knows everything perfectly and he does these wonderful things.
Job37:17 When the hot wind blows from the south
Job37:18 God puts the clouds across the whole sky
Job37:19 Tell us
Job37:20 Should I tell God that I want to speak to him? That would be the end of me!
Job37:21 Now the wind has blown all the clouds away. The sun shines brightly in the sky. It is too bright for us to look at.
Job37:22 God comes with his bright glory from the north! His glory is all around him. It makes us afraid.
Job37:23 Almighty God is so great that we cannot come near to him. He is very powerful. But he always does what is right and fair. He is not cruel to people.
Job37:24 So people respect God with fear. He does not listen to those who think that they are wise.’
Job38:1 The Lord spoke to Job from the middle of the storm. This is what he said:
Job38:2 ‘Who do you think you are? Why do you ask questions about my wisdom? You speak many words
Job38:3 Prepare yourself for a difficult job! I will ask you some questions and you must answer them.
Job38:4 Were you with me when I made the world? If you think you are so clever
Job38:5 It was me who decided the size of the world. You surely must know that! I measured it as I made it.
Job38:6 Tell me how I fixed the foundations of the earth. Who made it stay in its right place? I did!
Job38:7 At the dawn of that day
Job38:8 When the sea poured out of the ground
Job38:9 I caused the clouds to come over the sea. I covered it with complete darkness.
Job38:10 I put a border around the sea. I locked it in its place.
Job38:11 I said to the sea
Job38:12 But you
Job38:13 You have not told the light of day to cover the earth. You have not told it to chase away the wicked things that happen in the night.
Job38:14 When the light of day comes
Job38:15 The light of day is too bright for wicked people. It destroys their power to do cruel things.
Job38:16 You have not gone to the springs that fill the seas with water. You have not walked on the bottom of the deep seas.
Job38:17 Nobody has shown you the gates of death. People go through them to that very dark place.
Job38:18 You do not understand the great size of the earth. If you know all about that
Job38:19 You do not know where light comes from. You do not know where darkness has its home.
Job38:20 Can you take them to the places where they belong? Do you know the roads that go there?
Job38:21 Surely you must know all this
Job38:22 Have you visited the place where I store all the snow? Or the place where I keep the hail?
Job38:23 I keep the snow and the hail ready to use. I use them in times of trouble and wars.
Job38:24 You do not know where lightning comes from. Or where the east wind starts when it blows across the earth.
Job38:25 I make a path for the storms to travel along. I tell the thunder and the strong rain which way to go.
Job38:26 I cause rain to fall in places where no person lives. It makes even a desert become a wet place.
Job38:27 The rain falls on dry ground where nothing grows. It causes grass to grow there again.
Job38:28 Do you know if the rain has a father? Who is a father to the dew?
Job38:29 Who is a mother to the ice? Who gives birth to the frost that makes the air so cold?
Job38:30 It causes the water to become as hard as stone. The water of the sea also becomes ice.
Job38:31 You cannot make the stars of the Pleiades stay in their places. You cannot change the shape of Orion.
Job38:32 You cannot bring out the groups of stars for people to see at different times of the year. You cannot put the stars of the Great Bear and the Little Bear in their right places in the sky.
Job38:33 You do not know the rules that the stars above obey. You cannot make the earth obey those rules.
Job38:34 You cannot shout commands to the clouds. You cannot tell them to pour down their rain
Job38:35 You cannot send the lightning to hit the place where you want it to go. The lightning does not say to you
Job38:36 Who has given people wisdom
Job38:37 Is anyone wise enough to count the clouds? Can anyone cause the clouds to pour down rain from above?
Job38:38 The rain causes the dust of the dry ground to become hard. The dry soil becomes pieces of mud.
Job38:39 When the lions become hungry
Job38:40 You cannot do that for them when they sit in their caves. You cannot help them as they hide in the bushes
Job38:41 You cannot find food for the ravens to eat. You cannot help them when their babies cry to me because they are weak and hungry.’
Job39:1 ‘Job
Job39:2 Can you count the number of months that these animals are pregnant? How long must they wait until they give birth?
Job39:3 They bend down low to the ground. They give birth to the babies that they have carried inside them.
Job39:4 The young animals grow and they become strong in the fields. Then they leave their parents and they do not return to them.
Job39:5 Did you send out the wild donkeys to go wherever they want?
Job39:6 No! It was me who gave them the desert as their home. I let them live in places where the ground has salt.
Job39:7 They stay far away from the busy cities. They do not allow anyone to make them work.
Job39:8 Instead
Job39:9 Can you tell a wild ox to work for you? No! At night
Job39:10 It will not let you tie it to a plough. It will not agree to prepare your fields in the valleys.
Job39:11 A wild ox is very strong. But you cannot trust it to help you with your difficult work.
Job39:12 It will not help you to bring in your harvest of grain. It will not take the grain to your threshing floor.
Job39:13 When an ostrich is happy
Job39:14 A mother ostrich leaves her eggs on the ground. She lets the sand cause them to be warm.
Job39:15 She does not realize that people or wild animals might break the eggs with their feet.
Job39:16 She does not take care of her babies
Job39:17 This is because I did not give wisdom to ostriches. I did not give them minds that understand things.
Job39:18 But when an ostrich begins to run
Job39:19 Did you
Job39:20 You did not make horses able to jump like locusts. They frighten people when they blow air out through their noses.
Job39:21 They stamp their feet on the ground
Job39:22 A horse is brave and it is not afraid of anything. It does not run away from the enemy's weapons.
Job39:23 The soldier who is riding it has his arrows ready at the horse's side. Swords and spears shine brightly in the sun.
Job39:24 The horse shakes with joy as it runs to the battle. When the battle trumpet makes its noise
Job39:25 When it hears the sound of the trumpet
Job39:26 Was it your wisdom
Job39:27 Do eagles wait for your command to fly high into the sky? No! You could not teach them how to build their nests high up in the mountains.
Job39:28 They live among the highest rocks. That is where they stay at night. They are safe on the sharp rocks.
Job39:29 From the high rocks
Job39:30 They come together around the bodies of dead animals. The young eagles drink the blood.’
Job40:1 The Lord said to Job
Job40:2 ‘Do you still want to argue with me
Job40:3 Then Job answered the Lord. This is what he said:
Job40:4 ‘I am only a foolish man. I cannot answer your questions. I cover my mouth with my hand so that I say nothing.
Job40:5 I have already spoken too many times. I will say no more.’
Job40:6 Then the Lord spoke to Job from inside the storm. This is what he said:
Job40:7 ‘Prepare yourself for a difficult job! I will ask you some more questions and you must answer them.
Job40:8 Do you say that I am not a fair judge? Do you want to show that I am guilty
Job40:9 Do you have the same power to do great things as I have? Can you speak with a voice as loud as thunder? I can!
Job40:10 If you think that you can do all this
Job40:11 Show everyone your great anger! Look at all the people who like to boast and bring them down low!
Job40:12 Yes
Job40:13 Bury them all together in the dust of the ground. Cover their faces and put them in their graves.
Job40:14 If you can do these things
Job40:15 Listen! I created the monsters called Behemoth. I made you and I made them too. They eat grass
Job40:16 But look at the strength of their legs and their body!
Job40:17 Their tails are like the branches of a cedar tree. Their legs are thick and very strong.
Job40:18 Their bones are like sticks made of bronze. Their legs are as strong as iron.
Job40:19 These monsters are some of the greatest animals that I have made. But I who made them can take my sword and go near to them.
Job40:20 They eat the grass that grows on the hills. That is where the wild animals play.
Job40:21 They lie down under the thorn trees. They hide among the reeds at the edge of the water.
Job40:22 They rest in the shadow of the thorn trees
Job40:23 Even when the river is deep and dangerous
Job40:24 There is no way that anyone can catch one of them. No one can make its eyes blind. No one can put a ring in its nose and lead it away.’
Job41:1 ‘It is the same with the sea monsters called Leviathan. Can you catch them with a hook? Can you use a rope to keep their mouths shut?
Job41:2 Can you tie a rope through their noses? Can you put a hook into their mouths?
Job41:3 No! They will not ask you nicely to be kind and let them go free.
Job41:4 They will not make any agreement with you. You cannot make them your slaves to work for you as long as they live.
Job41:5 Leviathan is not something to enjoy at home
Job41:6 Traders do not buy it at the market. They do not cut it up to sell it.
Job41:7 Job
Job41:8 If you try to take hold of it
Job41:9 If anyone hopes to catch it
Job41:10 No one is brave enough to wake it. So is anyone brave enough to argue against me?
Job41:11 Nobody has ever won against me to make me pay them anything. Everything in the whole world belongs to me.
Job41:12 I must tell you how strong Leviathan's legs are. Their bodies are very strong and they have a wonderful shape.
Job41:13 Nobody is able to remove their thick skin. It is like armour that nobody can cut through.
Job41:14 Nobody is strong enough to open its mouth. Its teeth make everyone afraid.
Job41:15 Its back is like rows of shields. They are as hard as stone with no spaces between them.
Job41:16 Each shield is very near the next one. Not even air can pass between them.
Job41:17 They are strongly joined together
Job41:18 When Leviathan breathes out loudly
Job41:19 Fire comes out from its mouth. Bright flames shoot out!
Job41:20 Smoke pours out from its nose
Job41:21 Its breath is hot enough to light a fire! Flames pour out of its mouth.
Job41:22 Its neck is very strong. Wherever it goes
Job41:23 There are no weak places in its skin. Its skin is as hard as iron.
Job41:24 It is cruel and it has no fear. Its heart is as hard as stone.
Job41:25 Even the bravest people are afraid when Leviathan appears. They run away when it starts to move.
Job41:26 If you hit it with a sword
Job41:27 It can easily break iron or bronze weapons. It breaks them as easily as grass or soft wood.
Job41:28 If you shoot arrows at it
Job41:29 If you hit it with a heavy stick
Job41:30 Its stomach has sharp pieces
Job41:31 When it swims in the deep water of the sea
Job41:32 As it swims
Job41:33 There is no other animal like it in the whole world. It is not afraid of anything.
Job41:34 It does not respect any other great animal. It rules as king over all the proud animals.’
Job42:1 Then Job replied to the Lord. He said this:
Job42:2 ‘I know that you can do all things. You can do anything that you want to do. Nobody can stop you.
Job42:3 You asked me
Job42:4 You said to me
Job42:5 In the past

Job42:7 When the Lord had spoken to Job
Job42:8 You three men must now go to Job. Take seven bulls and seven male sheep with you. Burn them as sacrifices for yourselves. After you have done that
Job42:9 So Eliphaz
Job42:10 After Job prayed for his three friends
Job42:11 Then Job's brothers and sisters came to eat a big meal with him in his house. People who had been Job's friends came also. They all told him that they were sad about his troubles. They were upset because the Lord had caused him to suffer. Each of them gave Job a piece of silver and a gold ring.
Job42:12 After this
Job42:13 He had seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 The daughters' names were Jemimah
Job42:15 They were the most beautiful women in the whole land of Uz. Job said that they should receive some of his things after he died
Job42:16 Job lived for 140 years after this. He was still alive when his grandchildren and their grandchildren were born.
Job42:17 So when Job died

Proverbs7:19 My husband is not at home. He has gone away on a long journey.

Proverbs8:19 What you receive from me is better than the best gold. It is better than the best silver.

Proverbs8:21 In that way
Proverbs8:22 The Lord created me as the first thing that he did. That was the first of his acts long ago.
Proverbs8:23 He chose me as his servant from the beginning of time. That was before the world began.
Proverbs8:24 I was born before the deep seas were there. There were not yet springs that poured out water.

Proverbs8:34 Anyone who listens to me is in a happy place. You can wait outside my home each day
Proverbs8:35 Anyone who finds me
Proverbs8:36 But anyone who does not find me brings trouble on himself. Everybody who hates me loves death.’
Proverbs9:1 The woman who is called ‘ Wisdom ’ has built her house with seven pillars to make it strong.
Proverbs9:2 She has killed an animal for a big meal. She has mixed her wine. She has also prepared her table.
Proverbs9:3 She has sent her servants to shout a message from the highest places in the town.
Proverbs9:4 She says to foolish people
Proverbs9:5 ‘Come here and eat my food. Drink the wine that I have mixed.
Proverbs9:6 Leave foolish ways so that you may live. Learn to understand what is right
Proverbs9:7 If you warn a person who is proud
Proverbs9:8 So do not warn proud people. They will only hate you. But if you warn a wise person
Proverbs9:9 If you teach a wise person
Proverbs9:10 If you want to be wise in the way that you live
Proverbs9:11 Wisdom will add more days and years to your life.
Proverbs9:12 If you are wise
Proverbs9:13 The woman who is called ‘Fool’ speaks in a loud voice. She is silly and she does not know anything.
Proverbs9:14 She sits at the door of her house. She also has a seat at the highest part of the city.
Proverbs9:15 She shouts her message to people as they pass along the road to do their work.

Ecclesiastes1:1 These are the words of the Teacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 The Teacher says
Ecclesiastes1:3 People work very hard while they live on the earth. But in the end
Ecclesiastes1:4 Old people die and then their children live after them. But the earth continues and it never changes.
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises in the morning and it disappears in the evening. Then it quickly moves to rise again the next morning.
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind blows to the south. Then it turns to the north. It blows in every direction. Then it starts again.
Ecclesiastes1:7 All rivers pour water into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 Everything continues in the same way. There is nothing important to talk about. Nothing that we see is enough to make us happy. There are always more things that we want to hear.
Ecclesiastes1:9 Things that are happening now will continue to happen in the future. What people have done before other people will do again. There is nothing truly new anywhere on the earth.
Ecclesiastes1:10 People may say
Ecclesiastes1:11 People do not remember things that happened in the past. In the future
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 I decided to study everything that happens on the earth. I used wisdom to try to understand these things. I realized that God has given people hard work to do
Ecclesiastes1:14 I thought about all the things that people do on the earth. I realized that all these things have no purpose. It is like somebody who tries to catch the wind.
Ecclesiastes1:15 If someone has bent something
Ecclesiastes1:16 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 So I decided to study how much wisdom and knowledge really help a person. I wanted to learn if it was better than someone who only does foolish things. But this was also useless
Ecclesiastes1:18 The more wisdom you have
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said
Ecclesiastes2:3 I thought about wine. If I drink a lot of wine
Ecclesiastes2:4 I also did a lot of great things for myself. I built houses. I planted vineyards.
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made gardens and fields of trees. I planted many different kinds of fruit trees in them.
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made large pools of water. I used the water to help my trees to grow well.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought male slaves and female slaves. Their children became my slaves too. I had many animals
Ecclesiastes2:8 I stored a lot of silver and gold for myself. Other kings and rulers paid me with many valuable things. I had men and women who would sing for me. I had many beautiful slave wives to give me as much pleasure as any man could want.
Ecclesiastes2:9 As a result
Ecclesiastes2:10 If I saw something that I wanted I took it for myself. If something would give me pleasure
Ecclesiastes2:11 Then I thought about everything that I had done
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then I decided to think about wisdom. I also thought about foolish ways to live. I thought
Ecclesiastes2:13 I realized that it is better to be wise than foolish. In the same way
Ecclesiastes2:14 Wise people can see where they are going. Foolish people walk in the dark. But at the end of their lives
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then I thought
Ecclesiastes2:16 People forget about wise people as quickly as they forget about fools. One day
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life. Everything that happens on the earth only brings me trouble. Nothing has any purpose. It is as useless as somebody who tries to catch the wind.
Ecclesiastes2:18 I thought about all the hard work that I have done here on the earth. When I die
Ecclesiastes2:19 The next king may be a wise man
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I was very upset when I thought about all my hard work on the earth.
Ecclesiastes2:21 Some people are very wise and clever. They use their knowledge to provide good things for themselves. But after they die
Ecclesiastes2:22 People have to do a lot of hard work on this earth and it causes them to worry. But in the end
Ecclesiastes2:23 Every day
Ecclesiastes2:24 So I decided what is best for us to do. We should be happy to eat and to drink. And we should enjoy our work. I realized that all these things are gifts from God.
Ecclesiastes2:25 Without God's help
Ecclesiastes2:26 God gives wisdom
Ecclesiastes3:1 There is a right time for everything. Everything that we do on the earth has a proper time.
Ecclesiastes3:2 There is a time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 There is a time to kill people. There is a time to make people well. There is a time to destroy things. There is a time to build things.
Ecclesiastes3:4 There is a time to weep. There is a time to laugh. There is a time to be sad. There is a time to dance.
Ecclesiastes3:5 There is a time to throw stones away. There is a time to pick them up and use them. There is a time to hug people. There is a time to turn away from people.

Ecclesiastes3:8 There is a time to love. There is a time to hate. There is a time for war. There is a time for peace.

Ecclesiastes3:13 People should be happy to eat and to drink. They should enjoy the work that they do. All these things are gifts from God.
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that everything that God does continues for ever. Nobody can add anything to it. Nobody can take anything away from it. God does these things so that people will respect his authority.

Ecclesiastes3:21 Do the spirits of humans go up into the sky when we die? Do the spirits of animals go down into the ground? Nobody really knows.’

Ecclesiastes4:1 I saw again all the ways that people are cruel on the earth. I saw some people who were crying because cruel people were hurting them. There was nobody who could help them. Nobody could rescue them from those cruel people.
Ecclesiastes4:2 I thought about people who are already dead. I decided that they are happier than the people who are still alive.
Ecclesiastes4:3 But people who have not yet been born are even happier! They have not seen the evil things that happen on the earth.
Ecclesiastes4:4 I also thought about the clever work that people do. I saw that people work hard because they are jealous of each other. They want to do better than other people. I realized that this has no purpose. It is like somebody who tries to catch the wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 Foolish people refuse to work. They sit and they do nothing. So they become hungry and they die.
Ecclesiastes4:6 It is good to have only a little food and be happy. It is better than too much hard work that gives you a lot of food. That is as useless as somebody who tries to catch the wind.

Isaiah1:2 Hear the Lord
Isaiah1:3 An ox knows its master. A donkey knows the place where its master feeds it. But Israel's people do not know me. My people do not understand me.’
Isaiah1:4 Oh! Israel
Isaiah1:5 You continue to fight against God. Why do you do that? God will continue to punish you. You are weak and you hurt all over. Your mind and your body have great pain.
Isaiah1:6 From the bottom of your feet to the top of your head
Isaiah1:7 Your land has become useless. Fire has destroyed your towns and cities. Foreign people have taken the crops from your fields
Isaiah1:8 Jerusalem is now like a hut in a vineyard or in a field of crops. It stands there alone
Isaiah1:9 Unless the Lord Almighty had left some of us alive
Isaiah1:10 Yes
Isaiah1:11 The Lord says
Isaiah1:12 You come to worship me in my temple. But are you doing what I want? Did I ask you to march round the temple yards with all your animals?

Isaiah1:14 I hate your new moon feasts and your holy meetings. They have become like a heavy load for me. I cannot carry it any longer!
Isaiah1:15 When you lift up your hands to pray to me

Isaiah1:20 But if you refuse to obey me and you turn against me
Isaiah1:21 Oh! Jerusalem! Once you loved the Lord
Isaiah1:22 Your pure silver has become dirt. You have mixed water with your best wine.
Isaiah1:23 Your rulers have turned against God. They are friends of robbers. They all accept bribes and they love to receive gifts. But they do not help widows or children who have no family. They do not make sure that they receive justice.
Isaiah1:24 So the Lord says this. He is the Lord Almighty
Isaiah1:25 I will turn against you
Isaiah1:26 Then I will again give you honest judges
Isaiah1:27 God will use justice to make Zion free. He will do what is right for those who return to serve him.
Isaiah1:28 But he will destroy people who turn against him
Isaiah1:29 You will become ashamed because you trusted special oak trees to help you. You worshipped idols in your special gardens
Isaiah1:30 You will be like an oak tree with leaves that have become dry. You will be like a garden that has no water.
Isaiah1:31 Even your most powerful men will disappear. They will be like a piece of string that burns in a fire. Their sins will be the fire that destroys them. They and their sins will burn together

Isaiah34:1 Come near to me
Isaiah34:2 The Lord is angry against all the nations and all their armies. He has decided to destroy them

Isaiah34:5 The Lord says
Isaiah34:6 The Lord 's sword has blood all over it! It is the blood of young sheep and goats. It has the fat of meat on it. It is the fat from sheep's kidneys. Yes! The Lord is killing animals for a sacrifice in Bozrah. He will kill many people in Edom.
Isaiah34:7 He will kill their wild oxen and their young bulls. Blood will pour over Edom's land. Fat will feed their soil.
Isaiah34:8 The Lord has chosen a day for punishment. It will be a time when he punishes Zion's enemies
Isaiah34:9 The water in Edom's streams will become tar. Its soil will become sulphur. Its whole land will become tar that is burning.
Isaiah34:10 That fire will burn through the days and the nights. Its smoke will always continue to rise up. The land will be empty from one century to the next. Nobody will ever travel through it again.
Isaiah34:11 Owls
Isaiah34:12 There will be no kingdom left for anyone to rule. Edom's leaders and officers will all disappear.
Isaiah34:13 Thorn bushes will grow over its palaces. Bushes and weeds will grow over its strong cities. Jackals and ostriches will make their homes in the land.
Isaiah34:14 Wild animals and hyenas will meet there. Wild goats will call out to each other. The night monster will come there
Isaiah34:15 Owls will build their nests there. They will take care of their eggs until the baby birds come out. They will keep their babies safe under their wings. Hawks will meet there too. They will be together in pairs
Isaiah34:16 Read the Lord 's book very carefully: ‘All these animals and birds will be there. They will all be together in pairs. The Lord himself has commanded what must happen. His Spirit brings them all together.’
Isaiah34:17 The Lord has decided where each one will live. He measures a place for every kind of animal and bird. The land will always belong to them to live in. It will be their home from one century to the next.

Isaiah40:14 He does not ask anyone to be his guide. Nobody can teach him the proper way to do things. Nobody can give him knowledge. Nobody can teach him how to understand things.

Isaiah40:16 All the trees from Lebanon's forests are not enough to make a fire for his altar. All its animals are not enough to offer to him as burnt offerings.
Isaiah40:17 All the nations in the world are not important to the Lord. He sees them as empty and useless.
Isaiah40:18 You cannot find anyone else who is like God. There is no image that you can make to be like him.
Isaiah40:19 A worker may use special skill to make an idol. Another worker may cover it with gold. He may use silver chains to make it beautiful.
Isaiah40:20 A poor person may choose special wood for his idol. He chooses wood that will not quickly become spoiled. Then he finds a worker to make an idol for him. He makes it so that it will not fall over!
Isaiah40:21 You must surely know what is true! You must have heard about it. Somebody must have told you these things from the beginning. You should have understood it all since the time that God made the earth.
Isaiah40:22 God sits high above on his throne where the earth and the sky meet. The people on the earth seem like grasshoppers to him. He hangs out the sky like a curtain. He makes it like a tent for him to live in.

Isaiah42:1 ‘Here is my servant. I give him strength. I have chosen him. He makes me very happy. I have put my Spirit on him. He will bring justice to the nations.
Isaiah42:2 He will not cry out. He will not shout. He will not speak loudly in the streets.
Isaiah42:3 He will not hurt weak people
Isaiah42:4 He will remain strong and brave
Isaiah42:5 The Lord is God. He is the God who created the sky. He hung it up like a curtain. He created the earth and everything that lives on it. He gives life and breath to all the people who live on it. The Lord says to his servant:
Isaiah42:6 ‘I have chosen you to bring righteousness. I will take hold of your hand
Isaiah42:7 You will cause blind people to see. You will cause prisoners to be free. You will bring out into the open people who sit in dark prisons.
Isaiah42:8 I am the Lord. That is my name. I will not let anyone else share my glory. I will not let people praise idols. I am the only one that they must praise.
Isaiah42:9 Everything that I promised in the past has happened as I said that it would happen. Now I will tell you about new things. Before they start to happen
Isaiah42:10 Sing a new song to the Lord! All over the earth
Isaiah42:11 People who live in the towns of the desert should shout aloud to praise him. Kedar's people should praise him in the places where they live. The people who live in Sela should sing aloud with joy. They should shout from the tops of the mountains.
Isaiah42:12 They should all give honour to the Lord as he deserves. Those who live in lands far away should praise him for what he has done.

Isaiah42:15 I will destroy the mountains and the hills. I will cause the plants that grow on them to die. I will cause the rivers to become dry islands. I will cause the pools of water to become dry.

Isaiah42:17 But I will push away people who trust idols to help them. Those people will become ashamed. They say to images that they have made

Isaiah42:21 The Lord always does what is right. So he has made his Law great and important. He is happy when people respect his Law.

Isaiah42:23 Will any of you listen to my message? Will you think carefully about it in the future?

Isaiah44:12 This is how they make their idols: A man who works with iron takes his tool. He makes the metal hot in the fire. Then he uses hammers to hit it with his strong arms. He hits it so that it has the right shape. But then he becomes hungry. He is too weak to work any more. He does not drink any water and so he feels tired.
Isaiah44:13 A carpenter measures a piece of wood. Then he draws a shape for the image. He uses his tools to cut the wood. He carefully makes it have the right shape. He makes the image have the shape of a human. It looks like a beautiful person. Now it is ready to go in its special room.
Isaiah44:14 He may cut down cedar trees to make something. He may cut down a cypress tree
Isaiah44:15 He uses some of the wood to burn. He makes himself warm beside the fire. He may light a fire and then he may bake bread on it. Then he uses some of the wood to make a god that he will worship! Yes
Isaiah44:16 Half of the wood he burns in the fire. He uses it to cook his meat
Isaiah44:17 He uses the rest of the wood to make an idol to be his god. He bends down in front of it and he worships it. He prays to it and he says
Isaiah44:18 People like that are stupid. They do not understand anything. They cannot see the truth
Isaiah44:19 None of them think carefully about what they are doing. They do not say to themselves

Isaiah45:9 It will be very bad for people who argue with their Maker. They are like pieces of a pot that are lying on the ground among all the other pieces. The clay does not say to the potter

Isaiah45:13 Now I have woken up Cyrus to do for me things that are right. I will make all his paths easy to travel on. He will build again my city

Isaiah45:15 Yes

Jeremiah48:28 Now you must all leave your towns. Go and live among the rocks. Live like doves that make their nests high up among the rocks.
Jeremiah48:29 We have heard that Moab's people are very proud. They think that they are better than everyone else. We know how much they boast.’
Jeremiah48:30 The Lord says
Jeremiah48:31 So I am sad for Moab. I am crying for all their people. I weep for the people of Kir-Heres.
Jeremiah48:32 I weep for the vines of Sibmah
Jeremiah48:33 People will no longer be happy in the land where this fruit grows. I will stop them making wine from their grapes. They will not stamp on their grapes and shout with joy. Instead
Jeremiah48:34 In Heshbon and Elealeh people cry out because of their pain. The noise reaches as far as Jahaz. It reaches from Zoar to Horonaim and Eglath-Shelishiyah. Even the Nimrim River has become dry.’
Jeremiah48:35 The Lord says this: ‘I will remove the people of Moab who worship other gods. They offer sacrifices to them and they burn incense at their altars.
Jeremiah48:36 So I am very sad for the people of Moab. I weep like a flute that plays a funeral song. I am sad for the people of Kir-Heres
Jeremiah48:37 All the men will be very upset. They will cut the hair off their heads and their faces. They will use knives to cut their hands. They will wear sackcloth as their clothes.
Jeremiah48:38 People are standing on the roofs of their houses. They are standing in the streets. They are all crying and weeping. They are sad because I have destroyed Moab. I have broken it in pieces
Jeremiah48:39 ‘Yes
Jeremiah48:40 The Lord says
Jeremiah48:41 The enemy will take Moab's towns and its strong buildings. Moab's soldiers will be afraid
Jeremiah48:42 Moab has been proud and it has turned against the Lord. So they will no longer be a nation.’
Jeremiah48:43 The Lord says this: ‘People of Moab
Jeremiah48:44 Anyone who runs away from danger will fall into a deep hole. And a trap will catch anyone who climbs out of the deep hole. Yes
Jeremiah48:45 People will run away to Heshbon to be safe. They will be helpless as they stand outside its walls. Flames of fire will come out from Heshbon
Jeremiah48:46 It will be very bad for you
Jeremiah48:47 But one day I will cause Moab to be strong again.’ That is what the Lord says. That is the end of the Lord 's message against Moab.
Jeremiah49:1 The Lord gave this message about the people of Ammon: ‘There are plenty of Israel's descendants who are still alive. They are strong enough to live in the land that is their home. So why have people who worship the god Molech moved in to the land of Gad's tribe? They should not be living in towns that belong to Gad's people.’
Jeremiah49:2 The Lord says
Jeremiah49:3 ‘You people in Heshbon
Jeremiah49:4 You people of Ammon boast about your power. But that will soon come to an end! You have not been faithful. You have trusted in your riches and you say
Jeremiah49:5 But the Lord God Almighty says
Jeremiah49:6 But in future days
Jeremiah49:7 The Lord Almighty gave this message about Edom: ‘It seems that wisdom has disappeared from Teman. Edom's clever men have no good advice. Their great ideas have gone away.
Jeremiah49:8 You people who live in Dedan
Jeremiah49:9 If people came to pick your grapes
Jeremiah49:10 But I will take everything from Esau's descendants
Jeremiah49:11 But I will take care of your widows and the children who have no family. You can trust me to keep them safe.’

Jeremiah49:13 I

Jeremiah49:16 You are proud people. You boast that you cause other people to be afraid of you. But your thoughts have deceived you. You live high up among the rocks
Jeremiah49:17 ‘People will see how disgusting Edom is. Everybody who goes there will be very surprised. They will see all the terrible trouble that has happened to the nation
Jeremiah49:18 Edom will become a heap of stones where nobody lives. The same thing happened to Sodom and Gomorrah
Jeremiah49:19 ‘I will attack the people of Edom and I will chase them out of their land. I will be like a lion that has been hiding in the forest. It attacks the sheep in the fields beside the Jordan River. Like that lion
Jeremiah49:20 So listen to me
Jeremiah49:21 When Edom's people fall
Jeremiah49:22 A nation will rise up high
Jeremiah49:23 The Lord gave this message about Damascus: ‘The people of Hamath and Arpad will hear bad news. It will cause them to be upset. They will no longer feel brave. They will not be able to rest because they are afraid.
Jeremiah49:24 The people of Damascus will become weak. They will try to run away. They will become confused because of their fear. They will feel pain like a woman who is giving birth.
Jeremiah49:25 Damascus was a famous city. The people who lived in it were very happy. But now it will become empty.
Jeremiah49:26 Its young men will die in its streets. All its soldiers will be dead.
Jeremiah49:27 I will burn down the city walls of Damascus. Fire will destroy the palaces of King Ben-Hadad.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says.
Jeremiah49:28 The Lord gave this message about Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor that King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon attacked: ‘March to Kedar and attack the city! Destroy those people who live east of Judah.

Jeremiah49:31 The Lord says to King Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah49:32 Take their camels and their farm animals for yourselves. I will chase away those people who live in the desert and who cut their hair short. I will send them away in every direction. I will bring terrible trouble from every direction to hurt them.
Jeremiah49:33 Hazor will become as empty as a desert. Only wild animals will live there. It will always be a place where no people live.’
Jeremiah49:34 Soon after Zedekiah became king of Judah

Jeremiah50:1 The Lord gave this message to the prophet Jeremiah about Babylon city and the people of Babylonia:
Jeremiah50:2 ‘Tell this news to the nations! Wave a flag to make sure that people listen! Do not try to hide anything. Say this: “Enemies will take Babylon. Bel will become ashamed. Yes
Jeremiah50:3 A nation from the north will attack Babylon. They will destroy the whole land. People and animals will all run away
Jeremiah50:4 The Lord says
Jeremiah50:5 They will ask people to show them the way to Zion. They will travel in that direction. They will promise to serve the Lord. They will make a covenant with him that will continue for ever. Nobody will ever forget that covenant.
Jeremiah50:6 My people have been like sheep that are lost. Their leaders should have taken care of them like shepherds. Instead
Jeremiah50:7 Anyone who found them killed them. Their enemies said
Jeremiah50:8 People of Judah
Jeremiah50:9 I will call a group of strong nations from the north. They will join together to fight against Babylon. They will prepare to attack. They will come from the north and they will take power over Babylon. Their arrows will be like brave soldiers. They will kill everyone that they want to kill.
Jeremiah50:10 The people of Babylonia will lose all their things. Enemy soldiers will take everything that they want for themselves.’ That is what the Lord says.
Jeremiah50:11 ‘You people of Babylon are very happy
Jeremiah50:12 Babylon is like your mother who gave you life
Jeremiah50:13 Nobody will live in Babylon
Jeremiah50:14 Get ready to fight
Jeremiah50:15 Shout as you attack the city from all sides. Its people are ready to stop fighting. Its strong towers will fall down. You will knock down its walls. The Lord is punishing Babylon
Jeremiah50:16 Remove from Babylon all the farmers who plant crops. Remove all the people who cut the crops at harvest time. The foreigners must return to their own homes. They must run away to escape from the enemy army.
Jeremiah50:17 Israel's enemies have chased them away to many different places. They are like sheep that hungry lions have chased away. The first to catch them was the king of Assyria. Then King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon destroyed them
Jeremiah50:18 So the Lord Almighty
Jeremiah50:19 But I will bring Israel's people back to their own land. They will grow their crops on Carmel and in Bashan. They will have plenty of food to eat on the hills of Ephraim and in Gilead.
Jeremiah50:20 At that time

Jeremiah50:22 There is a noise of war in the land of Babylonia. The enemy is destroying everything!
Jeremiah50:23 Babylon attacked other nations of the world
Jeremiah50:24 Babylon
Jeremiah50:25 I have taken out my weapons from the place where I had stored them. I will use those weapons to punish the people of Babylonia
Jeremiah50:26 Now come from far away to attack Babylonia! Break open the store rooms and take out the grain. Knock down their buildings and break them into pieces. Completely destroy everything. Do not let anything remain!
Jeremiah50:27 Kill all their strong soldiers. Let them go to their place of death! Terrible trouble has come to the people of Babylon. The time of their punishment has arrived!’
Jeremiah50:28 Listen! People have run away from the war in Babylon. Now they are arriving in Jerusalem. They will tell the news in Zion about how the Lord is punishing Babylon. He is punishing the people of Babylon because they destroyed his temple.
Jeremiah50:29 ‘Bring all the soldiers with bows and arrows to attack Babylon. They must make their camp all around the city. Do not let anyone escape. Punish the people for the things that they have done. Do the same thing to them that they have done to others. Those people have insulted me
Jeremiah50:30 So Babylon's young men will die in its streets. All its soldiers will die in battle on that day.’ That is what the Lord says.
Jeremiah50:31 The Lord God Almighty says this: ‘You people of Babylon are very proud
Jeremiah50:32 You are proud but you will fall to the ground. Nobody will help you to get up. I will burn Babylonia's towns with fire
Jeremiah50:33 The Lord Almighty says
Jeremiah50:34 But my name is the Lord Almighty. I am strong and I will rescue my people. I will fight hard to save them. I will bring peace to their land. But the people of Babylonia will only have trouble.’
Jeremiah50:35 The Lord says
Jeremiah50:36 They will attack its false prophets
Jeremiah50:37 They will attack Babylonia's horses and chariots
Jeremiah50:38 There will be no rain on the land so that the rivers and streams become dry. I will send all this trouble because their whole land is full of idols. The people become crazy because they are afraid of their idols.
Jeremiah50:39 Hyenas and other wild animals will live there. Ostriches will live there
Jeremiah50:40 Babylonia will become a heap of stones where nobody lives. The same thing happened to Sodom and Gomorrah and the towns near to them. Nobody will live there any more.’ That is what the Lord says.
Jeremiah50:41 ‘Look! An army is coming from the north. A great nation and many kings are preparing to attack. They are coming from a place that is far away.

Jeremiah51:1 The Lord says this: ‘I will send an enemy to destroy Babylon and the people of Babylonia. It will come like a strong wind that blows them away.
Jeremiah51:2 I will send foreign soldiers to remove them from their land
Jeremiah51:3 Do not give Babylon's soldiers time to pick up their weapons! Do not give them time to put on their armour! Kill all their young men. Completely destroy their whole army.
Jeremiah51:4 Their dead bodies will lie on the ground all over Babylonia and in the streets of the city.
Jeremiah51:5 The Lord Almighty
Jeremiah51:6 Run away from Babylonia! Run quickly to escape from there! Do not stay there to die along with Babylonia's people. The time has come for the Lord to pay them back for their sins. He will punish them as they deserve.
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon has been like a gold cup of wine in the Lord 's hand. The nations of the world had to drink that wine. It made everyone become drunk
Jeremiah51:8 But now Babylon will quickly fall. An enemy will destroy it

Jeremiah51:10 The people from Judah who are in Babylon say

Jeremiah51:13 You people of Babylon

Jeremiah51:15 The Lord used his power to make the earth. He used his wisdom to make it strong. He knew how to put the sky to cover the earth.
Jeremiah51:16 When he shouts like thunder
Jeremiah51:17 People who worship idols are stupid. They do not understand what they are doing. The men who make idols will be ashamed. They make images of gods that are false. None of their idols have the breath of life in them.
Jeremiah51:18 They are useless things that people should laugh at. When the time for their punishment arrives
Jeremiah51:19 The God that Jacob's descendants worship is not like those idols. He created everything. He chose Israel's people to belong to him. His name is the Lord Almighty.

Jeremiah51:21 I have used you to destroy whole armies
Jeremiah51:22 I have used you to destroy men and women
Jeremiah51:23 I have used you to destroy shepherds and their sheep
Jeremiah51:24 But now I will punish Babylon and all the people who live in Babylonia. I will pay them back for all the evil things that they did in Jerusalem. And you will see me do that.’ That is what the Lord says.
Jeremiah51:25 The Lord says
Jeremiah51:26 Nobody will be able to use any of your stones as a foundation for a new building. You will remain as empty as a desert for ever.’ That is what the Lord says.

Jeremiah51:28 Tell the nations to prepare their armies for war against Babylonia. Tell the kings of Media to get ready
Jeremiah51:29 The whole earth will shake with pain as the Lord punishes Babylonia. He will certainly do what he has decided to do. He will make the land of Babylonia as empty as a desert. Nobody will live there.
Jeremiah51:30 The brave soldiers of Babylonia are no longer fighting! They remain inside their strong buildings. Their strength has gone! They are as weak as women. In their cities
Jeremiah51:31 Now people run to send the news to the king of Babylon. They pass this message along the line: “The enemy has taken power over the whole city!
Jeremiah51:32 They have guards to stop people escaping across the rivers. They have burned the reeds in the wet fields. All our soldiers are afraid.”
Jeremiah51:33 The Lord Almighty
Jeremiah51:34 ‘King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon has destroyed us with his army. He has made us weak and confused. He has eaten us like a hungry monster. He has filled himself with all our good things. Then he spat us out of his mouth.’

Jeremiah51:42 The sea will pour over it. Many waves of water will cover it.

He took the message when he went to Babylon with King Zedekiah of Judah. That happened in the fourth year that Zedekiah had ruled Judah as king.

Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was 21 years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for 11 years. His mother's name was Hamutal. She was the daughter of Jeremiah
Jeremiah52:2 Zedekiah did things that the Lord said were evil
Jeremiah52:3 All this trouble happened to Jerusalem and to Judah because the Lord was very angry with them. In the end
Jeremiah52:4 Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:5 Babylon's army stayed around the city until the 11th year that Zedekiah had been king.
Jeremiah52:6 By the ninth day of the fourth month there was a very bad famine in the city. There was no food for the people to eat.
Jeremiah52:7 Then Babylon's army broke down Jerusalem's wall so that they could go into the city. Their soldiers were all around the city. So the king of Judah and all his army tried to escape in the night. They went through the gate that was near the king's garden. The path went between the two walls of the city. They ran towards the Jordan Valley.
Jeremiah52:8 But the soldiers of Babylon's army chased after King Zedekiah. They caught him on the flat land near Jericho. All King Zedekiah's soldiers ran away from him in many directions.
Jeremiah52:9 Babylon's soldiers took hold of Zedekiah. They took him to King Nebuchadnezzar at Riblah
Jeremiah52:10 He told his soldiers to kill all Zedekiah's sons
Jeremiah52:11 Then they cut out Zedekiah's eyes to make him blind. They tied him with chains and they took him to Babylon as their prisoner. They kept him in prison until the day that he died.
Jeremiah52:12 King Nebuchadnezzar had an officer whose name was Nebuzaradan. He was the captain of the king's royal guards. Nebuzaradan came to Jerusalem when Nebuchadnezzar had ruled Babylon for 19 years. It was on the tenth day of the fifth month.
Jeremiah52:13 Nebuzaradan destroyed the Lord 's temple
Jeremiah52:14 Then Nebuzaradan commanded his whole army to knock down the walls around Jerusalem.
Jeremiah52:15 Captain Nebuzaradan sent away as prisoners some of the poor people and the rest of the people who remained in Jerusalem. He also sent away those people who had agreed to serve the king of Babylon
Jeremiah52:16 But he let some of the poorest people stay there. He gave them vineyards and fields to work in.
Jeremiah52:17 The soldiers from Babylon broke the two bronze pillars that were in the Lord 's temple. They also broke the carts which carried the buckets for water and the large bath called ‘the Sea’. They carried all the bronze pieces away to Babylon.
Jeremiah52:18 They also took away the pots
Jeremiah52:19 Captain Nebuzaradan also took away everything that was made from gold or silver. He took the dishes
Jeremiah52:20 The bronze from the things that King Solomon had made for the Lord 's temple was very heavy. They included the two bronze pillars
Jeremiah52:21 Each pillar was 8 metres high and 5 metres around. The bronze was 8 centimetres thick and each pillar was empty inside.
Jeremiah52:22 The bronze piece on the top of one pillar was more than a metre high. It had rows of chains with images of pomegranates made from bronze all around it. The other pillar
Jeremiah52:23 There were 96 images of pomegranates around the sides of the pillars. There were 100 images of pomegranates on the chains at the top.
Jeremiah52:24 Captain Nebuzaradan took hold of these people: Seraiah
Jeremiah52:25 He also took hold of these people who remained in Jerusalem: The palace officer with authority over the soldiers. Seven of the king's advisors. The army secretary who took men to join the army. 60 other people of Judah who were inside the city.
Jeremiah52:26 Captain Nebuzaradan took hold of all those people. He brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:27 There
Jeremiah52:28 This is a list of the number of people that Nebuchadnezzar took away as prisoners to Babylon: He took 3
Jeremiah52:29 He took 832 people from Jerusalem in his 18th year.
Jeremiah52:30 Captain Nebuzaradan took 745 people from Judah in Nebuchadnezzar's 23rd year as king. So Nebuchadnezzar took 4
Jeremiah52:31 37 years after King Jehoiachin of Judah had gone as a prisoner to Babylon
Jeremiah52:32 King Evil-Merodach spoke in a kind way to Jehoiachin. He gave him more honour than the other kings who were with him in Babylon.
Jeremiah52:33 Jehoiachin no longer had to wear the clothes of a prisoner. Every day for the rest of his life
Jeremiah52:34 The king of Babylon gave Jehoiachin everything that he needed each day of his life until he died.
Lamentations1:1 Look! The city at one time was full of people. But now it is completely alone. She was a famous city among the nations of the world. But now she is alone
Lamentations1:2 She weeps aloud in the night. There are tears on her face. She had many lovers. But now there is no one to comfort her. All her friends have turned away from her. They have become her enemies.
Lamentations1:3 Judah's enemies have taken her people away as prisoners. The people suffer as slaves of cruel enemies. Judah's people live among foreign nations. They have nowhere to rest safely. Their enemies chased them and they caught them. Judah's people could not escape.
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion are sad places now. Nobody travels along them to come to the festivals. No people meet at the city gates. The priests are sad. The young women weep. The people of Zion are very upset.
Lamentations1:5 Jerusalem's enemies now rule her people. They live an easy life. The Lord has caused Jerusalem to suffer because of the many sins of her people. Enemies have taken her children away as their prisoners.
Lamentations1:6 Zion is not a beautiful city any more. Her leaders are like deer that can find no food to eat. They are too weak to escape from the people who are chasing them.
Lamentations1:7 Jerusalem's people are suffering and they have no homes to live in. So they remember how great their city was
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem's sins are very bad. That is why it has become an unclean place. At one time
Lamentations1:9 Everyone can see that Jerusalem has become an unclean place. The people did not think about their sins. They did not worry about what would happen. But a terrible thing happened when enemies destroyed their city. There was nobody who could comfort them. They called to God for help
Lamentations1:10 Enemies have taken all Jerusalem's valuable things for themselves. Her people watched as foreigners went into the holy temple. But the Lord himself had said
Lamentations1:11 All Jerusalem's people cry as they look for a bit of food to eat. They have to sell their valuable things to buy enough food to keep them alive. The people say
Lamentations1:12 They say to those who go past the city
Lamentations1:13 He has sent down fire from above to hurt us. It has destroyed our strength. He has prepared a trap to catch our feet
Lamentations1:14 Our sins seem to be like a yoke around our necks. The Lord himself has tied that heavy load on us. We were strong
Lamentations1:15 The Lord God has removed all the strong fighters who were ready to fight for us. He brought a big army to fight against us
Lamentations1:16 We weep because these things have happened to us. Tears pour out of our eyes. There is nobody here who can comfort us. Nobody can make us strong and brave. Our enemies have won against us
Lamentations1:17 Zion's people call out for help
Lamentations1:18 ‘The Lord is right to punish us! We have refused to obey his commands. Listen to us
Lamentations1:19 We called to our lovers to help us. But they refused to help us. Our priests and our leaders died in the city's streets. They were looking for food to keep themselves alive.
Lamentations1:20 Lord
Lamentations1:21 People have heard that we are crying
Lamentations1:22 Remember all the wicked things that they have done. Punish them as you have punished us for all our sins. Now we are very sad and we cry a lot. We have no hope.’
Lamentations2:1 The Lord God has become very angry. He has caused a dark cloud to cover Zion's people. Jerusalem was the most beautiful city in Israel. But now
Lamentations2:2 The Lord God has destroyed all the homes of Jacob's descendants. He would not forgive them. Because of his anger
Lamentations2:3 Because of God's great anger
Lamentations2:4 He prepared his bow and his arrows
Lamentations2:5 The Lord God has become like Israel's enemy. He has destroyed Israel. He has destroyed all her palaces. He has caused her strong cities to become heaps of stones. He has caused all Judah's people to weep. They cannot stop crying.
Lamentations2:6 God has knocked down his temple
Lamentations2:7 The Lord God no longer accepts sacrifices on his altar. He has gone away from his temple. He has given Jerusalem's palaces to her enemies. They shout loudly in the Lord 's temple
Lamentations2:8 The Lord decided to destroy the strong walls of Zion. He did it carefully
Lamentations2:9 The gates of the city have fallen down on the ground. God has destroyed the metal bars that lock the gates. The king and his officers are prisoners in other lands. There is nobody to teach God's Law. The prophets no longer receive any visions from the Lord.
Lamentations2:10 Zion's leaders sit on the ground. They are saying nothing. They have dressed themselves in sackcloth. They have put dirt on their heads. The young women of Jerusalem are also sad. They turn their faces down towards the ground.
Lamentations2:11 I have cried so much that my eyes have become weak. Deep inside me
Lamentations2:12 Hungry children ask their mothers
Lamentations2:13 Dear people of Jerusalem
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets had useless visions that deceived you. They should have told you that your sins were very bad. Then you would have been safe from your trouble. But they deceived you with their messages
Lamentations2:15 Everyone who goes past the city laughs at you. They are happy to see your trouble. They insult beautiful Jerusalem and they say
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies laugh at you. They hate you and they insult you. They say ‘We have destroyed this city! We have waited a long time for this day. Now we have finally seen it happen!’
Lamentations2:17 The Lord has done everything that he decided to do. He has done what he promised to do a long time ago. He has destroyed your city and he has not felt sorry about it. He has given your enemies a reason to be happy. He has made them stronger than you.
Lamentations2:18 City of Zion
Lamentations2:19 Get up in the night and call for God's help. Do this all through every night. Tell the Lord God everything that you think and you feel. Let it pour out like water from a jar. Lift up your hands and pray to him. Ask him to save the lives of your children. They fall down on every street in the city because they have no food to eat.
Lamentations2:20 ‘Look at us
Lamentations2:21 Young people and old people lie dead in the streets. The enemy's swords have killed our young women and our young men. You killed our people because you were angry
Lamentations2:22 You called our enemies to come for a feast! They attacked us from all around. At that time of your great anger
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has suffered. God punished me because he was angry.
Lamentations3:2 He took me into very dark places. I had to walk in the dark instead of in the light.
Lamentations3:3 He has continued to attack me many times. All day
Lamentations3:4 He has caused my body and skin to spoil. He has broken my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He has attacked me from every side
Lamentations3:6 He has made me live in a very dark place
Lamentations3:7 He has put heavy chains on me like a prisoner. I cannot escape from my troubles.
Lamentations3:8 I call out loudly for God to help me
Lamentations3:9 He has used big rocks to build a wall across every road where I go. I cannot go anywhere.
Lamentations3:10 God waits to attack me
Lamentations3:11 He has pulled me away from the road. He has torn me in pieces. He has left me without any help.
Lamentations3:12 He is like a soldier who is ready to shoot his arrows. I am the one that he wants to hit!
Lamentations3:13 He has shot his arrows and he has hit me. It hurts me deep inside.
Lamentations3:14 All my people laugh at me. All the time
Lamentations3:15 God has caused me to suffer very much
Lamentations3:16 He has made me eat stones that break my teeth. He has pushed me down into the dirt on the ground.
Lamentations3:17 I have no peace in my mind. I cannot remember the happy days that I had before.
Lamentations3:18 I tell myself
Lamentations3:19 I think about my troubles
Lamentations3:20 I think about these things all the time
Lamentations3:21 But now I remember what God is like
Lamentations3:22 The Lord 's faithful love never comes to an end. He never stops being kind to us.
Lamentations3:23 Every day
Lamentations3:24 I say to myself
Lamentations3:25 The Lord is kind to people who trust in him. He helps those who turn to him.
Lamentations3:26 It is good when people wait quietly for the Lord to rescue them.
Lamentations3:27 It is good if you learn to accept trouble when you are still young.
Lamentations3:28 When the Lord causes us to suffer
Lamentations3:29 We should be humble as we suffer. Then hope may come again.
Lamentations3:30 If somebody hits us on one side of our face
Lamentations3:31 The Lord God will not turn against his people for ever.
Lamentations3:32 He may cause us to suffer for a time
Lamentations3:33 It does not please him to make people suffer. He does not like to give them pain.
Lamentations3:34 If anyone is cruel to all the prisoners in the land
Lamentations3:35 If anyone turns against the Most High God and cheats people
Lamentations3:36 If anyone tells lies in court
Lamentations3:37 Nobody can command things to happen unless the Lord God says it must happen.
Lamentations3:38 The Most High God says what will happen
Lamentations3:39 When God punishes us for our sins we have no reason to complain.
Lamentations3:40 Instead
Lamentations3:41 We should be honest with God in heaven
Lamentations3:42 ‘We have completely turned against you. You have not forgiven us.
Lamentations3:43 You have chased after us with your great anger. You have not felt sorry to kill us.
Lamentations3:44 You have hidden yourself from us in a cloud. So our prayers cannot reach you.
Lamentations3:45 You have caused us to become like dirty rubbish. So the people of other nations do not respect us.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies are happy to see that we have trouble.
Lamentations3:47 Terrible troubles have come to destroy us. So we are very afraid.’
Lamentations3:48 Tears pour from my eyes like a river
Lamentations3:49 My tears will continue to pour out. They will never stop
Lamentations3:50 until the Lord looks down from heaven. He will see what is happening to us.
Lamentations3:51 I see how much the young women of my city are suffering. It makes me very upset.
Lamentations3:52 My enemies had no reason to hate me
Lamentations3:53 They threw me into a deep hole in the ground and they threw stones at me to kill me.
Lamentations3:54 The water in the hole rose up my head. Then I thought
Lamentations3:55 From the bottom of the deep hole I called your name
Lamentations3:56 I said
Lamentations3:57 You came near to me when I prayed to you. You said to me
Lamentations3:58 Lord God
Lamentations3:59 You have seen how my enemies have been cruel to me. Judge fairly and show that I am right!
Lamentations3:60 You have seen that my enemies hate me and they make plans to hurt me.
Lamentations3:61 Lord
Lamentations3:62 My enemies say bad things about me. They are always thinking about ways to attack me.
Lamentations3:63 Watch them as they sing songs to insult me. They sing them all the time.
Lamentations3:64 Lord
Lamentations3:65 Take away all their hope. Curse them so that they suffer!
Lamentations3:66 Chase after them in your anger. Cause them to disappear from the whole earth.
Lamentations4:1 Look! The city's gold is no longer beautiful. Even pure gold has become useless. The jewels from the temple lie on the ground in all the city's streets.
Lamentations4:2 Zion's young men were more valuable to us than gold. But now they seem to have no value. They are like ordinary pots that a potter has made.
Lamentations4:3 Even jackals feed their babies at their breasts. But Jerusalem's young women have become cruel. They are like ostriches that leave their eggs in the desert.
Lamentations4:4 Babies' mouths are completely dry
Lamentations4:5 People who were rich and who ate the best food now die in the streets with nothing to eat. Rich people who wore expensive clothes now die among the rubbish.
Lamentations4:6 God destroyed the city of Sodom in a moment and nobody was there to help them. Now he has punished my people even more than he punished Sodom's people.
Lamentations4:7 The leaders of Jerusalem were very pure
Lamentations4:8 Now their faces are dirty
Lamentations4:9 It is better for people who died in a battle than for people who have nothing to eat. The fields provided no crops so people died slowly without food.
Lamentations4:10 Women who had been kind mothers have now cooked their own children. Their children became their food
Lamentations4:11 The Lord showed that he was very angry. He started a fire in Zion. The fire destroyed the whole city
Lamentations4:12 None of the kings of the earth
Lamentations4:13 But it happened because of the sins of Jerusalem's prophets and her priests. The city became an unclean place because its leaders caused good people to die there.
Lamentations4:14 They walked around in the city's streets like people who are blind. There was blood on their clothes and people were afraid to touch them.
Lamentations4:15 People shouted at them
Lamentations4:16 The Lord himself has sent them away. He does not take care of them any longer. People do not respect the priests. Nobody is kind to the leaders.
Lamentations4:17 Our eyes became weak as we watched to see if help would come. We continued to hope for help from another nation. But no nation was able to rescue us.
Lamentations4:18 Our enemies watched us wherever we went. We could not walk in our own streets. We knew that our end would soon come. Only a few days remained. The time had finally come!
Lamentations4:19 Our enemies could chase us more quickly than eagles that fly high in the sky. They chased us across the mountains. They hid to attack us in the desert.
Lamentations4:20 The king that the Lord had chosen to rule over us was the one who gave us life. We had thought
Lamentations4:21 Edom's people who live in the land of Uz
Lamentations4:22 People of Zion
Lamentations5:1 Lord
Lamentations5:2 Strangers have taken our land for themselves. Foreign people live in our homes.
Lamentations5:3 We have become children without fathers. Our mothers have become widows.
Lamentations5:4 We must pay money for the water that we drink. We must buy expensive wood for our fires.
Lamentations5:5 Our enemies make us work very hard. We are tired and we have no rest.
Lamentations5:6 We have agreed to serve the Egyptians and the Assyrians
Lamentations5:7 Our ancestors did wrong things but they are now dead. We receive the punishment that they deserved.
Lamentations5:8 Slaves now rule over us. There is nobody to rescue us from their power.
Lamentations5:9 Robbers hide in the fields
Lamentations5:10 Our skin feels as hot as an oven. We are so hungry that we have a fever.
Lamentations5:11 Our enemies have taken hold of the women in Zion. They have sex with the young women in Judah's cities.
Lamentations5:12 They have hung our rulers by their hands. They have been cruel to our leaders.
Lamentations5:13 They force the young men to work like slaves. Our boys fall down as they carry heavy loads of wood.
Lamentations5:14 Our leaders can no longer meet at the city gate. The young men no longer make their music.
Lamentations5:15 We are not happy any more. We no longer dance
Lamentations5:16 We have nothing to be proud of any more. Terrible trouble will happen to us
Lamentations5:17 Because of this
Lamentations5:18 Mount Zion has become a heap of stones. Wild animals walk all over it.
Lamentations5:19 But Lord
Lamentations5:20 Why do you still forget about us? Why have you not come to help us for so long?
Lamentations5:21 Lord
Lamentations5:22 We are sure that you have not completely turned away from us. Do not be so angry with us that we have no hope.
Ezekiel1:1 God spoke to me on the fifth day of the fourth month in the 30th year. I was among the prisoners from Judah at the side of the Kebar River. At that time the skies opened and I saw visions from God.
Ezekiel1:2 King Jehoiachin of Judah had been a prisoner in Babylon for five years. On the fifth day of the month
Ezekiel1:3 the Lord spoke to Buzi's son
Ezekiel1:4 As I looked
Ezekiel1:5 I could see something like four living things in the middle of the fire. Each of them had the shape of a human.
Ezekiel1:6 But each of them had four faces and four wings.
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight. Their feet were like the feet of young cows. They shone like bright bronze.
Ezekiel1:8 They had human hands under their wings on the four sides. Each of these four living things had faces and wings that were like this:
Ezekiel1:9 Their wings touched each other. All the living things moved forward together. They did not turn as they moved.
Ezekiel1:10 This is what their faces looked like: Each of the living things had four faces. There was a human face at the front. There was a lion's face on the right side. There was a bull's face on the left side. Each of them also had the face of an eagle.
Ezekiel1:11 Their wings waved up above them. Two wings of each living thing touched the wings of the living things on each side of them. Each living thing covered its body with its other two wings.
Ezekiel1:12 The living things each moved forward
Ezekiel1:13 In the middle of the living things was a fire. It looked like coals that were burning
Ezekiel1:14 The living things moved around very quickly
Ezekiel1:15 I continued to look at the four living things. Each of them had a wheel on the ground at its side.
Ezekiel1:16 The four wheels all looked the same. They shone like bright jewels. The wheels were made like this: Each wheel had another wheel that went across the middle of it.
Ezekiel1:17 They moved forward in the same direction that the living things moved. They did not turn in any other direction.
Ezekiel1:18 The wheels had high edges. Eyes completely covered the edges of all the wheels.
Ezekiel1:19 When the living things moved
Ezekiel1:20 The living things went wherever the spirit wanted them to go. The wheels would also go with them. That was because the spirit of the living things was in each wheel.
Ezekiel1:21 When the living things moved
Ezekiel1:22 I saw something over the heads of the living things. It looked like a high roof that shone brightly like ice.
Ezekiel1:23 The living things all stood under the roof. Each of them stood with its wings held out towards the living things on each side of it. They covered their bodies with their other two wings.
Ezekiel1:24 When they moved
Ezekiel1:25 While they stood with their wings down at their sides
Ezekiel1:26 Above the roof
Ezekiel1:27 The top part of his body shone like a fire with bright metal in the centre of it. The lower part of his body looked like flames of fire. All around the human shape
Ezekiel1:28 It shone with the colours of a rainbow in the clouds when there is rain. This bright light showed the glory of the Lord

Ezekiel3:7 But Israel's people will not agree to listen to you. That is because they refuse to listen to me. All the Israelites have decided not to obey me
Ezekiel3:8 But I will make you as strong in your mind as they are. You will not change what you think.
Ezekiel3:9 You will be as strong as the strongest stone that nobody can break. Do not be afraid of them. Do not be afraid of how they look at you. Remember that they are people who refuse to obey me.’

Ezekiel3:11 Now you must go to the Israelites who are prisoners in Babylon. They are your own people. Give my message to them and say

Ezekiel3:14 Then the Spirit lifted me up and he took me away. I was angry and upset in my mind. But the Lord gave me great power and strength.
Ezekiel3:15 I came to Tel Abib near the Kebar River

Ezekiel3:20 A righteous person may stop doing things that are right. He may start to do bad things. Then I will put him in danger. He will die as punishment for his sins. I will not remember the good things that he did. But if you have not warned him of the danger
Ezekiel3:21 But if you do warn that righteous person to stop doing bad things

Ezekiel4:1 ‘Son of man
Ezekiel4:2 Show that soldiers are ready to attack the city. Build heaps of earth around it so that nobody can escape. Put heavy pieces of wood around the city that the enemy could use to break its walls.
Ezekiel4:3 Then you must do this. Take a flat plate of iron and put it between you and the brick. It will be like a wall between you and the city. Look towards the city. Show that soldiers are around the city and they are ready to attack it. Nobody can escape. This will be a picture to show Israel's people what will happen to them.
Ezekiel4:4 Then lie down on your left side. Show that you are receiving the punishment for the sins of the Israelites. You will suffer like that for 390 days. It will show the number of years that I have decided to punish Israel's people. Each day that you suffer will show one year of their punishment.
Ezekiel4:5 
Ezekiel4:6 After you have finished all those days
Ezekiel4:7 Continue to look towards the brick which has the picture of Jerusalem on it. Show that you are ready to attack it
Ezekiel4:8 Listen! I will use ropes to tie around you. You will not be able to turn from one side to another. You will lie on one side until you have finished all the days to attack the city.
Ezekiel4:9 You must take some grains like wheat and barley
Ezekiel4:10 You should eat 220 grams of food each day. Eat it at the same time each day.
Ezekiel4:11 You must measure the water that you drink each day. You may drink two cups at the same times each day.
Ezekiel4:12 Prepare your food like a cake that is made from barley. Bake it on a fire that you have made with human dung. Do this where the people can see you.’

I will run after them with a sword.
Ezekiel5:3 But keep a few of your hairs. Tie them into the edge of your clothes.
Ezekiel5:4 Finally
Ezekiel5:5 I
Ezekiel5:6 But Jerusalem's people have refused to obey my rules and my commands. They have become more wicked than the nations and countries around them. They have not obeyed my laws.

Ezekiel5:8 So this is what the Almighty Lord says: I myself have become your enemy
Ezekiel5:9 I will punish you for all the disgusting things that you have done. I have never punished anyone like that before. And I will never do it again.
Ezekiel5:10 When I punish you like that
Ezekiel5:11 The Almighty Lord says this: I promise you as surely as I live that I will remove you. You have made my holy place unclean. You have put your disgusting idols in my house. You have done wicked things there. Because of that I will not be sorry for you. I will not save you.
Ezekiel5:12 Illness or famine will kill one third of your people inside the city. The soldiers who are around the city will kill a third of your people. And I will chase away a third of them to all parts of the earth. I will cause their enemies to run after them with their swords.
Ezekiel5:13 When I have done all that to them
Ezekiel5:14 I will cause your city to become a heap of stones. People of the other nations around you will laugh at you when they see what has happened to you.
Ezekiel5:15 They will all laugh at you and insult you. They will see that I was very angry with you and I punished you. Then they will be afraid of my great anger. I
Ezekiel5:16 I will take away your bread so that famine kills more and more of you. It will seem that I am shooting arrows at you to destroy you.
Ezekiel5:17 Famine or wild animals will kill you. Your children will no longer live. Illness and war will destroy you. I will send soldiers to attack you. I
