Ezekiel7:1 The Lord gave this message to me:
Ezekiel7:2 ‘Son of man
Ezekiel7:3 The time has come for your final punishment! You will know that I am very angry with you. I will punish you because of the bad way that you have lived. You will receive the punishment that you deserve for all the disgusting things that you have done.
Ezekiel7:4 I will not be sorry for you. I will not be kind to you. I will punish you as you deserve. You have done disgusting things. So I will punish you and you will know that I am the Lord.
Ezekiel7:5 This is what the Almighty Lord says: Terrible trouble is coming to you! It will be worse than any other trouble.
Ezekiel7:6 The end has now come! It is finally here to destroy you! Look out!
Ezekiel7:7 You people who live in the land will now receive terrible punishment. The day for that to happen is very near. There will be no more sound of happy parties on the mountains. Instead there will be shouts of fear.
Ezekiel7:8 Very soon
Ezekiel7:9 I will not be sorry for you. I will not be kind to you. I will punish you as you deserve
Ezekiel7:10 Yes
Ezekiel7:11 You use violence to help you to do wicked things. None of all those people will escape. None of their riches or valuable things will remain.
Ezekiel7:12 The day of punishment has now come! The person who buys things will no longer be happy. Nor will the person who sells things. My anger will bring pain to all of them.
Ezekiel7:13 Any man who sells his land will never get it back as long as he lives. God has shown what he will do to all those people. He will not change his mind. Each one will lose his life because of their sins.
Ezekiel7:14 The soldiers make a noise with trumpets to prepare for war. But nobody goes out to fight a battle because my anger has brought punishment for all the people.
Ezekiel7:15 The enemy is ready to attack outside the city. Inside the city
Ezekiel7:16 Those who remain alive will escape. They will run to the mountains. They will be like doves that hunters have chased out of the valleys. They will cry aloud because of their sins.
Ezekiel7:17 Their hands will be weak. Their knees will shake.
Ezekiel7:18 They will wear sackcloth. They will be very afraid. Their faces will show that they are ashamed. They will cut all the hair from their heads.
Ezekiel7:19 They will throw their silver away in the streets. They will think that their gold has no value
Ezekiel7:20 They were proud of their beautiful jewels. They used them to make disgusting idols and images. That is why I
Ezekiel7:21 I will allow foreigners and wicked people to take those things from them. They will use those valuable things in a disgusting way.
Ezekiel7:22 Those foreigners and robbers will go into my holy temple. They will cause it to become unclean. But I will look away and I will allow them to do that.
Ezekiel7:23 Prepare chains to tie my people. The whole land is full of murder and violence.
Ezekiel7:24 I will bring the most wicked people from other nations to attack. They will take the houses of my people and they will live in them. The strong men among my people will no longer boast. Enemies will spoil the holy places where my people worshipped.
Ezekiel7:25 Terrible trouble is coming to my people! When they are afraid
Ezekiel7:26 Many troubles will happen
Ezekiel7:27 The king will be very sad. His son will not hope for anything good to happen. The people will shake with fear. I will punish them as they deserve for the way that they have lived. I will judge them in the same way that they have judged other people. Then they will know that I am the Lord.’

Ezekiel16:1 The Lord gave this message to me:
Ezekiel16:2 ‘Son of man
Ezekiel16:3 Say to them
Ezekiel16:4 They did not take proper care of you on the day that you were born. They did not wash you properly. They did not cut your cord. They did not put salt or oil on your skin. They did not put cloths around you.
Ezekiel16:5 Nobody was kind enough to do any of these things for you. Instead
Ezekiel16:6 Then I saw you as I went that way. You were lying there on the ground in your blood. You were trying to move. While you lay there
Ezekiel16:7 I made you grow well
Ezekiel16:8 Later
Ezekiel16:9 Then I washed your body with water. I washed away the blood and I put sweet oil on your body.
Ezekiel16:10 I put a beautiful dress on you. I put the best leather shoes on your feet. I dressed you with clothes made from linen and other expensive cloths.
Ezekiel16:11 I put beautiful jewels on you. I put rings on your hands and a necklace around your neck.
Ezekiel16:12 I put a gold ring in your nose. I put rings in your ears. And I put a beautiful crown on your head.
Ezekiel16:13 I used gold and silver to make you beautiful. I gave you expensive clothes that were made of linen and other pretty cloth. I gave you good food to eat
Ezekiel16:14 The people of other nations heard news about your beauty. That was because of all the good things that I had given you to make you very beautiful. That is what the Almighty Lord says.
Ezekiel16:15 But you trusted your beauty to give you what you wanted. Because you were famous
Ezekiel16:16 You used some of your clothes to give honour to idols. You made their altars beautiful. You had sex with those men there on your beautiful clothes. Those things should never happen!
Ezekiel16:17 You took your beautiful gold and silver rings and your jewels. You used them to make images of male idols for yourself. You worshipped them instead of me.
Ezekiel16:18 You put your beautiful clothes on them. You gave them my oil and my incense as an offering.
Ezekiel16:19 You also offered to those idols the food that I had given to you
Ezekiel16:20 You also offered your sons and your daughters as sacrifices to those idols. They were my children too! But you offered them as food to your idols. Like a prostitute
Ezekiel16:21 But you did an even worse thing. You killed my children. You offered them as sacrifices to idols!
Ezekiel16:22 You forgot what you were like when I first found you. At that time you had no clothes to cover your bare body. You lay on the ground in your own blood. You did not remember this. Instead
Ezekiel16:23 Terrible trouble will come to you! That is what the Almighty Lord says. You did even more wicked things than these.
Ezekiel16:24 In the public places of every town you built huts to worship your idols.
Ezekiel16:25 You built these high places at the end of every street. There you made your beauty a disgusting thing. You offered your body to anyone who went past. Everyone knew that you were a prostitute.
Ezekiel16:26 You had sex with the Egyptians
Ezekiel16:27 So I decided to punish you. I took from you things that belonged to you. I gave your enemies
Ezekiel16:28 You had sex with the Assyrians
Ezekiel16:29 You also had sex with the traders from Babylon. Like a prostitute
Ezekiel16:30 The Almighty Lord says this: Your mind no longer knows what is right. You do disgusting things
Ezekiel16:31 You built places to worship your idols on every street. You offered yourself to people
Ezekiel16:32 You are like a wife who is not faithful. You want to have sex with strangers more than with your own husband!
Ezekiel16:33 Prostitutes receive money when they have sex with someone. Instead
Ezekiel16:34 Those men did not come and ask you to have sex with them. So you are different from other prostitutes. You paid money to those men. They did not pay money to you. So you are very different from those other women!
Ezekiel16:35 So listen to the Lord 's message
Ezekiel16:36 This is what the Almighty Lord says: You wanted to have sex with many men. You offered your bare body to them
Ezekiel16:37 So listen to me! I will bring all those men that you had sex with. You liked some of them and you hated others. I will bring all of them to stand around you. Then I will remove all your clothes and they will all see your completely bare body.
Ezekiel16:38 I will punish you as you deserve
Ezekiel16:39 I will put you under the power of those men that you gave yourself to. They will destroy the places that you built for your idols. They will remove all your clothes. They will take your beautiful jewels. Then they will leave you with nothing
Ezekiel16:40 They will bring a crowd of people to attack you. They will throw stones at you. They will cut you in pieces with their swords.
Ezekiel16:41 They will use fire to destroy your houses. Many women will watch while those people punish you. I will stop you from having sex as a prostitute. You will no longer pay men to have sex with you.
Ezekiel16:42 I will continue to punish you until I am no longer angry with you. I will not be upset or angry any more.
Ezekiel16:43 The Almighty Lord says this: I am punishing you as you deserve for all the things that you have done. You have forgotten how I helped you when you were young. You have done many disgusting things and you have also lived like a prostitute.
Ezekiel16:44 Look! Everyone says that this proverb is true about you: A daughter will do the same things that her mother does.
Ezekiel16:45 Yes
Ezekiel16:46 Your older sister was Samaria. She and her daughters lived north of you. Your younger sister was Sodom. She and her daughters lived south of you.
Ezekiel16:47 You lived like them and you did all the same disgusting things that they did. But you quickly became even worse than them.
Ezekiel16:48 Your sister Sodom and her daughters never did such wicked things as you and your daughters have done. That is true as surely as I live
Ezekiel16:49 Listen to me! This is the wicked thing that your sister Sodom did. She and her daughters were very proud. They had plenty of food to eat and they enjoyed life. But they did not help people who were poor and weak.
Ezekiel16:50 They were proud and they did whatever they wanted to do. When I saw the disgusting things that they were doing
Ezekiel16:51 Samaria's people did less than half as many sins as you have done. You have done many more disgusting things than they did. The many wicked things that you have done make your sisters seem righteous!
Ezekiel16:52 So now you must accept that you are guilty. You have done such wicked things that your sisters think that they are good people. You have done much worse things than they ever did. I must punish you even more than I punished them. So you should be ashamed and you should accept your punishment. You have made your sisters seem to be righteous.
Ezekiel16:53 But one day I will cause Sodom and her daughters to enjoy life again. I will also cause Samaria and her daughters to enjoy life again. And I will also do that for you
Ezekiel16:54 You will be sorry and ashamed that you did such wicked things. Your sisters will be happy that their punishment was less than yours.
Ezekiel16:55 Sodom and her daughters will become as happy as they were before. Samaria and her daughters will become as happy as they were before. And you and your daughters will also become as happy as you were before.
Ezekiel16:56 In the days when you were proud of yourselves
Ezekiel16:57 But now everyone can see how wicked you are. So the other nations around you laugh at you. The people of Syria and Philistia and the nations near to them all insult you.
Ezekiel16:58 You must accept your punishment because of the disgusting things that you have done. You have not been faithful to me. That is what the Almighty Lord says.
Ezekiel16:59 This is what the Almighty Lord says: You have not done the things that you promised to do when I made a covenant with you. So I will punish you as you deserve.
Ezekiel16:60 But I will remember the covenant that I made with you when you were still young. I will make that covenant strong and it will continue for ever.
Ezekiel16:61 Then you will remember the wicked things that you have done and you will be ashamed. You will welcome again your sisters
Ezekiel16:62 I will cause the covenant that I make with you to be strong. Then you will know that I am the Lord.
Ezekiel16:63 I will forgive you for your sins so that you are no longer guilty. Then you will remember your sins. You will be too ashamed to say anything. That is what the Almighty Lord says.” ’

Ezekiel18:1 The Lord gave this message to me:
Ezekiel18:2 ‘You say this proverb about Israel: “The fathers have eaten bitter fruit
Ezekiel18:3 Now I
Ezekiel18:4 Everyone who lives belong to me. I have authority over the life of the father and over the life of the son. It is the people who do wrong things who will die because of their sins.
Ezekiel18:5 There may be a truly good man who does what is right and fair.
Ezekiel18:6 He does not go up the mountains and eat the food of idols. He does not pray to the idols that other Israelites worship. He does not have sex with another man's wife. He does not have sex with a woman when she is unclean.
Ezekiel18:7 He is not cruel to other people. When he lends money to someone
Ezekiel18:8 He does not lend money to someone and then ask to receive more money back. He refuses to do anything that is wrong. He helps people in a way that is right and fair.
Ezekiel18:9 He faithfully obeys my laws and my rules. A man like that is righteous. He will continue to live. That is what the Almighty Lord says.
Ezekiel18:10 But that man may have a son who is cruel and violent. He may kill other people. He may do those bad things that his father never did.
Ezekiel18:11 He may go up the mountains and eat the food of idols. He might have sex with another man's wife.
Ezekiel18:12 He might be cruel to people who are poor and weak. He might take things that belong to other people. He might not give back things that he took as a guarantee. He prays to idols. He does disgusting things.
Ezekiel18:13 When he lends money to people
Ezekiel18:14 But this wicked man might have a son himself. The son sees all the sins that his father has done. He thinks about them. Then he decides that he will not live as his father has lived.
Ezekiel18:15 He does not go up the mountains to eat the food of idols. He does not pray to the idols that other Israelites worship. He does not have sex with another man's wife.
Ezekiel18:16 He is not cruel to anyone. He does not keep anything that he has taken from someone as a guarantee. He does not rob other people. But he gives food to hungry people. He gives clothes to poor people.
Ezekiel18:17 He refuses to do anything that is wrong. When he lends money to people
Ezekiel18:18 But his father will die as punishment for his own sins. He cheated people. He robbed people. He did evil things among his own people. So he will die because of his own sins.
Ezekiel18:19 You should not say
Ezekiel18:20 It is the person who does wrong things who will die. A son will not receive punishment because of his father's sins. And a father will not receive punishment because of his son's sins. Righteous people will receive the good things that they deserve for the good things that they have done. But wicked people will receive the punishment that they deserve for the wicked things that they have done.
Ezekiel18:21 But a wicked person might turn away from the sins that he has been doing. He might decide to obey my laws and do things that are right and good. If he does that
Ezekiel18:22 I would no longer say that he is guilty because of the sins that he has done. Instead
Ezekiel18:23 It does not make me happy when a wicked person dies. I want wicked people to stop doing wicked things. Then I am happy because they will continue to live. That is what the Almighty Lord says.
Ezekiel18:24 A righteous person may stop doing things that are good and right. He may start to do the same disgusting things that wicked people do. Then he will not continue to live. I will not remember any of the good things that he did. Instead
Ezekiel18:25 But you say
Ezekiel18:26 If a righteous person stops doing good things and he does bad things instead
Ezekiel18:27 And if a wicked person stops doing wicked things and he does things that are right
Ezekiel18:28 That wicked person realized that he was doing bad things. So he turned away from his sins. Because of that he will certainly continue to live. He will not die.
Ezekiel18:29 But you
Ezekiel18:30 The Almighty Lord says this to you
Ezekiel18:31 Stop doing all the sins that you have been doing. Instead
Ezekiel18:32 It does not make me happy when anyone has to die. Turn away from your sins so that you continue to live! That is what the Almighty Lord says.’
Ezekiel19:1 ‘Sing this sad funeral song about the leaders of Israel.
Ezekiel19:2 Say this: “Your mother was like a great lion among the other lions. She lay among strong young lions. She helped her children to grow safely.
Ezekiel19:3 She taught one of her children to catch animals for food. As he grew
Ezekiel19:4 The people of other nations heard about him. They dug a hole and they caught him in their trap. They put hooks in him and they took him to Egypt.
Ezekiel19:5 His mother waited for him to return home. He did not come and she no longer had any hope. She helped another of her sons to become a strong young lion.
Ezekiel19:6 He grew to be strong among the other lions. He learned to catch wild animals and to eat them. He also learned to eat people.
Ezekiel19:7 He attacked their strong buildings and he destroyed their cities. When he roared
Ezekiel19:8 Men from other nations near there came to attack him. They threw a net over him. They caught him in their trap.
Ezekiel19:9 They tied hooks around his neck. They took him to the king of Babylon. There they put him in prison to stop him roaring. The people on the hills of Israel could not hear his voice any longer.
Ezekiel19:10 Your mother was like a vine that was growing near a stream. It grew well because there was plenty of water there. It made many branches and it had a lot of fruit.
Ezekiel19:11 Its branches became big and strong. They were strong enough to make a ruler's stick. The vine grew taller than all the other trees around it. Everyone could see that it was very tall and strong.
Ezekiel19:12 But the Lord became angry with that vine. He pulled up its roots and he threw it down to the ground. The east wind made it dry and destroyed its fruit. Its strong branches broke and they became useless. Fire destroyed them.
Ezekiel19:13 Now that vine is growing in a desert. It is in a hot
Ezekiel19:14 Fire started to burn its strongest branch. From there the fire destroyed its other branches and its fruit. No strong branch remained in the vine. Now there was nothing that could make a ruler's stick.” This is a funeral song. It tells a very sad story.’
Ezekiel20:1 On the tenth day of the fifth month some of Israel's leaders came to me. That was in the seventh year that we had been prisoners in Babylon. The leaders sat in front of me. They wanted me to ask for a message from the Lord.
Ezekiel20:2 Then the Lord gave this message to me:
Ezekiel20:3 ‘Son of man
Ezekiel20:4 Son of man
Ezekiel20:5 Say to them
Ezekiel20:6 On that day
Ezekiel20:7 I said to them
Ezekiel20:8 But your ancestors did not obey me. They refused to listen to me. None of them destroyed their disgusting idols. They continued to worship the idols of Egypt. So I decided to show them that I was very angry. I said that I would punish them there in Egypt.

Ezekiel39:9 Then those who live in Israel's towns will go out. They will pick up the weapons and they will burn them. They will burn the small and large shields
Ezekiel39:10 They will not have to go into the fields or forests to find wood. They will rob things from those who robbed them
Ezekiel39:11 They will bury Gog in the valley east of the sea. They will bury Gog and all his armies there. So men will not be able to walk through this valley. They will call it the Valley of Hamon Gog (Gog's army).
Ezekiel39:12 Israel's people will need seven months to bury all the dead bodies. Then the land will be clean.
Ezekiel39:13 All the people in the country will bury the dead bodies. They will always remember that day
Ezekiel39:14 They will choose some men to clean the land. They will travel through all the country. Other men will bury the dead bodies that remain on the ground. They will begin to do this at the end of the seven months.
Ezekiel39:15 The men who walk through the country will find dead men's bones. They will put a sign by each one. Then the men who bury the bodies will bury them in the Valley of Hamon Gog.
Ezekiel39:16 (A town called Hamonah is also there.) That is how they will make the land clean.”
Ezekiel39:17 Son of man
Ezekiel39:18 You will eat the meat of great men and you will drink the blood of kings. You will eat them like the fat sheep
Ezekiel39:19 At this sacrifice
Ezekiel39:20 I will give you horses and their riders to eat. I will give you great men and many kinds of soldiers to eat
Ezekiel39:21 I will show my glory to all the nations. They will see that I can punish people. And they will know that I decide how and when to punish them.
Ezekiel39:22 From that time and for all the time after that
Ezekiel39:23 And the nations will know that I sent Israel's people away from their country. They will know that I punished my people because of their sin. They had not been faithful to me so I turned away from them. I let their enemies take them and kill them with swords.
Ezekiel39:24 I punished them because they had not kept themselves clean from sin. I hid myself from them because they had done so many evil things.’
Ezekiel39:25 So this is what the Lord God says. ‘Now I will bring back Jacob's descendants from the countries where they have been prisoners. I will be kind to all Israel's people. And I will make them see that I am holy.
Ezekiel39:26 They will live safely in their own land. Nobody will cause them to be afraid. In the past
Ezekiel39:27 I will bring them back from the lands where their enemies took them. The nations will see what I have done. So all the nations will know that I am holy.
Ezekiel39:28 Then Israel will know that I am the Lord their God. I sent my people away to far countries. But I will bring them all back to their own country. I will not leave any behind.
Ezekiel39:29 No longer will I turn away from them. I will pour my Spirit on Israel's people
Ezekiel40:1 In the 25th year that we had been prisoners in Babylon
Ezekiel40:2 In the vision
Ezekiel40:3 The Lord took me there. Then I saw a man who shone brightly like bronze. He was standing at the entrance of a building. He held a linen rope and a stick in his hand. He used them to measure things.
Ezekiel40:4 The man said to me
Ezekiel40:5 I saw a wall all the way round the place where the temple was. The stick in the man's hand was 3 metres long. He used it to measure the wall. The wall was 3 metres wide and 3 metres high.
Ezekiel40:6 Then he went to the gate on the east side. He climbed its steps and he measured the size of the entrance. It was 3 metres deep.
Ezekiel40:7 Beyond this was a passage with three rooms on each side. They were rooms for the guards. Each of these rooms was 3 metres long and 3 metres wide. The walls between the rooms were 2½ metres thick. Beyond them was a passage that was 3 metres long. This went to an entrance room to the yard of the temple.
Ezekiel40:8 The man measured the entrance room at the end of the passage.
Ezekiel40:9 It was 4 metres long. It had pillars that were 1 metre thick. It was the nearest part of the gate to the temple.
Ezekiel40:10 The three rooms on each side of the passage at the east gate were all the same size. The walls that were between each room were also the same size.
Ezekiel40:11 Then the man measured the passage that went through the gate's entrance. The passage was 6½ metres wide. The gate's entrance itself was 5 metres wide.
Ezekiel40:12 There was a low wall in front of the rooms on each side of the passage. It was ½ metre high. The rooms were 3 metres square.
Ezekiel40:13 The man also measured between the back wall of one room and the back wall of the room opposite. It was 12½ metres from one wall to the other wall
Ezekiel40:14 The entrance room had pillars on the outside that were 30 metres high. The yard was all around three sides of the room.
Ezekiel40:15 The passage was 25 metres long
Ezekiel40:16 The rooms for the guards all had small windows in their outside walls. The walls between the rooms also had small windows. The entrance room to the yard also had windows of the same kind. Men had cut pictures of palm trees on the inside walls of the passage.
Ezekiel40:17 The man took me through the entrance gate into the outside yard of the temple. I saw 30 rooms there
Ezekiel40:18 The path went all around the yard and it covered the space between the gates. It was called the lower path.
Ezekiel40:19 The man measured across the outside yard. He measured from the lower gate to the higher gate that went into the inside yard. The man measured 50 metres between the two gates.
Ezekiel40:20 Then the man measured the gate on the north side of the outside yard.
Ezekiel40:21 He measured the three rooms on each side of the passage and the walls between them. He also measured the entrance room itself. They all measured the same as those in the east gate. The whole entrance was 25 metres long and it was 12½ metres wide.
Ezekiel40:22 The entrance room
Ezekiel40:23 Opposite this gate
Ezekiel40:24 Next
Ezekiel40:25 The rooms in this entrance had windows that were the same as those in the other entrances. The whole entrance was 25 metres long and it was 12½ metres wide.
Ezekiel40:26 Seven steps went up to it. The entrance room to the yard was at the end of the passage. There were pictures of palm trees on the inside walls of the passage.
Ezekiel40:27 The inside yard of the temple also had a gate on the south side. The man measured 50 metres between the two gates.
Ezekiel40:28 The man took me through the south gate into the inside yard. He measured the gate. It was the same size as the gates in the outside wall.
Ezekiel40:29 The rooms for the guards
Ezekiel40:30 The entrance rooms around the inside yard were 12½ metres wide and 2½ metres long.
Ezekiel40:31 The entrance room of this gate opened towards the outside yard. There were pictures of palm trees on the walls of the passage. Eight steps went up to this gate.
Ezekiel40:32 The man took me to the east side the inside yard. He measured the gate. It was the same size as the other gates.

Ezekiel40:34 The entrance room of this gate opened towards the outside yard. There were pictures of palm trees on the walls of the passage. Eight steps went up to this gate.

Ezekiel40:36 with rooms for the guards

Ezekiel40:39 In the entrance room there were four tables
Ezekiel40:40 There were four more tables outside the entrance room of the north gate. Two tables were on each side of the steps at the entrance.
Ezekiel40:41 So there were eight tables on which they killed animals for sacrifices. Four tables were in the yard outside the entrance. Four tables were inside the entrance room.
Ezekiel40:42 They had used stone to make four tables for the burnt offerings. These tables were 50 centimetres high. Their tops were 75 centimetres square. They put the knives and the other tools that they used to kill the animals on these tables.
Ezekiel40:43 They put the meat for the offerings on the stone tables. There were hooks all around the inside walls of the room. Each hook was 7½ centimetres long.
Ezekiel40:44 There were two rooms for singers that had doors to the inside yard. One was beside the north gate and its door opened towards the south. The other room was beside the south gate and its door opened towards the north.
Ezekiel40:45 The man said to me
Ezekiel40:46 The room beside the south gate is for the priests who serve God at the altar. These priests are descendants of Zadok. They are the only descendants of Levi who may come near to the Lord to serve him.’
Ezekiel40:47 The man measured the inside yard. It was square
Ezekiel40:48 The man took me into the entrance room of the temple building. He measured the walls on each side of the entrance. They were 2½ metres thick. The entrance was 7 metres wide. The walls on each side of the entrance were 1½ metres wide.
Ezekiel40:49 The entrance room was 10 metres wide and 6 metres long. Steps went up to the entrance room. There were two pillars
Ezekiel41:1 Then the man took me into the big hall of the temple. He measured the walls on each side of the entrance. They were 3 metres thick.
Ezekiel41:2 The entrance itself was 5 metres wide. The walls on each side were 2½ metres wide. The big hall was 20 metres long and 10 metres wide.
Ezekiel41:3 Then he went into the inside room. He measured the walls at the entrance. They were 1 metre thick. The entrance was 3 metres wide. The walls on each side were 3½ metres wide.
Ezekiel41:4 He measured the inside room. It was 10 metres long and 10 metres wide. It was at the back of the big hall. The man said to me
Ezekiel41:5 Then the man measured the wall of the temple. It was 3 metres thick. There were small rooms along the outside of the wall
Ezekiel41:6 The side rooms were on three levels. Each level had 30 rooms. The temple's wall at each level was less thick than the wall below. So the wall itself held up each level of rooms. They did not have to fix the side rooms into the temple's wall.
Ezekiel41:7 The side rooms at each level were wider than the rooms on the level below them
Ezekiel41:8 I saw that there was a stone base all around the temple. The side rooms stood on this base as their foundation. It was 3 metres high.
Ezekiel41:9 The outside wall of the side rooms was 2½ metres thick. There was an open space between these rooms and the rooms that the priests used. It was 10 metres wide
Ezekiel41:10 
Ezekiel41:11 There were two doors for the side rooms. One door opened towards the north. The other door opened towards the south. They opened into a space that was 2½ metres wide.
Ezekiel41:12 There was a large building at the west side of the temple yard. It was 37 metres wide and 47 metres long. Its walls were 2½ metres thick.
Ezekiel41:13 Then the man measured the outside of the temple building. It was 50 metres long. He measured from the back of the temple across the open space to the back wall of the large building. That was also 50 metres.
Ezekiel41:14 He measured the front of the temple building and the open space on each side. That was 50 metres too.
Ezekiel41:15 He measured the building at the back of the temple yard
Ezekiel41:16 The boards covered the walls from the floor up to the windows
Ezekiel41:17 Workers had cut pictures in the wood above the doors and all over the walls. They were pictures of palm trees and cherubs. There was a picture of a palm tree between each cherub. They covered all the inside walls of the temple. Each cherub had two faces.
Ezekiel41:18 
Ezekiel41:19 A human face looked towards the palm tree on one side. A lion's face looked towards the palm tree on the other side. Workers had cut these pictures on the walls around the inside of the whole temple building.
Ezekiel41:20 The pictures of cherubs and palm trees covered all the walls
Ezekiel41:21 There were square posts on each side of the entrance to the temple's big hall. The entrance to the Most Holy Place seemed to be the same.
Ezekiel41:22 There was an altar that was made of wood. It was 1½ metres high and 1 metre wide on each side. Its corners
Ezekiel41:23 The big hall of the temple and the Most Holy Place both had doors that were in two parts.

Ezekiel41:25 Workers had cut pictures on the doors of the big hall. They were pictures of palm trees and cherubs
Ezekiel41:26 The entrance room had narrow windows in the walls on each side. The windows had pictures of palm trees on each side. Workers had cut shapes of palm trees on its walls. The side rooms around the temple also had roofs over the outside of their doors.
Ezekiel42:1 Then the man took me out through the north gate of the inside yard of the temple. We went into the outside yard. We arrived at a building that was in the north wall of the inside yard.
Ezekiel42:2 This building was 50 metres long and 25 metres wide. Its entrance was towards the north.
Ezekiel42:3 There was a 10 metre space between one side of this building and the temple in the inside yard. The building's other side was opposite the path in the outside yard. It had three levels of rooms in two rows that were opposite each other.
Ezekiel42:4 A path went between the two rows of rooms. It was 50 metres long and 5 metres wide. The entrances to the rooms were on the north side.
Ezekiel42:5 The rooms at the top level were not as wide as the rooms below them. And the rooms at the middle level were not as wide as the rooms at the lowest level. This was because the higher rooms needed more space in front of them.
Ezekiel42:6 They did not have pillars to hold them up as other rooms in the temple yards did. Instead
Ezekiel42:7 There was a wall that went across the outside yard in front of the lowest rooms. The wall was 25 metres long.

Ezekiel42:11 There was a path between the two rows. They looked the same as the rooms on the north side. The size of the rooms and the entrances were the same as those of the other building.
Ezekiel42:12 There was an entrance in the wall opposite the doors of the inside row of rooms. There was also an entrance at the east end of the path between the rows of rooms.

Ezekiel43:1 Then the man brought me to the east gate.
Ezekiel43:2 There I suddenly saw the bright glory of the God of Israel as it came from the east. His voice was like the noise of lots of water that pours along a river. The land around shone with the bright light of his glory.
Ezekiel43:3 I had seen a vision like this when I was beside the Kebar River. I had also seen a vision like this when God came to destroy Jerusalem. I bent down low with my face on the ground.
Ezekiel43:4 The Lord 's bright glory went through the east gate into the temple.
Ezekiel43:5 Then the Spirit lifted me up and took me into the temple's inside yard. As I watched
Ezekiel43:6 While the man stood beside me
Ezekiel43:7 The Lord said to me
Ezekiel43:8 Their kings built their palaces too near to my holy temple. There was only a wall between me and them. They did many disgusting things that spoiled the honour of my holy name. As a result
Ezekiel43:9 Now they must be faithful to me. They must no longer worship other gods. They must not give honour to the graves of their kings. If they stop doing these things
Ezekiel43:10 Son of man
Ezekiel43:11 Then they will be ashamed of everything that they have done. Explain the temple's plan to them. Describe its shape
Ezekiel43:12 This is the most important law about the temple: All the top of the mountain around the temple will be most holy.
Ezekiel43:13 This is the size of the altar. There is a hole
Ezekiel43:14 The lowest part of the altar
Ezekiel43:15 This top part is 2 metres high. They burn the sacrifices on this top part. Its four corners have points like horns that go up.
Ezekiel43:16 The top of the altar is square
Ezekiel43:17 The middle part is also square
Ezekiel43:18 Then he said to me
Ezekiel43:19 You must give a young bull to the priests to offer as a sacrifice to me. The priests must belong to Zadok's family in the tribe of Levi. They are the only men who can come near to me to serve me. That is what I
Ezekiel43:20 You will take some of the bull's blood and put it on the four horns on the top of the altar. Also put some blood on the corners of the middle part of the altar. And put some on the border around the edge of the altar. Do that to make the altar clean and pure for me.
Ezekiel43:21 You must take the bull to burn as a sin offering. Burn it in the proper place in the temple yard
Ezekiel43:22 The next day
Ezekiel43:23 After you have done that
Ezekiel43:24 Bring them to me
Ezekiel43:25 Each day for seven days
Ezekiel43:26 The priests will make the altar clean and pure on each of those seven days. That will make it ready for holy use.

Ezekiel44:1 Then the man brought me back to the outside gate on the east side of the temple yard. But the gate was shut.
Ezekiel44:2 The Lord said to me
Ezekiel44:3 Only Israel's ruler is allowed to sit in the gate's entrance. He may sit there to eat a holy meal in front of the Lord. He may only go in and out through the entrance room of that gate.’
Ezekiel44:4 Then the man took me through the north gate of the temple yard. We went to the front of the temple building. As I watched
Ezekiel44:5 The Lord said to me
Ezekiel44:6 Israel's people refuse to obey me. So
Ezekiel44:7 You brought foreigners into my temple. They were not circumcised and they did not want to belong to me. In that way
Ezekiel44:8 You did not take care of my holy things yourselves. Instead you chose foreigners to take care of my holy place on your behalf.
Ezekiel44:9 This is what the Almighty Lord God says: No foreigner may go into my holy place if he is not circumcised and he does not want to obey me. Even if that foreigner lives among my people
Ezekiel44:10 As well as other Israelites
Ezekiel44:11 But they may continue to serve me in the work of the temple. They can be guards at the temple gates. They can kill the animals that people bring for burnt offerings and other sacrifices. They can help the people who come to worship me.
Ezekiel44:12 But I promise that I will punish them as they deserve for their sin. That is because they helped the people to worship their idols. In that way
Ezekiel44:13 They must not come near to me to serve me as the priests do. They must not touch any of my holy things or the most holy offerings. Instead
Ezekiel44:14 But I will allow them to take care of the temple and all the ordinary work that is done there.
Ezekiel44:15 Only the priests who are descendants of Zadok
Ezekiel44:16 Only they can come into my holy place and serve me at the altar. They will do what I tell them to do.
Ezekiel44:17 When they come through the gates into the inside yard
Ezekiel44:18 They must wear linen cloths around their heads. The trousers that they wear next to their skin must also be made of linen. They must not tie anything around their body that makes them sweat.
Ezekiel44:19 Before they go out to the outside yard to meet the ordinary people
Ezekiel44:20 The priests must not cut all the hair off their heads. They must not let it grow long either. They must keep their hair short.
Ezekiel44:21 When a priest goes into the inside yard of the temple
Ezekiel44:22 Priests must not marry a widow
Ezekiel44:23 They must teach my people that holy things are different from ordinary things. They must also show them how to know if something is clean or unclean.
Ezekiel44:24 If people have an argument
Ezekiel44:25 A priest must not go near to a dead person. It would make him unclean. But if the dead person is his father
Ezekiel44:26 After this
Ezekiel44:27 Then he can go into the inside yard of the temple to serve me there. On the day that he goes back there
Ezekiel44:28 Do not give the priests their own land in Israel to keep for their descendants. I myself will be the gift that they receive. I will take care of them and their families.
Ezekiel44:29 They will receive the grain offerings
Ezekiel44:30 The best of all the first crops from people's land and all their special gifts will belong to the priests. You must also give to the priests the first flour that you make from your grain. Then I will bless the people who live in your house.
Ezekiel44:31 The priests must not eat any bird or any animal that people find dead. They must not eat anything that a wild animal has killed.” ’
Ezekiel45:1 ‘You must divide the land into different parts to belong to each tribe. When you do this

Ezekiel45:3 Inside the holy piece of land
Ezekiel45:4 The priests will have this holy part of the land. They are the men who work in the temple and come near to the Lord to serve him. The priests' houses will be there
Ezekiel45:5 The other half of the holy piece of land will be for the Levites
Ezekiel45:6 There will also be a piece of land for the city

So his land will be as long as a part of the land that belongs to each tribe.
Ezekiel45:8 This will be the part of Israel's land that belongs to the ruler. Because my rulers will have their own land
Ezekiel45:9 The Almighty Lord says this: Rulers of Israel
Ezekiel45:10 You must use correct measures for size and for weight.
Ezekiel45:11 The ephah that you use to measure dry things must be the same measure that you use for wet things. Ten ephahs will be equal to one homer.
Ezekiel45:12 20 gerahs will be equal to one shekel. 60 shekels will be equal to one mina.
Ezekiel45:13 This is the offering that you must give. Take from your harvest one homer from every 60 homers of wheat or barley.
Ezekiel45:14 Take one litre of olive oil from every 100 litres.
Ezekiel45:15 Take one sheep from every 200 sheep that feed on the grass in Israel. These gifts will be for grain offerings
Ezekiel45:16 All Israel's people must bring these offerings to the ruler of Israel.
Ezekiel45:17 The ruler must provide the animals for the burnt offerings. He must provide the grain for the grain offerings and the wine for the wine offerings. He must provide these things for the holy festivals that I have chosen for Israel's people. Those include feasts for the new moon and for the Sabbath days. He must provide these things for the sin offerings
Ezekiel45:18 This is what the Almighty Lord says: Each year
Ezekiel45:19 The priest will take some of the blood of this sin offering and he will put it on the posts of the temple doors. He must also put some of the blood on the four corners on the top of the altar. He must also put some blood on the gate posts at the entrance to the inside yard of the temple.
Ezekiel45:20 You must also do this on the seventh day of the first month. That will cause me to forgive anyone who has done a wrong thing by mistake. Perhaps they did not know that it was a sin. In this way you will make the temple a clean place for me.

Ezekiel45:22 On the first day of the festival

Ezekiel47:1 The man brought me back to the entrance of the temple. There I saw water that was pouring out from under the entrance. It was pouring towards the east. Ezekiel47:2 The man took me out through the north gate of the temple. We went around the outside to the gate that opens towards the east. There I saw a small stream of water. It was coming out from the south side of the gate.
Ezekiel47:3 The man went towards the east. He measured 500 metres in that direction. He took me through the water there. The water was as high as my ankles.
Ezekiel47:4 Then he measured another 500 metres along the stream. He took me through the water again. Here
Ezekiel47:5 Then he measured another 500 metres. Here the water had become a deep river. It was too deep for me to go across. Nobody could walk through it. They would need to swim.
Ezekiel47:6 The man said to me
Ezekiel47:7 When I got there
Ezekiel47:8 He said to me
Ezekiel47:9 Wherever the river goes
Ezekiel47:10 Men will stand on the edge of the sea to catch fish. They will use their nets to catch fish
Ezekiel47:11 But the water in the pools and the wet places around the Salt Sea will not become pure. There will still be salt for people there.
Ezekiel47:12 All kinds of fruit trees will live on each side of the river. Their leaves will never become dry. They will always give fruit every month. This is because the water that feeds them comes from the holy temple. The fruit from these trees will provide food. And their leaves will make sick people well.’

Ezekiel47:23 Give them land among the tribe where they have decided to live.’ That is what the Almighty Lord says.
Ezekiel48:1 ‘This is the list of Israel's tribes
Ezekiel48:2 South of Dan's land will be the land for Asher.
Ezekiel48:3 South of Asher's land will be the land for Naphtali.
Ezekiel48:4 South of Naphtali's land will be the land for Manasseh.
Ezekiel48:5 South of Manasseh's land will be the land for Ephraim.
Ezekiel48:6 South of Ephraim's land will be the land for Reuben.
Ezekiel48:7 South of Reuben's land will be the land for Judah.
Ezekiel48:8 South of Judah's land will be a special part of the land
Ezekiel48:9 The part that belongs to the Lord for his temple will be 12½ kilometres long and 10 kilometres wide.
Ezekiel48:10 The priests will have a part of this holy land. Their part will measure 12½ kilometres from east to west. It will measure 5 kilometres from north to south. The Lord 's temple will be in the middle of this part.
Ezekiel48:11 This holy part of the land will belong to the priests who are descendants of Zadok. They are the priests who served me faithfully. They did not turn away from me as the other Levites did when Israel's people did not obey me.
Ezekiel48:12 So the priests will have that special part of the land
Ezekiel48:13 The Levites will also have a special part
Ezekiel48:14 The special part of the land that belongs to the Lord is the best of the whole land. You must not give his land to anyone else or sell it to anyone else. It is holy and it belongs to the Lord.
Ezekiel48:15 The other part of the special land is 12½ kilometres from east to west and 2½ kilometres from north to south. That part is not holy. Ordinary people may use it. They may live there and use the fields. There will be a city in the middle of it.
Ezekiel48:16 The city will be square
Ezekiel48:17 You must leave an open space 125 metres wide on each side of the city.
Ezekiel48:18 There will be two other pieces of land south of the special holy land. There will be a piece 2½ kilometres wide on the east side of the city and a piece 2½ kilometres wide on the west side. People who live in the city may use these pieces of land to grow crops.
Ezekiel48:19 There will be people from the different tribes of Israel who live in the city. Any of them may use the land to grow things.
Ezekiel48:20 The whole of the special land
Ezekiel48:21 The parts of land on each side of the square will belong to the ruler. The part on the east side will go as far as Israel's border in the east. The part on the west side will go as far as the Mediterranean Sea. The holy part of land where the temple is will be in the middle
Ezekiel48:22 The land that belongs to the Levites and the priests
Ezekiel48:23 The other tribes will each have a part of the land that is south of this special part. The land for each of these tribes will go all the way from Israel's border in the east to the Mediterranean Sea in the west. South of the special land will be the land for Benjamin.
Ezekiel48:24 South of Benjamin's land will be the land for Simeon.
Ezekiel48:25 South of Simeon's land will be the land for Issachar.
Ezekiel48:26 South of Issachar's land will be the land for Zebulun.
Ezekiel48:27 South of Zebulun's land will be the land for Gad.
Ezekiel48:28 The border on the south side of Gad's land goes from Tamar to the springs of water near Meribah Kadesh. From there
Ezekiel48:29 That is how you must divide the land among Israel's tribes. Each part will belong to one tribe and their descendants. I
Ezekiel48:30 These will be the entrance gates on each side of the city. The wall on the north side of the city will be 2
Ezekiel48:31 There will be three gates on that side. Each gate will have the name of one of Israel's tribes
Ezekiel48:32 The wall on the east side will also be 2
Ezekiel48:33 The wall on the south side will also be 2
Ezekiel48:34 The wall on the west side will also be 2
Ezekiel48:35 So the walls all around the city will measure 9
Daniel1:1 King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came to Jerusalem with his army. He put his soldiers around the city to attack it. This happened when Jehoiakim had been king of Judah for nearly three years.
Daniel1:2 The Lord God gave Nebuchadnezzar power over King Jehoiakim. Nebuchadnezzar took away some special things that belonged in God's temple in Jerusalem. He took those things back to Babylon. He put them among the valuable things in the temple of his own god.
Daniel1:3 Nebuchadnezzar had an officer who was called Ashpenaz. Ashpenaz had authority over the king's other officers. One day
Daniel1:4 Nebuchadnezzar wanted young men who were strong and handsome. They must be clever so that they could learn and understand things quickly. They must be good enough to become King Nebuchadnezzar's servants. They would learn the language of Babylon
Daniel1:5 Every day
Daniel1:6 Some of the young men from Judah that Ashpenaz chose were Daniel
Daniel1:7 The king's officer gave new names to them. Daniel was called Belteshazzar. Hananiah was called Shadrach. Mishael was called Meshach. Azariah was called Abednego.
Daniel1:8 But Daniel decided that God did not want him to eat the king's food and wine. He knew that it might be against God's rules. So he asked the king's special officer that he might not have to eat the king's food.
Daniel1:9 God had already caused the officer to like Daniel and to be kind to him.
Daniel1:10 But the officer said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 The officer had chosen a guard to watch Daniel
Daniel1:12 He said
Daniel1:13 After ten days
Daniel1:14 The guard agreed to what they said. He watched them for ten days.
Daniel1:15 After ten days
Daniel1:16 So the guard no longer gave them the king's food and wine to eat. He gave vegetables and water to them instead.
Daniel1:17 God helped these four young men to become clever and wise. While they studied
Daniel1:18 King Nebuchadnezzar had said that the young men must study for three years. After that time finished
Daniel1:19 The king spoke with all the young men. He discovered that there was nobody else like Daniel
Daniel1:20 Whenever the king needed help to understand something important
Daniel1:21 Daniel lived in Babylon until the year when Cyrus became king.

Daniel5:27 Tekel means “weighed”. It shows that God has weighed you. But the scales show that you are too light.

Daniel6:1 King Darius decided to choose 120 men to rule the regions of his nation. Together
Daniel6:2 He also chose three other men to be his officers. He gave them authority over the 120 rulers. The king wanted to be sure that they were working well. Daniel was one of the three officers.
Daniel6:3 Daniel did his work much better than the other officers and rulers. He was very good at his job. Daniel was so good that the king wanted to make him ruler over the whole kingdom.
Daniel6:4 The other officers and rulers were jealous. They wanted to find something bad about Daniel so that they could tell the king. They watched how Daniel worked to serve the king. But they could not find that he did any wrong thing. That was because Daniel was good and honest. He always did everything properly.
Daniel6:5 Then these men said to each other
Daniel6:6 So the officers and rulers agreed together what they would do. They went to the king and they said

Daniel6:9 King Darius agreed. He wrote his name on this new law.
Daniel6:10 Daniel heard that the king had written this new law. Then he went home. He went upstairs to his room where the windows opened in the direction of Jerusalem. He bent down on his knees and he prayed to God
Daniel6:11 Then the officers and rulers who were jealous of Daniel went to see what he would do. They saw that Daniel was praying to God and he was asking God for help.

Daniel6:17 They took a big stone and they covered the top of the lions' cave. The king put a mark on the stone with his ring of authority. He also marked it with the rings of his important men. This meant that nobody could move the big stone to save Daniel.
Daniel6:18 Then the king returned to his palace. He ate no food. Nobody brought anything to him to give him pleasure. He was unable to sleep.
Daniel6:19 The king got up very early in the morning
Daniel6:20 When he got near to the cave
Daniel6:21 Then Daniel said to the king
Daniel6:22 My God sent his angel
Daniel6:23 The king was very happy when he heard Daniel's voice. He told his servants that they must pull Daniel up out of the lions' cave. So they lifted him out. When they looked at him
Daniel6:24 Then the king gave a command to his servants. They brought the jealous men who had said bad things against Daniel. They threw them into the lions' cave
Daniel6:25 After that
Daniel6:26 I have made a law for every part of my kingdom. All people must respect the God that Daniel serves. They must respect him with fear. He is the God who always lives. He will continue for ever. Nobody will ever destroy his kingdom. He will rule for ever.
Daniel6:27 He rescues his people and he keeps them safe. He does powerful miracles in the heavens and on the earth. He has saved Daniel from the lions' power!’

Daniel7:7 Then

Daniel7:9 While I looked
Daniel7:10 A river of fire poured out from the place where he was sitting. Thousands of servants were ready to do whatever he wanted. Many more thousands stood around

Daniel8:5 While I was thinking about this
Daniel8:6 It came towards the male sheep that had two horns. I had seen that sheep as it stood beside the river. The goat was very angry. It ran to attack the sheep with all its strength.
Daniel8:7 I saw it as it attacked the sheep. It was so angry that it hit the sheep very hard. It broke off the sheep's two horns. The sheep had no power to stop the goat. The goat knocked the sheep down to the ground and it stamped on the sheep. Nobody could save the sheep from the power of the goat.
Daniel8:8 The male goat boasted even more about its power. Its large horn became very strong. But then the horn broke. Four other large horns came up in its place. Each of these horns pointed in a different direction.
Daniel8:9 Then a little horn grew out of one of these large horns. It grew to become very big. Its power reached towards the south
Daniel8:10 Its power became so great that it reached up into heaven. It attacked heaven's army and the stars in the sky. It threw some of them down to the ground and it stamped on them.
Daniel8:11 It boasted that it was more powerful than the Captain of heaven's army. It stopped people from offering their sacrifices to God each day. It destroyed God's temple.

Daniel11:1 I myself helped Michael in the first year that Darius ruled as king. I fought with Michael against his enemies.’
Daniel11:2 Then the angel said to Daniel
Daniel11:3 Then a great king will appear. He will rule with great power. He will do whatever he wants to do.
Daniel11:4 While he rules powerfully like that
Daniel11:5 Then the king who rules in the south will become strong. But one of his own army officers will become even stronger. That officer will rule a greater kingdom than the king of the south.
Daniel11:6 After some years
Daniel11:7 Later
Daniel11:8 He will take the images of their gods back to Egypt with him. He will also take away valuable things that they had made from gold and silver. Then for several years
Daniel11:9 After that
Daniel11:10 Then his sons will prepare to fight a war. They will bring together a large army. Their soldiers will march forward like a powerful river. They will fight a battle all the way into the strong place of their enemy.
Daniel11:11 Then the king of the south will be very angry. He will march with his army to fight against the king of the north. The king from the north will also have a large army
Daniel11:12 The king of the south will boast that he has destroyed this large army. He will be proud that he has killed thousands of people. But he will not continue to win battles.
Daniel11:13 The king of the north will again bring together a large army. It will be even bigger than the army that he had before. After a few years
Daniel11:14 At that time
Daniel11:15 Then the king from the north will march south with his army. He will build up heaps of earth around the walls of a strong city. He will take the city for himself. The soldiers of the kingdom in the south will not be able to stop him. Even their best soldiers will not be strong enough to win the fight.
Daniel11:16 The king who attacked from the north will do whatever he wants to do. Nobody will be able to stop him. He will march into the Beautiful Land. He will have the power to destroy it.
Daniel11:17 The king from the north will decide to march south with all his kingdom's strong army. He will make an agreement with the king of the south. He will give one of his daughters to marry the king of the south. He will try to destroy that kingdom
Daniel11:18 After this
Daniel11:19 So the king of the north will return to the strong cities in his own country. But he will lose his power and he will fall. Nobody will ever see him again.
Daniel11:20 Then another king will rule in the north. He will try to make his kingdom even greater. He will send out a cruel man to take taxes from people. But that king will soon die. This will not happen in a battle or because people are angry with him.
Daniel11:21 The king who rules after him will be an evil man. He will not belong to the royal family
Daniel11:22 He will quickly destroy any army that tries to attack him. He will even kill the leader of God's covenant.
Daniel11:23 He will agree to be friends with other leaders. But then he will deceive them. He will become more and more powerful
Daniel11:24 His army will suddenly attack a region that is very rich. The king will do things that none of his ancestors did. He will take valuable things from his enemies when he fights against them. He will share those things with the men who help him. He will make plans to attack strong cities. But he will only be able to do this for a short time.
Daniel11:25 The evil king of the north will bring together a large
Daniel11:26 Even those who eat the king's food will try to kill him. Many of his soldiers will die in the battle. His army will run away.
Daniel11:27 These two kings will sit at the same table to make plans. But they will deceive each other with evil lies. Their plans will be useless. God has already decided when their time as kings will finish.
Daniel11:28 Then the king of the north will return to his own country. He will take many valuable things with him. As he returns
Daniel11:29 At the time that God chooses
Daniel11:30 People will come in ships from the west to attack him. He will be afraid and he will turn back. He will be angry as he travels home. So he will cause more trouble for the people who obey God's covenant. He will give honour to those people who turn away from the holy covenant.
Daniel11:31 He will send his soldiers to attack Jerusalem's strong place. They will go into the holy place of the temple and they will make it unclean. They will stop people from offering the sacrifices each day. They will put a disgusting thing that causes trouble in the holy place.
Daniel11:32 The king will deceive the people who are no longer faithful to God. He will make them help him. But the people who are still faithful to God will work hard to stop him.
Daniel11:33 Wise Israelite leaders will teach many other people. But these teachers will suffer for some time. People will kill them in battle
Daniel11:34 When God's people are in a time of trouble
Daniel11:35 Some of the wise leaders will suffer. As a result
Daniel11:36 The king will do whatever he wants to do. He will say that he is greater than any god. He will even say proud things to insult the God who rules all other gods. He will be successful until the time when God's anger finishes. God will finish doing what he has decided to do.
Daniel11:37 This king will not respect the gods that his ancestors worshipped. He will not even respect the god that women love. He will not respect any god. He will think that he is greater than all the gods.
Daniel11:38 Instead
Daniel11:39 He will ask a foreign god to help him as he attacks the strongest cities of his enemies. He will give great honour to those people who accept him as their leader. He will give them authority to rule many people. He will give them land because they have helped him.
Daniel11:40 At the time of the end
Daniel11:41 He will march into the Beautiful Land. He will kill many thousands of the people there. But these people will escape from his power: the people of Edom and Moab
Daniel11:42 He will have power over many countries. The country of Egypt will not escape.
Daniel11:43 He will take away from Egypt the gold
Daniel11:44 But he will become afraid when he hears news from the east and the north. It will cause him to be very angry. He will go with his army to destroy many people.
Daniel11:45 He will put up his royal tents between the seas

Joel1:9 There is no grain or wine that we can bring as offerings to the Lord 's temple. So the priests who serve the Lord are weeping.

Joel2:2 It will be completely dark on that day. Black clouds will cover the whole earth. A great army is coming. Like darkness it covers the mountains. No army has ever been as powerful as this one is. There will never be an army like it in the future.
Joel2:3 As they move
Joel2:4 They look like horses. They are like horses that are running to a battle.
Joel2:5 They make a noise like chariots as they jump over the tops of the mountains. They make the sound of a fire as it burns dry grass. They are like a great army that is preparing for a battle.
Joel2:6 Nations are very afraid when they see this army. Everyone's face becomes white with fear.
Joel2:7 The great army runs forward to attack. Like soldiers
Joel2:8 They do not push against each other. Each one of them goes straight forward. No weapons are able to stop them as they move forward together.
Joel2:9 They quickly attack a city and they run over its walls. They climb into the houses. as they go through the windows like robbers.
Joel2:10 The earth shakes as they move forward. Even the sky itself shakes. The sun and moon become dark. The stars no longer give any light.
Joel2:11 The Lord 's voice shouts like thunder as he leads his army. His soldiers are too many to count. They obey all his commands. The day of the is great and terrible! It seems that nobody will remain alive.
Joel2:12 The Lord says
Joel2:13 Show me that you are really upset. Do not only tear your clothes. That is not enough.’ Return to the Lord your God. He is kind and he forgives people. He does not quickly become angry
Joel2:14 No one knows what the Lord your God will do. Perhaps he will change his mind. Perhaps he will not punish you as he has said he will. After that he may bless you. He may give you grain and wine
Joel2:15 Make a loud noise with a trumpet in Zion! Tell the people that they must fast. Tell them to meet to worship the Lord.
Joel2:16 Bring all the people together. Prepare them to worship the Lord. Bring the old people together

Joel2:18 Then the Lord showed that he loved his land. He chose to be kind to his people.

Joel2:20 I will take the army from the north far away from you. I will send it to a dry land where nothing grows. Those who march in the front of that army I will send into the Salt Sea in the east. And I will send those who are at the back into the Mediterranean Sea in the west. Their dead bodies will make a disgusting smell.’ Yes

Jonah1:4 Then the Lord sent a strong wind on the sea. The storm was so powerful that it almost broke the ship into pieces.
Jonah1:5 The sailors were very frightened. Each of them called out to his own god for help. They threw into the sea all the things that the ship carried. They wanted to make the ship as light as they could. Jonah had gone down into the bottom of the ship. He was lying down and he was asleep.

Jonah3:6 The king of Nineveh heard about God's message. He got up from his throne and he removed his royal clothes. He dressed himself in sackcloth and he sat in ashes on the ground.
Jonah3:7 Then the king sent this message to all the people in Nineveh. He said

Jonah3:9 Then perhaps God may agree to forgive us. He may decide that he will not be angry with us. Then perhaps we will not all die.’

Zechariah11:17 Terrible things will happen to the useless shepherd who leaves his sheep. His enemy's sword will cut his arm and his right eye. His arm will become weak and useless. His right eye will become completely blind.’

Malachi1:6 The Lord Almighty says this to the priests: ‘A son respects his father. A servant respects his master. If I am really your father and your master
Malachi1:7 You insult my name when you offer unclean sacrifices to me on my altar. But you ask
Malachi1:8 You offer blind animals as a sacrifice to me. You know that is wrong. You offer to me animals that cannot walk or animals that are ill. That is also a wrong thing to do. You would not give gifts like that to your city's ruler. If you did
Malachi1:9 ‘Now you ask me to be kind to you. You want me to forgive you. But when you offer unclean animals as gifts to me
Malachi1:10 ‘It would be better if one of you shut the temple doors! Then you would not be able to offer useless sacrifices on my altar. I am not happy with you. I will not accept any more gifts from you.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says.
Malachi1:11 ‘People in every nation of the whole world will respect my name. People everywhere will offer incense and pure sacrifices to worship me. They will all know that I am great.’ That is what the Lord Almighty says.
Malachi1:12 ‘But you priests insult my name. You say that the Lord's altar is not special. You think that the food which you offer to me has no value.

Matthew1:1 This is a list of the ancestors of Jesus Christ. Jesus was a descendant of King David. David was a descendant of Abraham.
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
Matthew1:3 Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah. Tamar was their mother. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Ram.
Matthew1:4 Ram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
Matthew1:5 Salmon was the father of Boaz. Rahab was his mother. Boaz was the father of Obed. Ruth was his mother. Obed was the father of Jesse.
Matthew1:6 Jesse was the father of King David. David was Solomon's father. Solomon's mother was the wife of Uriah.
Matthew1:7 Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asa.
Matthew1:8 Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Jehoram. Jehoram was the father of Uzziah.
Matthew1:9 Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amon. Amon was the father of Josiah.
Matthew1:11 Josiah was the father of Jeconiah and his brothers. This was at the time when Babylon's army took the Israelites away to Babylon as their prisoners.
Matthew1:12 Here is a list of Jesus' ancestors who lived after that time: Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
Matthew1:14 Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Akim. Akim was the father of Eliud.
Matthew1:15 Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
Matthew1:16 Jacob was the father of Joseph. Joseph married Mary. Mary was the mother of Jesus. Jesus is the one who is called Christ
Matthew1:17 So there were 14 generations from Abraham to David. There were another 14 generations from David to the time they took the Israelites away to Babylon. Then there were 14 generations from that time until Jesus
Matthew1:18 This is how Jesus Christ was born. His mother's name was Mary. She had promised to marry a man called Joseph. Then she discovered that she was pregnant. This happened before they started to live together. It was the Holy Spirit that caused this to happen to her.
Matthew1:19 Joseph was a good man. He wanted to do what was right. He did not want Mary to become ashamed in front of people. So he decided to stop their marriage secretly.
Matthew1:20 He thought about what he should do. Then an angel came from the Lord God to see him. The angel came to Joseph while he was dreaming. The angel said
Matthew1:21 Mary will give birth to a son. He will save his people from their sins. Because of that
Matthew1:22 The Lord God had given a message about this to one of his prophets long ago. Now that message would become true.
Matthew1:23 The prophet had said
Matthew1:24 Then Joseph woke up from his sleep. He did what God's angel had said to him. He married Mary and he took her to his home.
Matthew1:25 Joseph did not have sex with Mary until after the baby boy was born. He said that the boy's name should be Jesus.

Matthew2:17 In this way

Matthew3:1 While Jesus still lived in Nazareth
Matthew3:2 He told people
Matthew3:3 God's prophet Isaiah spoke about this man John long ago. He said: ‘Somebody's voice is shouting in the wilderness
Matthew3:4 John wore clothes made from the hair of a camel. He also wore a belt made from leather. His usual food was locusts and honey from the wilderness.
Matthew3:5 Many people went to listen to John. They lived in Jerusalem and all the other places in Judea and near the Jordan River.
Matthew3:6 The people agreed that they had done wrong things. Then John baptized them in the Jordan River.
Matthew3:7 Many Pharisees and Sadducees came to John. They wanted John to baptize them. He said to them
Matthew3:8 You have to show that you are sorry. Change how you live. Stop doing things that God does not like.
Matthew3:9 Do not say to yourselves
Matthew3:10 You are like trees that have bad fruit. God has an axe ready to use. He will cut down every tree that does not make good fruit. He will throw those trees into the fire.
Matthew3:11 I baptize you with water. This shows that you want to change the way that you live. But another person will come soon. He is greater than I am. I am not good enough even to carry his shoes for him. This other person will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
Matthew3:12 He is like a farmer who brings the wheat home from his field. He uses a special tool to throw the wheat up in the air. He does this to make the wheat seeds separate from what remains. Then he cleans his yard. He carefully stores all the seeds to keep them safe. But he burns everything else in a great fire that nobody can stop.’
Matthew3:13 Then Jesus went from Galilee to the Jordan River. He wanted John to baptize him.
Matthew3:14 But John tried to stop him. He said to Jesus
Matthew3:15 Jesus replied
Matthew3:16 When John baptized him
Matthew3:17 Then a voice spoke from heaven

Matthew4:2 Jesus did not eat anything for 40 days and 40 nights. At the end of this time
Matthew4:3 Then the devil came to Jesus and said to him
Matthew4:4 But Jesus replied
Matthew4:5 Then the devil led Jesus to Jerusalem. He made him stand on the highest part of the temple.
Matthew4:6 He said to Jesus
Matthew4:7 Jesus replied
Matthew4:8 Then the devil led Jesus up a very high mountain. He showed Jesus all the kingdoms in the whole world. They were very great and beautiful.
Matthew4:9 The devil said
Matthew4:10 Jesus then said to him
Matthew4:11 Then the devil left Jesus alone. Immediately angels came to Jesus and they took care of him.
Matthew4:12 Then Jesus heard that Herod had put John the Baptist in prison. So Jesus returned to Galilee.
Matthew4:13 But he did not stay in Nazareth. He went to live in Capernaum
Matthew4:14 This caused God's message to become true. Long ago God had said through his prophet Isaiah:
Matthew4:15 ‘I am speaking to you people who live in Zebulun and Naphtali. Your land is on the way to Lake Galilee. It is across the Jordan River in Galilee
Matthew4:16 They are like people who are living in a dark place. They live in the shadow which death brings. Now God will send a light to shine on them.’
Matthew4:17 Then Jesus began to speak God's message to people. He said
Matthew4:18 One day
Matthew4:19 Jesus said to them
Matthew4:20 Simon and Andrew immediately put their nets down and they went with Jesus.
Matthew4:21 They continued to walk along the shore. Soon Jesus saw two other men who were brothers. They were called James and John. They were in their boat with their father
Matthew4:22 The brothers immediately left the boat and their father. They went with Jesus to be his disciples.
Matthew4:23 Jesus travelled everywhere in Galilee. He taught the people in the Jewish meeting places. He told them the good news about God's kingdom. He also caused sick people to become well again from all their different pains and illnesses.
Matthew4:24 More and more people in all parts of Syria heard about Jesus. So they brought all the sick people to see him. These people had many different illnesses
Matthew4:25 Because of this
Matthew5:1 Jesus saw that crowds of people were following him. So he went up a hill and he sat down there to teach them. His disciples came near to listen to him.
Matthew5:2 He began to teach them. This is what he said:
Matthew5:3 ‘Happy are the people who know that they need God very much. The kingdom of heaven belongs to people like that.
Matthew5:4 Happy are the people who are sad now. God will come near to help them.
Matthew5:5 Happy are the people who are kind. The whole earth will belong to them.
Matthew5:6 Happy are the people who want to obey God
Matthew5:7 Happy are the people who are kind and who forgive other people. God will forgive people like that.
Matthew5:8 Happy are the people who have pure thoughts. They will come near to God and see him.
Matthew5:9 Happy are the people who help people to live in peace. God will call them his children.
Matthew5:10 Happy are the people who do what is right but other people hurt them because of it. The kingdom of heaven belongs to people like that.
Matthew5:11 Yes
Matthew5:12 God is preparing many good things for you in heaven. So you should be very happy when people do these bad things to any of you. People did these same bad things to God's prophets who lived long ago.’
Matthew5:13 Jesus said
Matthew5:14 You are like a light for everybody in this world. If people build a city on a hill
Matthew5:15 Nobody lights a lamp and then puts it under a pot. He will put the lamp in a high place. Everybody who is in the house can then see the light from the lamp.
Matthew5:16 You must be sure that your light shines well. Then you will give light to other people. They will see all the good things that you do. Then they will praise God
Matthew5:17 Jesus said
Matthew5:18 I tell you this. One day
Matthew5:19 A person may choose not to obey a rule in God's Law. He may think it is the least important rule. He may also teach other people to think like that. Then God will call him the least important person in the kingdom of heaven. But a person may choose to obey all the rules in God's Law. And he may teach other people to obey them too. God will call that person great in the kingdom of heaven.
Matthew5:20 I tell you this. You must always do what is right. You must live in a way that is better than the teachers of God's Law and the Pharisees. You will never come into the kingdom of heaven if you do not do better than them.’
Matthew5:21 Jesus then said
Matthew5:22 But what I now say to you is this. Anybody who is angry with his brother will have to stand in front of a judge. If someone calls his brother by a bad name
Matthew5:23 Maybe you go to the temple to give your gift to God. You take it to the altar. But then you remember that your brother is angry with you.
Matthew5:24 You must leave your gift there in front of God's altar. First you must go and find your brother. Tell him that you are sorry. Then you can both become friends with each other again. After that
Matthew5:25 Also
Matthew5:26 I tell you this. You will remain in prison until you have paid all the money
Matthew5:27 Jesus then said
Matthew5:28 But what I tell you is this: A man may look at a woman who is not his wife. And he may want to have sex with her. In his thoughts he has had sex with her
Matthew5:29 If your right eye causes you to do wrong things
Matthew5:30 Also
Matthew5:31 ‘ God's Law also says: “Any man who wants to send his wife away must give her a letter. This letter must say that they are no longer married.”
Matthew5:32 But what I tell you is this: A man must not send his wife away except for one reason. He may only send her away if she has had sex with another man. If he sends her away for any other reason
Matthew5:33 ‘You also know the rule that God gave to your ancestors: “If you make a promise to the Lord God
Matthew5:34 But what I tell you is this. Do not use any name to make a promise stronger. Do not use the words “by heaven ” to make a promise stronger. Heaven is the throne where God sits to rule. Do not use the words “by the earth” to make a promise stronger. The earth is the place where God rests his feet.
Matthew5:35 Do not use the words “by Jerusalem” to make a promise stronger. Jerusalem is God's city. He is the great King who rules from there.
Matthew5:36 Do not use the words “by my head” to make a promise. You cannot make the colour of even one of your hairs black or white.
Matthew5:37 Instead
Matthew5:38 Jesus then said
Matthew5:39 But what I tell you is this. If somebody does something bad to you
Matthew5:40 Maybe somebody wants to take you to the judge. He says that you should give him your shirt. Give the man your shirt
Matthew5:41 Or a soldier may tell you
Matthew5:42 When people ask you for something
Matthew5:43 You have heard people say
Matthew5:44 What I tell you is this: You should love the people who want to hurt you. If people cause you to have trouble
Matthew5:45 If you live like this
Matthew5:46 Do you only love people who love you? God will not give you good things just for doing that. Even the men who take taxes love their friends!
Matthew5:47 If you are only kind to your friends
Matthew5:48 So you must be good in every way

Matthew6:2 When you give something to a poor person
Matthew6:3 So when you give something to a poor person

Matthew6:5 ‘Also
Matthew6:6 But this is how you should pray. Go to a place in your house where you can be alone. You can shut the door and you can pray to God your Father. He is in that secret place. He sees what you do there. In return
Matthew6:7 When you pray
Matthew6:8 Do not pray like they do. God your Father already knows what you need. He knows this even before you ask him.
Matthew6:9 So this is how you should pray: “God

Matthew6:11 Please give us the food that we need today.
