Matthew6:18 Then other people will not know that you are doing a special thing. But God
Matthew6:19 Do not want to have many valuable things here in the world. Insects or water can destroy those kinds of things. Or people may come into your house and they can rob you of those things.
Matthew6:20 Instead
Matthew6:21 Then you will be thinking a lot about heaven
Matthew6:22 Your eyes are like lamps and your body is like a room. Make sure that your eyes are like a clean lamp
Matthew6:23 If your eyes are not good
Matthew6:24 Jesus said
Matthew6:25 So I tell you this. You should not worry about how to stay alive. Do not worry about the food and drink that you need. Do not worry about the clothes that you need to wear. Your life is more important than the food that you eat. Your body is more important than your clothes.
Matthew6:26 Think about the wild birds. They do not plant seeds in the ground. They do not cut down plants to eat. They have no buildings to store food. But God
Matthew6:27 Even if you always worry about your life
Matthew6:28 You should not worry about your clothes. Think about how the wild flowers grow. They do not work or make clothes for themselves.
Matthew6:29 But I tell you this. Even one wild flower is more beautiful than King Solomon was. And King Solomon wore the most beautiful clothes.
Matthew6:30 It is God that gives beautiful clothes even to the grass. One day the grass is growing in the field
Matthew6:31 So do not worry about these things. Do not always say
Matthew6:32 People who do not know God are always trying to get these things. But as for you
Matthew6:33 Instead
Matthew6:34 So do not worry about what might happen tomorrow. Tomorrow will have its own problems. It is enough for you to be thinking each day about the problems of that day.’

Matthew7:5 Do not be like that person. You think that you are better than your friend. But you are not. First

Matthew7:13 ‘Go in through the narrow gate to get true life with God. There is a wide gate that is easy to go through. The wide path is easy to travel on. Many people find that wide gate
Matthew7:14 It is difficult to go through the small gate. And it is difficult to walk on the narrow road. But when you do go that way
Matthew7:15 ‘Some people say that they are prophets from God. But their words are false. They seem to be like sheep that are not dangerous. But they are really like hungry wild dogs. What they teach will hurt you.
Matthew7:16 You will know these people by the way that they live. The things that they do are like their fruit. Grapes do not grow on thorn bushes. Figs do not grow on weeds.
Matthew7:17 Good fruit grows on a tree that is good. Bad fruit grows on a tree that is not good.
Matthew7:18 A good tree cannot make bad fruit. A bad tree cannot make good fruit.
Matthew7:19 A farmer will cut down any tree that does not make good fruit. He will burn it on a fire.
Matthew7:20 In the same way

Matthew7:26 Other people listen to my message but they do not obey it. Those people are like a fool who built his house on sand.
Matthew7:27 Then a storm came with a lot of rain and wind. The rivers rose up high. Strong winds blew hard against the house. That house fell down. The storm destroyed it completely.’

Matthew10:19 When they take you to stand in front of their leaders
Matthew10:20 You will not be speaking your own words. The Spirit of your Father God will give you his own words to say.
Matthew10:21 At that time
Matthew10:22 All people will hate you because you are my disciples. But God will save the person who remains strong until the end.
Matthew10:23 If people in any town hurt you because of me
Matthew10:24 A student is not more important than his teacher is. A servant is not more important than his master is.
Matthew10:25 A student should be happy if he becomes like his teacher. A servant should be happy if he becomes like his master. People have called me
Matthew10:26 So do not be afraid of these people. They hide the things that they do. But there will be a time when other people will see those things. Everyone will know all their secrets.
Matthew10:27 I tell you things secretly
Matthew10:28 Do not be afraid of those people who can kill only your body. These people cannot kill your soul. No
Matthew10:29 Think about this. People sell two birds for just one small coin. But God takes care of these little birds. Not one of them can fall to the ground unless God allows it to happen.
Matthew10:30 God even knows how many hairs there are on your head.
Matthew10:31 So do not be afraid of those people who are against you. You are more valuable to God than many little birds.
Matthew10:32 You should say clearly to other people
Matthew10:33 But if you say to other people
Matthew10:34 I will tell you why I have come into the world. I did not come so that everyone would agree with each other. No
Matthew10:35 A man will fight against his father. A daughter will fight against her mother. A woman will fight against her husband's mother.
Matthew10:36 A man's own family will fight against him.
Matthew10:37 Nobody should love his own father and mother more than he loves me. If he does
Matthew10:38 If you really want to be one of my disciples
Matthew10:39 Whoever wants to keep his life safe will lose it. But whoever agrees to lose his life because of me will have true life.’

Matthew10:41 Somebody may accept a prophet into his home because the visitor is a prophet of God. God will bless that person as he blesses a prophet. Or somebody may accept a good man who obeys God

Matthew13:1 On that same day
Matthew13:2 The crowd that came to listen to him was very large. So he went and sat in a boat that was on the water. The big crowd of people stood on the shore.
Matthew13:3 Jesus used stories to teach them many things. He said to them: ‘Listen to me! A farmer went out to plant seeds in his field.
Matthew13:4 While he was throwing the seeds
Matthew13:5 Other seeds fell on ground which had rocks. There was not much soil in that place. The seeds quickly began to grow
Matthew13:6 But when the sun rose
Matthew13:7 Some other seeds fell among thorn bushes. Those bushes grew up with the young plants. They stopped the seeds from growing into strong plants.
Matthew13:8 But some seeds fell on good soil and strong plants grew from those seeds. Some plants made 100 new seeds. Some plants made 60 new seeds and some plants made 30 new seeds.’
Matthew13:9 Then Jesus said to the crowd
Matthew13:10 Then Jesus' disciples came to him. They asked him
Matthew13:11 Jesus replied
Matthew13:12 A person who has received some things will receive even more. He will have lots of good things. Some other people have nothing. Those people will lose even the little bit that they do have.
Matthew13:13 The reason I use stories to talk to other people is this: These people look
Matthew13:14 Long ago
Matthew13:15 These people do not want to understand. They are like people who have shut their ears. They are like people who have shut their eyes. If they did want to look
Matthew13:16 Then Jesus said to his disciples
Matthew13:17 I tell you this. Many prophets and good people from a long time ago wanted to see these things. But they did not see the things that you are seeing. They wanted to hear the message that you are hearing. But they did not hear it.’
Matthew13:18 Jesus then said
Matthew13:19 Some people hear the message about God's kingdom but they do not understand it. Satan comes and he quickly takes the message away from their minds. The seeds that fell on the path show what those people are like.
Matthew13:20 Other seeds fell on soil with rocks in it. This is like some people who hear God's message. These people are happy to believe it for a time.
Matthew13:21 But they are like plants that have not put roots down into the soil. So they only believe for a short time. They may have problems. Or other people may do bad things to them
Matthew13:22 Some seeds fell among thorn bushes. This is like people who hear the message from God. But they have many troubles in their minds. They want to get more money and more things. They think that this will make them happy. So they do not let God's message change them. They are like plants that do not grow well and make new seeds.
Matthew13:23 But some seeds fell on good soil. This is like other people who hear the message from God and they understand it. These people are like good plants that grow well. From one seed
Matthew13:24 Then Jesus told the people another story. He said
Matthew13:25 But one night
Matthew13:26 The good seeds grew and the plants began to make new grain. But when this happened
Matthew13:27 So the farmer's servants came to him. They asked him
Matthew13:28 The farmer said to his servants
Matthew13:29 The farmer replied
Matthew13:30 Let the good plants and the weeds grow up together. At harvest time
Matthew13:31 Jesus told the people another story. He said
Matthew13:32 It is the smallest seed that there is. But when it starts to grow
Matthew13:33 Jesus told the people another story. He said

Matthew24:8 All those things will be like the first pains that a mother has before her baby is born.

Matthew24:19 That will be a bad time for women who are soon to have a baby! It will also be a bad time for those women who have little babies!
Matthew24:20 You must pray to God about this time when you have to run away quickly. Pray that this time of trouble will not happen in winter. Pray that it will not happen on your day of rest.
Matthew24:21 Great troubles will happen to people at that time. Nothing as bad has ever happened before

Matthew24:31 People will hear the sound of a loud trumpet. The Son of Man will send out his angels. They will go in every direction. They will bring together all the people that God has chosen from every part of the earth and sky.

Matthew24:33 In the same way

Matthew24:38 In Noah's time

Matthew24:40 At that time

Matthew24:42 So you must watch carefully! You do not know the day when your Master will come back.
Matthew24:43 You can be sure about this. The master of a house does not know at what time of night a man would come to rob him. If he knew the time
Matthew24:44 So you must also be ready. The Son of Man will come at a time when you are not thinking about it.’

Matthew24:47 Yes

Matthew24:50 Then the master of that servant will come home and he will surprise the servant. The servant did not think that his master would come home on that day or at that time.
Matthew24:51 Then the master will punish him a lot. He will put the bad servant with those people who do not obey him. The people in that place will cry and they will bite their teeth together.’

Matthew27:4 He said to them

Mark3:1 Another time

Mark4:3 ‘Listen to me! A farmer went out to plant seeds in his field.
Mark4:4 While he was throwing the seeds
Mark4:5 Other seeds fell on ground which had rocks. There was not much soil in that place. The seeds quickly began to grow
Mark4:6 But when the sun rose
Mark4:7 Other seeds fell among thorn bushes. Those bushes grew up with the young plants. They stopped the seeds from growing into strong plants. So the plants could not make any new seeds.
Mark4:8 But some seeds fell on good soil. Good strong plants grew from these seeds. Some plants made 30 new seeds. Some plants made 60 new seeds. And some plants made 100 new seeds.’

Mark4:11 Jesus said to them

Mark4:15 Some seeds fell on the path. That is like some people who listen to the message. But then Satan comes and he quickly takes the message away from their minds.
Mark4:16 Some of the seeds fell on soil with rocks in it. This is also like some people who hear God's message. These people are happy to believe it for a time.
Mark4:17 But they are like plants that have not put roots down into the soil. So they only believe for a short time. They may have problems. Or because they obey God's message
Mark4:18 Some seeds fell among thorn bushes. This is like some other people who hear God's message.
Mark4:19 But they have many troubles in their minds. They think that more money and other valuable things will make them happy. So they do not let God's message change them. They are like plants that do not grow well and make new seeds.
Mark4:20 But some seeds fell on good soil. This is like other people who listen to the message from God. They understand the message and they believe it. These people are like good plants that grow well. From one seed

Luke1:4 People have already taught you about these things. Now you can be sure that they are true.
Luke1:5 There was a man whose name was Zechariah. He lived when Herod was king of Judea. Zechariah was a priest and he belonged to a group of priests from the family of Abijah. He had a wife called Elizabeth. She also was a descendant of Aaron.
Luke1:6 Zechariah and Elizabeth obeyed all God's commands and rules. God saw that they did what was right. They did not do wrong things.
Luke1:7 But they had no children. Elizabeth could not give birth and they were both getting old.

Luke3:1 This report is about John
Luke3:2 At this time
Luke3:3 John went to many places near the Jordan River and he spoke a message from God. He taught people
Luke3:4 The prophet Isaiah wrote this message from God in his book: ‘Somebody's voice is shouting in the wilderness
Luke3:5 Fill in every valley. Make every mountain and hill flat. Take away every bend so that the road is straight. Take away all the rocks so that the road is flat.
Luke3:6 Then everyone will see how God can save them.” ’
Luke3:7 Crowds of people were going out into the wilderness. They wanted John to baptize them. He told them
Luke3:8 You have to show that you are sorry. Change how you live. Stop doing things that God does not like. Do not begin to say to yourselves
Luke3:9 You are like trees that have bad fruit. God has an axe ready to use. He will cut down every tree that does not make good fruit. He will throw those trees into the fire.’
Luke3:10 People in the crowd asked John
Luke3:11 John answered
Luke3:12 In the crowd
Luke3:13 John told them
Luke3:14 Then some soldiers asked John
Luke3:15 At this time people were thinking
Luke3:16 John said to the people
Luke3:17 He is like a farmer who brings the wheat home from his field. He uses a special tool to throw the wheat up in the air. He does this to make the wheat seeds separate from the chaff. Then he cleans his yard. He carefully stores all the seeds to keep them safe. But he burns everything else in a great fire that nobody can put out.’
Luke3:18 John said many more things to the people. He was telling them the good news about how God could change their lives.
Luke3:19 One day John told Herod
Luke3:20 So then Herod did an even worse thing. He locked John up in prison.
Luke3:21 While John was still baptizing all the people
Luke3:22 The Holy Spirit came down onto Jesus. When he came down like that
Luke3:23 Jesus was about 30 years old when he began his work. People thought that he was the son of Joseph. Joseph was from the family of Heli.
Luke3:24 Heli was from the family of Matthat. Matthat was from the family of Levi. Levi was from the family of Melchi. Melchi was from the family of Jannai. Jannai was from the family of Joseph.
Luke3:25 Joseph was from the family of Mattathias. Mattathias was from the family of Amos. Amos was from the family of Nahum. Nahum was from the family of Esli. Esli was from the family of Naggai.
Luke3:26 Naggai was from the family of Maath. Maath was from the family of Mattathias. Mattathias was from the family of Semein. Semein was from the family of Josech. Josech was from the family of Joda.
Luke3:27 Joda was from the family of Joanan. Joanan was from the family of Rhesa. Rhesa was from the family of Zerubbabel. Zerubbabel was from the family of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was from the family of Neri.
Luke3:28 Neri was from the family of Melchi. Melchi was from the family of Addi. Addi was from the family of Cosam. Cosam was from the family of Elmadam. Elmadam was from the family of Er.
Luke3:29 Er was from the family of Joshua. Joshua was from the family of Eliezer. Eliezer was from the family of Jorim. Jorim was from the family of Matthat. Matthat was from the family of Levi.
Luke3:30 Levi was from the family of Simeon. Simeon was from the family of Judah. Judah was from the family of Joseph. Joseph was from the family of Jonam. Jonam was from the family of Eliakim.
Luke3:31 Eliakim was from the family of Melea. Melea was from the family of Menna. Menna was from the family of Mattatha. Mattatha was from the family of Nathan. Nathan was from the family of David.
Luke3:32 David was from the family of Jesse. Jesse was from the family of Obed. Obed was from the family of Boaz. Boaz was from the family of Salmon. Salmon was from the family of Nahshon.
Luke3:33 Nahshon was from the family of Amminadab. Amminadab was from the family of Ram. Ram was from the family of Hezron. Hezron was from the family of Perez. Perez was from the family of Judah.
Luke3:34 Judah was from the family of Jacob. Jacob was from the family of Isaac. Isaac was from the family of Abraham. Abraham was from the family of Terah. Terah was from the family of Nahor.
Luke3:35 Nahor was from the family of Serug. Serug was from the family of Reu. Reu was from the family of Peleg. Peleg was from the family of Eber. Eber was from the family of Shelah.
Luke3:36 Shelah was from the family of Cainan. Cainan was from the family of Arphaxad. Arphaxad was from the family of Shem. Shem was from the family of Noah. Noah was from the family of Lamech.
Luke3:37 Lamech was from the family of Methuselah. Methuselah was from the family of Enoch. Enoch was from the family of Jared. Jared was from the family of Mahalalel. Mahalalel was from the family of Cainan.
Luke3:38 Cainan was from the family of Enosh. Enosh was from the family of Seth. Seth was from the family of Adam. Adam was from God.
Luke4:1 Then Jesus returned from the Jordan River. He was full of the Holy Spirit and the Holy Spirit led him into the wilderness.
Luke4:2 He stayed there for 40 days and he did not eat anything. During this time the devil tried to cause Jesus to do wrong things. At the end of 40 days
Luke4:3 The devil said to Jesus
Luke4:4 Jesus replied
Luke4:5 After that
Luke4:6 He said to Jesus
Luke4:7 You must bend down low and worship me. Then I will give you power over the whole world.’
Luke4:8 Jesus replied
Luke4:9 The devil now took Jesus to Jerusalem. He took him to stand on the highest part of the temple. Then he said to Jesus
Luke4:10 You know that the Bible says: “God will tell his angels to keep you safe.
Luke4:11 They will hold you safely in their hands. They will not let you hurt your foot on a stone.” ’
Luke4:12 Jesus replied
Luke4:13 In those ways
Luke4:14 Then Jesus returned to Galilee. The Holy Spirit continued to give him power. Everyone who lived near there heard the news about him.
Luke4:15 He taught people in the Jewish meeting places. Everyone said good things about him.
Luke4:16 Jesus went to Nazareth
Luke4:17 They gave him the scroll that had the messages of God's prophet Isaiah. Jesus opened the scroll. He found the place where Isaiah wrote:
Luke4:18 ‘The Spirit of the Lord God is on me. He has chosen me to tell good news to poor people. He has sent me to tell people who are in prison
Luke4:19 He has sent me to say

Luke4:22 Everyone was talking about Jesus. They were surprised at the good way in which he spoke. They said to each other
Luke4:23 Jesus then said to them
Luke4:24 I tell you this. People do not accept a prophet of God who comes from their own town.
Luke4:25 What I tell you is true. There were many widows in the country of Israel when Elijah was alive. At that time
Luke4:26 But God did not send Elijah to help a widow in Israel. Instead
Luke4:27 Here is another example. When God's prophet Elisha was alive
Luke4:28 The people in the meeting place heard what Jesus said. They became very angry.
Luke4:29 They stood up and they made him leave the town. They took him to the top of the hill outside the town. They wanted to throw him over the edge to the bottom.
Luke4:30 But Jesus walked through the middle of the crowd and he went away.
Luke4:31 Jesus went to a town in Galilee called Capernaum. On the Jewish day of rest
Luke4:32 The people were very surprised at the things that Jesus taught them. He spoke with authority.
Luke4:33 There was a sick man in the meeting place. He had a spirit of a bad demon inside him. The spirit caused the man to shout loudly.
Luke4:34 He said
Luke4:35 Jesus said to the spirit
Luke4:36 All the people were very surprised and they said to each other
Luke4:37 So they began to tell everyone about Jesus. Because of this
Luke4:38 Jesus then left the meeting place and he went to Simon's home. The mother of Simon's wife was ill and her body was very hot. So they asked Jesus to make her well.
Luke4:39 Jesus came and he stood near her. He told the illness to go away and immediately she was well again. She got up and she prepared food for her visitors.
Luke4:40 When the sun was going down
Luke4:41 Also
Luke4:42 Early the next morning
Luke4:43 Jesus replied
Luke4:44 Then Jesus travelled to many towns in Judea. He went into the Jewish meeting places and he taught people there.

Luke5:33 Then the Jewish leaders said to Jesus: ‘The disciples of John the Baptist often choose to fast for a time. Then they can pray to God a lot. The disciples of the Pharisees also do that. But your disciples never do that. They continue to eat and drink every day.’

Luke6:13 In the morning
Luke6:14 Simon (Jesus gave him the name Peter)
Luke6:15 Matthew (he was also called Levi)

Luke6:17 After Jesus had chosen these 12 men
Luke6:18 These people had all come to hear Jesus teach. Those people who were ill wanted him to make them well. He also made well those people who had bad spirits.
Luke6:19 All the people were trying to touch him
Luke6:20 Jesus looked at his disciples. He spoke this message to them: ‘Happy are you people who are poor. The kingdom of God belongs to you.
Luke6:21 Happy are you people who are hungry now. God will feed you until you are full. Happy are you people who are crying now. Later
Luke6:22 People may hate you
Luke6:23 God will prepare many good things for you in heaven. So be very happy when people do these bad things to you! Jump up and down because you are so happy. Their ancestors did the same bad things to God's prophets long ago.
Luke6:24 Listen
Luke6:25 Listen
Luke6:26 If people always say good things about you now
Luke6:27 Jesus then said
Luke6:28 Say good things to people that say bad things against you. Pray for people who give you pain.
Luke6:29 Someone may hit you on one side of your face. Then you should let him hit the other side of your face too. Someone may take away your coat. If he does
Luke6:30 When anyone asks you for something
Luke6:31 Do the good things for other people that you would want them to do for you.
Luke6:32 Do you only love people who love you? God will not praise you for doing that! Even bad people love those people who love them.
Luke6:33 Do you only do good things to people that do good things to you? God will not praise you for doing that! Even bad people do the same thing.
Luke6:34 Do you lend things only to people who will give your things back to you? God will not praise you for doing that! Even bad people lend things to other people when they believe that they will get them back again.
Luke6:35 But I am telling you to love people who want to hurt you. Do good things to them. Lend things to people even though you may not receive your things back again. Then God will prepare good things for you. He is kind even to people who do not say “thank you”. He is kind even to very bad people. If you live like this
Luke6:36 You should be kind to other people
Luke6:37 Do not judge anyone
Luke6:38 Give to other people and God will give to you. He will give to you even more than you gave. He will fill your pockets until no more will go in. They will be so full that it will come out over the top. Think about how you give to other people. God will give in the same way to you.’
Luke6:39 Jesus also used a story to teach the people. He said
Luke6:40 A student is not more important than his teacher is. But a student who has learned everything will be like his teacher.
Luke6:41 Perhaps you want to tell your friend about his little mistake. If you want to tell him
Luke6:42 If you do not do that
Luke6:43 Jesus said
Luke6:44 In this way you can know if a tree is good or bad. You can know this by the fruit that the tree makes. Figs and grapes do not grow on thorn bushes.
Luke6:45 People are also like that. A good man keeps good things in his mind. In his life
Luke6:46 Jesus said
Luke6:47 Some people come to me. They listen to my message and they obey it. Let me tell you what those people are like. They are like a man who built a house.
Luke6:48 This man dug down in the ground to the rock. He put the first line of stones on the rock. After that
Luke6:49 Some other people hear my message but they do not obey it. Those people are like another man who built a house. This man did not dig down deep to the rock. Instead

Luke7:38 She went into the house and she stood behind Jesus. She was crying and she made his feet wet with her tears. Then she used her hair to make his feet dry again. She kissed Jesus' feet and she poured the oil out of the jar onto them.

Luke8:1 After this
Luke8:2 Some women also went with them. These women had been ill
Luke8:3 Another of the women was Joanna
Luke8:4 Lots of people were coming to Jesus from many towns. A large crowd had already come together when Jesus told this story:
Luke8:5 ‘A farmer went out to plant seeds in his field. While he was throwing the seeds
Luke8:6 Some seeds fell on ground which had rocks. The seeds started to grow. But the young plants died because the ground was not wet enough in that place.
Luke8:7 Some seeds fell among thorn bushes. Those bushes grew up with the young plants. They stopped the seeds from growing into strong plants.
Luke8:8 But some seeds fell on good soil. Strong plants grew from those seeds. Each of these plants made 100 seeds.’ When Jesus finished the story
Luke8:9 Jesus' disciples asked him about this story. They asked him what it meant.
Luke8:10 Jesus said to them
Luke8:11 Jesus then said
Luke8:12 Some seeds fell on the path. That is like some people who hear the message
Luke8:13 Some seeds fell on ground with rocks in it. This is like people who hear the message from God and they are very happy for a time. But they are like plants that have not put roots down into the soil. They believe in God for a short time. But when they have problems
Luke8:14 Some seeds fell among thorn bushes. This is like some other people who hear the message from God. But they start to have many troubles in their minds about how they will live. They want to get more money and more things. They think that this will make them happy. So they do not let God's message change them. They are like plants that do not grow into strong plants.
Luke8:15 But some seeds fell on good soil. This is like other people who hear the message from God. They understand the message and they keep it in their minds. They are good and honest. They continue to believe. These people are like a good plant that grows well and makes lots of new seeds.’
Luke8:16 Jesus then said
Luke8:17 God hides some things now. But there will be a time when people will see them. God keeps some things secret now. But there will be a time when people will understand those things clearly.
Luke8:18 So you should think carefully about what you hear. The person who has received some things will receive more. But some other people have nothing. They think that they have a little bit. But they will lose even that.’

Luke21:18 But your life will be safe with me. You will not lose even one hair from your head.

Luke21:31 In the same way

Luke21:33 One day

Luke21:35 It will happen to everyone who lives anywhere on the earth.

Luke23:51 He was from the town of Arimathea in Judea. He had not agreed with the other leaders that Jesus should die. He was waiting for the kingdom of God to come.

Luke23:53 Joseph then went to the place where Jesus had died. He took the dead body down from the cross. He put a piece of linen cloth around the body. Then he put the body into a grave hole in the rock. It was a place where nobody had ever put a dead body before.
Luke23:54 This all happened on the day before the Jewish day of rest. It was nearly time for the Jewish day of rest to begin.

John1:1 In the beginning
John1:2 He was with God from the beginning.
John1:3 God made all things through the Word. God did not make anything without him.
John1:4 It is the Word who caused everything to live. Because of this
John1:5 The light shines in the dark
John1:6 God sent a man to bring his message. His name was John.
John1:7 He came to tell people about the light. God wanted everyone to believe in the one who is the light.
John1:8 John himself was not that light. God sent him to tell people about the light.
John1:9 The true light gives light to every person. That light was now coming into the world.
John1:10 The Word was now in the world. God had made the world through him. But the people in the world did not know who he was.
John1:11 He came to his own place
John1:12 But some people did accept him and they believed in him. He gave authority to those people to become God's children.
John1:13 They did not become God's children in the usual human way. They were not born because some people wanted to have children. They were not born because of what any man decided. No! They were born from God.
John1:14 The Word became human. He lived among us. We saw his great glory. He has the glory of the Father's one true Son. He is full of God's grace and his truth.
John1:15 John told people about the Word. He shouted
John1:16 The Word is full of everything that we need. We have all received one good thing after another good thing.

John3:12 I have told you about things that happen in this world. And you do not believe me. But now I am telling you about things that happen in heaven. So I do not think that you will ever believe me about those things.
John3:13 The Son of Man came down from heaven. Nobody else has gone up to heaven except him.
John3:14 Long ago Moses lifted up the metal snake in the wilderness to save his people. In the same way
John3:15 As a result
John3:16 God loved the people in the world so much that he gave his one and only Son to save them. As a result
John3:17 God did not send his Son into the world because he wanted to punish people. He sent his Son to save the people in the world from punishment.
John3:18 God will not punish anyone who believes in his Son. But if people refuse to believe in the Son
John3:19 God's Son has brought light into the world. But some people did not love the light. They loved the dark instead. That was because they were doing evil things. That is why God has decided to punish those people.
John3:20 Everyone who does evil things hates the light. A person like that will not come to the light because he is afraid. He does not want the bad things that he has done to show clearly.
John3:21 But anyone who does what is true will come to the light. The light will show clearly that he has been doing what is right. Everyone can see that he has been obeying God.
John3:22 After this

John3:32 He tells people about the things that he has seen. He tells about what he has heard. But people do not believe his message.
John3:33 Anyone who does believe his message agrees that God is true.
John3:34 The one that God has sent speaks God's words. God has given his Spirit to fill that person completely.
John3:35 The Father loves the Son. He has given his Son authority over all things.
John3:36 Anyone who believes in the Son has life with God for ever. But anyone who refuses to obey the Son will never have life with God. God will continue to be angry with people like that.’

John5:21 The Father raises dead people to make them alive again. In the same way
John5:22 More than that
John5:23 Then all people will respect the Son as a great person. They will respect him in the way that they respect the Father. The Father sent the Son. So if people refuse to respect the Son
John5:24 I tell you this. Everyone who hears my message should believe in the Father who sent me. If they believe in me
John5:25 I tell you this. A time will soon come when those who are dead in their spirits will hear the voice of the Son of God. Yes
John5:26 The Father himself can give people life. In the same way
John5:27 The Father has also given authority to the Son to judge people. That is because I am the Son of Man.
John5:28 Do not be surprised by this. There will be a time when all the dead people will hear the Son's voice.
John5:29 They will come out from under the ground. Those people who have done good things will rise from death and they will live with God. Those people who have done evil things will also rise from death. But they will rise so that God can punish them as guilty people.
John5:30 I can do nothing by myself. I hear what the Father says to me. Then
John5:31 If I say great things about myself
John5:32 But there is someone else who speaks about me. I know that what he says about me is true.
John5:33 You have sent your people to ask John about me. What he has told you about me is true.
John5:34 I do not need any man to speak on my behalf. But I am telling you this to help you. Then God will be able to save you.
John5:35 John was like a light that shone brightly. For a certain time
John5:36 But it is not only John who shows who I am. There are other things that show it more strongly. My Father has given work to me so that I could finish it. These are the things that I do
John5:37 Also
John5:38 I am the one that he has sent. You do not receive his message because you do not believe in me.
John5:39 You study carefully what the Bible teaches. You think that those books will give you life with God. And it is true
John5:40 But then you refuse to come to me so that you may have life with God!
John5:41 I do not think that it is important for people to praise me.
John5:42 But I know what kind of people you are. I know that you do not really love God.
John5:43 I have come with my Father's authority
John5:44 You like to praise each other. That makes you happy. But what about God himself? You do not try to do good things so that he will praise you. That is why you will never believe in me.
John5:45 But I will not have to tell the Father that you are guilty. No
John5:46 Moses wrote about me. So
John5:47 But you do not believe what Moses wrote. So you will never believe what I say.’

John19:40 The two men covered Jesus' dead body with these spices. And they tied long pieces of linen cloth around it many times. That is how the Jewish people prepare a dead body before they bury it.

Acts7:21 Then they had to put him outside. But Pharaoh's daughter found him and she took him to her home. She took care of him as if he was her own son.
Acts7:22 Moses had teachers who taught him all the wise things that the Egyptians knew. He learned how to speak well. He could also do powerful things.
Acts7:23 When Moses was 40 years old
Acts7:24 He saw that an Egyptian man was being cruel to one of the Israelite men. So he went to help the Israelite man. He killed the Egyptian man to punish him.
Acts7:25 Moses thought that his own people would understand him. They would know that God was using him to save them. They would no longer be slaves to work for the Egyptians. But his people did not understand all this.
Acts7:26 On the next day

Acts7:30 Moses lived in Midian for 40 years. Then

Acts7:32 God said
Acts7:33 Then the Lord God said to Moses
Acts7:34 I have seen that the Egyptians are being cruel to my people. My people are crying with pain and I have heard them. Now I have come down to save them. So come here and listen to me. I will send you back to Egypt.” ’
Acts7:35 Then Stephen said
Acts7:36 So it was Moses who led the people of Israel out of Egypt. He did some powerful things before the people left Egypt. This showed that God was with him. He did more miracles at the Red Sea. Then he led the people through the wilderness for 40 years. He also did more miracles there.
Acts7:37 It was Moses who said to the people of Israel
Acts7:38 This is the same Moses who was leader of our people in the wilderness. He was there with our ancestors when the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. He received the message from God to give to us. Those words from God bring life to us.
Acts7:39 But our ancestors would not obey Moses. They did not accept him as their leader. They wanted to go back to Egypt.
Acts7:40 So the people said to Aaron
Acts7:41 It was then that the people made something that would be an idol for them. They made it from gold with the shape of a young bull. They killed some animals and they burned them as a gift for their idol. The people then had a big meal together because they were very happy. They thought that they had made something that was very good.

Acts7:43 No. You carried with you the idol of the god called Molech. You also carried an idol with the shape of a star

Acts7:51 Then Stephen said
Acts7:52 Your ancestors did bad things to every prophet that God sent to them. They even killed the prophets who told them about the Righteous Man that God would send. Now you have given that Man to his enemies. You have made them kill him.
Acts7:53 You are the people who received God's Law. God caused his angels to give that Law to your ancestors. But you have not obeyed it.’

Acts8:1 Saul was standing there while the Jewish leaders killed Stephen. He agreed with what they did. On the same day that Stephen died

Acts8:3 But Saul brought great trouble to the believers. He wanted to destroy their whole group. So he went to all their houses. He took hold of the believers and he put them into prison. He did that to both men and women.
Acts8:4 The believers who had left Jerusalem went to many different towns. In each place
Acts8:5 One of them who was called Philip went to a city in Samaria. There he told the people the message about Jesus Christ
Acts8:6 Crowds of people came together to hear Philip speak. They saw him do many miracles which showed that God was with him. So they listened carefully to his message.
Acts8:7 Many people there had bad spirits
Acts8:8 As a result
Acts8:9 A man called Simon lived in that city. For a long time

Acts23:18 Then the officer led the young man to the soldiers' leader. He said to him

They will not eat or drink anything until they have killed Paul. They are now ready to do this. They will be waiting to hear your answer.’
Acts23:22 The leader of the soldiers said to him
Acts23:23 Then the soldiers' leader told two of his officers to come to him. He said to them

Acts26:1 King Agrippa said to Paul

Acts27:1 After some time
Acts27:2 We went onto a ship that had come from Adramyttium. This ship was ready to leave. It would sail to the towns on the coast of Asia region. A man called Aristarchus also sailed with us. He came from a city in Macedonia called Thessalonica.
Acts27:3 The next day after we had left Caesarea
Acts27:4 Then we travelled across the sea again. But the wind was blowing against our ship. So we sailed round the island called Cyprus. We sailed on the side of the island where the wind was not strong.
Acts27:5 When we were near to Cilicia and Pamphylia
Acts27:6 The Roman officer found another ship there. It had sailed from Alexandria and it would sail to Italy. So the officer put us on this ship.
Acts27:7 We sailed slowly for several days. It was difficult to sail
Acts27:8 It was still difficult to sail

Acts27:10 He said

Acts27:12 This port was not a good place for a ship to remain during the winter. Most of the men on the ship wanted to continue the journey. They wanted to sail as far as Phoenix
Acts27:13 The wind began to blow from the south
Acts27:14 But soon a very strong wind began to blow. This wind blew from the north-east and it blew strongly across the island.
Acts27:15 The storm hit the ship very powerfully. It was not possible for the sailors to sail the ship straight into the wind. So they did not try to do that any more. Instead
Acts27:16 After that
Acts27:17 They tied it on the ship with ropes. Then they tied some more ropes under the ship so that it would not break in pieces. The men were afraid of what might happen. There were some places along the coast of Libya where the water was not very deep. The ship might hit one of these places and then it might break. So they took the ship's largest sail down. Then they let the wind blow the ship along.
Acts27:18 The strong storm continued to blow against the ship. The ship was carrying many things. So the next day
Acts27:19 The day after that
Acts27:20 For many days

Acts27:26 But the wind will blow the ship so that we hit an island.’
Acts27:27 The storm had continued for 14 days and nights. The strong wind was blowing the ship across the Mediterranean Sea. About midnight
Acts27:28 So they used a rope to measure how deep the water was. They saw that the water was nearly 40 metres deep. A short time later they did this again. This time the water was only 30 metres deep.
Acts27:29 The sailors were afraid that the ship would hit some rocks. So they dropped four anchors on ropes from the back of the ship into the sea. After that
Acts27:30 Some of the sailors tried to leave the ship. They put the small boat into the sea. They tried to go away secretly. They said
Acts27:31 Paul said to the army officer and to the soldiers
Acts27:32 So the soldiers cut the ropes that held the small boat to the ship. The small boat fell into the water and the wind blew it away.
Acts27:33 Now it was almost dawn. Paul said to everyone
Acts27:34 You must eat some food now. Then you will be strong enough to stay alive. None of you will die. You will not even lose one hair from your head.’
Acts27:35 After Paul said this
Acts27:36 Everyone became less afraid and we all ate some food.
Acts27:37 There were 276 people on the ship.
Acts27:38 After everyone had eaten enough
Acts27:39 In the morning
Acts27:40 So the sailors cut the ropes which had the anchors on them. They left the anchors there in the sea. They also removed the ropes which had tied the rudders. Then they raised the sail at the front of the ship. Now the wind could blow the ship straight towards the shore.
Acts27:41 But there was a place in the sea where the water was not deep. The ship sailed onto the sand in this place and it stayed there. The front of the ship pushed into the sand and it could not move. The sea was very strong and it hit against the back of the ship. As a result
Acts27:42 The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners that were on the ship. They did not want these men to swim to the land and escape.
Acts27:43 But the army officer wanted to save Paul. So he commanded the soldiers not to kill the men. Instead he said

Acts28:1 So then we were on the shore and we were all safe. We discovered that the island was called Malta.
Acts28:2 The people who lived on the island were very kind to us. Rain was falling and the weather was cold. So the people lit a fire and they helped all of us.
Acts28:3 Paul picked up some small branches to put on the fire. When he did this
Acts28:4 The people who lived on the island saw the snake. They saw that it was hanging from Paul's hand. So they said to each other
Acts28:5 Then Paul waved his hand about so that the snake fell off into the fire. The snake had not hurt Paul in any way.
Acts28:6 So the people watched Paul carefully. They thought that his body would become very sick. They thought that he might die suddenly. They waited for a long time. But they did not see anything bad happen to Paul. So then they thought something different about Paul. They said
Acts28:7 There was an important officer who ruled the island. He was called Publius. He had some fields near to the shore where we had made the fire. He was very kind to us. He asked us to stay in his house. We stayed there for three days.
Acts28:8 Publius's father was lying in bed because he was ill. His body was hot and he was very sick. Paul went into his room to see him. Paul prayed for him and he put his hands on the man's head. As a result

Romans1:1 This letter is from me
Romans1:2 God promised this good news long ago
Romans1:3 This good news is about God's Son
Romans1:4 
Romans1:5 Because of Jesus Christ
Romans1:6 You Christians in Rome are also among those people! God has chosen you to belong to Jesus Christ.
Romans1:7 So I am writing to all you Christians who are in Rome. God loves you and he has chosen you to be his own people. I pray that God
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 God is the one that I serve with my spirit. I tell people the message about his Son
Romans1:10 Every time that I pray for you
Romans1:11 I want very much to come and visit you. Then I can give you some gift from God's Spirit. That will help you to be strong in your spirits.
Romans1:12 We can help each other in this way
Romans1:13 My Christian friends
Romans1:14 It is my duty to tell God's message to all people. I must tell his good news to people who have learned many things
Romans1:15 Because of that
Romans1:16 I am not ashamed to tell people the good news. God uses that good news to show his great power. He uses it to save all people who believe in Jesus Christ. First
Romans1:17 The good news explains how God accepts people as right with himself. He does this when they believe in Christ. It is only because of their faith. Nothing else. This is what was written in the Bible long ago: ‘The righteous person will live because he trusts God.’
Romans1:18 God in heaven shows that he is angry. He is angry because people do not respect him and they do bad things. Because they continue to do those bad things
Romans1:19 They should understand what God is like
Romans1:20 Ever since God made the world
Romans1:21 Those people did know about God. But they did not respect him as God
Romans1:22 They said that they were wise
Romans1:23 They refused to worship the great God who can never die. Instead
Romans1:24 So God let those people do all the bad things that they wanted to do. God let those disgusting things rule their lives. As a result
Romans1:25 They refused to believe the true things about God. Instead
Romans1:26 Because those people turned against God
Romans1:27 Also
Romans1:28 Those people decided that they did not need to know anything about God. So God let their minds become spoiled. As a result
Romans1:29 They only think about wrong and bad things that they want to do. They want many things for themselves. They want to hurt other people. They are always jealous of other people. They kill people. They quarrel and fight with people. They are not honest or kind. They say silly things about other people.
Romans1:30 They tell lies about people. They hate God. They insult other people. They are proud and they say how important they are. They think of many ways to do bad things. They do not obey their parents.
Romans1:31 They do not understand what is right. They do not do what they have promised to do. They do not love anyone and they are not kind to anyone.
Romans1:32 These people understand God's message about what is right. They know that people who do bad things like this deserve to die. But they still continue to do those bad things. They are even happy when other people do those bad things too.
Romans2:1 So I say this to each one of you: Do not judge other people. If you decide that someone else has done a bad thing
Romans2:2 When God judges people
Romans2:3 Remember that you are only human. Do you still judge people who do bad things
Romans2:4 God is very kind and patient with us. He waits a long time before he punishes us. Do not forget that! You should understand that God is giving you a chance to change. He waits patiently
Romans2:5 But you refuse to change the way that you live. You are not sorry about the wrong things that you have done. As a result
Romans2:6 God will give each person what they deserve for the things that they have done.
Romans2:7 Some people continue patiently to do good things. They want to do what is right so that God will praise them. They want to live the kind of life which death will not spoil. God will give people like that life which continues for ever.
Romans2:8 But other people think only about themselves. They refuse to obey God's true message. Instead
Romans2:9 God will punish everyone who does evil things. He will do that first to the Jews
Romans2:10 But God will praise every person who does good things. They will receive honour and they will have peace in their minds. He will do that first for the Jews
Romans2:11 God is always fair when he judges people
Romans2:12 The Gentiles do not have God's Law that he gave to Moses. If Gentiles do wrong things
Romans2:13 Who does God accept as right with himself? It is those people who obey his Law. It is not the people who only know his Law but do not obey it.
Romans2:14 The Gentiles do not have God's Law. But they may still do the right things that the Law teaches. They do right things because of what they themselves think is right. In that way they are showing that they have a certain law in their minds
Romans2:15 Those people show that they know what is right. It is as if God has written his Law deep inside them. Their thoughts tell them the difference between what is right and what is wrong. Sometimes their thoughts say: ‘You did something that is wrong.’ And sometimes their thoughts say: ‘You did what is right.’
Romans2:16 That is the message that I tell people. God knows everything that people think secretly. One day he will judge everyone. He has given Jesus Christ the authority to do that.
Romans2:17 Do you say that you are a Jew? Maybe you trust in the Law that God gave to Moses. You are proud that you belong to God.
Romans2:18 You know what God wants people to do. His Law teaches you what is good and right.
Romans2:19 You are sure that you yourself can teach other people about God. You think that you can be like a guide for blind people. You think that you can give light to people who are in the dark.
Romans2:20 You are sure that you can teach fools and people who do not know very much. You know that God's Law tells you everything that is true. Because you know so much
Romans2:21 So you teach other people
Romans2:22 You say
Romans2:23 You are proud that you know God's Law. But you do not obey that Law. As a result
Romans2:24 This is written in the Bible: ‘Because of you Jews
Romans2:25 As a Jewish man
Romans2:26 But a Gentile may obey what God's Law teaches. Then he has shown that he belongs to God even though nobody has circumcised him.
Romans2:27 As a result
Romans2:28 Not everyone who seems to be a Jew is a true Jew who belongs to God. God does not accept a man just because someone has circumcised him in his body.
Romans2:29 No! A true Jew is someone who belongs to God in his spirit. He belongs to God because God's Spirit has changed his life. It is as if God has circumcised him in his spirit. The rules of God's Law cannot do that work in a person. God will praise a person like that
Romans3:1 So someone may ask
Romans3:2 Yes
Romans3:3 But you might say
Romans3:4 No
Romans3:5 Some people might say

Romans3:8 But if that is true
Romans3:9 So

Romans3:12 Everyone has turned away from God. All people have become completely bad. Nobody does what is good or kind. There is not even one person who is like that.

Romans3:14 They are always saying bad things against people
Romans3:15 They hurry to kill people.
Romans3:16 Everywhere they go
Romans3:17 They have never known how to live in peace.
Romans3:18 They refuse to respect God in any way.’
Romans3:19 We know that God's Law belongs to the Jews. God told them to obey everything that his Law teaches. As a result
Romans3:20 Nobody can become right with God because they obey the Law's commands. Instead
Romans3:21 But now we know how to become right with God. God has shown us the way that he will accept people as right with himself. This is not part of the Law that he gave to Moses. But God's Law and the messages of his prophets have told us about it.
Romans3:22 God accepts people as right with himself because Jesus Christ did what God wanted. God accepts every person who believes in Christ. It is the same way for everyone
Romans3:23 All people have done wrong things. Nobody is as good as God in his glory wants them to be.
Romans3:24 But because God is very kind
Romans3:25 God showed clearly that Jesus Christ died on the cross as a sacrifice for our sins. God forgives us if we trust in Jesus and the blood of his sacrifice. God did this to show clearly that he is always right and fair. In past times
Romans3:26 He did this to show us today that he is completely right and fair. He accepts people as right with himself if they believe in Jesus.
Romans3:27 So nobody should boast about themselves. Nobody becomes right with God because they have done good things. No! God accepts someone only if they believe in Jesus Christ.
Romans3:28 So this is what we are saying: A person becomes right with God if they trust in Christ. It is not because they do the good things which God's Law teaches.
Romans3:29 God is not God only for Jewish people. He is also God for the Gentiles. Yes! He is the God for all people.
Romans3:30 There is only one God. He will accept Jews as right with himself if they believe in Christ. He will also accept other people as right with himself if they believe in Christ.
Romans3:31 What does this show us about God's Law which he gave to Moses? When we say that all people need to believe in Jesus
Romans4:1 Abraham was the ancestor of all of us who are Jews. Think about what he discovered.
Romans4:2 God did not accept Abraham as right with him because Abraham had done good things. If God had done that
Romans4:3 Remember what the Bible tells us. It says: ‘Abraham believed God. As a result
Romans4:4 When a person works to get money
Romans4:5 But someone who believes in God does not work to cause God to accept him. God accepts that person because of their faith. God says that people who have turned away from him are right with him
Romans4:6 David also wrote about the same thing. He describes those people that God has accepted as right with himself. God has not accepted them because they have done good things. It is his gift to them. David tells us how happy people like that are.
Romans4:7 David says: ‘The people that God has forgiven are truly happy. They have done wrong things
Romans4:8 A person is truly happy when the Lord God accepts him. The Lord does not think about that person's sin any more.’
Romans4:9 God does this for all people
Romans4:10 Someone circumcised Abraham to show that he belonged to God's people. Think about the time when God accepted Abraham. Did it happen before someone circumcised him
Romans4:11 Abraham's circumcision was a mark on his body to show that God had already accepted him. God had accepted Abraham because Abraham believed in him. This shows that Abraham is like a father to everyone that God has accepted. Like Abraham
Romans4:12 But Abraham is also like the father of all Jews who believe in God. Someone has circumcised those Jews
Romans4:13 God promised Abraham and his descendants that the world would belong to them one day. God did not promise this because Abraham obeyed any rules. God promised it because Abraham believed in him. That is why God accepted Abraham as right with him.
Romans4:14 People cannot receive what God has promised because they obey rules. If they could get it like that
Romans4:15 The rules in God's Law say that God will punish people who do not obey those rules. But without any rules

Galatians5:22 But God's Spirit causes us to live in a different way. We love other people. We are happy and we have peace in our minds. We are patient
Galatians5:23 We respect other people and we control ourselves properly. God's Spirit helps us to live in that way. There is no law that says these things are wrong.
Galatians5:24 We belong to Christ. As a result
Galatians5:25 Now it is God's Spirit that gives us life. So we must let God's Spirit lead us in the way that we live.
Galatians5:26 We must not become proud. We must not be jealous of each other
Galatians6:1 My friends
Galatians6:2 Help each other when someone has problems. If you do that
Galatians6:3 You must not think that you are too important to help other people. If you are not really so important
Galatians6:4 Everyone should think carefully about the things that they themselves do. If those things are good
Galatians6:5 Each person must live in a way that they know is right for them.
Galatians6:6 Everyone who is learning about God's message should share all his good things with his teacher.
Galatians6:7 Do not deceive yourselves. Nobody can make God seem foolish. Think about a farmer who plants seeds in his field. The same kind of crops will grow as the seeds which he plants. That will be his harvest.
Galatians6:8 It is the same in our lives. A person may do the things that please his own human nature. The harvest from their human nature will be punishment and death. Or a person may do the things that please God's Spirit. The harvest from God's Spirit will be life with God for ever.
Galatians6:9 We must not become tired as we do things that are good. We must not become so weak that we stop. Then we will receive a good harvest at the proper time.
Galatians6:10 Because of this
Galatians6:11 Now I am using my own hand to write these last words to you myself. You can see how large the letters are!
Galatians6:12 Some people want to make you obey the Jewish rules. They say that you must let someone circumcise you. They want people to think that they are important. They do not want to receive any trouble from others. So they are afraid to teach that Christ's death on the cross is enough to save us.
Galatians6:13 Even those men who are circumcised do not obey all the Jewish rules. They want to circumcise you so that they can boast about you. They want to show that they have authority over your bodies.
Galatians6:14 But I only want to boast about one thing. That is our Lord Jesus Christ's death on the cross. Because of that
Galatians6:15 It does not matter whether anyone has circumcised you or not. The important thing is that God has made you a new person.
Galatians6:16 I pray that God will bless everyone who lives by that rule. May God give them peace in their minds and be kind to them. I pray this for all who belong to God's true people.
Galatians6:17 I want to receive no more trouble from anyone after this. The marks on my body show that I am a true servant of Jesus.
Galatians6:18 My Christian friends

Philippians1:2 We pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will continue to help you. We pray that they will give you peace in your minds.
Philippians1:3 Every time that I think about you
Philippians1:4 I am always praying for you. Every time that I pray for you
Philippians1:5 I am happy because together we tell people the good news about Christ. You have always helped me with that work
Philippians1:6 I know that God has begun to do good things in you. And I am sure that he will continue to work in you. Then
Philippians1:7 It is right for me to feel like this about all of you
Philippians1:8 God knows that I very much want to be with all of you. I love you very much
Philippians1:9 This is what I pray for you. I pray that you will continue to love each other more and more. I pray that you will know God better and better. I pray that you will understand all those things that are true.
Philippians1:10 Then you will be able to choose what is best in the way that you live. Then
Philippians1:11 Because of the work that Jesus Christ has done in you
Philippians1:12 My friends
Philippians1:13 I am in prison because I am Christ's servant. The soldiers in the king's house and all the people here know that.
Philippians1:14 Also
Philippians1:15 It is true that some of these Christians are jealous of me. They want to show that they are better than me. That is why they tell people the message about Christ. But other Christians speak about Christ because they want to help me.
Philippians1:16 They tell people about him because they love me. They know that God has put me here in this prison. And he wants me to show people that the good news is true.
Philippians1:17 Those other people speak about Christ because they want to seem important. They do not really want to help people. They just want to cause trouble for me while I am here in this prison.
Philippians1:18 But it does not matter to me! All of them are telling people about Christ. That is the most important thing. Whether they speak because of wrong reasons or because of right reasons
Philippians1:19 You are praying for me and Jesus Christ's Spirit is helping me. So I know that what has happened to me will have a good result. Yes
Philippians1:20 I never want to do anything that will cause me to be ashamed. I hope very much that I will never do that. I want to be brave now
Philippians1:21 Christ is everything that I live for. If I die
Philippians1:22 But if I continue to live
Philippians1:23 Both of them seem very good to me. I want to leave this world so that I can be with Christ. That is a much better thing.
Philippians1:24 But it is necessary for me to live here in this world so that I can help you.
Philippians1:25 Yes
Philippians1:26 Then
Philippians1:27 The most important thing is that you continue to live in a good way. Live in the way that the good news about Christ teaches. I want to know that you agree together about the good news. And that you work together to show others that the good news is true. Whether I come to visit you or not
Philippians1:28 I also want to know that you are not afraid of the people who speak against you. If you are brave
Philippians1:29 God is helping you to serve Christ well. This means that you believe in Christ. But it also means that you suffer on his behalf. Both of these things are gifts from God.
Philippians1:30 You are receiving the same kinds of trouble that I myself receive. You saw that when I was with you. Now you hear that it is still happening to me.

Colossians1:7 It was our good friend Epaphras who first told you this good news. Like us
Colossians1:8 He has told us about the love that God's Spirit has given to you.
Colossians1:9 Because of all that
Colossians1:10 Then you will be able to live in a way that the Lord wants you to live. Everything that you do will please him. We pray that all these good things will have good results. We pray that you will know God better and better.
Colossians1:11 God rules from heaven with great power. We pray that he will make you strong with every kind of strength. Then

Colossians1:13 God has saved us from the dark kingdom where Satan rules. He has brought us into the kingdom of his Son

Colossians1:19 God chose to put his nature in Christ completely.
Colossians1:20 He decided that Christ would die on the cross as a sacrifice. In that way

Colossians1:24 At this time
Colossians1:25 I have become God's servant

Colossians1:27 God wanted them to understand how great this secret is. It shows that God will bless people who live in all countries of the world. This secret message is that Christ lives in you. And because of that
Colossians1:28 That is why we tell everyone the message about Christ. God helps us to be very wise as we warn people and we teach them. We want every person who belongs to Christ to become complete
Colossians1:29 That is why I work very hard. Christ works powerfully in me to make me strong for this difficult work.
Colossians2:1 I want you to know that I am doing a difficult work on your behalf. I am also working to help the people at Laodicea and other people who have never met me.
Colossians2:2 I want all the believers to become strong in their spirits. I want them to help each other because they love one another. Then God will bless them very much
Colossians2:3 Christ is the one who holds everything that makes people wise. He helps people to know the true message that God had hidden.
Colossians2:4 I am telling you these things so that false teachers will not deceive you. They may teach ideas that seem good
Colossians2:5 My body is not there with you
Colossians2:6 You have accepted Christ Jesus as your Lord. So remember that you belong to him as you live your lives.
Colossians2:7 Get your strength from Christ himself
Colossians2:8 Be careful that you do not believe any false teachers. The ideas that they teach may seem clever. But they really want to deceive you. They are ideas that have come from the human minds of our ancestors. Or ideas that come from spirits that belong to this world. They are not the true message about Christ.
Colossians2:9 The whole nature of God himself lives in Christ
Colossians2:10 And because you are united with Christ
Colossians2:11 Also
Colossians2:12 When they baptized you
Colossians2:13 At one time
Colossians2:14 We are guilty of many wrong things that are against God's laws. But Christ died on the cross on our behalf. He paid the debt of our sins. It is like he has destroyed the list of all the debts that we should pay to God.
Colossians2:15 On the cross
Colossians2:16 Some people may tell you that it is wrong for you to eat or to drink certain things. Or they make rules about special festival days
Colossians2:17 Rules like that are only like shadows of real things that would happen later. But now we have what is real
Colossians2:18 Some people may tell you that you must always serve other people like a slave. They may tell you that you must worship angels. Do not let those people speak bad things against you. They like to talk a lot about the special things that God has shown them. They think that they are very clever. But their ideas only come from human minds.
Colossians2:19 False teachers like that do not receive their message from Christ. As God's people
Colossians2:20 Remember that your old nature has died with Christ. So the powerful spirits that rule this world no longer have any authority over you. You do not need to serve them
Colossians2:21 They teach
Colossians2:22 Rules like that are only about things that spoil when you use them. They are rules that come from human ideas.
Colossians2:23 They teach you ideas about what you must do to worship God. They teach you to serve other people like a slave. They say that you must hurt yourself. You must show that you control your body. Rules like that may seem to be wise
Colossians3:1 God has raised you with Christ to a new life. So you should want more and more the things that belong to heaven. That is where Christ is now. He sits there at God's right side.
Colossians3:2 So think about the good things that are there in heaven above. Do not think only about things that are on the earth.
Colossians3:3 Remember that your old nature has died. God is keeping your new life safe with Christ.
Colossians3:4 You have that new life because you belong to Christ. One day
Colossians3:5 Your old nature likes to do bad things that belong to this earth. But your old nature is dead
Colossians3:6 God will certainly punish people who do those kinds of bad things.
Colossians3:7 At one time
Colossians3:8 But now you must refuse to do all kinds of things like that. Do not become angry with people or shout at them. Do not do anything or say anything that will hurt other people. Do not speak any bad or disgusting words.
Colossians3:9 Do not deceive each other with lies. Remember that you have taken off your old nature
Colossians3:10 Now you have received a new nature. It is God who made it for you so that you can become more and more like him. You will learn to know him better and better.
Colossians3:11 People have this new nature only because they belong to Christ. That is the important thing. Jews are not different from Gentiles. It does not matter whether someone has circumcised you or not. It does not matter which country you come from or how clever you are. It does not matter whether you are a slave or a free person. Christ is all that matters. He lives in every believer.
Colossians3:12 God has chosen you to be his own special people. You belong to him and he loves you very much. So this is how you should live: Be kind to other people and help them. Do not think that you are better than other people. Instead
Colossians3:13 Do not become angry with each other. If you think that someone has done something wrong against you
Colossians3:14 And you must also love each other. That is the most important thing that you should do. Love holds all these good things completely together.
Colossians3:15 Christ has given you peace in your minds. So let that peace rule your thoughts. God has chosen you to be like one body
Colossians3:16 Let Christ's message always be in your thoughts
Colossians3:17 Show that you belong to the Lord Jesus with everything that you do and with everything that you say. Always thank God the Father in the way that you serve Jesus.
Colossians3:18 Wives
Colossians3:19 Husbands
Colossians3:20 Children
Colossians3:21 Fathers
Colossians3:22 Slaves
Colossians3:23 Whatever things you are doing
Colossians3:24 You know that you will receive the good things that the Lord has promised to his people. He will give you what is right for you. The Lord Jesus Christ is the master that you serve.
Colossians3:25 Remember that God will punish anyone who does wrong things. He always judges people correctly
Colossians4:1 Masters
Colossians4:2 Continue to pray seriously. Stay awake
Colossians4:3 Also pray for us. Ask God to make it possible for us to tell people his message. We want people to understand the message about Christ that was a secret before. I am now in a prison because I taught people those things.
Colossians4:4 Pray that I will be able to tell people this message clearly. That is what I should do.
Colossians4:5 When you are with people who do not believe in Christ
Colossians4:6 When you speak to people
Colossians4:7 Our Christian friend
Colossians4:8 I want him to tell you what is happening to us
Colossians4:9 Onesimus will travel with him. As you know
Colossians4:10 Aristarchus is also here with me in the prison. He says ‘hello’ to you. Barnabas's cousin
Colossians4:11 Jesus
Colossians4:12 Epaphras says ‘hello’ to you. He is also one of your people
Colossians4:13 I can tell you this about Epaphras: he has worked very hard for you
Colossians4:14 Our good friend Luke
Colossians4:15 Say ‘hello’ for me to all the believers at Laodicea. Also say ‘hello’ to Nympha and to the group of believers that meet in her house.

Colossians4:17 Say to Archippus

1Thessalonians3:2

1Thessalonians3:4 Even when we were there with you
1Thessalonians3:5 That is why I had to send Timothy to come to you. I could not wait to hear news about you any longer. I wanted to know whether you still trusted Christ. I was afraid that Satan had deceived you. If he had made you turn away from Christ
1Thessalonians3:6 But now Timothy has come back to us. And he has told us good news about you. He has told us that you continue to trust Christ and you love one another. He says that you always think about us in a good way. You want very much to meet us again
1Thessalonians3:7 So we have become happy again because of you

1Thessalonians4:1 Christian friends

1Thessalonians4:3 This is how God wants you to live: as his own people
1Thessalonians4:4 Each of you men must learn to control his own body properly. Then you will do only what is pure and right.
1Thessalonians4:5 People who do not know God want to have sex in a wrong way. They cannot control themselves. You must not be like those people.
1Thessalonians4:6 No man among you should ever have sex with the wife of a Christian friend. That would be the same as if you robbed him. It would be a bad thing to do against him. Remember that the Lord will punish everyone who does things like that. We have already told you this. We warned you very seriously about it.
1Thessalonians4:7 God has chosen us to belong to him. He does not want us to do disgusting things. As his people
1Thessalonians4:8 You should accept what I am saying. If not
1Thessalonians4:9 But I do not need to write to you about how you should love other believers. God himself has taught you how to love each other.
1Thessalonians4:10 Certainly
1Thessalonians4:11 Try very much to live at peace with other people. Work hard at your own job. We have already told you that you must do these things.
1Thessalonians4:12 If you live in this way
1Thessalonians4:13 Our Christian friends

1Thessalonians4:15 We are telling you the message that the Lord has given to us. This is what will happen when the Lord returns. Those of us who are still alive will not go to meet the Lord first. We will not go before those people who have already died.

1Thessalonians5:7 It is at night that people sleep. It is at night that people become drunk.
1Thessalonians5:8 But we belong to the day
1Thessalonians5:9 God did not choose us as his people so that he could be angry with us. He chose us so that he could save us because of what our Lord Jesus Christ has done.

2Thessalonians1:2 We pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will continue to help you. We pray that they will give you peace in your minds.
2Thessalonians1:3 Our Christian friends
2Thessalonians1:4 As a result
2Thessalonians1:5 This shows us that God judges people in a way that is right. Now you are suffering because you belong to God's kingdom. Because of that
2Thessalonians1:6 God will punish those people who are causing trouble for you. He will cause trouble for them. That is right and fair.
2Thessalonians1:7 God will also do what is right and fair for you. You have troubles now. But one day God will cause you to have rest
2Thessalonians1:8 and with a very hot fire. He will punish those people who do not know God. Yes
2Thessalonians1:9 Their punishment will continue for ever
2Thessalonians1:10 This will happen when Jesus comes on that special day. His own people will praise him for the great things that he has done for them. All of us who are believers will be surprised when we see his great power. This includes you
2Thessalonians1:11 So we always pray for you. Our God has chosen you to be his own people. We pray that he will help you
2Thessalonians1:12 As a result
2Thessalonians2:1 Our Christian friends
2Thessalonians2:2 Please do not become easily confused about this. Do not let yourselves become afraid because of false messages about the Lord's return. Some people are telling you that the great day of the Lord has already happened. Some of these people say that God's Spirit has shown them a message like that. Other people might say that the message came from us. Or they might say that we wrote it in a letter.
2Thessalonians2:3 Do not let anyone deceive you like that in any way. That day will not happen until certain other things have happened. Before that day
2Thessalonians2:4 This man will be God's great enemy. He will say that he is greater than everything that people call their gods. He will say that he is greater than everything that people worship. As a result
2Thessalonians2:5 I told you these things when I was with you. I am sure that you remember that.
2Thessalonians2:6 Something makes it impossible for this man to come now. And you know what it is. But this man will appear at the proper time.
2Thessalonians2:7 His power is already working secretly to turn people against God. But there is someone who makes it impossible for him to work freely. That person will continue to make it impossible
2Thessalonians2:8 Then the man who is against God will appear. But the Lord Jesus will come
2Thessalonians2:9 When the enemy of God appears
2Thessalonians2:10 He will do all kinds of bad things that deceive people. The people that he deceives are people that God will destroy. Those people believe false things because they have refused to accept God's true message. If they had loved that true message
2Thessalonians2:11 For this reason
2Thessalonians2:12 As a result
2Thessalonians2:13 We always thank God for you
2Thessalonians2:14 We told you God's good news. That is how God called you to come to him. He saved you so that you would join with our Lord Jesus Christ in heaven.
2Thessalonians2:15 Because of that
2Thessalonians2:16 We ask our Lord Jesus Christ himself
2Thessalonians2:17 We pray that he will continue to help you and make you strong. Then you will always be able to do good things and to say good things.
2Thessalonians3:1 Now
2Thessalonians3:2 Pray also that God will not let bad people cause trouble for us. Not everyone believes the message about our Lord.
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord always does what he has promised. So he will help you to be strong inside yourselves. He will keep you safe when Satan attacks you.
2Thessalonians3:4 Because you belong to the Lord
2Thessalonians3:5 We pray that the Lord will lead you in your thoughts. Then you will understand God's love better and better. And you will be patient and strong
2Thessalonians3:6 Our friends
2Thessalonians3:7 You yourselves know that you ought to copy our example. You know that we were not lazy when we lived among you.
2Thessalonians3:8 We always paid for the food that anyone gave to us. We worked hard all the time
2Thessalonians3:9 We could have told you to give us what we needed. We have authority to do that. But we wanted to show you how to live properly. Then you could copy our example.
2Thessalonians3:10 Even when we were there with you
2Thessalonians3:11 We say this because someone has told us news about you. They have told us that some people among you are living in a lazy way. Those people do not work at their own jobs. Instead
2Thessalonians3:12 We have something to say to people who are lazy like that. We warn them with the authority that the Lord Jesus Christ gives to us. They must stop causing trouble. They must work properly
2Thessalonians3:13 But you must never stop doing what is right
2Thessalonians3:14 Maybe someone among you will not obey the message that we have written in this letter. Make sure that everyone knows about someone like that. Do not be friends with him
2Thessalonians3:15 But remember that he is a believer
