1Timothy1:1 This letter is from me
1Timothy1:2 I am writing to you
1Timothy1:3 When I was going to Macedonia
1Timothy1:4 Tell people not to listen to false stories. They should not always be studying long lists of their families' names from years ago. Those things do not help people. They only cause them to argue. Instead
1Timothy1:5 We need to teach people to love one another with pure thoughts. They need to be sure in their minds that they are living in a good way. They need to trust God completely.
1Timothy1:6 But certain people have failed to live in that way. They would rather talk about things that do not help anyone.
1Timothy1:7 They want to be teachers of God's Law. They speak very strongly
1Timothy1:8 We know that God's laws are good
1Timothy1:9 We also know that God did not make those laws for people who already live in a good way. He made them for people like this
1Timothy1:10 people who have sex in a wrong way; men who have sex with other men; people who catch other people to sell them; people who tell lies; people who do not speak the truth in court; people who do any wrong things that are against God's true message.
1Timothy1:11 That true message is the good news that comes from our great God. We praise him for it. He has chosen me to tell this good news to people.
1Timothy1:12 I thank Christ Jesus our Lord
1Timothy1:13 Before he chose me
1Timothy1:14 Our Lord continued to be very good and kind to me. He caused me to believe in Christ Jesus and to love him.
1Timothy1:15 Here is a true message that everyone should believe: ‘Christ Jesus came into the world to save people who do wrong things.’ I am the worst person like that!
1Timothy1:16 But God was kind to me. Christ Jesus showed that he was completely patient with me
1Timothy1:17 Praise God
1Timothy1:18 Timothy
1Timothy1:19 You must continue to trust God. Always do what you know to be right. Some people have refused to do what is right
1Timothy1:20 Hymenaeus and Alexander are people like that. I have given them to Satan so that he may punish them. Then they will learn not to say bad things against God any more.
1Timothy2:1 So then
1Timothy2:2 Pray in that way for kings and for all rulers and people who have authority. Pray for God to help them
1Timothy2:3 It is good that we should pray like that. It makes God
1Timothy2:4 He wants to save all people. He wants everyone to know his message about what is true.
1Timothy2:5 There is only one God. There is also only one person who brings God and people together. That person is Christ Jesus
1Timothy2:6 He died as a sacrifice on behalf of all people
1Timothy2:7 God chose me as his apostle
1Timothy2:8 So then
1Timothy2:9 I also want the women to dress themselves properly in a way that is right. They should not bring shame on themselves. What makes a woman beautiful? It is not the special way that she prepares her hair. It is not the gold things or jewels that she wears. It is not her expensive clothes.
1Timothy2:10 Christian women should not dress themselves like that. Instead
1Timothy2:11 When you meet together
1Timothy2:12 I do not let a woman teach men. I do not let a woman have authority over men. Instead
1Timothy2:13 I say this because God made Adam first. Then he made Eve.
1Timothy2:14 It was Eve
1Timothy2:15 But even as women give birth to children
1Timothy3:1 These words are true: ‘If you want to become a leader of God's people
1Timothy3:2 For that reason
1Timothy3:3 He should not drink too much wine. He must not get angry quickly or quarrel with people. Instead he should be kind to people. He must not want lots of money.
1Timothy3:4 He must take care of his own family. His children must obey him and respect him.
1Timothy3:5 A man must know how to take care of his own family. If he cannot do that
1Timothy3:6 A leader of the church must not be someone who has believed in Christ for only a short time. Someone like that might boast that he is great and important. Then he would receive punishment
1Timothy3:7 Also
1Timothy3:8 In the same way
1Timothy3:9 They must completely believe the true message that God has shown us about Christ. They must be sure in their minds about it.

1Timothy3:11 Deacons' wives must live good lives
1Timothy3:12 A deacon must be the husband of one wife. He must take care of his children and everyone who lives in his house.
1Timothy3:13 If a deacon serves God's people well
1Timothy3:14 I hope to come to visit you soon. But anyway

1Timothy4:1 God's Spirit tells us clearly that
1Timothy4:2 The people who teach those false things are hypocrites. They no longer understand that what they teach is wrong.
1Timothy4:3 They say that it is wrong for anyone to marry. They tell people not to eat certain kinds of food. But God made those foods for people to eat! We should understand God's true message and trust him. Then we will receive the food that he has given us and we will thank him for it.
1Timothy4:4 Everything that God has made is good. So we should not refuse anything that he has made. Instead
1Timothy4:5 God has said that it is good. We should pray that he will bless it. Because of that
1Timothy4:6 You must explain these things to the believers and teach them God's true message. If you do that
1Timothy4:7 But you must refuse to listen to false stories. Stories like that are not from God and only silly people believe them. Instead
1Timothy4:8 People say this: ‘If a person works to make his body strong and healthy
1Timothy4:9 We know that those words are true. Everyone should agree with them.
1Timothy4:10 So that is why we work to serve God well. We continue to work
1Timothy4:11 Teach people to do these things.
1Timothy4:12 You are still a young man
1Timothy4:13 Until I come
1Timothy4:14 Remember the spiritual gift that God has given to you. You received that gift when the group of church leaders put their hands on you. They spoke messages from God about how you would serve him. So do not stop using that gift from God.
1Timothy4:15 Be careful to do all these things well. Then everyone will see that you are serving God better and better.
1Timothy4:16 Think carefully about how you live
1Timothy5:1 Do not speak strongly to an older man and tell him what he must do. Instead
1Timothy5:2 Speak to older women as you would speak to your mother. Speak to younger women as you would speak to your sisters. Always respect them properly.
1Timothy5:3 Respect those widows in the church who are really alone.
1Timothy5:4 If a widow has children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 Some widows are really alone and they have nobody to take care of them. A widow like that trusts God that he will help her. As a result
1Timothy5:6 But some widows only want to enjoy themselves. For a widow like that
1Timothy5:7 Teach everyone clearly to do these things. Then nobody can say that the believers are doing anything wrong.
1Timothy5:8 Every believer must take care of his own family. Certainly
1Timothy5:9 If you want to put a widow's name on the list of widows
1Timothy5:10 People should know that she has done good things. For example
1Timothy5:11 But do not put the names of younger widows on the list. They may decide to marry again
1Timothy5:12 As a result
1Timothy5:13 Young widows may learn to be lazy. They may go around from house to house. Not only do they become lazy
1Timothy5:14 So I think that widows who are still young women should marry again. Then they can have children and they can take care of their own homes. If they live well like that
1Timothy5:15 Some of the younger widows have already turned away from Christ
1Timothy5:16 A woman who believes in Christ should take care of any widows in her own family. Then the church will not have to do that work. Instead
1Timothy5:17 Everyone should respect leaders of the believers who lead the church well. The church should also pay them properly for their work. That is certainly true for leaders who teach God's people and speak God's message to them.
1Timothy5:18 The Bible says: ‘Do not tie shut the mouth of an ox while it walks on your wheat seeds.’ The Bible also says: ‘You must pay a worker what his work is worth.’
1Timothy5:19 Somebody may tell you that a leader of the believers has done something wrong. But do not accept that as true
1Timothy5:20 If a leader is really guilty and continues to do wrong things
1Timothy5:21 I say this to you very strongly: Obey what I am telling you to do. Remember that God himself and Christ Jesus
1Timothy5:22 Do not hurry to put your hands on anyone to choose him as a leader of the church. Perhaps that man has done something wrong. Then people might think that you agree with his sins. You yourself must always be careful to do what is right and good.
1Timothy5:23 I know that you often become ill. So do not drink only water
1Timothy5:24 Some people do wrong things that everyone can see clearly. When someone judges those people
1Timothy5:25 It is the same with the good things that people do. Many good things are clear for everyone to see. And even the good things that people do secretly will not remain a secret for ever.
1Timothy6:1 All slaves should know that it is right to respect their masters. Then other people will not insult God's name or the message that we teach.
1Timothy6:2 Slaves who have masters who are also believers must continue to respect them. As believers
1Timothy6:3 Some people teach a false message that is different. Their message does not agree with the true message that our Lord Jesus Christ taught. They do not teach people to live in a way that pleases God.
1Timothy6:4 People like that think that they are very clever. But they do not really understand anything. They like to argue about things which are not important. They like to quarrel about words. As a result
1Timothy6:5 Their minds have become confused and they argue all the time. They refuse to believe God's true message. They think that they will become rich if they say that they are serving God.
1Timothy6:6 What makes our life really rich? We should serve God in a way that pleases him and be happy with the things that we have.
1Timothy6:7 When we were born
1Timothy6:8 So
1Timothy6:9 But some people want to have lots of money. This causes them to do wrong things. They cannot get free from their bad ways
1Timothy6:10 When people like to have lots of money
1Timothy6:11 But you
1Timothy6:12 Continue to believe God's true message. Be like someone who fights well to keep it safe. God has chosen you to live with him for ever. You stood in front of many people and said clearly that you believed in Jesus Christ. So live in a way that shows that you belong to him.
1Timothy6:13 Christ Jesus himself spoke God's true message clearly when he stood in front of Pontius Pilate. I now say this to you
1Timothy6:14 You must obey the commands that you have received. Obey them completely without any mistake until our Lord Jesus Christ returns.
1Timothy6:15 God will cause Christ to appear at the proper time. God is great
1Timothy6:16 God alone lives for ever. He lives in very bright light that nobody can come near. Nobody has ever seen God. Nobody is able to see him. So we praise him! He will rule for ever with great authority! Amen.
1Timothy6:17 Warn those people who are rich with many things that belong to this world. Tell them not to think that they are more important than other people. They must not think that their money will keep them safe. They could easily lose all of it. Instead
1Timothy6:18 Tell those rich people to do many good things to help other people. They should be very kind to other people and share their things with them.
1Timothy6:19 In that way they will store valuable things for themselves in heaven. These things will be a strong help for them in the future. As a result
1Timothy6:20 Timothy
1Timothy6:21 Some people have believed those wrong ideas and now they no longer trust God. I pray that God will continue to be very kind to all of you.

2Timothy1:2 I am writing to you

2Timothy1:5 I also remember how you truly believe in Christ. Your grandmother
2Timothy1:6 For that reason
2Timothy1:7 Be strong
2Timothy1:8 So do not be ashamed of the message about our Lord that we tell people. And do not be ashamed of me because I am in a prison. I am a prisoner because I serve Christ. You must understand that you will have trouble because you tell people the good news about Jesus Christ. So be ready to have the same kind of trouble as I have. Let God make you strong when you have trouble.
2Timothy1:9 God is the one who has saved us. He has chosen us to serve him with good
2Timothy1:10 Now Christ Jesus has come to save us
2Timothy1:11 That is the same good news that God has chosen me to tell to people. He has sent me as his apostle to teach his message to people.
2Timothy1:12 That is why I am suffering like this. But I am not ashamed to be in prison. I have believed in Christ and I know him well. I know that I can trust him to keep his message safe. God has given me the authority to speak that message to people at this time. Christ has the power to keep it safe until that great day when he comes back.
2Timothy1:13 You have heard me tell you about God's true message. Let those words be an example to you

2Timothy2:1 Timothy
2Timothy2:2 You have heard the message that I have taught. Many other people also heard it and they agree that it is true. Now you must tell the same message to other people who will be able to teach it to even more people. They should be people that you can trust as honest people and who can teach well.
2Timothy2:3 Be patient through the troubles that we Christians have. Be brave and strong
2Timothy2:4 Any soldier who fights in a war does not become busy with other things. He only wants to fight well to make his captain happy.
2Timothy2:5 It is the same with an athlete who runs a race. He cannot win the race unless he obeys the rules.
2Timothy2:6 A farmer works hard to grow plants for food. So he should be the first person to receive some of that food.
2Timothy2:7 Think carefully about what I am saying to you. The Lord will help you to understand all these things.
2Timothy2:8 Jesus Christ is a descendant of King David. God caused Jesus to become alive again after his death. Remember that! These facts are part of the good news that I tell people.
2Timothy2:9 And I have many troubles because I tell that good news to people. They have even put me in a prison
2Timothy2:10 So I continue to be patient and brave during all these troubles. I do that so that I can help the people that God has chosen to belong to him. I want God to save them too
2Timothy2:11 We all know that this is true: ‘If we have died with Christ in this world
2Timothy2:12 If we continue to be strong during troubles here
2Timothy2:13 Even if we no longer trust him
2Timothy2:14 Continue to tell these things to God's people there
2Timothy2:15 Always try very much to serve God in a way that makes him happy. Then he will accept you as a good worker that he can trust. You must teach God's true message correctly. Then you will have no reason to be ashamed of your work.
2Timothy2:16 But stay away from people who talk about silly things that are not important to God. A message like that causes people to live more and more in a way that does not please God.
2Timothy2:17 It causes people to become sick in their spirits. More and more people become sick as a result. Hymenaeus and Philetus are two of the people who teach that kind of false message.
2Timothy2:18 They no longer teach God's true message. They say that God will not raise dead people any more. They say that he has already given life to dead people. In this way they have confused the minds of some people
2Timothy2:19 But the group of true believers remains strong
2Timothy2:20 In a big house
2Timothy2:21 It is like that with people who serve God as their Master. People who keep themselves clean from those bad things will be like valuable dishes. Their master can use them for a special purpose. They will be ready for him to use them for every kind of good work.
2Timothy2:22 Be careful not to do the kind of bad things that young people often want to do. Instead
2Timothy2:23 Do not join with people who want to argue about silly things that do not mean anything. You know that silly things like that only cause people to quarrel.
2Timothy2:24 As the Lord's servant
2Timothy2:25 When people speak against God's true message
2Timothy2:26 Their minds will start to think clearly. It is the devil who has made them believe wrong things. He has made them do what he wants. Now they can get free from his power.

2Timothy4:8 So now a gift is waiting for me. The Lord will make me completely right with him. That will be like a crown which the Lord will give me on that great day when he judges people. James5:3 You have stored silver and gold coins to spend for yourselves. But dirt has spoiled those coins. That dirt will show that you are guilty. It will become like fire that is burning your bodies. In the last days of this world you have stored all this money for yourselves.
James5:4 But you have not paid the workers in your fields the money that you should have paid them. You have cheated them. Now they are shouting against you. The Lord of great power has heard them when they cry to him for help.
James5:5 During your life here on this earth
James5:6 You say that a righteous person is guilty. You have even killed him and he could not stop you.
James5:7 So
James5:8 You also must be patient
James5:9 My Christian friends
James5:10 Think about how God's prophets were also patient. They spoke messages on behalf of the Lord. Many troubles happened to them but they were patient. So
James5:11 We know that God blessed people who were like that. They continued to trust God when trouble happened to them. You have heard about Job. He waited patiently for God to help him in his troubles. You know how the Lord helped him in the end. Because of that
James5:12 Here is another important thing to remember
James5:13 Is anyone among you in trouble? Then they should pray to God about it. Or
James5:14 Is anyone ill? They should ask the leaders of the church to come and to pray for them. The leaders should put some olive oil on the sick person. They should pray for him with the authority of the Lord Jesus.
James5:15 If they pray in that way and they trust that God will answer them
James5:16 So you should tell each other about any wrong things that you have done. Then you will be able to pray for each other
James5:17 For example
James5:18 After that time
James5:19 My Christian friends
James5:20 Understand this: if you turn a person back from their bad ways
1Peter1:1 This letter is from me
1Peter1:2 God the Father already decided that you should be his people. God's Spirit has made you special people who would obey Jesus Christ. God chose you so that Jesus Christ's blood would make you really clean. I pray that God will help you and give you peace in your minds more and more.
1Peter1:3 We praise God
1Peter1:4 We wait to receive all the good things that he has prepared for us. Nobody can ever destroy those things. Nothing can spoil them. They will never become old or less beautiful. God is keeping those things in heaven for you.
1Peter1:5 God is using his great power to keep you safe
1Peter1:6 All this causes you to be very happy
1Peter1:7 The purpose of these troubles is to show whether you really trust God. When you believe in Jesus
1Peter1:8 You have never seen Jesus
1Peter1:9 You are happy now because God is saving you from sin. You believe in Jesus
1Peter1:10 A long time ago
1Peter1:11 They wanted to know when God's Messiah would come and who he would be. Christ's Spirit showed those prophets that the Messiah would suffer very much. After that
1Peter1:12 God showed his prophets that his messages about these things were not really to help them. Instead
1Peter1:13 So now
1Peter1:14 Obey God
1Peter1:15 God has chosen you to be his own people. He is holy. So you must be holy too
1Peter1:16 In the Bible God says this: ‘You must be holy
1Peter1:17 God knows what each person has done
1Peter1:18 Your ancestors lived in a way that did not make them truly happy. That is the way that you learned to live as well. But now God has saved you from that way of life. You know that he has bought you for himself. He did not buy you with things that do not continue for ever
1Peter1:19 Instead
1Peter1:20 Before the world began
1Peter1:21 Yes
1Peter1:22 You have obeyed God's true message. As a result
1Peter1:23 You now have a new life! It is not like a human life that quickly comes to an end. It is a new life that will never spoil. You received it because you believed God's message. That message continues for ever and it does not change.
1Peter1:24 The Bible says: ‘All people are like grass. They may seem beautiful for a short time
1Peter1:25 But the message of the Lord God will continue for ever.’ That message is the good news about Jesus Christ
1Peter2:1 So you must refuse to do any evil things. Do not tell lies to deceive people. Do not be hypocrites. Do not be jealous of other people. Do not say bad things against other people.
1Peter2:2 Instead
1Peter2:3 Do this
1Peter2:4 So come to the Lord Jesus. He is like a strong stone that a builder uses to build a house. But he is alive. People decided that he was not important. So they refused to accept him. But God had chosen him. To God
1Peter2:5 You yourselves are also like stones that are alive. God is using you
1Peter2:6 In the Bible
1Peter2:7 This stone is very valuable to you who believe in Jesus. But the Bible says this about people who do not believe in him: ‘The builders refused to use a certain stone. They thought that it had no value. But now that stone has become the most important stone at the corner of the building.’
1Peter2:8 The Bible also says this: ‘This stone will cause people to fall to the ground. It is a rock that will make them fall down.’ They fall down like that because they refuse to obey God's message. God already decided that this would happen to them.
1Peter2:9 But you are not like that. You are a special group of people that God has chosen. You serve God like priests who serve a King. You are his own holy people who belong to him. He has taken you out of the dark place of sin
1Peter2:10 In past times
1Peter2:11 My friends
1Peter2:12 You are living among people who do not know God. So be very careful that you always live in a good way. Sometimes these people may wrongly say that you do bad things. But if you continue to live in a good way
1Peter2:13 Obey all people who have authority in this world. Obey them
1Peter2:14 Also obey the officers that serve the king. He has sent them to punish people who do bad things. And they praise people who do good things.
1Peter2:15 God wants you to do only what is good. Then silly people will not be able to say bad things against you. Those people do not really know what is true.
1Peter2:16 You are free people. But you are servants of God. So do not think
1Peter2:17 Respect all people. Love the believers as you would love your family. Obey God as your master. Respect the king.
1Peter2:18 You who are slaves must obey your masters. Always respect them. Do that to every kind of master. Obey those who are good and kind. But also obey masters who are not fair to you.
1Peter2:19 This makes God happy. Maybe your master will punish you when you have done nothing wrong. If you know that you are serving God

1Peter3:1 I need to speak to you wives. You should each respect the authority of your own husband. Even if your husband does not believe God's message about Jesus Christ
1Peter3:2 He will see that you obey God and that you do things that are completely good.
1Peter3:3 Think about what makes you beautiful. The way that you prepare your hair does not really make you beautiful. Gold things or beautiful clothes do not make you beautiful.
1Peter3:4 Do not only think about your body
1Peter3:5 That was how holy women made themselves beautiful many years ago. They trusted God that he would help them. They respected the authority of their husbands.
1Peter3:6 Sarah was a woman like that. She obeyed her husband
1Peter3:7 You husbands must also respect your wives. Be kind to your wife and help her. Remember that she is not as strong as you are. Because God is very kind
1Peter3:8 Now I want to say this to you before I finish my letter. All of you should agree with each other in what you think. You should try to understand how other people are feeling. Love each other as brothers and sisters. Be kind to each other. Do not think that you are more important than other people.
1Peter3:9 If people do bad things to you
1Peter3:10 The Bible says this: ‘Do you want to enjoy your life? Do you want to be happy? Then you must be careful not to say evil things. Do not tell any lies.

1Peter3:18 Christ himself always did what was right. But he suffered on behalf of people who do what is wrong. He died once to take away all those wrong things. He did that to bring you near to God. People killed Christ's body
1Peter3:19 Like this
1Peter3:20 Those were the spirits of people who had refused to obey God long ago. At that time God waited patiently for them to turn to him while Noah was building a large ship. But only a few people agreed to go into that ship. God saved only eight people when the ship sailed on the deep water.
1Peter3:21 That shows how God now saves you. It is like a picture of baptism. The water of baptism does not wash dirt from a person's body. Instead

1Peter4:2 As a result
1Peter4:3 You have already had enough time in your life to do bad things. Those are the kinds of things that people who do not know God like to do. You did many evil things that your bodies wanted to do. You had sex in a wrong way. You often became drunk. You went to wild parties where you drank too much alcohol. You also worshipped useless idols.
1Peter4:4 But now you no longer live in the same wicked way as those people do. They continue to enjoy those wrong things all the time. But you do not join with them. So they insult you.
1Peter4:5 But those people will have to explain to God what they have done. He is ready to judge every person
1Peter4:6 That is why even people who have already died needed to hear the good news about Christ. Their bodies had to die
1Peter4:7 All things in this world will soon come to an end. So you must think clearly. Then you will be able to pray in a serious way.
1Peter4:8 Continue to love each other very much. That is more important than anything else. If you love people
1Peter4:9 Always be happy to have other believers as visitors in your homes.
1Peter4:10 God has helped each of you with a certain gift. Think carefully about how to use that gift from God well. Remember that God has given many different gifts to his people so that they can help each other.
1Peter4:11 Has God helped you to speak well? Then speak God's messages to people. Or has God helped you to serve other people? Then let God make you strong to do that. Do everything so that people will praise God. They will say that God is great
1Peter4:12 My friends
1Peter4:13 Instead
1Peter4:14 People may insult you because you belong to Christ. But God will bless you when that happens. It shows that the Holy Spirit is with you. He is God's great Spirit.
1Peter4:15 Do not murder anyone. Do not rob anyone. Do not do evil things. Do not try to cause trouble for other people. If you do those things
1Peter4:16 But if you receive trouble because you are a Christian
1Peter4:17 The time has come when God will start to judge people. He will first judge the people who belong to him. If he will start with his own people

1Peter5:1 Now I want to say something to you who are leaders of the groups of believers. Like you
1Peter5:2 Watch over the group of believers that God has given to you. Be like a shepherd that takes care of his sheep. Lead them well because you want to do it. Do not do it only because you have to do it. Do it in the way that God wants for you. Do not be a leader because you want to receive money for yourself. Instead
1Peter5:3 God has given you a group of believers to take care of them. Do not try to rule over them with power. Instead
1Peter5:4 Then our master
1Peter5:5 I want to say the same thing to you who are young men. You must respect the authority of the leaders. All of you who are believers must respect one another. Do not think that you are more important than other people. The Bible says: ‘God is against proud people. But he is kind to humble people.’
1Peter5:6 Remember that God is strong and powerful. So be humble in front of him. Then he will lift you up to a good place at the right time.
1Peter5:7 If you have any kind of trouble in your mind
1Peter5:8 Think seriously about how you live. Watch carefully for danger. Remember that the devil is your enemy. He wants to hurt you. He walks about like a hungry lion. He is looking for someone to kill and then eat!
1Peter5:9 But you must be strong to fight against him. Trust God completely. Remember this: Believers all over the world are having the same kind of trouble.

2Peter1:2 I pray that God will help you and give you peace in your minds more and more. You will grow as believers because you know God and you know Jesus
2Peter1:3 God is very powerful because he is God. He has used that power to help us. He has given us everything that we need to live in a good way. We can do things that please God. This is possible because now we know him. God has chosen us to be his people because he is very great and very good.
2Peter1:4 Because of this
2Peter1:5 God has done all this to help you. So now that you believe in Christ
2Peter1:6 Then you should learn to control yourselves properly. That is not all. You should also learn to be patient and brave. And you should live in a way that pleases God.
2Peter1:7 You should not only please God
2Peter1:8 You should continue to grow as believers in all these ways. Then it will show that you really know our Lord Jesus Christ. People will see that your life has good results.
2Peter1:9 But some people do not live in these good ways. They do not think that these things are important. They are like blind people. They cannot really see anything properly. They have forgotten that God has made them clean from all the sins that they did in past times.
2Peter1:10 So
2Peter1:11 Then God will bring you safely into the kingdom of our Lord Jesus Christ that continues for ever. Jesus Christ is our Saviour
2Peter1:12 You already know these things. You know that they are completely true. But because they are important
2Peter1:13 While I am still alive on this earth I will continue to make sure that you remember them. I think that it is right for me to do that.
2Peter1:14 I know that I will die soon. Our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me that clearly.
2Peter1:15 After I am no longer with you
2Peter1:16 We told you that our Lord Jesus Christ is powerful. We also told you that he will return to earth again. We were not telling you false stories that came from people's clever ideas. Instead
2Peter1:17 We were with him on the holy mountain when his Father God spoke to him. God gave honour to his Son
2Peter1:18 
2Peter1:19 We also have the prophets' messages from long ago. They show us very clearly that these things are true. The prophets' words are like a light that shines in a dark place. That light continues to shine in the dark until the dawn comes. So you need to think about those messages carefully. Then the morning star will rise at dawn and it will bring light deep inside you.
2Peter1:20 It is very important that you should understand this: The prophets' messages in the Bible did not come from their own ideas. That never happens.
2Peter1:21 None of the prophets' messages came because any person wanted to say something. Instead

2Peter2:8 Lot lived among those bad people. Every day
2Peter2:9 We know that the Lord God has done all these things in past times. When people who respect him have trouble
2Peter2:10 God will certainly punish those people who want to enjoy bad things for themselves. They do the wrong things that their bodies want to do. They do not want to obey anyone's authority. These false teachers are not afraid of what other people think about them. They think that they themselves are very important. They are not afraid to say many bad things against the great angels in heaven.
2Peter2:11 The angels are more powerful than the false teachers are. But even these angels will not say bad things against them when the Lord is present.
2Peter2:12 But these false teachers speak against things that they do not understand. They are like wild animals. Their minds cannot think properly. They only do what their nature causes them to do. Wild animals are born so that people can catch them and kill them. In the same way
2Peter2:13 They have caused other people to have much trouble. In return
2Peter2:14 All the time
2Peter2:15 These false teachers have left the way which is good and right. Like Balaam
2Peter2:16 But a donkey told him that he was not obeying God. We know that donkeys cannot talk. But that donkey spoke with a human voice. It stopped the prophet Balaam from doing more stupid things.
2Peter2:17 These false teachers promise good things that will never happen. They are like a dry well that has no water. They are like clouds in a storm which the wind blows away so that no rain comes. God will certainly send them to the darkest place in hell. He has kept that place ready for them.
2Peter2:18 The messages that these teachers speak seem very important. But their words really mean nothing. In that way
2Peter2:19 The false teachers tell these believers to do whatever they want to do. They promise that this will make them really free. But the false teachers themselves are not free. Instead
2Peter2:20 People like that learned about our Lord Jesus Christ
2Peter2:21 It would have been better for them if they had never known God's good way. But they did understand about the right things that God wants us to do. And then they turned away from that way of life. They stopped obeying God's good rules

2John1:1 This letter comes from me
2John1:2 I love you because we have all come to understand what is true. It will remain with us for ever.
2John1:3 God our Father
2John1:4 I was very happy to hear about some of your children. I am happy because they are living in a good way. They are obeying the true message about God. They are doing what God our Father has told us to do.
2John1:5 So now
2John1:6 How do we show real love? We show it when we live in a way that makes God happy. We obey his commands. He tells us to love one another
2John1:7 I say this because many false teachers are travelling around everywhere. They do not agree that Jesus Christ was born as a human baby. People like that are telling lies. They are enemies of Christ.
2John1:8 So be careful! Do not believe their lies. Do not lose the good things that we have worked for. Make sure that you receive all the good things that God has promised to give you.
2John1:9 Remember the message which Christ taught. Anyone who teaches his own ideas instead of Christ's true message does not belong to God. Continue to believe what Christ taught. Anyone who does that belongs to God the Father and to his Son
2John1:10 Someone may come to you and teach you something that is different. Do not receive that person into your house. Do not receive him as a Christian.
2John1:11 If you receive people like that as Christian people
2John1:12 I want to tell you many more things
2John1:13 The believers here say ‘hello’ to you. They are the children of your sister that God has chosen.
3John1:1 This letter comes from me
3John1:2 My friend
3John1:3 I was very happy when I heard news about you. Some Christian friends came here to tell me about you. They said that you are living in a good way. That is the way that God's true message tells us to live.
3John1:4 I am happy when I hear that my children are living like that. When they obey God's true message
3John1:5 My friend
3John1:6 These men have spoken to the group of believers here about your love. Please continue to help them when they visit you. Give them the things that they need to continue on their journey. That will make God happy.
3John1:7 They are travelling to different places to tell people about Jesus Christ. They do not accept any gifts from those people who are not Christians.
3John1:8 Because of that
3John1:9 I have already written something to the group of believers. But Diotrephes refuses to accept our authority. He really wants to be the only leader among them.
3John1:10 So
3John1:11 My friend
3John1:12 All the believers say that Demetrius is a good person. He obeys God's true message. That also shows clearly that he is a good person. We also say the same thing about him. You know very well that what we say about him is true.
3John1:13 I would like to tell you many more things. But I do not want to write them in a letter.
3John1:14 Instead
3John1:15 I pray that God will give you peace. All your Christian friends here say ‘hello’ to you. Say ‘hello’ for me to each of my friends there
Jude1:1 This letter is from me
Jude1:2 I pray that God will be kind to you and that he will give you peace in your minds more and more. I want you to know more and more how much God loves you.
Jude1:3 My friends
Jude1:4 I say this because certain bad people have secretly come among you. They have turned away from God. They are trying to change God's true message. They teach that we can do any evil things that we want to do. They say that God will be kind and he will forgive us for all those things. They do not accept the authority of Jesus Christ
Jude1:5 Remember that the Lord brought Israel's people out of Egypt where they had been slaves. But later he destroyed all those same people who did not trust him. I want you to remember these important things. You should already know all about them
Jude1:6 Also remember that some angels refused to obey God. They took more authority for themselves than God had given them. They left their own proper place with God in heaven. So God has tied them up as prisoners for ever in a completely dark place. He will keep them there until that great day when he will judge everyone.
Jude1:7 Also
Jude1:8 These false teachers who have come among you do the same kind of bad things. Their dreams deceive them so that they use their bodies to do disgusting things. They refuse to obey God's authority. They insult God's powerful angels.
Jude1:9 Even the leader of God's angels
Jude1:10 But these false teachers speak bad things against anything that they do not understand. They are like animals that cannot think properly. They just do the things that their nature causes them to do. Those are the things that are destroying them.
Jude1:11 It will be very bad for them! They have lived in the way that Cain lived. They have made the same mistake that Balaam made
Jude1:12 When you eat special meals together as believers
Jude1:13 Those false teachers are like water in the sea that the wind blows during a storm. The dirt that comes to the top of the water is like the disgusting things that those people do. They are like stars that have moved away from their proper places in the sky. God has prepared a completely dark place for them. God will keep them there for ever.
Jude1:14 Long ago
Jude1:15 He will judge all people. He will punish everyone who lives in a way that does not please him. He will punish them because of all the wicked things that they have done. He will punish them because of all the bad words that they have spoken against him.’
Jude1:16 Those people are always complaining. They like to accuse other people. They do any bad things that will make themselves happy. They speak great things about themselves
Jude1:17 But as for you
Jude1:18 They said to you
Jude1:19 It is people like this who cause you to argue with one another
Jude1:20 But you
Jude1:21 Continue to live in a way that shows God's love. As you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to come
Jude1:22 Help those people who do not know whether to trust God. Be kind to them.
Jude1:23 Some people are in great danger. You must save them from the fire of God's punishment. Also be kind to people who have done wicked and disgusting things. But be careful! Do not let their sins make you bad too.
Jude1:24 God is able to keep you safe so that you continue to trust him. One day you will stand in front of him and you will be very happy. God will bring you to that place and you will be completely good. You will be able to stand near to God
Jude1:25 He is the only God. He is the one who saves us because of our Lord Jesus Christ. So we praise him! We say that he is very great! He rules with power and authority. This has always been true. It is true now

Revelation2:1 ‘Now write this to the angel of the church at Ephesus: “This is the message to you from the one who holds the seven stars. He holds them carefully in his right hand. He walks among the seven gold lampstands.
Revelation2:2 I know everything that you do. You have worked much for me. You have continued to work patiently. I know that you do not accept bad people as your friends. Some of these bad people say that they are apostles of Jesus. You have thought carefully about what they teach people. And you have discovered that it is not true.
Revelation2:3 You have been patient and strong in times of trouble. You have continued to serve me because you believe in me. You have not stopped when you become tired.
Revelation2:4 But I have something to say against you. You loved me very much when you first believed in me. You do not love me as much now.
Revelation2:5 Think carefully about this! You do not now live in the way you did at the beginning. So you must change the way you live. Do again the good things that you did at the beginning. If you do not change the way you live
Revelation2:6 But this is something that you do well: you hate the bad things that the Nicolaitans are doing. I also hate what they do.
Revelation2:7 God's Spirit is now speaking to people in the churches. You should recognize that the Spirit is speaking. Everyone who knows that should listen to what he says. I will let everyone who wins against Satan eat my fruit. This fruit is from the tree that gives people true life. And this tree grows in God's own garden.” ’

Revelation2:11 God's Spirit is speaking to you in the churches. You should understand what the Spirit is saying to you. You have ears

Revelation2:18 ‘Write this to the angel of the church at Thyatira: “This is the message to you from the Son of God. He has eyes that burn brightly like flames of fire. His feet shine like bright metal.
Revelation2:19 I know everything that you do. I know that you have great love. I know that you believe in me. I know that you continue to serve me well. You are patient and strong. You do these good things now more than you did at the beginning.
Revelation2:20 But I have something to say against you. You let that woman Jezebel teach you. She says that she is a prophet from God. But she leads my servants into bad ways. She teaches them to have sex in wrong ways. And she teaches them to eat food that people have given to idols.
Revelation2:21 I have given her time to stop doing these bad things. But she refuses to change how she lives. She does not stop having sex with other men.
Revelation2:22 Listen to this! I will cause her to suffer on her bed
Revelation2:23 I will send a terrible illness to kill those who obey her. Then all the people in the churches will know that I look deep inside them. I know what you really want. I know what you really think. I will give each of you what is right for you
Revelation2:24 But I say this to you other people in Thyatira. You have not agreed with what Jezebel taught. You have not learned what they call ‘Satan's deep secrets’. So I say this to you: I will not tell you to obey any other difficult rules.
Revelation2:25 But continue to obey my message that you have already received. Believe in me until I come.
Revelation2:26 Continue to obey me until the end and win the fight against Satan. If anyone does that

Revelation2:28 He will have the same authority that I received from my Father. I will also give him the morning star.

Revelation3:1 ‘Write this to the angel of the church at Sardis: “This is the message to you from the one who holds the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. I know everything that you do. I know that people praise you. They think that you are alive. But that is not true
Revelation3:2 Wake up! You must become strong again. Do not let the little bit of life that you have completely disappear! I know that there are still more things for you to do to please my Father God.
Revelation3:3 So remember God's message that you have heard. Obey what God has said. Turn away from the wrong things that you do. Change the way that you live. If you do not wake up
Revelation3:4 But you have a few people among you in Sardis who have not done these bad things. They are like people who have kept their clothes clean. They will walk with me and they will wear white clothes. They are good people and it is right for them to wear these white clothes.
Revelation3:5 Everyone who wins against Satan will wear white clothes like they do. I will never remove their names from God's book of life. I will say clearly to my Father and his angels that those people belong to me.

Revelation3:12 Everyone who wins against Satan will be safe in God's house. I will make them like a strong pillar in the temple of my God. They will never leave that place again. I will write on them the name of my God and the name of his city. The name of that city is the new Jerusalem. It will come down from heaven. My God will send it down to the earth. Revelation8:1 The Lamb opened the seventh seal. Then it became completely quiet in heaven for about half an hour.
Revelation8:2 Then I saw the seven angels who stand in front of God. Someone gave them seven trumpets.
Revelation8:3 Then another angel came. He stood at the altar where people offered gifts to God. He had a gold bowl to put incense in. He received a lot of incense and he burned it as a gift to God. It made a nice smell and it mixed with the prayers of God's people. The angel offered the incense and the prayers on the gold altar that was in front of God's throne.
Revelation8:4 When he did that
Revelation8:5 Then the angel took the gold bowl and he filled it with fire from the altar. Then he threw it all down onto the earth. There were loud noises and the sound of thunder. There was bright lightning
Revelation8:6 Then the seven angels with the seven trumpets prepared to make a noise with their trumpets.
Revelation8:7 The first angel made a sound with his trumpet. Strong rain with stones of ice fell on the earth. The rain was mixed with fire and blood and it poured down. It burned a third of the earth. It destroyed a third of the trees and all the green grass.
Revelation8:8 Then the second angel made a sound with his trumpet. I saw something like a very big mountain that was burning with fire. This mountain fell down into the sea. A third of the sea became blood.
Revelation8:9 A third of the animals that lived in the sea died. It also destroyed a third of the ships on the sea.
Revelation8:10 Then the third angel made a sound with his trumpet. I saw a very big star
Revelation8:11 The name of the star means ‘It tastes bad’. A third of the water on the earth became bad. Many people died because they drank that bad water.
Revelation8:12 Then the fourth angel made a sound with his trumpet. A third of the sun became dark. A third of the moon and a third of the stars also became dark. There was no light for a third of the day and there was no light for a third of the night.
Revelation8:13 Then I looked up. I saw an eagle that was flying high in the sky above me. I heard it say with a loud voice

Revelation9:3 Then locusts came out of the smoke and they came down on the earth. They received power to cause great pain
Revelation9:4 They knew that they must not do anything bad to the grass on the earth. They must not attack any plant or any tree. They could only attack people who did not have God's seal on the front of their heads.
Revelation9:5 The locusts did not have authority to kill those people. But they could cause them very bad pain for five months. Their pain was like the great pain from a scorpion when it hurts someone.
Revelation9:6 During that time of great pain
Revelation9:7 The locusts were like horses that were ready to fight in a war. They wore something on their heads that seemed to be gold crowns. Their faces looked like human faces.
Revelation9:8 Their hair was like women's hair. Their teeth were like lions' teeth.
Revelation9:9 They had shields over the front of their bodies
Revelation9:10 They had poison in their tails
Revelation9:11 The king that ruled them was the angel of the deep hole. His name is ‘Abaddon’ in the Hebrew language. In the Greek language his name is ‘Apollyon’.
Revelation9:12 The first great trouble has now finished. But after all these things
